Cascading consumer updates in `Pylkkanen2008`, `SeeligerRepp2018`, `Tay2024`, `AkinboFwangwar2026`, `Fragments/Japanese/Prosody.lean`, `Fragments/Hausa/Tone.lean` (`= true := rfl` ŌåÆ `:= by decide`; tuple-conjunct positions get `by decide`; computational `if`/`==` consumers wrap `decide`).

## [0.230.37] - 2026-04-19

### Removed
- **`Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/Hudson1984.lean` deleted** along with its import from `Linglib.lean` and the orphan `@book{hudson-1984}` entry in `references.bib`. Hudson1984 was an early, redundant study file: its theorems duplicated `Theories/Syntax/DependencyGrammar/Core/NetworkIntegration.lean` (the canonical WG-network definition of subject-auxiliary inversion as subtype inheritance) and added no Phenomena-level data. The bib entry's DOI (`10.1093/oxfordhb/9780199738632.013.0019`) was hallucinated ŌĆö that DOI points to a 2012 Oxford Handbook chapter, not the 1984 Blackwell book; the chain of `@cite{hudson-1984}` calls picked up its claims by reference rather than from the actual source. Per `CLAUDE.md` "Never cite specific page ranges from memory", the citations referencing "pp. 117-118" were also unverifiable.

### Changed
- **`@cite{hudson-1984}` ŌåÆ `@cite{hudson-2010}`** in 7 source files (`Theories/Syntax/DependencyGrammar/{Coordination, LongDistance, Formal/NonProjective, Core/Basic, Core/LexicalRules, Core/NetworkIntegration}.lean`). The 2010 textbook (`hudson-2010`, doi:`10.1017/cbo9780511781964`) is the verified source covering the same WG content; the 1984 monograph entry is dropped.
- **`NetworkIntegration.lean`**: word-class string `interrogative_auxiliary` ŌåÆ `inverted_auxiliary` (the term Hudson 2010 actually uses) and theorem renames `interrogative_aux_inherits_main_verb_slot` ŌåÆ `inverted_aux_inherits_main_verb_slot`, `network_interrogative_aux_argStr` ŌåÆ `network_inverted_aux_argStr`. Hallucinated "pp. 117-118" page citations and the fabricated AVM-style block quote dropped from the module docstring; replaced with a plain prose summary of the subtype-inheritance analysis.
- **`Fragments/English/Auxiliaries.lean`** consumer-list docstring updated to drop the deleted Hudson1984 study.

## [0.230.36] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/RonderosEtAl2024.lean` critical-review pass v2**: four fixes from a mathlib-PR-style review of v1 (0.230.35) against the actual @cite{ronderos-etal-2024} PDF. **(1) Hallucinated cite key**: `bierwisch-1987` (used in 2 places in `SedivyEtAl1999.lean` and propagated into the v1 Ronderos rewrite) replaced with the actual bib entry `bierwisch-1989` ("The Semantics of Gradation"); flagged by `python3 blog/scripts/gen_bibliography.py --check`. **(2) Empirically too-strong `material_effect_smaller`**: v1's `Cell` carried a `Language : english | hindi | hungarian` field and the interaction predicate quantified universally over languages ŌĆö but Figure 3 of the paper shows English material contrast effect Ōēł English color contrast effect (0.24 vs 0.25), so universal pairwise dominance fails. The paper deliberately treats LANGUAGE as a clustering unit (random effect) rather than a typed predictor ŌĆö cross-linguistic generalization is *methodology*, not paradigm structure. **Dropped `Language` enum entirely**; Cell is now `{ contrast : ContrastCondition; adjType : AdjType }`. **(3) Missing second empirical family**: the paper's ┬¦3 ┬Č4 reports a SECOND finding beyond the contrast-effect family ŌĆö in the No-Contrast condition, baseline total looks (target + cross-category competitor) for scalar adjectives are *lower* than for color (╬▓ = 0.25, z = 2.80, p < 0.01) and material (╬▓ = 0.24, z = 2.40, p < 0.05). v1 dropped this entirely. Added two new `SatisfiesRonderosPattern` fields: `scalar_baseline_lower_than_color` and `scalar_baseline_lower_than_material`, both built on a new paradigm-level predicate. **(4) New cross-study bridge `two_mechanisms_factorise`**: articulates the orthogonal-routes claim ŌĆö `requiresComparisonClass` (semantic route, captured by Sedivy/Bierwisch's "scalar requires comparison-class binding") and `noiseDiscrimination` (perceptual route, captured by `RSA.Noise`'s color > size > material ordering) make independent predictions; Ronderos's pattern is the joint envelope. The theorem records the type-level identity `(d.requiresComparisonClass, d.noiseDiscrimination) = (sedivy_lookup d, ronderos_noise_lookup d)` for each `PropertyDomain`. Proof witness `trivialLooks` extended to provide target/competitor values in the no-contrast condition (color 5/5, scalar 1/4, material 6/2 ŌĆö yielding scalar baseline sum 5 < color 10 and < material 8). Module docstring gains an "Open architectural threads" section flagging two deferred deeper integrations: (a) noise-perturbed `IncrementalSemantics` witness instantiating `SatisfiesRonderosPattern` directly from RSA + `RSA.Noise`; (b) `Theories/Semantics/Gradability` classification bridge (relative vs absolute scalar adjectives) so the scalar/material/color axis is theory-derived rather than enum-stipulated.
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean` ┬¦7 added**: paradigm-level lift for the baseline-restrictiveness pattern that the Ronderos finding instantiates. **`roleSum [Add R] [Zero R] (roles : List ObjectRole) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) (c : Cell) : R := (roles.map (fun r => looks r c)).sum`** ŌĆö sums look proportions across a role list at a given cell; the natural building block for "total looks to inference-relevant referents" measures (target + cross-category competitor for adjective-conditioned displays). **`RoleSumLowerInBaselineWhen [HasContrastCondition Cell] [Add R] [Zero R] [LT R] (baseline : ContrastCondition) (roles : List ObjectRole) (P Q : Cell ŌåÆ Prop) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) : Prop`** ŌĆö the qualitative claim that, for every `P`-cell `cP` and `Q`-cell `cQ`, `roleSum roles looks (setContrast baseline cP) < roleSum roles looks (setContrast baseline cQ)`. Anchored on @cite{aparicio-xiang-kennedy-2015}, @cite{aparicio-2017} (which establish the scalar-adjective baseline-restrictiveness pattern Ronderos extends cross-linguistically); the paradigm contract documents this as a *recurring* visual-world pattern, not a Ronderos one-off. **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`**: `bierwisch-1987` ŌåÆ `bierwisch-1989` in 2 places (lines 71, 289) ŌĆö pre-existing hallucination, not introduced here. Build clean: 921 jobs through Ronderos2024, 2672 jobs through the entire Reference subtree (Sedivy + Ronderos + Cohn-Gordon all green).

### Added
- **Bibliography entries**: `aparicio-xiang-kennedy-2015` (SALT 25:413ŌĆō432, doi:10.3765/salt.v25i0.3128 ŌĆö visual-world processing of gradable adjectives, anchoring study for the baseline-restrictiveness pattern); `aparicio-2017` (PhD thesis, University of Chicago, *Processing context-sensitive expressions: The case of gradable adjectives and numerals* ŌĆö extended the SALT 25 line of work). Both entries verified against the Ronderos paper's reference list (page 1226).

## [0.230.35] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean` ┬¦6**: stratified and effect-size predicates for multi-factor visual-world designs. **`contrastEffect [HasContrastCondition Cell] [Sub R] (role : ObjectRole) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) (c : Cell) : R`** projects out `looks role (setContrast .noContrast c) ŌłÆ looks role (setContrast .contrast c)` ŌĆö the paradigm-level shape of the "target advantage" / "competitor reduction" measure that downstream studies report; the lens laws on `setContrast` ensure this depends only on the non-contrast factors of `c`. **`ContrastReducesRoleLooksWhen [HasContrastCondition Cell] [LT R] (P : Cell ŌåÆ Prop) (role : ObjectRole) (looks : LookProportion Cell R) : Prop`** + abbrev **`ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen P looks`** restrict the contrast-effect claim to cells satisfying a sub-cell predicate `P` ŌĆö needed because a 2 ├Ś *k* design (e.g. @cite{ronderos-etal-2024}'s 2 ├Ś 3 contrast ├Ś adjective-type) can have one stratum where the universal `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` claim is empirically *false* (Ronderos's material adjectives). **`ContrastEffectLargerFor role P Q looks`** is the qualitative shape of an "X ├Ś condition" interaction: every `P`-cell's contrast effect strictly exceeds every `Q`-cell's. Universal quantification over both strata is the strongest qualitative reading; mean-over-mean readings need a real-valued aggregator (out of scope for the paradigm contract per `CLAUDE.md` Processing scope). **`ContrastReducesRoleLooks_iff_when_True`** records that the ┬¦5 universal-form predicate is the `P := True` case of the stratified version, so studies migrating to the stratified API can recover their existing universal claims by passing the trivial filter.
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/RonderosEtAl2024.lean`** rewritten end-to-end ("mathlib-quality" overhaul matching the @cite{cohn-gordon-goodman-potts-2019} / @cite{sedivy-etal-1999} treatment). **Deleted**: every `ClusterResult` / `TargetAdvantageResult` / `BaselineResult` Float-statistics struct (`cluster_color`, `cluster_scalar`, `cluster_material`, `cluster_interaction`, `targetAdv_*`, `baseline_*`, `Language.n`, `totalN`, `itemsPerType`, `totalTrials`); every `_significant` Bool theorem and every `native_decide` proof over Float literals; every `rfl`-over-stipulated-statistics theorem (`contrastive_inference_pattern`, `effect_size_ordering`, `discrimination_predicts_contrastive`, `noise_ordering_matches_effect_ordering`, `converging_evidence_with_kursat_degen`, `agrees_with_sedivy_on_scalar`, `disagrees_with_sedivy_on_color`, `two_routes`) ŌĆö the "encoding conclusions as definitions" anti-pattern called out in `CLAUDE.md`. **Added**: `inductive AdjType := color | scalar | material` (with `Fintype` deriving) + `AdjType.toDomain` mapping each to its `Core.PropertyDomain` (color ŌåÆ `.color`, scalar ŌåÆ `.size`, material ŌåÆ `.material`); `inductive Language := english | hindi | hungarian` (study-local factor with no paradigm consumer, scoped by future cross-linguistic stratification predicates); `structure Cell { contrast : ContrastCondition; adjType : AdjType; language : Language }` with `HasContrastCondition` lens (struct-update implementations + three `rfl` lens-law proofs) and `HasDisplayKind` projection (between-study constant `.objectArray`); explicit absence of `HasTask` instance documented inline (the task is fixed across all cells, and a constant projection cannot satisfy `setTask`/`taskOf` lens laws ŌĆö studies that hold a paradigm factor constant should *omit* the lens, not stub it with a constant). **Sub-cell predicates** (the strata): `ColorCells`/`ScalarCells`/`MaterialCells` as `Cell ŌåÆ Prop` filters on `c.adjType`; `InferenceTriggeringCells := ColorCells Ōł© ScalarCells` (the union scoped by the interaction predicate). **`structure SatisfiesRonderosPattern (looks : LookProportion Cell R) : Prop`** with three fields: `color_contrast_reduces_competitor : ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen ColorCells looks` (paper ┬¦3.1 cluster 240ŌĆō600 ms; ┬¦3.2 ╬▓ = 0.24, t = 2.41, p < 0.05 ŌĆö documented in field docstring), `scalar_contrast_reduces_competitor : ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen ScalarCells looks` (cluster 260ŌĆō500 ms; ╬▓ = 0.19, t = 2.02), `material_effect_smaller : ContrastEffectLargerFor .crossCategoryCompetitor InferenceTriggeringCells MaterialCells looks` (interaction cluster 280ŌĆō600 ms). Material null encoded by *absence* of a positive clause, not by `┬¼ ContrastReducesCompetitorLooksWhen MaterialCells looks` ŌĆö statistical power and direction-of-null are prose, not theorem; the interaction field is the stronger qualitative claim that survives without statistical machinery (material's effect strictly smaller, not exactly zero). **Non-vacuity witness**: `trivialLooks : LookProportion Cell ŌäÜ` constructed so that competitor looks are 1 in every contrast cell and depend on adjective type in no-contrast cells (color 5, scalar 4, material 2 ŌĆö yielding contrast effects 4/3/1 with strict inference-triggering > material ordering); `trivial_satisfies_pattern : SatisfiesRonderosPattern trivialLooks` proved by `obtain Ō¤©k, a, lŌ¤® := c; simp only [SubCellPred] at hc; subst hc; cases k <;> simp [trivialLooks]` for the per-stratum fields, and the interaction field by destructuring both cells, casing on the `InferenceTriggeringCells` disjunction, and `cases kP <;> cases kQ <;> simp [contrastEffect, trivialLooks] <;> norm_num`. **┬¦7 cross-study bridges** are *type-level* (no `rfl`-over-stats): `scalar_shares_sedivy_domain` (both studies place scalar effect on `.size`, which `requiresComparisonClass`); `color_does_not_require_comparison_class` and `material_does_not_require_comparison_class` (`PropertyDomain.requiresComparisonClass = false` for both ŌĆö Sedivy/Bierwisch lexical-only mechanism predicts no color effect, contradicted by `color_contrast_reduces_competitor`); `effect_ordering_aligns_with_noise_discrimination` (delegates to existing `RSA.Noise.color_gt_size`/`size_gt_material`, no re-proof); `adjType_to_noise_discrimination` (records the structural identity that `Core.PropertyDomain.noiseDiscrimination Ōłś AdjType.toDomain` resolves to the corresponding `RSA.Noise` constant). Build clean: 921 jobs through Ronderos2024, 2672 jobs through the entire Reference subtree (Sedivy + Ronderos + Cohn-Gordon all green); preexisting Phonology/`hasValue` errors in `Fragments/{Korean,Akan,English}/Phonology.lean` are from the in-progress f1f44769 phase-C refactor and unrelated to this change.

## [0.230.34] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean`** polymorphised over codomain. **`LookPrediction (Cell : Type) := ObjectRole ŌåÆ Cell ŌåÆ ŌäÜ`** renamed and generalised to **`LookProportion (Cell R : Type) := ObjectRole ŌåÆ Cell ŌåÆ R`** ŌĆö paradigm consumers may now choose `R := ŌäÜ` (exact rationals, common for tableaux) or `R := ŌäØ` (default for RSA-derived posteriors that arise from `Real.exp`/`Real.log`-mediated speakers). Paradigm-level look-prediction predicates (`ContrastReducesRoleLooks`, `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks`, `ContrastSpeedsTargetLooks`) re-stated with `[LT R]` predicates so the Cohn-Gordon RSA-derived bridge can apply them directly to ŌäØ-typed posteriors without first coercing to ŌäÜ. RT predicates kept on ŌäÜ (RT is conventionally rational in this paradigm). **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`** threaded the new type parameter through the local API: the study's `SatisfiesSedivyPattern` and the trivial non-vacuity witness now take `(R : Type)` with the same `[LT R]`/`[Sub R]` instances. Existing call-sites within the study unchanged (instances default to ŌäÜ).
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean`** rewired the ┬¦10 Sedivy bridge onto the polymorphised `Paradigms.VisualWorld`. **New `SedivyScene_NoContrast` namespace**: a fresh four-word `IncrementalSemantics` (`Word := tall | cup | pitcher | key` ŌĆö `.short` deliberately omitted because the shortCup referent is absent from the no-contrast display, leaving the speaker with no scene-anchored use for the word) over the three remaining referents (`tallCup`/`tallPitcher`/`key`, sharing `SedivyScene.Referent` so a single Cell-typed projection can read off both scenes). Rationale documented inline: CG's `IncrementalSemantics` is a *scene-specific* production model (the `wordApplies` tier is a Boolean truth predicate over the scene's referent set), not a lexicon-wide model ŌĆö keeping `.short` in `Word` with no extension would yield `0/0` in `incrementalSem`, which is mathematically `0` in mathlib but not reducible by `rsa_predict`'s RExpr evaluator. **`cgSedivyLooks : LookProportion SedivyEtAl1999.Cell ŌäØ`** is the Cell-typed projection that reads off both scenes' L1 posteriors after the prefix `[.tall]`. The definition carries a load-bearing docstring naming the *Bayesian posterior linking hypothesis* ŌĆö the assumption that the proportion of fixations on referent `r` at a moment when L1's posterior over referents has shape `p` is itself proportional to `p(r)` ŌĆö and acknowledges this hypothesis is editorial: Cohn-Gordon do not state it (they only run the comprehension model and report posteriors), so the linking layer is the formaliser's bridge, flagged honestly in prose. A weaker alternative (Luce-choice activation monotone in posterior) is mentioned as the conservative fallback. **Bridge theorem `cgSedivyLooks_satisfy_contrast_reduces_competitor`**: proves `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks (R := ŌäØ) cgSedivyLooks` directly ŌĆö for every cell `c`, `incRSA_sedivy_noContrast.L1 .tall .tallPitcher > incRSA_sedivy.L1 .tall .tallPitcher` (1/2 vs 2/5) ŌĆö by destructuring `c` (lens preserves `c.task` and `c.typicality`, so the goal collapses to a single inequality), `show`-ing the goal in unfolded form, then closing via `rsa_predict` (single 76ms reflection). **Removed**: the old `sedivyBundle_noContrast` (which kept `.short`, broken by the 0/0 issue) and the ┬¦11.5 `sedivyLooksAfterTall : ObjectRole ŌåÆ ŌäØ` paradigm-flavor projection plus `sedivyLooksAfterTall_target_gt_competitor` ŌĆö both superseded by the typed `LookProportion`/`ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` bridge. Removed unverified Allopenna/Magnuson/Tanenhaus 1998 citation rather than fabricate metadata. ┬¦8 docstring updated to drop the prior "out of scope" caveat about Cell-typed `LookPrediction`. Build clean: 2670 jobs, 0 errors, 0 new sorries.

## [0.230.33] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/EventRelativity.lean` BoolŌåÆProp migration**: `possibility`, `necessity`, `orderedPossibility`, `orderedNecessity` now take `(p : W ŌåÆ Prop) [DecidablePred p]` instead of `(p : W ŌåÆ Bool)`. `duality`, `empty_ordering_reduces`, `doxastic_necessity_eq`, `doxastic_possibility_eq` re-stated and re-proved over `Prop` predicates (Bool boolean negation `!p w` becomes `┬¼ p w`; `p w' = true` collapses to `p w'`). In-file test theorems use `(┬Ę = .took)` etc. instead of `(┬Ę == .took)`. `Decidable` instances preserved via `[DecidablePred p]` guards on the boundary.
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/ModalIndefinites.lean` cascade**: `modalComponent`, `modalIndefiniteSat`, `upperBoundedSat` now take `P Q : Entity ŌåÆ W ŌåÆ Prop` with `[ŌłĆ y, DecidablePred (P y)] [ŌłĆ y, DecidablePred (Q y)]` decidability witnesses. Worked-example predicates `isBook`, `isAvailable`, `isCard`, `canGrab` migrated to `Prop` with explicit per-case `Decidable` instances (`instDecidableTrue`/`instDecidableFalse`). Removes the deferred TODO at line 151.
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/Assert.lean` cascade**: speech-act test theorems (`declarative_leave_possible`, `declarative_stay_possible`, `imperative_leave_permitted`, `imperative_stay_not_permitted`, `speech_act_modulates_domain`) updated `(┬Ę == .leave)` ŌåÆ `(┬Ę = .leave)` predicates. Other 9 listed consumers of `EventRelativity` only used the unaffected anchor/binder/projection API and required no changes.

## [0.230.32] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/PhonologicalAlternation/Studies/Belth2026.lean` rewrite**: deleted the fabricated "Cser-style fine tier" apparatus (`fineTier`, `latinDissimFine`, `latinFineOCP`, `predictedFine`, every `_fine` theorem, `coarse_fine_disagree_on_lunaris`, `latinDissimFine_covers_all`) ŌĆö @cite{belth-2026} learns ONE rule on the `[+cons]` tier (rule 54), and *lunaris* is one of the ~3% errors the Tolerance Principle tolerates, not a case the fine tier "saves". Renamed `latinDissimCoarse` ŌåÆ `latinDissimRule` and `predictedCoarse` ŌåÆ `predicted`. **Fixed `/v/` treatment**: `LatSeg.isCons .v => false` (Belth treats orthographic Ō¤©vŌ¤® as `[w]`, `[ŌłÆcons]`) ŌĆö the choice that lets the new *pluvalis* example surface as `[r]` (tier-preceding consonant of `/pluv-/` is `/l/`, not `/v/`). Added *pluvalis* (`pluvalis_ur`, `pluvalis_predicts_r`, `pluvalisERC`, etc.) to complete @cite{belth-2026} ex. 5. Reframed `lunaris_predicts_l_INCORRECT` as the genuine empirical limit. **Wired Tolerance Principle**: `tolerates_six_one : tolerates 6 1` (proof via `Real.log_le_sub_one_of_pos` + `le_div_iffŌéĆ`) and `latinDissimRule_tolerated_on_examples` connecting `latinData.length` and the filter-based exception count to the threshold. **Subregular bridge**: `consTier_apply_eq_tierProject` grounding `LatSeg.consTier` in the canonical `Core.Computability.Subregular.tierProject` from the TSL formalism (@cite{lambert-2022}, @cite{heinz-rawal-tabor-2011}). **┬¦9 OT tableau bridge rebuilt on the actual `[+cons]` tier**: `latinOCP := mkOCPOnTier "OCP/[+cons]" LatSeg.consTier id`; per-example optimum theorems for *navalis*/*popularis*/*pluvalis*/*floralis*/*legalis*; honest `lunaris_optimal_is_l_INCORRECT` showing the OT analysis fails the same way the rule does; per-example ERCs (`popularisERC_is_simple`, `pluvalisERC_is_simple`, `lunarisERC_isContradictory`, three `_isTrivial`); `latinERCSet_consistent_without_lunaris` (consistency holds on the 5 non-lunaris contrasts); `latin_OCP_dominates_starR` derives the unique Hasse arrow.
- **`Core/Constraint/OT/ERC.lean`**: promoted three reusable lemmas. **`simpleERC_consistent : i ŌēĀ j ŌåÆ ERCSet.consistent [simpleERC i j]`** with explicit witness construction (identity ranking when `i.val < j.val`; `Equiv.swap i j` otherwise) ŌĆö replaces ad-hoc `Ō¤©Ranking.id n, by decideŌ¤®` patterns at study sites. **`@[simp] simpleERC_apply_W` / `simpleERC_apply_L`** characterising the W- and L-positions. **Decidable instance** for `ERC.isContradictory` (`inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ Ōł¦ _))`) ŌĆö was missing despite `isTrivial` having one; enables `by decide` proofs of contradictoriness in study files.
- **`Core/Constraint/Evaluation.lean`**: cleaned up scaffolding `noncomputable example : LinearOrder (ViolationProfile 3) := inferInstance` and the two unnamed `example`s for pointwise `Add`/`Zero`. Replaced with named (non-simp) theorems `ViolationProfile.add_apply` and `ViolationProfile.zero_apply` ŌĆö accessible to consumers, no behavioral change to simp set.

### Added
- **Bibliography entries**: `cser-2010` (Cser 2010, *The -alis/-aris allomorphy revisited*, John Benjamins, doi:10.1075/cilt.310.02cse ŌĆö feature inventory and descriptive blocking generalisation cited by Belth); `yang-2016` (Yang 2016, *The Price of Linguistic Productivity*, MIT Press, doi:10.7551/mitpress/9780262035323.001.0001 ŌĆö Tolerance Principle source); `ringen-heinamaki-1999` (Ringen & Hein├żm├żki 1999, NLLT 17(2):303ŌĆō337, doi:10.1023/A:1006158818498 ŌĆö Finnish vowel harmony OT account cited for D2L's Finnish rule).

## [0.230.31] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Pragmatics/RSA/Incremental.lean`**: graduated the scene-specific incremental-RSA bundle pattern out of `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean` into a reusable theory module. **`structure IncrementalSemantics (U W : Type) [DecidableEq U]`** bundles the three pieces that uniquely determine an @cite{cohn-gordon-goodman-potts-2019} scene: `wordApplies : U ŌåÆ W ŌåÆ Bool` (word-level Boolean truth), `completeUtterances : List (List U)` (closed set of full utterances), `worlds : List W` (referents to normalize over). Every other model ingredient is *derived*: `uttSem` (utterance-level conjunction), `trueExtCount`/`viableExtCount` (extension counts via `List.filter` + `List.isPrefixOf`), `noncomputable incrementalSem` (the ┬¦2.2 Ō¤”pfxŌ¤¦(r) ratio), `noncomputable l0Utt` (literal listener over complete utterances), and `noncomputable toRSAConfig : RSAConfig U W` (the No-Brevity sequential RSA model with `s1Score = L0`, ╬▒ = 1, no cost, `transition := fun ctx w => ctx ++ [w]`, `initial := []`). **Promoted deep theorem `l0Utt_ge_inv_card`**: for *any* complete utterance `utt` true of `r Ōłł worlds`, `sem.l0Utt utt r Ōēź 1 / sem.worlds.length` ŌĆö the ┬¦2.4 weakly-informative bound that certifies greedy chain-rule unrolling never produces an utterance arbitrarily worse than uniform. Proof is purely combinatorial (`List.length_pos_of_mem` + `List.length_filter_le` + `one_div_le_one_div_of_le`) and reusable across scenes ŌĆö concrete studies need only prove their candidate utterance is in `completeUtterances` and is true of the target. **New bib entry**: `sedivy-2007` (Philosophy Compass 2(3): 475ŌĆō496, doi:10.1111/j.1747-9991.2007.00082.x), the methodological review CG actually cite for the contrastive-inference paradigm.

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean`**: rewired onto the graduated `IncrementalSemantics` bundle. Three triplicated definition blocks (Figure 1 scene + Sedivy contrast scene + Rubio-Fern├Īndez STOP scene) collapsed into three one-line bundle instantiations (`figureOne`, `SedivyScene.sedivyBundle`, `RubioFernandezScene.rfBundle`) plus three `noncomputable def incRSA := bundle.toRSAConfig` derivations. **┬¦2.4 case-analysis replaced**: `greedyUnroll_weakly_informative` now invokes the generic `figureOne.l0Utt_ge_inv_card` once per call site, with the bundle-side proof carrying the combinatorics ŌĆö replaces the earlier 3-case `cases r` enumeration that re-derived the bound per referent. **┬¦8 D&R bridge deleted**: removed the `incRSA_s1Score_is_L0 := rfl` thin theorem, the editorial Dale-Reiter table, and the `import Linglib.Phenomena.Reference.Studies.DaleReiter1995` ŌĆö the No-Brevity classification is already true by construction once `toRSAConfig` is the canonical builder, and the editorial alignment table belongs in prose, not Lean. **┬¦3.2 citation fix**: replaced hallucinated `@cite{sedivy-etal-1999}` provenance for the contrastive-inference scene with `@cite{sedivy-2007}` (the review CG actually cite); kept `sedivy-etal-1999` mention in the docstring as the editorial empirical bridge to the original 1999 study. All 7 Findings (`adj_first_for_target` through `incremental_prefers_bare_noun`) preserved and close via `rsa_predict` against the bundle-derived configs (struct projection + def unfolding reduces correctly through `toRSAConfig`). `Linglib.lean` updated to import the new theory module before `Quantities`.

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/CohnGordonEtAl2019.lean`**: completed the audit of @cite{cohn-gordon-goodman-potts-2019} formalisation against the paper, fixing two hallucinations and adding four sections for previously unmodelled paper content. **Hallucinations fixed**: dropped the false `@cite{dale-reiter-1995}` provenance from the module docstring header (Cohn-Gordon does not cite Dale-Reiter ŌĆö the ┬¦8 conceptual comparison is editorial); replaced the content-free `incremental_rsa_is_no_brevity` (which was `rfl` over a stipulated table ŌĆö the "encoding conclusions as definitions" anti-pattern called out in CLAUDE.md) with `incRSA_s1Score_is_L0`, a real theorem about the actual `incRSA` proving its `s1Score` is literally L0 with no cost re-weighting (the formal sense in which `incRSA` is in the No-Brevity regime). **┬¦7b weakly-informative greedy unrolling (paper ┬¦2.4)**: added `l0Utt` (literal listener over complete utterances), `greedyUnroll : Referent ŌåÆ List Word` (Figure 1 scene), and the bound theorem `greedyUnroll_weakly_informative : ŌłĆ r, l0Utt (greedyUnroll r) r Ōēź 1/3` proving the architectural sanity property the paper establishes ŌĆö even without a global view of the utterance space, greedy chain-rule unrolling cannot do worse than uniform. Proof factors through a `incSem_val` private lemma and `decide` on the `trueExtCount`/`viableExtCount` natural-number computations. **┬¦9 global RSA + divergence theorem (paper ┬¦2.4)**: added `inductive Utterance := dress | redDress | redObject` (the three complete utterances treated as atomic options), `globalRSA : RSAConfig Utterance Referent` (one-shot, ╬▒=1, no cost), and `global_prefers_red_dress : globalRSA.S1 () .redDress .redDress > globalRSA.S1 () .redDress .dress` proving the standard-RSA preference for the more-informative full utterance ŌĆö the central empirical wedge between global and incremental architectures, opposite of Finding 7 (`incremental_prefers_bare_noun`). **┬¦10 Sedivy ┬¦3.2 paradigm bridge**: added `namespace SedivyScene` containing the contrast-condition reference game from @cite{sedivy-etal-1999} (5 words: tall/short/cup/pitcher/key; 4 referents: tallCup/shortCup/tallPitcher/key; 6 complete utterances including bare nouns), `incRSA_sedivy : RSAConfig Word Referent` (same architecture as the main `incRSA`, only the lexicon differs), and the prediction theorem `sedivy_contrast_inference : incRSA_sedivy.L1 .tall .tallCup > incRSA_sedivy.L1 .tall .tallPitcher` (3/5 vs 2/5) ŌĆö the anticipatory contrastive-inference effect. Bridge to `Paradigms.VisualWorld` via `sedivyLooksAfterTall : ObjectRole ŌåÆ ŌäØ` mapping each role to the L1 posterior on the corresponding referent, plus `sedivyLooksAfterTall_target_gt_competitor` restating the prediction in paradigm vocabulary. Honest scope statement in docstring: only the contrast condition is modelled, so a full `SedivyEtAl1999.LookPrediction Cell` typed by the `HasContrastCondition` lens is out of scope (would require a no-contrast variant model that Cohn-Gordon does not separately construct). **┬¦11 Rubio-Fern├Īndez ┬¦3.1 STOP machinery (paper ┬¦3.1)**: added `namespace RubioFernandezScene` with `inductive Word := dress | hat | red | blue | stop`, `inductive Referent := redDress | blueHat`, `wordApplies` (with `stop` not applying to any referent), `completeUtterances` (all four terminating in `stop`), and structural invariants `completeUtterances_terminate_in_stop` and `stop_never_applies` (proved by `decide`). Cost-dependent over-modification theorem (`S1^UTT-IP(red dress, STOP | R1) > S1^UTT-IP(dress, STOP | R1)` for some cost regime) deferred ŌĆö formalising it requires `Real.exp` over a cost schedule and a quantitative argument that doesn't reduce via `rsa_predict`; structural framework is in place for future work. **Citation cleanup**: rewrote module docstring header to position cross-study connections (Sedivy 1999, Rubio-Fern├Īndez 2016) explicitly and acknowledge the ┬¦8 D&R comparison is editorial. New imports: `Linglib.Paradigms.VisualWorld`. Build clean: 5201 jobs, 0 new errors. New `rsa_predict` invocations: 2 (`global_prefers_red_dress`, `sedivy_contrast_inference`) ŌĆö the latter is the largest model (4 referents ├Ś 5 words = 20 cells, sequential context = `List Word`).

## [0.230.29] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Semantics/Questions/Answerhood/ProbabilisticAnswerhood.lean`**: three `of_witness` constructor lemmas (`relevantS.of_witness`, `probAnswersS.of_witness`, `someResolutionStrengthenedS.of_witness`) absorb the `convert h` boilerplate that bridges the `Classical.propDecidable` instances baked into the `noncomputable` `open Classical in` predicate bodies with the user-supplied structural `[DecidablePred]` instances at consumer sites. Rationale: `relevantS`/`probAnswersS`/`someResolutionStrengthenedS` deliberately use Classical decidability so the spec body type-checks without per-alternative `[DecidablePred]` hypotheses (avoiding a `[ŌłĆ a, DecidablePred (┬Ę Ōłł a)]` quantifier that's too universal to synthesize); concrete consumers compute `condProbSet`/`probOfSet` values with their own structural instances, and the two instance choices agree because `Decidable` is a `Subsingleton`. Each helper takes the alternative `a` plus a structural-instance proof of the inequality and produces the existential witness with the Classical-instance form. The added module-level docstring section "Witness constructors ŌĆö Classical/structural Decidable bridge" documents the call-site pattern (`refine probAnswersS.of_witness s q prior a hAltMem ?_; rw [...]; norm_num`) and notes that destructuring (`obtain` + `convert hStr`) on the negative direction in infelicity proofs remains the residual idiom (because the destructured hypothesis carries Classical instances and must be bridged at use sites). New infrastructure available for future consumers; existing Thomas2026 proofs unchanged. Build clean: 973 jobs through `ProbabilisticAnswerhood`, 1012 jobs through `Thomas2026`.

## [0.230.28] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Constraint/` consolidation**: unified scattered OT machinery under `Core/Constraint/` with mathlib-style sub-namespaces. **Moves**: `Core/Logic/ConstraintEvaluation.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Constraint/Evaluation.lean` (namespace `Core.Constraint.Evaluation`); `Core/Logic/OT.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Constraint/OT/Basic.lean` (namespace `Core.Constraint.OT`); `Core/OT/{Defs.lean, ERC.lean}` merged ŌåÆ `Core/Constraint/OT/ERC.lean`; `Core/OT/{Antimatroid.lean, ERCAntimatroid.lean}` merged ŌåÆ `Core/Constraint/OT/Antimatroid.lean`; old `Core/OT/` directory deleted. **Rationale**: prior layout scattered constraint/OT code across `Core/Logic/`, `Core/OT/`, and `Core/Constraint/`; the sub-namespace approach (mathlib pattern: flat top + named subpackages) consolidates without the `LexProfile` collision that a flat namespace would have caused (`Core.Constraint.LexProfile` is an abbrev for `Lex (Fin n ŌåÆ ╬▓)` while the evaluation-side struct lives at `Core.Constraint.Evaluation.LexProfile`). **Consumer updates**: 47 files updated (33 `open Core.OT` ŌåÆ `open Core.Constraint.OT`; ~16 `open Core.ConstraintEvaluation` ŌåÆ `open Core.Constraint.Evaluation`; ~15 `import Linglib.Core.Logic.OT` ŌåÆ `import Linglib.Core.Constraint.OT.Basic`; ~7 `import Linglib.Core.Logic.ConstraintEvaluation` ŌåÆ `import Linglib.Core.Constraint.Evaluation`; qualified refs `Core.OT.NamedConstraint`, `Core.OT.ConstraintFamily` in Belth2026.lean; docstring references in Profile.lean/Weighted.lean/SiptarTorkenczy2000.lean/Krifka2007.lean/Wang2023.lean). Build clean: 5201 jobs, 0 errors.

## [0.230.27] - 2026-04-19

### Fixed
- **`Theories/Pragmatics/Particles/Additive.lean`** + **`Phenomena/Focus/AdditiveParticles/Studies/Thomas2026.lean`**: closed all 5 outstanding `sorry`s in the Thomas2026 study and fixed an over-strong quantification in `maximalityCondition`. **Spec fix**: the Set/Prop port of `maximalityCondition` quantified the witnesses-capture obligation over *every* alternative `a Ōłł Issue.alt RQ`; the original Bool spec only iterated over the *strengthened* resolutions (those where `P(a | ANT Ōł® ŽĆ) > P(a | ANT)`). Re-stated the predicate as `ŌłĆ a Ōłł alt, strengthened a ŌåÆ ŌłĆ w, prior w > 0 ŌåÆ w Ōłł ANT ŌåÆ w Ōłł a ŌåÆ w Ōłł ŽĆ` so the obligation is vacuous on unstrengthened cells. **Proofs**: structural derivations over `Set (Fin 4)` for all 5 verification theorems ŌĆö `hotel_too_felicitous` (felicitous, polar question, all four conditions), `ecstatic_too_infelicitous` (3-cell `ofList` partition, non-triviality fails because ŽĆ entails the only strengthened cell), `pizza_spaghetti_too_felicitous` (4-cell `ofList` partition, all four conditions including maximality with three vacuous branches), `cello_too_infelicitous` (polar question, maximality fails because `wŌéü Ōłł ANT Ōł® baileyPlays \ baileyPlaysCello`), `cross_product_too_infelicitous` (polar question, conjunction-strengthening half fails because both alternatives stay at conditional-probability extremes). Each scenario's proof rests on three layers of helpers: per-set `DecidablePred` instances enabling `Fin.sum_univ_four`-driven probability computations, named lemmas for each `probOfSet` / `condProbSet` value, and an `alt`-characterisation lemma (`Issue.alt_polar_of_nontrivial` for polar contexts, `Issue.alt_ofList_of_pairwise_disjoint_nonempty` for partition contexts). The `convert h` idiom bridges the `Classical.propDecidable` instances baked into `noncomputable` predicates with the user-supplied structural ones via `Subsingleton (Decidable P)`. Build clean: 1012 jobs in the Thomas2026 dependency cone, 0 sorries remaining in this file.

## [0.230.26] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean`** + **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`**: made the paradigm typeclasses *load-bearing* by giving `HasContrastCondition` and `HasTask` proper lens APIs and lifting the qualitative empirical patterns to the paradigm level. **Lensified typeclasses**: `HasContrastCondition` now carries `contrastOf : Cell ŌåÆ ContrastCondition`, `setContrast : ContrastCondition ŌåÆ Cell ŌåÆ Cell`, and three lens laws as fields (`contrastOf_setContrast`, `setContrast_contrastOf`, `setContrast_setContrast`); same shape for `HasTask` (`taskOf`/`setTask` + 3 laws). The lens laws are what make the typeclasses load-bearing ŌĆö they let paradigm-level predicates universally quantify over a base cell and apply the lens to swap the manipulated factor, so any study whose `Cell` instances the class inherits the predicates without re-deriving them per design. `HasDisplayKind` deliberately stays projection-only (display kind is between-study constant, not within-study manipulation; documented). **Paradigm-level predicates** (new): `ContrastSpeedsResponse (rt : RTPrediction Cell) := ŌłĆ c, rt (setContrast .contrast c) < rt (setContrast .noContrast c)`, `ContrastReducesRoleLooks (role : ObjectRole) (looks : LookPrediction Cell) := ŌłĆ c, looks role (setContrast .contrast c) < looks role (setContrast .noContrast c)`, abbrev `ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` specialising `role := .crossCategoryCompetitor`, and `ContrastSpeedsTargetLooks` (the dual, target-look acceleration from Sedivy Tables 7, 11). **`ObjectRole` expanded** from 4 to 7 constructors per Huettig et al. 2011 ┬¦2.1.2: added `cohortCompetitor`, `rhymeCompetitor`, `semanticCompetitor` to cover Allopenna et al. 1998 + Huettig & Altmann 2005 paradigms (extending the type rather than encoding role distinctions in study-local enums). **Sedivy migration**: `Cell` now derives `Fintype` (added `Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Sigma` import); `HasContrastCondition Cell` and `HasTask Cell` instances now provide structure-update lens implementations (`{ c with contrast := k }`) with `rfl` proofs of all three lens laws; new `HasDisplayKind Cell` instance pinning Sedivy's displays to `.objectArray`. **Predicate cleanup**: dropped local `PredictsContrastSpeedsTarget` and `PredictsContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` (superseded by paradigm-level versions); kept study-specific `ContrastAttenuatesTypicality` (typicality isn't a paradigm primitive ŌĆö it's Sedivy's operationalisation of "exemplar goodness" via the norming study). `SatisfiesSedivyPattern` now bundles the paradigm-level `ContrastSpeedsResponse`/`ContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` + study-specific `ContrastAttenuatesTypicality`. `trivial_satisfies_pattern` non-vacuity witness re-proved against the new lens-based predicates by destructuring the cell and casing on `typicality`. **Empirical discriminator surfaced**: the lens-based predicates *enforce* task invariance by quantifying over `c` while `setContrast` preserves `c.task` ŌĆö so any theory satisfying the patterns is committed to the task-invariant mechanism that Sedivy Exp 3 uses to discriminate the lexical comparison-class account from the presupposition account. Build clean: 5203 jobs, 0 errors.

## [0.230.25] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Linglib/Paradigms/`** (new top-level directory) + **`Paradigms/VisualWorld.lean`** + **CLAUDE.md** "Paradigms/ - Experimental-Task Contract Layer" section: introduces a fifth top-level architectural layer sitting between `Theories/` and `Phenomena/Studies/`. **Architectural role**: paradigms are the *contract layer* ŌĆö they specify the typed input an experiment provides (display kind, condition cells, object roles) and the typed shape of output a theory must produce (RT prediction, look prediction). Theories themselves remain paradigm-agnostic; bridge theorems in `Studies/` translate theory-native predictions into paradigm-typed predictions and prove the qualitative patterns documented in the study file. Dependency-wise `Paradigms/` depends on `Core/` only and is a sibling of `Theories/`/`Fragments/` rather than nested under `Phenomena/` (which would have buried it among ~76 phenomenon directories). **Anchoring discipline**: each `Paradigms/X.lean` file is anchored on a methodological review or meta-analysis of paradigm X, with the review's data-field ontology serving as the file's type structure ŌĆö no invented categories. **`VisualWorld.lean`** anchored on @cite{huettig-rommers-meyer-2011} (Acta Psychologica 137(2): 151ŌĆō171, doi:10.1016/j.actpsy.2010.11.003), with each type citing the section of the review it comes from: `DisplayKind` (semiRealisticScene/objectArray/printedWord, ┬¦2.1.1), `ObjectRole` (target/contrastingObject/crossCategoryCompetitor/distractor), `ContrastCondition` (contrast/noContrast), `Task` (directAction/lookAndListen/verification/description, ┬¦2.1.3), `RTPrediction (Cell : Type) := Cell ŌåÆ ŌäÜ`, `LookPrediction (Cell : Type) := ObjectRole ŌåÆ Cell ŌåÆ ŌäÜ` (note the typed `ObjectRole` slot ŌĆö predicates over look predictions can therefore make role-specific claims at the type level), plus `HasContrastCondition`/`HasTask`/`HasDisplayKind` typeclasses for studies to extend with their own condition cells. Per the CLAUDE.md "Processing scope" rule, deliberately omits stimulus-timing details (preview duration), participant population metadata, data-analysis pipeline (ROIs, time windows, ANOVA vs. growth curves), and eye-tracker apparatus enums ŌĆö paradigms specify the *experimental contract*, not the *measurement apparatus* or *analysis pipeline*. New bib entry `huettig-rommers-meyer-2011` (subfield `semantics/methodology`, role `foundational`). Added `Linglib.Paradigms.VisualWorld` import to `Linglib.lean` between `Tactics/` and `Fragments/` blocks. Updated CLAUDE.md layered diagram, directory structure, and added "Paradigms/" subsection documenting the contract-layer role and review-anchoring discipline.

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Reference/Studies/SedivyEtAl1999.lean`** migrated to consume `Paradigms.VisualWorld`. **Removals**: dropped local `ObjectRole` (was dead code ŌĆö defined but never used in the file), local `ContrastCondition`, local `Task` (now imported with the canonical four-element enum from Huettig et al. 2011 ┬¦2.1.3), local `RTPrediction`/`LookPrediction` abbreviations. **Renamings**: `Trial` ŌåÆ `Cell` (per the per-cell-not-per-trial naming critique ŌĆö the structure represents a cell in the 2├Ś2├Ś2 design, not a single eye-tracking observation). **Type-safety improvement**: `LookPrediction` now has the paradigm-typed shape `ObjectRole ŌåÆ Cell ŌåÆ ŌäÜ`; `PredictsContrastReducesCompetitorLooks` selects `looks .crossCategoryCompetitor` at the type level rather than relying on a docstring convention that the prediction function silently models competitor looks. **New typeclass instances**: `HasContrastCondition Cell` and `HasTask Cell`, hooking the study's condition cells into the paradigm's typeclass machinery. **New theorem `adjDomain_requires_comparison_class`**: the size domain requires comparison-class binding, derived by `rfl` from `Core.PropertyDomain.requiresComparisonClass` rather than asserted in prose ŌĆö making the load-bearing connection between Sedivy's empirical paradigm and the comparison-class typology in `Core.PropertyDomain` machine-checkable. **New non-vacuity witness**: `trivialRT`/`trivialLooks` + `trivial_satisfies_pattern` proves `SatisfiesSedivyPattern` is satisfiable (without this the structure could in principle be uninhabited and the empirical contract could be vacuous). **Proof-style cleanups**: `unfold X; norm_num` ŌåÆ `norm_num [X]` idiom for the two norming-verification theorems. Cross-study consumers (`EngelhardtEtAl2006.lean`, `RonderosEtAl2024.lean`) reference `SedivyEtAl1999.adjDomain` (kept) and don't reference any of the dropped types ŌĆö both build clean unchanged. Build clean: 5203 jobs, 0 errors.

## [0.230.24] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/Tier.lean`** + **`Theories/Phonology/Subregular/TierProjection.lean`**: collapsed the two-tier-formalisms duplication onto a single source of truth. **Before**: `Subregular.tierProject T xs := xs.filter (decide Ōłś T)` and `Core.Tier.apply (Core.Tier.byClass p) xs := List.filterMap (fun y => if p y then some y else none) xs` were definitionally distinct (`List.filter` vs `List.filterMap`) but operationally identical on class-membership tiers, with `0.230.20`'s `apply_byClass_eq_tierProject` discharging the equality via `Tier.apply_byClass`. **After**: `tierProject T xs` is defined directly as `Core.Tier.apply (Core.Tier.byClass (fun x => decide (T x))) xs` ŌĆö a definitional alias that commits the codebase to `Core.Tier` (in `Core/StringHom.lean`) as the single primitive for tier projection. The bridge theorem `apply_byClass_eq_tierProject` now reduces to `rfl`. New helper lemma `tierProject_eq_filter` (`tierProject T xs = xs.filter (decide Ōłś T)`) preserves Lambert's filter-form characterisation as a public theorem rather than the definition. The simp lemmas `tierProject_nil` (still `rfl`), `tierProject_cons` (re-proved via `tierProject_eq_filter`), and `tierProject_univ` (re-proved via `tierProject_eq_filter`) keep their existing call-sites working unchanged. `tierProject_ofByClass` in `TierProjection.lean` re-proved via `tierProject_eq_filter` + `Tier.apply_byClass`. Added `import Linglib.Core.StringHom` to `Subregular/Tier.lean`. Mathlib-style payoff: phonology code that already uses `Core.Tier` (autosegmental tonal/featural tiers, the harmony machinery, `mkOCPOnTier` via `Tier.apply`) and subregular code that uses `tierProject` (TSL grammars, `OCP.TSLGrammar.ocp`, `Hierarchy.lean`'s `IsStrictlyLocal.toIsTierStrictlyLocal`) now share a single underlying primitive ŌĆö no parallel formalisms to keep in sync, and the autosegmentalŌåösubregular bridge is no longer a theorem to discharge but a definitional consequence. Build clean across all five affected files (`Tier.lean`, `Hierarchy.lean`, `TierProjection.lean`, `OCP.lean`, `OptimalityTheory/Constraints.lean`).

## [0.230.22] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Subregular/OCP.lean`**: second new bridge under the phase-G `Subregular/` umbrella, connecting the Obligatory Contour Principle (the prototypical phonological dissimilation constraint, expressed in OT as a markedness constraint via `mkOCPOnTier` in `OptimalityTheory/Constraints.lean`) to the subregular complexity class TSL_2 (`Core.Computability.Subregular.TSLGrammar`). **`ocpForbidden ╬▒ : Set (Augmented ╬▒)`**: the forbidden 2-factor set `{[some x, some x] | x : ╬▒}`, parameterised over the alphabet ŌĆö boundary-adjacent factors `[none, some x]` and `[some x, none]` are deliberately excluded so the OCP penalises only interior adjacent identicals. **`TSLGrammar.ocp (p : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Bool) : TSLGrammar 2 ╬▒`**: the TSL_2 grammar with tier predicate `(p ┬Ę = true)` and permitted set `(ocpForbidden ╬▒)ßČ£`, capturing "no two adjacent identical symbols on the tier defined by `p`." **`adjacentIdentical_eq_zero_iff_isChain`**: the OT score's 0-violation condition reformulated as the mathlib-idiomatic relational chain `List.IsChain (┬Ę ŌēĀ ┬Ę)` ŌĆö proved by structural induction with `List.isChain_cons_cons`, requiring `[BEq ╬▒] [LawfulBEq ╬▒]`. **`mkOCPOnTier_zero_iff_isChain`** (the relational-form bridge, fully proved): a candidate's OCP score is zero iff its raw string projects under `Tier.byClass p` to a list with no two adjacent identical elements; the proof composes the previous lemma with `Tier.apply_byClass`. **`mkOCPOnTier_zero_iff_in_ocp_lang`** (the full TSL_2 language-form bridge, stated with `sorry` + TODO): a candidate's OCP score is zero iff its raw string is in `(TSLGrammar.ocp p).lang`. The remaining proof step needs to characterise `kFactors 2 (boundary 2 ys)` as the consecutive-pair list and show that "no factor lies in `ocpForbidden`" iff `IsChain (┬Ę ŌēĀ ┬Ę) ys`. New file lives in `Theories/Phonology/Subregular/`, sitting next to `TierProjection.lean`. 1 new file (~99 lines), 1 new sorry (the language-form bridge ŌĆö relational form is fully proved), builds clean against the full library.

### Fixed
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/Hierarchy.lean`** + **`Core/Computability/Subregular/Tier.lean`**: hallucinated section/figure references to @cite{lambert-2022} corrected. `Hierarchy.lean` previously cited "Lambert (2022) Figure 3.1" for the piecewise-local subregular hierarchy ŌĆö the correct location is **Figure 2.3** (Ch 2 on language-theoretic preliminaries). `Tier.lean` previously cited "Lambert ┬¦3.4" for the segmental-tier formulation of TSL ŌĆö Lambert's Ch 3 has ┬¦3.1 (model-theoretic descriptions), ┬¦3.2 (language-theoretic, with subsections on SL/LT/LTT/Piecewise) and ┬¦3.3 (automata); ┬¦3.4 doesn't exist. Updated to cite **Ch 3 ┬¦3.2** and describe the formal content (erasing string homomorphism keeping tier symbols) rather than locating by section number alone.

- **`Theories/Semantics/Questions/Answerhood/Support.lean` + `Theories/Pragmatics/Particles/Additive.lean` + `Phenomena/Focus/AdditiveParticles/Studies/Thomas2026.lean`**: continued BoolŌåÆSet/Prop migration, eliminating the legacy `Discourse.Issue` (List of `W ŌåÆ Bool`) consumers in the additive-particles subtree. **Support.lean** rewritten around `Core.Issue W` + `Set W`: `probSupportsS` (wraps `isPositiveEvidenceS`) + `noncomputable fullSupportS` (IKW Def 13: `Ōłā q Ōłł Issue.alt sentDen, dox ŌŖå q Ōł¦ isPositiveEvidenceS q answer prior`) + `fullSupport_fails_unbelievedS` (info-seeking interrogatives block discourse *only* via doxastic failure, regardless of probabilistic component). 218ŌåÆ75 lines. **Additive.lean** rewritten as `AdditiveContext W` (resolvedQuestion : Core.Issue W, antecedent : Set W, prior : Prior W) with four `noncomputable` Prop felicity conditions: `antecedentCondition` (probAnswersS), `conjunctionCondition` (probAnswers Ōł¦ someResolutionStrengthenedS), `nonTrivialityCondition`, `maximalityCondition` (universal closure over Issue.alt). `tooFelicitous = ant Ōł¦ conj Ōł¦ nonTrivial Ōł¦ maxim`. ## [0.230.20] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Subregular/TierProjection.lean`**: phase G of the mathlib-shaped Phonology regrouping ŌĆö first new bridge connecting the autosegmental tier formalism (`Core.Tier`, the Kleisli morphism `╬▒ ŌåÆ Option ╬▓` lifted via `List.filterMap`) to the subregular TSL formalism (`Core.Computability.Subregular.tierProject`, the predicate-filter `xs.filter (decide Ōłś T)`). The two are operationally identical in their shared common case ŌĆö a class-membership tier that keeps symbols of one type without relabeling ŌĆö and the bridge makes the connection explicit so phonological tiers (autosegmental tonal tier, sibilant tier, nasal tier) can be reused as the segmental tier of a TSL_k grammar without restating the projection. **Main theorem `apply_byClass_eq_tierProject`**: for any `T : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Prop` `[DecidablePred T]` and `xs : List ╬▒`, `Tier.apply (Tier.byClass (decide Ōłś T)) xs = tierProject T xs` ŌĆö both reduce to `List.filter`. **Adapter `TSLGrammar.ofByClass`**: builds a `TSLGrammar k ╬▒` from a `Bool` predicate `p : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Bool` and a permitted-factor set; the resulting tier predicate is `(p ┬Ę = true)`. **`tierProject_ofByClass` simp lemma**: `tierProject (TSLGrammar.ofByClass k p Ōłģ).tier = Tier.apply (Tier.byClass p)`. New file lives in `Theories/Phonology/Subregular/`, opening the umbrella for follow-up bridges (planned: OCP-as-TSL_2 forbidding adjacent identical interior factors on the projected tier; long-distance vowel/consonant harmony as TSL_2 over a featural tier; word-final neutralization as D_2 from `Subregular/Definite.lean`). 1 new file (~95 lines), 0 sorrys, 0 warnings, builds clean against the full library.

## [0.230.19] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Phonology/Process/`**: phase F of the mathlib-shaped Phonology regrouping. Two subdirectories and one standalone file moved from the flat `Theories/Phonology/` top level into a new `Process/` umbrella that clusters the derivational/process-oriented machinery: `RuleBased/` ŌåÆ `Process/RuleBased/` (subdir with `Defs.lean`: SPE-style rewrite-rule infrastructure); `Alternation.lean` ŌåÆ `Process/Alternation.lean` (input-output alternation patterns); `Harmony/` ŌåÆ `Process/Harmony/` (subdir with `Defs.lean` + `OT.lean`: vowel/consonant harmony engines, both rule-based and OT-based). Path-only refactor: namespaces preserved to avoid the per-symbol cascade; consumer imports updated across all 16 affected files (`Phenomena/{VowelHarmony, PhonologicalAlternation, CompensatoryLengthening, Phonotactics, Phonology}/Studies/`, `Fragments/{Akan, Finnish (├Ś2), English, Turkish, Hungarian, Korean}/`, `Theories/Phonology/Process/Harmony/{Defs, OT}.lean` self-imports); `Linglib.lean` reorganized so all four Process files cluster contiguously, immediately after the `Prosodic/` block. Net effect: completes the mathlib-shaped regrouping started in 0.230.16 ŌĆö `Theories/Phonology/` now consists of `Tier.lean` (alone at the top) plus four thematic subdirectories: `Featural/` (4 files), `Autosegmental/` (5 files), `Prosodic/` (8 files including `Moraic/` + `Syllable/` subdirs), `OptimalityTheory/` (6 files), `Process/` (4 files). Build clean across the moved files and their consumers.

## [0.230.18] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Phonology/OptimalityTheory/`**: phase E of the mathlib-shaped Phonology regrouping. Six standalone files moved from the flat `Theories/Phonology/` top level into a new `OptimalityTheory/` umbrella that clusters the OT family: `Constraints.lean` ŌåÆ `OptimalityTheory/Constraints.lean` (markedness/faithfulness primitives + `ConstraintSystem`); `Correspondence.lean` ŌåÆ `OptimalityTheory/Correspondence.lean` (correspondence theory @cite{mccarthy-prince-1995}); `OptimalParadigms.lean` ŌåÆ `OptimalityTheory/OptimalParadigms.lean` (output-output correspondence / paradigm uniformity); `StratalOT.lean` ŌåÆ `OptimalityTheory/StratalOT.lean` (stem/word/phrase strata); `CophonologyTheory.lean` ŌåÆ `OptimalityTheory/CophonologyTheory.lean` (lexical-stratum cophonologies); `Doubling.lean` ŌåÆ `OptimalityTheory/Doubling.lean` (reduplicative doubling). Path-only refactor: namespaces preserved to avoid the per-symbol cascade; consumer imports updated across all 24 affected files (`Phenomena/{PhonologicalAlternation, TemplaticMorphology, Phonology, Allomorphy, Tone, Case, Phonotactics, Reduplication}/Studies/`, `Fragments/{English/TDDeletion, Amharic/ConsonantalRoots}`, `Theories/Phonology/{Autosegmental/DominantCophAgreement, Prosodic/Templates, OptimalityTheory/{OptimalParadigms, Correspondence, Doubling}}`); `Linglib.lean` reorganized so all six OT files cluster contiguously. Net effect: `Theories/Phonology/` shrinks further as the OT-family files migrate under one umbrella; mirrors mathlib's clustering of related infrastructure under thematic subdirectories. Build clean across the moved files and their consumers.

Three standalone files and two subdirectories moved from the flat `Theories/Phonology/` top level into a new `Prosodic/` umbrella that unifies the prosodic hierarchy (mora < syllable < foot < prosodic word): `Accent.lean` ŌåÆ `Prosodic/Accent.lean`; `ProsodicWord.lean` ŌåÆ `Prosodic/Word.lean` (renamed since `Prosodic/` supplies the prefix); `Templates.lean` ŌåÆ `Prosodic/Templates.lean`; `Moraic/` ŌåÆ `Prosodic/Moraic/` (subdir with `Defs.lean` + `CompensatoryLengthening.lean`); `Syllable/` ŌåÆ `Prosodic/Syllable/` (subdir with `Defs.lean` + `Foot.lean` + `NaturalClass.lean`). Net effect: `Theories/Phonology/` shrinks further as the prosodic-tier files migrate under one umbrella, mirroring how mathlib clusters `Group/`, `Ring/` infrastructure under thematic subdirectories. ## [0.230.16] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Phonology/Featural/`**: phase C of the mathlib-shaped Phonology regrouping. Three featural-theory files moved from the flat `Theories/Phonology/` top level into the existing `Featural/` subdirectory (which previously contained only `Bundle.lean` from 0.229.949): `Features.lean` ŌåÆ `Featural/Features.lean` (Hayes 26-feature inventory + classification predicates + `Segment` representation @cite{hayes-2009}); `FeatureGeometry.lean` ŌåÆ `Featural/Geometry.lean` (Clements/Sagey hierarchical organization with 8 `GeomNode` constructors + dominance/subsumption theorems @cite{clements-1985} @cite{sagey-1986}); `ComplexSegments.lean` ŌåÆ `Featural/ComplexSegments.lean` (multi-articulator segment classification @cite{sagey-1986}). Renamed `FeatureGeometry.lean` ŌåÆ `Geometry.lean` since the directory name `Featural/` already supplies the prefix. Path-only refactor: namespaces (`Phonology`, `Phonology.FeatureGeometry`, `Phonology.ComplexSegments`) preserved to avoid cascading per-symbol updates across all 17 consumers; namespace alignment to `Phonology.Featural.{Features, Geometry, ComplexSegments}` deferred to a follow-up if/when the tradeoff makes sense. 17 import paths updated across `Theories/Phonology/{Autosegmental, Syllable, Harmony, RuleBased}/` infrastructure files, `Phenomena/{Allomorphy/Studies/Stojkovic2026, Phonology/Studies/Sagey1986}.lean`, and 8 `Fragments/{Korean, Turkish, Farsi, English, Hungarian, Finnish (├Ś2), Akan}/` consumers; `Linglib.lean` reorganized so all 4 Featural files cluster together at the top of the Phonology section. Net effect: `Theories/Phonology/` shrinks from 14 top-level files to 11 (removing the three featural ones); `Featural/` grows from 1 file to 4. Build clean across the whole consumer set.

## [0.230.15] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/`**: deep mathlib-quality refactor of all 7 files (B3 + B4) per a self-review that flagged 22 idiom drift issues. **Basic.lean**: copyright header added; `kFactorsList k xs` rewritten as `(xs.tails.filter (k Ōēż ┬Ę.length)).map (┬Ę.take k)` and renamed `kFactors` so it composes with mathlib's existing `List.tails`/`List.filter`/`List.take` rather than recursing structurally; the redundant `lift : List ╬▒ ŌåÆ Augmented ╬▒` alias removed in favor of inline `w.map some`; `lift_nil`/`lift_cons` deleted (subsumed by `List.map_nil`/`map_cons`); `boundary_zero` deleted in favor of the more useful `boundary_one`; the private `isInfix_cons_right` helper deleted in favor of Init's `IsPrefix.isInfix`/`IsInfix.trans`; `length_of_mem_kFactors`/`isInfix_of_mem_kFactors` reproven structurally via `List.mem_map`/`List.mem_filter`/`List.mem_tails`/`List.take_prefix` (3-line proofs vs the previous 12-line `induction xs with`/`by_cases hk` traversals); `Type u`/`universe u` switched to `Type*`. **StrictlyLocal.lean**: `IsSLk` ŌåÆ `IsStrictlyLocal`; `SLGrammar.isSLk_lang` ŌåÆ `SLGrammar.isStrictlyLocal_lang`; `@[ext]` added to `SLGrammar`; `Inhabited` instance added; the namespace-shadowing `SLGrammar.univ`/`empty` and the awkward `mem_empty_lang_iff` (whose proof contorted around `Set.notMem_empty`) deleted (lattice-style top/bot deferred to a later commit when `OrderTop`/`OrderBot` instances are wired); `mem_ofForbidden_lang` proof tightened from `simp [ofForbidden]` (with linter-flagged `lang` arg) to `simp only [mem_lang, ofForbidden, Set.mem_compl_iff]`. **LocallyTestable.lean**: `IsLTk`/`IsLTTk` ŌåÆ `IsLocallyTestable`/`IsLocallyThresholdTestable`; `mem_factorSet : f Ōłł factorSet k w Ōåö f Ōłł kFactors k (boundary k w)` added as a `@[simp]` characterization; banner-comment dividers replaced with markdown `## Main definitions`. **Tier.lean** (biggest change): the structurally-recursive `project T xs := match xs with | [] => [] | x :: xs => if T x then ...` rewritten as `tierProject T xs := xs.filter (decide Ōłś T)` ŌĆö `project_nil`/`_cons_pos`/`_cons_neg` collapse into the single `tierProject_cons` characterization (built from `List.filter_cons`); `decTier : DecidablePred tier` lifted to an instance field via `[decTier : DecidablePred tier]` + `attribute [instance] TSLGrammar.decTier`, eliminating the `have := G.decTier` boilerplate at every projection use site; `IsTSLk` (was nested under `namespace TSLGrammar`) ŌåÆ top-level `IsTierStrictlyLocal`; `mem_lang` simp lemma added. **Hierarchy.lean**: `IsSLk.toIsLTk` ŌåÆ `IsStrictlyLocal.toIsLocallyTestable`; `IsSLk.toIsTSLk` ŌåÆ `IsStrictlyLocal.toIsTierStrictlyLocal`; `IsLTk.toIsLTTk` ŌåÆ `IsLocallyTestable.toIsLocallyThresholdTestable`; the SLŌåÆTSL proof simplified via `ext` + `simp only [TSLGrammar.mem_lang, SLGrammar.mem_lang, tierProject_univ]` instead of `funext` + `show ... = ...` + multi-arg `simp only`; the `key : ŌłĆ w, w Ōłł G.lang Ōåö ŌłĆ f Ōłł factorSet k w, f Ōłł G.permitted` lemma kept inline (the proof is `Iff.rfl` so extracting a top-level lemma adds no clarity). **Piecewise.lean**: copyright header; `IsSPk` ŌåÆ `IsStrictlyPiecewise`; `Sublist` notation `<+` made available via `open List`; `@[ext]` + `Inhabited` instance added. **Definite.lean** (parameterization refactor): `DefiniteGrammar`/`ReverseDefiniteGrammar` unified under a single `EdgeDefiniteGrammar k e ╬▒` parameterized over a new `Edge` enum (`.left | .right`) with `Edge.takeAt e k xs` dispatching to `xs.take k` vs `xs.drop (xs.length - k)`; `DefiniteGrammar k ╬▒ := EdgeDefiniteGrammar k .right ╬▒` and `ReverseDefiniteGrammar k ╬▒ := EdgeDefiniteGrammar k .left ╬▒` are now `abbrev`s; `IsDefiniteK`/`IsReverseDefiniteK` ŌåÆ `IsDefinite`/`IsReverseDefinite`; the `DefiniteGrammar.lastK` helper folded into `Edge.takeAt`; the duplicated `lang`/`mem_lang`/`univ`/`lang_univ`/`isDefiniteK_lang` for `ReverseDefiniteGrammar` eliminated. Net effect: 7 files, ~30% LOC reduction overall, all proofs unchanged or shorter, kFactors and tierProject directly compose with mathlib's `List.filter`/`List.tails`/`List.take` infrastructure, no downstream consumers (these files were brand-new at 0.230.11/0.230.14 with no callers yet). Build clean, 0 sorrys, 0 warnings on the 7 refactored files.

## [0.230.14] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/{Piecewise, Definite}.lean`**: precedence-based and edge-based subregular classes (B4 of the mathlib-shaped Subregular family begun in 0.230.11). `Piecewise.lean` defines `SPGrammar k ╬▒` (set of permitted length-`k` *subsequences* ŌĆö non-contiguous selections, no boundary augmentation since position-blind) with `SPGrammar.lang w := ŌłĆ s, s.length = k ŌåÆ List.Sublist s w ŌåÆ s Ōłł G.permitted`, the `IsSPk` property, and a trivial `SPGrammar.univ` accepting all strings. SP_k captures long-distance precedence patterns (sibilant harmony as forbidden length-2 subsequence `[s, ╩ā]`) where SL_k's contiguous factors cannot, per @cite{rogers-pullum-2011} @cite{lambert-2022}. `Definite.lean` defines `DefiniteGrammar k ╬▒` (suffix-based, with `lastK k xs := xs.drop (xs.length - k)` for the length-`k` suffix; truncating `Nat` subtraction handles short strings as their own suffix) and `ReverseDefiniteGrammar k ╬▒` (prefix-based via `List.take k`), each with `lang`/`mem_lang`/`univ`/`lang_univ`/`IsXxxK` and `_isXxxK_lang` constructor witnesses. D_k and RD_k are weaker than SL_k (look at one fixed edge position rather than every contiguous window) but foundational for word-final neutralization (D_1, D_2) and word-initial prosodic templates (RD_k). Following the SL convention, the classical definite vs strictly-definite distinction is collapsed to a single generative formulation. Both files wired into `Linglib.lean`. 2 new files, 0 sorrys, 0 warnings.

## [0.230.13] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/CausalBayesNet.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the five validity/correlation predicates on `NoisyOR` and `WorldState`. `NoisyOR.isValid (n) : Prop := 0 Ōēż n.background Ōł¦ n.background Ōēż 1 Ōł¦ 0 Ōēż n.background + n.power Ōł¦ n.background + n.power Ōēż 1` (was `Bool := decide (...) && decide (...) && ...`); `WorldState.isValid (w) : Prop` likewise inlined as a 7-way conjunction (was a `&&`-chain over `decide` wrappers); `WorldState.isIndependent (w) : Prop := w.pAC = w.pA * w.pC` (was `Bool := w.pAC == w.pA * w.pC`); `WorldState.isPositivelyCorrelated`/`isNegativelyCorrelated` likewise drop the `==` Ōåö `=` and `>`/`<` Bool wrappers in favor of `Prop`. All five carry `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (...))`. The duplicate `WorldState.IsValid` (capital-I `Prop` mirror that previously coexisted with the `Bool` version) is removed ŌĆö `isValid` is now the single Prop-valued source of truth. Six internal theorems migrate (`valid_implies_pA_bounded`/`pC_bounded`/`pAC_bounded`/`pCGivenA_bounded`/`pCGivenNotA_bounded`/`law_of_total_probability`) ŌĆö hypothesis types switch `w.IsValid` ŌåÆ `w.isValid`, proofs unchanged. One external consumer (`Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Assertability.lean::assertability_score_bounded`/`conditional_probability_bounded`) updates `ws.IsValid` ŌåÆ `ws.isValid`.
- **`Core/Relativization/Hierarchy.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the AH-rank order predicates. `AHPosition.atLeastAsAccessible (p1 p2) : Prop := p1.rank Ōēź p2.rank` (was `Bool := decide (p1.rank Ōēź p2.rank)`); `AHPosition.moreAccessible (p1 p2) : Prop := p1.rank > p2.rank` (was `Bool := decide (...)`). Both carry `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ Ōēź _))` / `(_ > _)`. `ah_reflexive`/`ah_transitive` drop `= true`. One external consumer (`Phenomena/FillerGap/Studies/KeenanComrie1977.lean::welsh_kc_covers_deeper_than_wals`) drops `= true` and switches `native_decide` ŌåÆ `decide`. Skipped: the `contiguousOnAH`/`hasAHRank`/`RelClauseMarker.{isContinuous,covers}` family stays `Bool` ŌĆö the `prc_from_hc2`/`contiguous_intermediate` proofs unfold the `contiguousOnAH` Bool body and rely on `simp only [ŌåōreduceIte]` reductions, and `RelClauseMarker.covers` is consumed as a Bool filter argument across `KeenanComrie1977.lean`. Migrating those would require restructuring the proofs around `Decidable.casesOn` instead of Boolean reductions; tracked but out of scope for this batch.

### Notes
- **Skip decisions** for transparency: `Core/SyntaxConstraints/NullSubject.lean` (the `subjectAssignment_overtPRO_iff` bridge would cascade into a `ProDropProfile` structure-field migration that touches many `Fragments/*.lean` Bool literal initializers ŌĆö deferred); `Core/Semantics/InfoState.lean` (the file's own docstring already documents the Bool/`List W` design as an intentional dynamic-semantics computational substrate ŌĆö migration is its own project).

## [0.230.12] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/VoiceSystem.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of `VoiceSystemProfile`'s three derived predicates. `promotesRole (p) (r) : Prop := Ōłā v Ōłł p.voices, v.promotes = r` (was `Bool := p.voices.any (┬Ę.promotes == r)`); `distinguishesObliques (p) : Prop := Ōłā v Ōłł p.voices, v.promotes = .locative Ōł© ... Ōł© v.promotes = .circumstantial` (was a `match`-style `.any`); `isActivePassive (p) : Prop := p.voiceCount = 2 Ōł¦ p.promotesRole .agent Ōł¦ p.promotesRole .patient` (was a `&&` chain). All three carry `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (Ōłā _ Ōłł _, _))` / `(_ Ōł¦ _ Ōł¦ _)`. Mathlib idiom: `.any (┬Ę == x)` ŌåÆ `Ōłā _ Ōłł _, _ = _` (`List.Mem` + Ōłā). Cascade through 11 consumer sites in 8 files: `Fragments/Mayan/Kaqchikel/AgentFocus.lean::kaq_not_active_passive`, `Fragments/Mayan/Mam/VoiceSystem.lean` (`mam_not_simple_active_passive`/`mam_no_oblique_pivots`), `Fragments/Mayan/Kiche/VoiceSystem.lean::kiche_not_simple_active_passive`, `Fragments/Chuj/VerbBuilding.lean` (`chuj_not_simple_active_passive`/`chuj_no_oblique_pivots`), `Fragments/TobaBatak/Basic.lean` (`tb_is_active_passive`/`tb_promotes_agent`), `Fragments/Indonesian/VoiceSystem.lean` (`promotes_agent`/`promotes_patient`), `Fragments/English/Predicates.lean::english_is_active_passive`, `Fragments/Finnish/Predicates.lean::finnish_is_active_passive`, `Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Studies/Creissels2025.lean::english_is_active_passive` ŌĆö all `... = true := rfl`/`= true := by native_decide` ŌåÆ `... := by decide`, `... = false := rfl`/`= false := by native_decide` ŌåÆ `┬¼ ... := by decide` (also eliminates four `native_decide` invocations in favor of kernel-checked `decide`).
- **`Core/ScopeTypes.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of `AvailableScopes` query predicates. `hasReading (a) (r) : Prop := r Ōłł a.readings` (was `Bool := a.readings.contains r`); `isAmbiguous (a) : Prop := a.readings.length > 1` (was `Bool := a.readings.length > 1`). Both carry `Decidable` instances. Mathlib idiom: `.contains` ŌåÆ `List.Mem`. No external consumers.
- **`Core/Lexical/RootFeatures.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the two derived `RootProfile` predicates. `RootProfile.constrainsPatient (rp) : Prop := rp.patientRob.isConstrained = true` (was `Bool := rp.patientRob.isConstrained`); `RootProfile.overlaps (rpŌéü rpŌéé) : Prop := rpŌéü.forceMag.overlaps rpŌéé.forceMag = true Ōł¦ ... Ōł¦ rpŌéü.agentControl.overlaps rpŌéé.agentControl = true` (was a six-way `&&` chain). Both carry `Decidable` instances. The inner `Range.overlaps`/`Range.isConstrained` predicates remain `Bool`-returning (they wrap `.any`/`.contains` over a polymorphic `╬▒`-list ŌĆö moving them too would force `[DecidableEq ╬▒]` constraints on every consumer); the `= true` reflection bridges the layers without forcing that cascade. No external consumers.

## [0.230.11] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Core/Computability/Subregular/{Basic, StrictlyLocal, LocallyTestable, Tier, Hierarchy}.lean`**: foundational subregular hierarchy primitives (B3 of the mathlib-shaped relocation begun in 0.230.6/0.230.8), grounded in @cite{lambert-2022} ┬¦3.1-3.4 and shaped to mirror `Mathlib.Computability.Language`. `Basic.lean` defines `Augmented ╬▒ := List (Option ╬▒)` (mathlib idiom: single `none` boundary marker collapses Lambert's distinct ŌŗŖ/Ōŗē since edges only appear at fixed positions by construction), `lift`/`boundary k w := replicate (k-1) none ++ lift w ++ replicate (k-1) none`/`kFactorsList k`/`IsKFactor` plus structural lemmas (`length_of_mem_kFactorsList`, `isInfix_of_mem_kFactorsList`, `length_boundary`, `boundary_zero`/`boundary_one`). `StrictlyLocal.lean` defines `SLGrammar k ╬▒` (positive `permitted : Set (Augmented ╬▒)`), `SLGrammar.lang : Language ╬▒` (membership iff every `k`-factor of `boundary k w` is permitted), `ofForbidden` (forbidden-set presentation as set complement), `IsSLk k L := Ōłā G, G.lang = L`, and trivial `univ`/`empty` grammars. `LocallyTestable.lean` defines `factorSet`/`IsLTk` (presence-of-factor closure) plus `factorCount k t f w := min t (count)` saturation and `IsLTTk` (LTT_{k,t} threshold-testable closure), with `isSLk_imp_isLTk` proving SL_k ŌŖå LT_k from the SL test depending only on the *set* of factors. `Tier.lean` defines `project T : List ╬▒ ŌåÆ List ╬▒` (decidable-tier projection with `project_cons_pos`/`project_cons_neg`/`project_univ` simp lemmas), `TSLGrammar k ╬▒` (tier + SL grammar), and `IsTSLk`. `Hierarchy.lean` proves `IsSLk.toIsLTk` (re-export), `IsSLk.toIsTSLk` (universal-tier construction reducing TSL to SL), and `IsLTk.toIsLTTk` (saturation at `t Ōēź 1` preserves the count-zero distinction, hence factor-set equality follows from saturated-count equality via `lt_min_iff` + `List.count_pos_iff`). The cross-`k` inclusions (SL_k ŌŖå SL_{k+1}) are deferred ŌĆö proving them directly requires reconciling boundary-padding mismatch between `boundary k` and `boundary (k+1)`, which is cleanest via the equivalent finite-state characterization (deferred until DFA-side infrastructure lands). 5 new files, 0 sorrys, all wired into `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.230.10] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Temporal/Tense.lean` + `Theories/Semantics/Tense/Decomposition.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the tense-pronoun mode predicates and the derived deicticity predicate. `TensePronoun.isIndexical (tp) : Prop := tp.mode = .indexical` and `TensePronoun.isBound (tp) : Prop := tp.mode = .bound` (were `Bool := tp.mode == .indexical`/`.bound`) plus `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (tp.mode = ...))`. The derived `KratzerDecomposition.canBeDeictic (d) : Prop := d.tensePronoun.isIndexical` (was `Bool := d.tensePronoun.isIndexical`) cascades the migration into the Kratzer decomposition layer with its own `Decidable` instance. Five consumer cascades: `Decomposition.lean`'s `english_deictic_german_anaphoric` (uses `nofun` for the universally-quantified anaphoric branch ŌĆö `decide` can't reduce with free `n`) and `reflexive_simultaneous_parallel` (drops `= true`); `Phenomena/TenseAspect/ComparePartee.lean`'s `zero_tense_parallels_reflexive` (drops `= true`); `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/HeimKratzer1998.lean` migrates three "full chain" theorems (`kratzer_english_chain`/`kratzer_german_preterit_chain`/`kratzer_german_perfekt_chain`) ŌĆö the `... = true`/`... = false` deictic clauses become bare/negated propositions, and the dataŌĆōfragment Bool-equality clauses `(... == .indexical) = canBeDeictic` become `Ōåö`-biconditionals proved by `decide`; three Fragment files (`Fragments/{English,Italian,German}/Tense.lean`) migrate `kratzerSimplePast_deictic`/`kratzerPassatoProssimo_deictic`/`preterit_not_deictic`/`perfekt_deictic`/`preterit_perfekt_contrast` from `... = true/false := rfl` to `... := by decide`/`┬¼ ... := by decide`.

## [0.230.9] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Modality/ModalIndefinite.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of `hasEpistemic`/`hasCircumstantial` using mathlib idiom. `hasEpistemic (e : ModalIndefiniteEntry) : Prop := .epistemic Ōłł e.flavors` (was `Bool := e.flavors.any (┬Ę == .epistemic)`); same for `hasCircumstantial`. `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ Ōłł _))`. Mathlib-quality move: `.any (┬Ę == x)` ŌåÆ `x Ōłł xs` (List.Mem) per `feedback_mathlib_quality_migration` ŌĆö replaces handrolled `.any`+`==` with mathlib's `List.Mem`. Cascade through 8 consumers: 5 fragment files (`Chuj`/`German`/`Italian`/`French`/`Spanish` `ModalIndefinites.lean`) ŌĆö `... = true := rfl` ŌåÆ `... := by decide` (List.Mem not `rfl`-reducible), `... = false := rfl` ŌåÆ `┬¼ ... := by decide`, conjunctive forms refold; `Phenomena/ModalIndefinites/Studies/AlonsoOvalleRoyer2024.lean` ŌĆö four filter sites (`yalnhej_unique_profile`, `multi_flavor_items`, `epistemic_only_items`, `rc_only_items`) wrap `hasEpistemic`/`hasCircumstantial` in `decide ...` to bridge to `&&`/`!`'s Bool context, three propositional theorems (`volitional_blocks_epistemic`/`unrestricted_with_epistemic`/`unrestricted_without_epistemic`) drop `= true`/`= false`; `Phenomena/ModalIndefinites/Studies/AlonsoOvalleMenendezBenito2010.lean` `alg├║n_entry_epistemic_only` switches to bare `Ōł¦ ┬¼` form proved by `decide`; `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Bubnov2026.lean` `irgend_compatible_classifications` drops `= true`.

## [0.230.8] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/FormalLanguageTheory/{CFGTree, PumpingLemma, NonContextFree}.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Computability/{CFGTree, PumpingLemma, NonContextFree}.lean`**: continuation of the mathlib-shaped relocation begun in 0.230.6. The `Theories/FormalLanguageTheory/` directory is removed (those files were context-free-grammar / pumping-lemma infrastructure, framework-agnostic and properly belonging under `Core/Computability/` next to `Mathlib.Computability.ContextFreeGrammar`/`Language`). Namespaces (`CFGTree`, mathlib-extension `ContextFreeGrammar`) unchanged. Seven import lines rewritten via a single `Linglib.Theories.FormalLanguageTheory.X` ŌåÆ `Linglib.Core.Computability.X` substitution: `Linglib.lean` (3), `Theories/Syntax/CCG/Formal/GenerativeCapacity.lean`, `Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/{PullumGazdar1982,Shieber1985}.lean`, plus 2 internal cross-imports inside `NonContextFree.lean` and `PumpingLemma.lean`. With B1 this completes consolidation of existing formal-language infrastructure under `Core/Computability/`; the `Subregular/` family (B3-B4) will land next as pure additions.

## [0.230.7] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Register.lean` + `Core/Modality/ModalTypes.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of register-variant predicates. `Core.Register.areVariants (a b : Level) : Prop := a ŌēĀ b` (was `Bool := a != b`) plus a `Decidable` instance via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (a ŌēĀ b))`. `ModalItem.areRegisterVariants` follows suit (`Prop` + `Decidable`). Two consumer theorems migrate: `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/RotterLiu2025Concord.lean`'s `register_opposition` drops `= true` and proves by `decide` (was `native_decide`); `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/LiuRotter2025.lean`'s `must_certainly_same_register` and `may_possibly_same_register` switch from `... = false := by native_decide` to `┬¼ ... := by decide`.
- **`Core/InformationStructure.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the IS-clash predicate underlying both @cite{abeille-et-al-2020}'s FBC and @cite{erteschik-shir-1973}'s Dominance Condition. `extractionISClash (fillerStatus domainStatus : DiscourseStatus) : Prop := fillerStatus = .focused Ōł¦ domainStatus = .given` (was `Bool := fillerStatus == .focused && domainStatus == .given`) plus a `Decidable` instance. The three in-file lemmas drop `= true`/`= false`: `extractionISClash_focused_given := Ō¤©rfl, rflŌ¤®`; the two `_focused_new`/`_given_given` use `┬¼ ... := by decide`. Two consumer cascades: `Theories/Semantics/Focus/BackgroundedIslands.lean` migrates 5 theorems (`backgrounded_extraction_clashes`/`atissue_extraction_compatible`/`mos_extraction_clashes`/`bridge_no_extraction_clash`/`prosodic_focus_resolves_clash`) to `Prop` form (positive: `simp ...; exact Ō¤©rfl, rflŌ¤®`; negative: `┬¼ ... := by ... ; decide`). `Phenomena/Islands/Studies/CartnerEtAl2026.lean` migrates `fbcPredictsIsland`/`fbcRevisedViolation`/`fbcRevisedPredictsIsland` to `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; downstream theorems (`fbc_predicts_whq_island`/`fbc_predicts_no_rc_island`/`fbc_predicts_no_top_island`/`both_fbcs_same_predictions`/`fbc_predicts_construction_dependence`/`fbc_falsified_for_rc`/`_top`/`fbc_revised_falsified_for_rc`/`_top`/`argument_chain`) drop Bool-equality patterns ŌĆö `= true` becomes bare proposition, `= false` becomes `┬¼`, the Bool-equality clauses in `both_fbcs_same_predictions` and `argument_chain` become `Ōåö`-biconditionals proved by `decide`.
- **`Core/Lexical/MorphRule.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the paradigm-cell predicates. `ParadigmCell.isGap (c : ParadigmCell F) : Prop := c.form = none` (was `Bool := c.form.isNone`) plus a `Decidable` instance. `ParadigmCell.isIrregular (c : ParadigmCell F) : Prop := c.regular = false Ōł¦ c.form ŌēĀ none` (was `Bool := !c.regular && c.form.isSome`) plus a `Decidable` instance. No external consumers ŌĆö the shapes are picked up by future Fragment paradigms via the `Decidable` instances.

## [0.230.6] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/FormalLanguage.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Computability/FormalLanguage.lean`**: mathlib-shaped relocation under a new `Core/Computability/` subdirectory mirroring `Mathlib/Computability/` (first step of a phased move that will pull `Theories/FormalLanguageTheory/{PumpingLemma, NonContextFree, CFGTree}.lean` and a fresh `Subregular/` family under the same root). Namespace stays `Core` (the file's `inductive FormalLanguageType` declarations are unchanged). Five import lines updated: `Linglib.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/CCG/Formal/GenerativeCapacity.lean`, `Phenomena/WordOrder/CrossSerial.lean`, `Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/PullumGazdar1982.lean`, `Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/Shieber1985.lean`.

## [0.230.5] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Featural/Bundle.lean`** (NEW): parametric feature-bundle primitive `FeatureBundle (F V : Type) := F ŌåÆ Option V` with mathlib-aligned operations (`single`/`assimilate`/`delete`/`set` all built on `Function.update`). Underspecified (`none`) vs specified (`some v`); `V = Bool` recovers classical `[┬▒F]`, `V = ŌäÜ`/`ŌäØ` recovers GSC/probabilistic features (@cite{smolensky-goldrick-2016} @cite{pater-2009}), `V = Unit` recovers privative. Parametric over the feature index `F` per @cite{reiss-2017}'s Substance-Free Phonology.
