Also includes `Tier F V := List (FeatureBundle F V)` plus `merge` (OCP-style), `mergeAdjacent`, and `assimilateLeftward` building blocks for autosegmental rule infrastructure.

### Changed
- **`Theories/Phonology/Autosegmental/RegisterTier.lean`**: substantial rewrite to @cite{lionnet-2022} architecture. Renames `ToneFeature` ŌåÆ `TRN` (Tonal Root Node); reformulates the tier as a bundle of *paradigmatic* `[┬▒upper]`/`[┬▒raised]` subtonal features (vs @cite{snider-1999}'s overloaded paradigmatic+syntagmatic `h`/`l`). Now grounded on `Featural/Bundle.lean` rather than ad-hoc inductive. Cascade renames `ToneFeature` ŌåÆ `TRN` through `Autosegmental/{BasemapCorrespondence, DominantCophAgreement, GrammaticalTone}.lean` and `Theories/Phonology/Tier.lean` (`Phonology.Tier.tonal` retypes to `TRN`).
- **`Theories/Phonology/Correspondence.lean`**: dropped the `Bool`-valued `isContiguous` (only consumer was `IsContiguous`'s decidability instance); the two callers (`contigIViol`, `contigOViol`) switch to the `Prop` form via the existing `Decidable (IsContiguous l)` instance.
- **`Theories/Phonology/OptimalParadigms.lean`**: `majorityRules : Prop` (was `Bool`) + `Decidable` instance via `inferInstance`.
- **`Theories/Phonology/StratalOT.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the cross-stratal ranking API. `isPromoted`/`isDemoted`/`immediatelyDominates`/`dominates` (Ōłā-form, depth-3 transitive closure)/`isOutputFeeding` (per-stratum match) all become `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; the three `stem_feeds_word`/`word_feeds_phrase`/`stem_not_feeds_phrase` lemmas drop `= true`/`= false` (the negative one switches `rfl` ŌåÆ `decide`).
- **`Theories/Phonology/Syllable/Foot.lean`**: `isDegenerate`/`isWellFormedFoot` migrate to `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; the six well-formedness/degeneracy lemmas switch to `by decide` proofs (the `iamb` case switches `&&` to `Ōł¦`).

## [0.230.4] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/Lexical/PolarityItem.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the polarity-item predicate API. `isLicensedIn item ctx : Prop := ctx Ōłł item.licensingContexts` (was `Bool := item.licensingContexts.contains ctx`); `isNPI`/`isFCI` switched from match-on-polarityType returning Bool to `Ōł©`-disjunctions of the relevant `polarityType = ...` cases; `isPPI` and `canonicityConsistent` likewise migrated to `Prop` (the `||`-of-`==` form for `canonicityConsistent` becomes a three-way `Ōł©` of equalities). All five predicates carry `Decidable` instances via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ Ōł© _))` / `Decidable (_ Ōłł _)` / `Decidable (_ = _)`. Fragment/study consumer cascade ŌĆö `Fragments/English/PolarityItems.lean`, `Fragments/English/TemporalAdverbials.lean`, `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/Israel2001.lean`: ~12 `#guard X.isNPI`/`#guard X.canonicityConsistent` style assertions converted to mathlib-style `example : X.isNPI := by decide` (rather than wrapping `decide` inside `#guard` to satisfy its Bool requirement); two `#guard List.all (┬Ę.canonicityConsistent)` Bool-folds rewritten as `example : ŌłĆ p Ōłł ..., p.canonicityConsistent := by decide`. `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/DenicEtAl2021.lean`: `tested_npis_are_npis` (was `... = true Ōł¦ ... = true Ōł¦ ... = true := Ō¤©rfl, rfl, rflŌ¤®`) now `... Ōł¦ ... Ōł¦ ... := by decide` since the `Ōł©`-disjunction proofs vary by which constructor matches; `tested_ppi_is_ppi` drops `= true`, proves by `rfl`. `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/VonFintel1999.lean` and `Theories/Semantics/Entailment/PolarityBuilder.lean`: 5 paired `fragment_builder_agree_*` theorems each, `isLicensedIn ... = true` ŌåÆ bare Prop / `isLicensedIn ... = false` ŌåÆ `┬¼ isLicensedIn ...`; first conjunct proof switches from `rfl` to `by decide` (List membership), second conjunct (`Builder.licensesItem ... = true/false`) unchanged. `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/Rouillard2026.lean`: `npi_status_from_fragment` migrated to `inGTIA.isNPI Ōł¦ ┬¼ inETIA.isNPI := by native_decide`.

## [0.230.3] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Core/SpecificityCondition.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the Specificity Condition predicates. `isSpecific (d : Definiteness) : Prop := d = .definite` (was `Bool` via match) plus a `Decidable` instance via `DecidableEq Definiteness`. `blocked (_op : ExternalOperator) (d : Definiteness) : Prop := isSpecific d` plus a `Decidable` instance via `inferInstanceAs`. The five exposition theorems migrate accordingly: `demonstratives_are_specific`/`blocks_definite`/`wh_indefinite_blocked_in_specific_dp` drop the `= true`, prove by `rfl`; `allows_indefinite`/`wh_indefinite_allowed_in_nonspecific_dp` become `┬¼ blocked ...` proved by `Definiteness.noConfusion` (the `_op` underscore makes `decide` choke on the free variable, so the explicit term-mode proof is cleaner); `operator_insensitive` switches from a `Bool`-equality `rfl` to an `Iff.rfl` (`blocked opŌéü d Ōåö blocked opŌéé d`). Two consumer cascades: `Phenomena/Possession/Studies/AissenPolian2025.lean`'s `specificity_convergence_on_stranding`/`specificity_divergence_on_piedpiping` drop `= true` from the right conjunct (proof `Ō¤©rfl, rflŌ¤®` unchanged); `Phenomena/Islands/Studies/ShenHuang2026.lean`'s `specificity_predicts_exp3` becomes `blocked ... Ōł¦ ┬¼ blocked ...` proved by `Ō¤©rfl, by decideŌ¤®`.

## [0.230.2] - 2026-04-19

### Removed
- **`Core/Semantics/CommonGround.lean`**: deleted the `BContextSet W := W ŌåÆ Bool` namespace and its `HasContextSet` instance ŌĆö a parallel Bool API to `ContextSet W := Set W` with zero external consumers. The `BContextSet.toProp`/`trivial`/`absurd`/`update`/`filterWorlds`/`entails` defs and the `trivial_toProp`/`update_toProp` bridge theorems are gone; callers requiring computation on context sets attach `[DecidablePred]` to a `Set W` directly per mathlib practice. File-level docstring updated to drop the "decidable variants" line; `PresuppositionContext.lean:133` docstring's `BContextSet W` reference replaced with `Commitment.CommitmentSlate W` / `LocalCtx W` (extant `HasContextSet` instances).
- **`Core/Inquisitive.lean`**: deleted the redundant `isPartition_iff` rewrite lemma ŌĆö the new `Prop` form of `isPartition` IS the iff, so the bridge theorem is vacuous.

### Changed
- **`Core/Scales/Scale.lean` + `Core/Discourse/AtIssueness.lean`**: BoolŌåÆProp migration of the gradient at-issueness/projectivity threshold predicates. `Rat01.exceeds (d ╬Ė : Rat01) : Prop := ╬Ė.val < d.val` (was `Bool := decide (╬Ė.val < d.val)`) plus a `Decidable (exceeds d ╬Ė)` instance via `inferInstanceAs`. Cascade through `AtIssueness`: `isAtIssue` and `isProjective` now `Prop` with `Decidable` instances; the `if isAtIssue d ╬Ė then .new else .given` consumer in `Core/InformationStructure.lean` continues to work via the `Decidable` instance.
- **`Core/Inquisitive.lean`**: `Discourse.Issue.isPartition (q : Issue W) (worlds : List W) : Prop := ŌłĆ w Ōłł worlds, (q.alternatives.filter (┬Ę w)).length = 1` (was `Bool := worlds.all (...)`) plus a `[DecidableEq W] ŌåÆ Decidable (q.isPartition worlds)` instance. The legacy Bool/List Hamblin form is preserved for finite-world consumers (`ProbabilisticAnswerhood`, etc.); the canonical Set-based partition predicate lives in `Core/Issue/Partition.lean` (`Core.Issue.IsPartition`, backed by mathlib's `Setoid.IsPartition`).
- **`Theories/Semantics/Focus/BackgroundedIslands.lean`**: consumer cascade for the `isAtIssue` BoolŌåÆProp migration. `IslandPrediction.isIsland` field stays `Bool`; `predictIsland` wraps the Prop with `decide` at the boundary (`isIsland := !decide (isAtIssue d ╬Ė)`). Helper lemmas migrated to positive/negative `Prop` forms (`isAtIssue_lexical : ┬¼ isAtIssue ...`, `isAtIssue_none : isAtIssue ...`, `isAtIssue_factive : isAtIssue ...`); the three `predict_*` theorems use `decide_eq_false`/`decide_eq_true` to bridge; the two `*_rank_le_*` theorems use `if_pos`/`if_neg` instead of plain `if_true`/`if_false`; `predictIsland_monotone` uses `by_cases` on the Decidable Prop instead of `cases` on the Bool.
- **`Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Roberts2012.lean`**: dropped the stale "Migration note (in progress)" docstring claiming 17 sorries pending ŌĆö the file has had zero sorries since the `Core/Issue/Hamblin.lean` infrastructure (`mem_alt_ofList_of_disjoint_others`, `ofList_le_polar_of_classified`, `polar_inf_polar_le_ofList_of_corners`) and `Core/Issue/Partition.lean` (`Core.Issue.IsPartition`) landed in 0.229.999. Replaced with a brief "Representation" note pointing to the active infrastructure.

### Added
- **`Core/Semantics/CommonGround.lean`**: `@[simp] CG.contextSet_add : (cg.add p).contextSet = p Ōł® cg.contextSet` exposes the cons-step of the foldr-intersection definitionally (only the `ŌŖå` form `add_restricts` was previously public); `Inhabited (CG W)` instance with `default := CG.empty`; `empty_contextSet` promoted to `@[simp]`.

## [0.230.1] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Fragments/Numee/Prosody.lean`** (NEW): Num├©├© (Glottocode `numa1247`, Southern Oceanic, New Caledonia) prosodic fragment, drawn from the Goro dialect as analysed by @cite{lionnet-2025} ┬¦3.3ŌĆō┬¦3.4. **(1)** `Syllable` structure (`form : String`, `specs : List RegisterSpec`) makes mora structure explicit so that `morae`/`isLightCV`/`isRegisterless`/`isDownstepped` predicates address the syllable-level eligibility conditions for the boundary phenomenon ŌĆö neither expressible in the flat `List RegisterSpec` representation Drubea uses. **(2)** `BoundaryEffect` inductive (`none`/`single`/`double`) models the three outcomes of the utterance-final boundary downstep `Ōü║%`, including the stacked double-downstep `Ōü║Ōü║` that preserves the registerless/downstepped contrast utterance-finally (@cite{lionnet-2025} ex. 24 vs 25). **(3)** `numeeBoundaryEffect : Utterance ŌåÆ BoundaryEffect` reverses the syllable list and inspects the final + penult: light CV final + registerless penult enables the boundary, with `single` for registerless finals and `double` for already-downstepped finals; heavy finals or downstepped penults yield `.none`. **(4)** Nine lexical entries instantiated from ┬¦3.4 ex. 22ŌĆō29: `jaa` 'juice', the minimal pair `niCoconut`/`niBreast` 'coconut'/'breast', `mii` 'low', `ku` 'yam', `beTii` '(three)', `kwe` 'sand', `paa` 'down' + abstract fillers `regCV`/`regCVV`. The fragment models ┬¦3.4's mora-weight and preceding-context conditions explicitly ŌĆö `RegisterTier`'s tier-level operations apply to underlying specs and do not yet model the boundary process, which lives at the prosodic-word/utterance interface.
- **`Phenomena/Tone/Studies/Lionnet2025.lean` ┬¦13** (Num├©├© Boundary Downstep): eight theorems exercising the fragment against ┬¦3.4 ex. 24ŌĆō29: `numee_registerless_final_single` (`[jaa, niCoconut] Ōå” .single`, ex. 24), `numee_downstepped_final_double` (`[jaa, niBreast] Ōå” .double`, ex. 25 ŌĆö the stacked double-downstep), `numee_minimal_pair_distinguished` (the `n─®`/`Ōü║n─®` minimal pair survives the boundary as `.single` vs `.double`), `numee_heavy_final_blocks` (CVV final `mii` blocks, ex. 26), `numee_after_downstepped_blocks`/`numee_after_downstepped_blocks'` (downstepped penult blocks even when final is light CV, ex. 28: `Ōü║t─®─® ku`, ex. 29: `Ōü║paa kw╔ø╠ā`), `numee_singleton_no_boundary` (Ōēź2 syllables required by structural description), and `numee_lexical_culminative` (`niBreast`/`beTii`/`paa` are `IsCulminative` from `RegisterTier`, confirming the boundary is *postlexical* and does not feed underlying culminativity). All eight theorems close by `decide` over the explicit syllable lists. `Linglib.lean` adds the `Fragments.Numee.Prosody` import.

## [0.230.0] - 2026-04-19

### Changed
- **`Theories/Phonology/Autosegmental/RegisterTier.lean`**: mathlib-discipline overhaul of Snider's Register Tier Theory (@cite{snider-1999}, @cite{snider-2020}, @cite{lionnet-2025}). **(1)** Reinvented mathlib primitives replaced: `pointwiseLE` and `decPointwiseLE` deleted in favour of `List.ForallŌéé (┬Ę Ōēż ┬Ę)` from `Mathlib.Data.List.Forall2`; the bespoke `regSpecLE`/`specsMoreLowering` Bool ordering replaced with `RegisterSpec.le sŌéü sŌéé := sŌéü.pitchEffect Ōēż sŌéé.pitchEffect` (Prop) plus a `DecidableRel` instance ŌĆö derived from a single primitive `RegisterSpec.pitchEffect : RegisterSpec ŌåÆ Int`, not stipulated separately. **(2)** BoolŌåÆProp migration for the propositional predicate: `isCulminative : List RegisterSpec ŌåÆ Bool` ŌåÆ `IsCulminative : List RegisterSpec ŌåÆ Prop := specs.count (some .l) Ōēż 1` (uses mathlib `List.count`) with `DecidablePred` instance. **(3)** `realizePitch` switched from `Nat ŌåÆ List RegisterSpec ŌåÆ List Nat` to `Int ŌåÆ List RegisterSpec ŌåÆ List Int` ŌĆö the old `Nat`-truncating subtraction silently violated the cumulativity property (Leben 2018 (c)) at the floor and broke `realizePitch_baseline_mono` for low offsets. New `terracing_below_zero` regression test exercises the Int fix. **(4)** `pitchDeltas := realizePitch 0` introduced as the **theory-primary view**: cumulative register shifts from the start, no privileged zero. Docstring frames it as the syntagmatic content (only differences are linguistically meaningful ŌĆö RTT is relational). `realizePitch n specs = (pitchDeltas specs).map (┬Ę + n)` proved as `realizePitch_eq_pitchDeltas_shift` via the helper `realizePitch_shift` (offset acts additively). **(5)** `@[simp] realizePitch_cons` lemma bridges the explicit-branch definition to a uniform `pitchEffect`-based form, collapsing `realizePitch_baseline_mono` and `realizePitch_mono` to ~3-line proofs each. **(6)** Code organization: top-level `section` blocks (`Features`/`Geometry`/`Realization`/`Constraints`/`Postlexical`/`Typology`/`Verification`/`Monotonicity`); `hEpenthesisSpread.mark` helper inlined into the top-level pattern. **(7)** Docstring/citation cleanup: removed the false claim that `hEpenthesis` "models the abrupt raising pattern (the most common case)" ŌĆö per @cite{lionnet-2025}, "abrupt" refers to the spreading variant `hEpenthesisSpread` (now correctly identified); dropped unverified `┬¦4` from the @cite{beckman-pierrehumbert-1986} reference (paper unread); `DownstepProperties` docstring now flags Bool fields as stipulative annotations rather than derivable properties. **(8)** Cascade through downstream consumers: `Phenomena/Intonation/Studies/BeckmanPierrehumbert1986.lean` (`pointwiseLE` ŌåÆ `List.ForallŌéé (┬Ę Ōēż ┬Ę)` in `more_catathesis_lower_pitch`/`catathesis_blocking_concrete`; `catathesis_blocking` reframed from `(n k : Nat)` with truncating sub to `{m n : Int} (h : m Ōēż n)`; `catathesis_terracing`/`catathesis_lowers` switched to `Int`); `Phenomena/Tone/Studies/Lionnet2025.lean` (5├Ś `pattern_culminative_N` migrated from `isCulminative ... = true` Bool form to `IsCulminative` Prop form, proved by `decide`; `all_stems_culminative` reformulated as `ŌłĆ e Ōłł allStems, IsCulminative e.specs`; new `four_downsteps_deltas : pitchDeltas [some .l, some .l, some .l, some .l] = [-1, -2, -3, -4]` test exercises the theory-primary view alongside the existing offset-anchored `four_downsteps_terrace`). All four touched files build clean; pre-existing failures elsewhere are from concurrent in-progress work on unrelated Core/Semantics files.

## [0.229.999] - 2026-04-19

### Added
- **`Core/Issue/Partition.lean`**: bridge from `Core.Issue W` to mathlib's `Setoid.IsPartition` and to the legacy Bool-based `QUD W`. Closes the missing piece flagged in the Discourse.Issue ŌåÆ Core.Issue migration memo: a Set-based way to recover partition-shaped inquisitive content and the Roberts-style cell-equivalence relation. **(1)** `IsPartition (P : Issue W) : Prop := Setoid.IsPartition (alt P)` ŌĆö predicate that the alternative set is a partition of `W` in mathlib's sense (no empty cell, every world in a unique alternative). Forces non-informativity (`info_eq_univ`); inherits `pairwiseDisjoint`, `nonempty_of_mem_alt`, `sUnion_alt_eq_univ` from mathlib's `Setoid.IsPartition` API rather than reproving them. **(2)** `toSetoid h : Setoid W := Setoid.mkClasses (alt P) h.2` ŌĆö equivalence relation derived from a partition issue: two worlds are equivalent iff they share an alternative cell. `@[simp] classes_toSetoid : (toSetoid h).classes = alt P` and `toSetoid_rel_iff : (toSetoid h) w v Ōåö Ōłā p Ōłł alt P, w Ōłł p Ōł¦ v Ōłł p` make the cell semantics machine-checkable. **(3)** `noncomputable def toQUD h : QUD W` ŌĆö bridge to the legacy Bool-based `QUD W` via classical decidability of the underlying equivalence; `toQUD_sameAnswer_iff` characterizes via shared cell, `toQUD_cell_eq_alt` shows `(toQUD h).cell w` recovers the alternative cell of `w` in `P`. **(4)** Round-trip with `fromSetoid` (from `Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean`): `isPartition_fromSetoid [Nonempty W] (r : Setoid W) : (fromSetoid r).IsPartition` (the `Nonempty W` rules out the degenerate `Ōłģ`-alternative when `W` is empty); `toSetoid_fromSetoid : toSetoid (isPartition_fromSetoid r) = r` proves the `Setoid ŌåÆ Issue ŌåÆ Setoid` direction is identity. Mention-some, intermediate-exhaustive, and conditional-question alternative sets are not partitions and so fail `IsPartition` ŌĆö the predicate carves out exactly the partition slice of inquisitive content. Zero sorries; `Linglib.lean` adds the import between `Issue.Hamblin` and `Issue.Singleton`.

## [0.229.998] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/ArealTypology/Studies/Haspelmath2001.lean`**: Standard Average European as a `LinguisticArea SAELanguage SAEFeature`, the first consumer of `Theories.Diachronic.Areal`. New top-level `Phenomena/ArealTypology/` directory (cross-cutting category like Negation/Coordination), housing the first areal-typology study. **(1)** `SAELanguage`: 41-constructor inductive (deriving `DecidableEq`, `Repr`, `Fintype`) covering Romance, Germanic, Slavic, Balkan, Baltic, marginal Finno-Ugric (area), Celtic + Basque + Turkic + Maltese (adjacent), Persian/Hindi-Urdu/Armenian (cofamilial ŌĆö other IE branches), and a worldwide reference sample (Mongolian, Indonesian, Yoruba, Japanese, Mandarin, Swahili, Quechua, Lezgian, Georgian). **(2)** `SAEFeature`: 12-constructor inductive enumerating @cite{haspelmath-2001} ┬¦2.1ŌĆō┬¦2.12 ŌĆö the dozen major Europeanisms (`definiteIndefiniteArticles`, `relativeClausesWithRelPro`, `havePerfect`, `nominativeExperiencers`, `participialPassive`, `anticausativeProminence`, `dativeExternalPossessor`, `negativePronounsNoVerbalNeg`, `particleComparative`, `relativeBasedEquative`, `strictAgreement`, `intensifierReflexiveDifferentiation`). **(3)** Per-feature `Finset SAELanguage` isoglosses (`articleLgs`, `havePerfectLgs`, `particleCompLgs`, etc.) encoding @cite{haspelmath-2001} Maps 107.1ŌĆō107.12; `isoglossFinset : SAEFeature ŌåÆ Finset SAELanguage` dispatches and `isoglossFor : SAEFeature ŌåÆ Isogloss SAELanguage` lifts to the schema's `Set`-valued isogloss via Finset coercion. **(4)** `europeanReference : ArealReference SAELanguage` partitions the language sample into the four schema fields (area = 26 core + marginal SAE; cofamilial = 3 eastern IE; adjacent = 6 non-IE neighbors; world = 18 incl. cofamilial+adjacent for the worldwide-frequency criterion). **(5)** `sae : LinguisticArea SAELanguage SAEFeature` instance with `features := Finset.univ`, `isogloss := isoglossFor`; the `areal` proof obligation is currently `sorry` (TODO: closes by `decide` once schema density takes `[DecidablePred (┬Ę Ōłł I)]` instead of `Classical`; this is the v1 architectural punch list, documented in ┬¦5 docstring). **(6)** `clusterCount : SAELanguage ŌåÆ ŌäĢ` is a local computable mirror of `LinguisticArea.clusterScore` so that concrete predictions discharge by `decide`. Six verification theorems exercise the cluster-map gradience of @cite{haspelmath-2001} ┬¦4: `french_has_all_features` and `german_has_all_features` (= 12 ŌĆö the Charlemagne nucleus), `english_in_outer_band` (Ōēź 7 ŌĆö the ┬¦4 outer band of English/Greek/Romanian), `turkish_low_score` and `persian_low_score` (Ōēż 1 each ŌĆö adjacent and cofamilial respectively, criteria (ii) and (iii)), `mongolian_zero_score` (= 0 ŌĆö criterion (iv), worldwide reference). All six pass `by decide` against the encoded Map data, confirming internal consistency: French and German really do score 12/12 on the 12-feature inventory as encoded, English really does pass the 7-feature threshold, and Mongolian/Persian/Turkish really are below the boundary as the paper claims. The architectural payoff: future areal studies (Balkan, Mesoamerican, Circum-Baltic) instantiate the same `LinguisticArea` schema; cross-areal comparison (does any feature qualify as both an SAE and a Balkan Europeanism?) becomes a structural query rather than informal prose. The architectural debt: per-feature isoglosses are hard-coded from the paper's Maps rather than derived from existing per-phenomenon `Typology.lean` profiles (Comparison, Negation, Anaphora, Possession, Causation) ŌĆö the next refactor pass should replace each `*Lgs` Finset with a derivation from the relevant `Typology` profile, making the connection between SAE and the per-phenomenon analyses true by construction rather than by editorial coincidence.

## [0.229.997] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Theories/Diachronic/Areal.lean`**: framework-neutral schema for typological linguistic areas (Sprachb├╝nde) following @cite{haspelmath-2001}'s discrete-criteria model. `Isogloss L := Set L` (`abbrev` so `Ōł®`/`Ōł¬`/`ŌŖå`/`Ōłł` inherit from mathlib); `Isogloss.density I S : ŌäÜ` is the proportion of a `Finset` reference sample inside an isogloss (classical, since `Set L` membership isn't decidable). `ArealReference L` bundles the four reference samples (`area`, `cofamilial`, `adjacent`, `world`) needed for criteria (i)ŌĆō(iv). `IsAreal R I : Prop` is a `structure` (not a `Prop` conjunction) with one named field per criterion (`area_majority`, `cofamilial_lacks`, `adjacent_lacks`, `not_worldwide`), thresholds hard-coded at `1/2` for v1. `LinguisticArea L F` bundles a reference, a `Finset F` of features, an `isogloss : F ŌåÆ Isogloss L`, and a proof that every listed feature satisfies `IsAreal`. Derived gradient API: `clusterScore` (count of features a language shares), `isopleth k` (Ōēź `k` features), `nucleus` (all but at most one feature) ŌĆö recovers Haspelmath ┬¦4's cluster-map methodology from discrete data without requiring gradient features themselves. Feature type `F` is intentionally abstract: each areal study (SAE, Balkan, Mesoamerican) instantiates against its own inductive feature type. Docstring acknowledges the discrete framing as a first approximation per Stolz/Grambank/AUTOTYP-era critiques and notes that a future `GradientLinguisticArea` sibling can swap `Set L` for `L ŌåÆ ŌäÜ` strength functions without disturbing this schema. The schema is the foundation for an upcoming `Phenomena/ArealTypology/Studies/Haspelmath2001.lean` SAE study that composes existing per-phenomenon Typology profiles (Comparison, Negation, Anaphora, Possession, Causation) into a single `LinguisticArea SAELanguage SAEFeature` instance.

## [0.229.996] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Napoli & Nespor 1976 formalization + shared `Pragmatics.Bias` abstraction.** First sustained formalization of bias-conditioned negation as a *cross-construction* licensing predicate, treating the Italian *nonŌéé* paradigm of @cite{napoli-nespor-1976} as one instantiation among several (biased polar questions, VERUM focus, use-conditional rejection, commitment-update rejection moves). **(1)** `Theories/Pragmatics/Bias.lean` (NEW, ~215 lines): the framework-neutral `BiasLicensingProfile` structure bundles N&N ┬¦2's four conditions as named `Bool` fields (`contradictsPriorBelief`, `isAssertive`, `matrixUnnegated`, `imprecise`) with `DecidableEq`, `Repr`, and a `Fintype` instance via `BoolŌü┤` equivalence. `BiasLicensingProfile.licenses` is the conjunctive predicate; five canonical profiles (`licensedProfile` plus four one-axis blocking profiles `questionedProfile`/`matrixNegatedProfile`/`preciseProfile`/`noContradictionProfile`) cover N&N ┬¦2.1ŌĆō┬¦2.3's distributional witnesses; `licenses_iff_all_true`, `unique_licensing_profile` (`(univ.filter ...).card = 1` by `decide` over the 16-element `Fintype`), `blocking_axes_independent`, and the five `*_blocks` `rfl` theorems make the architecture mathlib-style. The bridge `BiasLicensingProfile.toPolarityLicensing : BiasLicensingProfile ŌåÆ Core.PolarityLicensing` connects N&N ┬¦3.11's *pur* data to @cite{greco-2020}'s weak-EN classification (licensed bias profile Ōå” `weakENProfile`, blocked profile Ōå” no licensing). **(2)** `Phenomena/Negation/Studies/NapoliNespor1976.lean` (NEW, ~300 lines): consumes `Pragmatics.Bias` end-to-end. ┬¦ 1 encodes Dario/Paolo dialogue contexts (┬¦1 ex. 6ŌĆō9). ┬¦ 2 instantiates the five canonical profiles per blocking environment (`questionedComparative`, `matrixNegatedComparative`, `equalityComparative`, `precisionComparative`) and proves each blocks via `rfl`. ┬¦ 3 the six morphosyntactic diagnostics from ┬¦3.1ŌĆō┬¦3.2 (`predictsSubjunctive`, `predictsPurNPI`, `predictsNegativeSpecificityRestriction`, `predictsNeancheConjunction`, `predictsCheComplementizer`, `predictsLoClitic`) all derive thinly from `p.licenses` per N&N's claim that *nonŌéé* triggers the same morphosyntactic profile in all licensing environments. ┬¦ 4 `WeakNPIClass` distinguishes `purClass` (licensed in N&N ┬¦3.11 ex. 46ŌĆō48) from `affattoClass` (precision-requiring, blocked by ┬¦3.22's imprecise condition); `affatto_blocked_in_non2` derives the exclusion from `licenses`'s conjunctive structure (precision + imprecise are mutually exclusive ŌĆö *not* a stipulation). ┬¦ 5 the `chissaSeNon` indirect-question profile uses the same `licensedProfile` as comparatives (N&N ┬¦4 ex. 88ŌĆō91), making the cross-construction generalization machine-checked. ┬¦ 6 explicit bridge theorem `napoli_nespor_pur_is_weakEN` connecting *pur* licensing to @cite{greco-2020}'s weak-EN profile. **(3)** `Phenomena/Negation/Studies/Rett2026.lean` patched: `italianComparative.licensesWeakNPIs` flipped from `false` to `true` per N&N ┬¦3.11 ex. 46ŌĆō48 (the *pur* paradigm). Added load-bearing docstring referencing N&N ŌĆö previously `@cite{napoli-nespor-1976}` appeared decoratively in headers but no theorem actually depended on N&N's empirical content; now the data field is N&N-grounded with a comment distinguishing *pur* (weak NPI, licensed by N&N ┬¦3.11) from *affatto* (precision-requiring, blocked by N&N ┬¦3.22 fn 6 for orthogonal precision reasons). Verified `high_en_blocks_npis` still holds (italianComparative is `.low`). **(4)** `Linglib.lean` adds two new imports: `Linglib.Theories.Pragmatics.Bias` and `Linglib.Phenomena.Negation.Studies.NapoliNespor1976`. The architectural payoff: future formalizations of @cite{romero-han-2004} (biased polar questions), @cite{repp-2013} (VERUM focus), @cite{gutzmann-2015} (use-conditional rejection), and @cite{farkas-bruce-2010} (commitment-update rejection moves) plug into the same `BiasLicensingProfile`, making the cross-construction unification true by construction rather than by ad-hoc bridge theorems. The historical Generative-Semantics deep-structure-with-deletion-rule machinery (1968ŌĆō1976) is omitted as artifact; the licensing predicate itself is foundational.

## [0.229.995] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **BoolŌåÆProp migration: `rootLicensesTemplate` (ArgDerivation).** The rootŌĆōtemplate licensing predicate is propositional ("can this root fill this template?"), so it migrates to `Prop` + `[Decidable]`. **(1)** `Theories/Semantics/Verb/ArgDerivation.lean`: `rootLicensesTemplate : RootEntailments ŌåÆ Template ŌåÆ Bool` ŌåÆ `RootLicensesTemplate : RootEntailments ŌåÆ Template ŌåÆ Prop` (PascalCase, mathlib `*Licenses*` style); per-Template-case decidability instance via `cases t <;> unfold ... <;> infer_instance`. The `licensedTemplates : RootEntailments ŌåÆ List Template` filter bridges to Bool with `decide` at the call site (`List.filter` requires Bool). **(2)** Six verification theorems migrated from `= true Ōł¦ = true Ōł¦ ...` to direct Prop conjunctions proved by `decide`: `pc_licenses`, `pureManner_licenses` (uses `┬¼` for the false cells, replacing `= false`), `pureResult_licenses`, `causativeResult_licenses`, `fullSpec_licenses`, `pc_result_stative_difference`. **(3)** `primary_licensed_canonical` rewritten: was `(primaryTemplate X).any (rootLicensesTemplate X) = true Ōł¦ ...` (a five-fold `Option.any` of Bool predicates), now five direct `RootLicensesTemplate X T` instances naming the actual primary template per case (`.propertyConcept .state`, `.pureResult .achievement`, etc.) ŌĆö sharper since it spells out *which* template each root primaries. **(4)** Cascaded `Phenomena/Causation/Studies/Krejci2012.lean`: `eat_licenses_accomplishment` migrated to `by decide`; ┬¦ 1 docstring pointer updated. ArgDerivation + Krejci2012 build clean.

## [0.229.994] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **BoolŌåÆProp migration: Verb subtheory predicates (Affectedness, LevinTheory, AgentivityLattice).** Three rounds bringing the verb subtheory in line with mathlib discipline (`Prop` + `[Decidable]` over `Bool`-returning functions; bridge to Bool via `decide` only at use sites that demand a Bool). **(1)** `Affectedness.lean`: dropped the redundant `AffectednessDegree.ge` Bool wrapper (the existing `LE` instance already provides `Ōēż`/`<`); `Beavers2010.lean`'s `MapHolds : AlternationContrast ŌåÆ Prop := ╬╗ c => c.obliqueDegree Ōēż c.directDegree` (was `Bool` using `ge`), `MAP_holds_all_alternations : ŌłĆ c Ōłł allContrasts, MapHolds c` (was `allContrasts.all mapHolds = true`); `BeaversUdayana2022.lean`'s `LicensesDispositionalMiddle : Prop`. **(2)** `LevinTheory.lean`: dissolved three Bool helper defs (`spec_implies_bkg_manner`, `causativeResult_always_cos`, `root_cause_accounted_for`) ŌĆö they were the encode-conclusions-as-Bool anti-pattern (universal `ŌłĆ c, fooImplication c = true` over a Bool function whose body was `!p || q`). Replaced with direct `Prop`-implication theorems (e.g. `c.meaningComponents.mannerSpec = true Ōł© c.meaningComponents.instrumentSpec = true ŌåÆ c.rootEntailments.manner = true`), proved by `cases c <;> decide`. **(3)** `AgentivityLattice.lean`: migrated six validity/region predicates to `Prop` with `[Decidable]` instances ŌĆö `AgentivityNode.Valid`, `GrimmNode.AgentivityValid`/`CrossValid`/`Valid`/`InTransitiveRegion`, `DomPredictedByLattice`, `SubjectInAgentRegion` (PascalCase per mathlib `Is*`/`*Valid` convention). The 17 verification theorems and the dom-monotonicity pair migrated alongside. Cascaded through `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Grimm2011.lean` (~10 sites): `russian_matches_lattice`/`spanish_subset_of_lattice`/`animacy_dom_within_lattice` use `decide (DomPredictedByLattice ...)` inside Bool comparisons against `DOMProfile.marks`; `latticeDOM`'s `marks` field bridges via `decide`; `transitivity_membership`/`verb_class_dom_behavior`/`creation_dom_inapplicable`/`lattice_matches_aissen_type2` migrated to direct Prop statements; all `native_decide` proofs replaced with `decide`. The DOMProfile.marks field stays Bool (genuine bitfield data ŌĆö typology table values), but every predicate that *means* something propositional is now a Prop. AgentivityLattice + Grimm2011 + downstream Beavers2010/BeaversUdayana2022/LevinTheory all build clean.

## [0.229.993] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Discourse.Issue ŌåÆ Core.Issue migration: Strategy.lean, QUDStack.lean, Roberts2012.lean (pilot).** Migrates the rose-tree strategy, QUD stack, and the canonical Roberts DŌéĆ worked example off the legacy Bool/List `Discourse.Issue` onto the Set-based `Core.Issue` with Prop+Decidable Roberts QUD predicates. **(1)** `Core/Discourse/QUDStack.lean`: imports `Core/Issue/Basic.lean` instead of `Core/Inquisitive.lean`; `open Core` so the structural `List (Issue W)` field resolves to `Core.Issue`. Pure structural migration ŌĆö no operations on Issue contents. **(2)** `Core/Discourse/Strategy.lean`: `isComplete` now `Prop`-valued (was `Bool`); uses lattice meet `ŌŖō` and top `ŌŖż` (was `Issue.inter ... worlds` and `Issue.ofAlternatives [trivialState]`); calls `Core.Issue.questionEntails` (Prop) instead of the legacy 3-arg Bool predicate; `moveRelevantToStrategy` likewise restructured around `Issue.alt` and `Issue.partiallyAnswers`. **(3)** `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Roberts2012.lean`: DŌéĆ atoms (`hannahBeans`/`hannahTofu`/`rogerBeans`/`rogerTofu`) migrated from `D0World ŌåÆ Bool` to `Set D0World`; per-set `DecidablePred` instances + nontriviality lemmas (├Ś8); polar questions via `Issue.polar`; wh-questions `q_a`/`q_b` via `Issue.ofList` of partition cells; Big Question `q_1 := q_a ŌŖō q_b`; D0World gets a `Fintype` instance via the `Bool├ŚBool├ŚBool├ŚBool` equivalence (`card_D0World = 16` by `decide`). **Polar-polar theorems decide cleanly** via the `*_polar_iff` rewrite lemmas (positive/negative partial answers to `q_ai`, `qai_not_entails_qbi`, etc.). **Sorries with TODO markers** for theorems that depend on missing infrastructure: (a) `HasAltList` instance for `Issue.ofList L` when `L` is pairwise-disjoint nonempty (would yield `alt (ofList L) = L`), needed for `qa_entails_qai` and the wh-question entailment chain; (b) `HasAltList` for `P ŌŖō Q` from witnesses on each operand, needed for `q1_entails_qa`/`q1_entails_qb` and Strategy completeness. The `at-issueness` and `isPartition`/`toQUD round-trip` theorems were dropped pending Set-based Core.Issue ŌåÆ QUD bridge infrastructure. 17 sorries land in this file (was: 0 in pre-migration version; the legacy version proved everything via `native_decide` over Bool/List alternatives). **(4)** Removed the `Compare.lean`-style `bridge_*` theorems testing `refinesOn Ōåö questionEntails` agreement (the LHS uses partition refinement on `q.toQUD`, which doesn't exist on Core.Issue yet). Full `lake build` clean (only the expected sorry warnings).

## [0.229.992] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Zheng2025 ┬¦6: empirical closure of ┬¦1Ōåö┬¦5 on the dripping-raincoat scenario.** Closes the data-theory loop that 0.229.988 left half-open: ┬¦5 introduced `nandaoFullFelicity` as the conjunction of Issue-singleton (┬¦4) and Kernel-bias (┬¦2) layers, but no ┬¦1 datum was wired to a concrete instance. **(1)** Promoted `wearingRaincoat`, `expectDry`, `isRaining`, their `DecidablePred` instances, `raincoatK`, `dryU` from `private` to public in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean` (paper-replica scenario data, intended to be reusable for the Phenomena bridge). **(2)** New ┬¦6 in `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean`: `biasedUse_integrated_felicity : nandaoFullFelicity (Core.Issue.declarative isRaining) raincoatK dryU isRaining` proven by `nandaoFullFelicity_declarative_iff` + the existing `raincoat_nandao_felicitous` (Layer 2 carries the cardinality work, Layer 1 reduces via `alt_declarative`). **(3)** Bridge theorem `biasedUse_witnesses_integrated_felicity` packages the ┬¦1 datum's empirical fields (`evidentialBias = true`, `epistemicBias = true`, `felicitous = true`) with the ┬¦5 theoretical prediction ŌĆö the data Ōåö theory correspondence on @cite{zheng-2025} ex. 2 is now explicit and machine-checked. **(4)** `kernel_requires_evidence` signature migrated `World ŌåÆ Bool` ŌåÆ `World ŌåÆ Prop` for consistency with the post-migration Kernel. **(5)** Header docstring `## Predictions verified` block updated to list the new theorems.

### Fixed
- **Two upstream Kernel.lean errors from the parallel-session BoolŌåÆProp migration.** (a) Line 305 in `foldl_refine_uniform_aux`: `rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hq'_p with rfl` substituted `p` away (rather than `q'` ŌĆö both were free locals, `subst` kept the more recently introduced `q'`). The call to `refine_cell_uniform_p partition p hcell' hw' hv'` then failed with "Unknown identifier `p`". Fix: use `q'` instead of `p` (the surviving name post-substitution), with a one-line comment explaining why. (b) Line 1000 in `raincoat_nandao_felicitous`: `decide` couldn't see through the opaque `def evidenceRaises` to its underlying `Nat` `>` to derive `Decidable (evidenceRaises wearingRaincoat isRaining)`. Fix: `unfold evidenceRaises; decide` to expose the decidable comparison.

## [0.229.991] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage J of Numerals API cleanup: derive Class A/B and Bound direction structurally from `NumeralExpr`; collapse ┬¦5 to universal theorems.** Stage H/I left `ModifierClass`/`BoundDirection` from ┬¦1 as a pure conceptual taxonomy with zero connection to `NumeralExpr` ŌĆö the ┬¦5 theorems were named `classA_gt_excludes_bare`, `classB_ge_includes_bare`, etc., but the classification didn't drive any proof. Per CLAUDE.md "do not encode conclusions as definitions, derive them from deeper principles", the classification is now structural and the inclusion/exclusion theorems are universally quantified, so adding a new modifier requires extending the classifier *and* the case analysis. **(1)** New `NumeralExpr.argument : NumeralExpr ŌåÆ Nat` (extracts `m` from any constructor), `NumeralExpr.modifierClass : NumeralExpr ŌåÆ Option ModifierClass` (`bare Ōå” none`, `moreThan/fewerThan Ōå” some classA`, `atLeast/atMost Ōå” some classB`), `NumeralExpr.boundDirection : NumeralExpr ŌåÆ Option BoundDirection` (`bare Ōå” none`, `atLeast/moreThan Ōå” some lower`, `atMost/fewerThan Ōå” some upper`). **(2)** Replaced four per-modifier ┬¦5 theorems (`classA_gt_excludes_bare`, `classA_lt_excludes_bare`, `classB_ge_includes_bare`, `classB_le_includes_bare`) with two universal theorems: `classA_excludes_bare_world (bare) (e) (h : e.modifierClass = some .classA) : ┬¼ e.meaning bare e.argument` and `classB_includes_bare_world (bare) (e) (h : e.modifierClass = some .classB) : e.meaning bare e.argument`. Both proved by `cases e <;> simp_all [...]` over the classifier ŌĆö the case discipline forces every constructor to either be classified or fail compilation. The bare-numeral-semantics parameter is universally quantified, so the result is independent of Kennedy/Horn theory choice. **(3)** Deleted `classA_not_entailed_by_bare` (subsumed by `classA_excludes_bare_world` plus `bareMeaning m m = rfl`). **(4)** Generalized the four `decide`-based spot-check theorems: `bare_not_monotonic` ŌåÆ `bare_not_upward_monotone` + `bare_not_downward_monotone` (both stated as `┬¼ ŌłĆ m m' n, ...` and refuted with explicit witnesses); `bare_not_horn_scale` deleted (now follows trivially from the upward+downward monotonicity refutations); `atLeast_strictly_weaker_than_bare` and `moreThan_disjoint_from_bare` rewritten as universal theorems quantified over `m` (and over `n` for the disjointness one) instead of fixed-`m=3` spot-checks. **(5)** Added ┬¦4 alternative-set classification theorems: `lowerAlternatives_classification (n) : (lowerAlternatives n).map (┬Ę.argument, ┬Ę.modifierClass, ┬Ę.boundDirection) = [(n, none, none), (n, some .classA, some .lower), (n, some .classB, some .lower)]` and the upper analogue. Both `rfl` ŌĆö they catch reordering, omission, or wrong-direction bugs in the alternative-set definitions. **(6)** Updated docstring reference in `Theories/Semantics/Alternatives/Lexical.lean` (renamed theorem). Numerals.Basic builds clean.

## [0.229.990] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Condoravdi 2002 mathlib-PR-style cleanup pass** (`Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Condoravdi2002.lean`). Audit the file from a mathlib-reviewer perspective and apply the entire punch list. (1) **Drop `C` author-tag suffix on operators**: `presC`/`perfC`/`mayC`/`wollC` ŌåÆ `pres`/`perf`/`may`/`woll`. The namespace `Condoravdi2002` already disambiguates; tagging identifiers with the paper author is non-mathlib style. (2) **Delete the four `_eq` simp rfl lemmas** (`presC_eq`/`perfC_eq`/`mayC_eq`/`wollC_eq`): they're definitional unfoldings, and `simp [pres]` (etc.) suffices on demand ŌĆö `@[simp]` rfl wrappers can destabilise simp normal forms. (3) **Collapse `Perspective ├Ś Orientation` (4 cells, 1 unattested) into 3-constructor `inductive ModalReading | present | epistemic | counterfactual`**: the unattested past-perspective + past-orientation cell is now impossible by construction. `ModalReading.perspective` and `ModalReading.orientation` are derived projections. Deletes `modalsForPresent`/`modalsForPastEpistemic`/`modalsForPastCF`, `attestedReadings`, and the `no_past_past`/`readings_distinct` decide-theorems (subsumed by exhaustion). (4) **Demote single-operator example glosses**: `mightBeHere` and `mightRun` deleted (pure aliases for `may MB .stative/.dynamic ... now`); the two scope-relevant compositions remain as `mayEpistemic` and `mightCounterfactual` (load-bearing for the epistemic-vs-counterfactual scope distinction). (5) **Variable hygiene**: file-scope `variable {W Time}` split into per-section `Operators`/`KleinBridge`/`ScopeCorrelation`/`Adverbs` blocks; the `Adverbs` section uses only `Time`, eliminating the auto-included-unused-W lint. (6) **Drop equation-number citations** (`[20]`/`[21]`/`[22]`/`[28]`/`[33]`/`[44]`/`[45]`/`[1]`/`[2]`) per CLAUDE.md "most-hallucinated category" rule ŌĆö replaced with content descriptions ("the perfect operator", "the diversity condition"). (7) **Fix stale architecture pointer**: header referenced `Theories/Semantics/Tense/BranchingTime.lean` (does not exist) ŌĆö corrected to `Core/Modality/HistoricalAlternatives.lean` (the actual import). (8) **Replace ╬╗-calculus snippets in docstrings with prose** (mathlib-style informal-English documentation). (9) **`compatible` now takes `ModalReading` directly** (was `Orientation`) and projects via `reading.orientation`; theorem renames `yesterday_past_compat` ŌåÆ `yesterday_epistemic_compat`, `tomorrow_past_incompat` ŌåÆ `tomorrow_epistemic_incompat`. (10) **`compatible` docstring honesty**: explicit note that the eventive/stative distinction is *not* recorded ŌĆö the AT relation permits `now = e.start` for `atEventForward`, so the paper's "??He may get sick now" marking is documented as a pragmatic event-duration fact, not a temporal-logic incompatibility. (11) **Fragment-binding theorem** drops `condoravdi_` prefix (redundant with namespace): `condoravdi_might_have_distinct_aux_types` ŌåÆ `might_have_distinct_aux_types`, moved to its own `Fragment binding` section header. (12) **Operator argument-naming consistency**: `pres` now takes `(t : Time)` matching `perf`/`may`/`woll`. Downstream (`CarianiSantorio2018.lean` docstring reference to `wollC`) updated to `woll`. Hacquard2006 (consumes `Perspective` ŌĆö kept as a separate enum for downstream stability) builds clean. Net: file is ~30 lines shorter, eliminates 1 enum + 1 structure + 2 example defs + 4 simp wrappers + 2 decide theorems, with no loss of mathematical content.

## [0.229.989] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Hungarian focus fragment + Kiss 1998 study + lifted MSMH predicate.** Built the contrasting language to Hausa called for by 0.229.983's mathlib-PR-review pass on `HartmannZimmermann2007.lean`. **(1)** `Theories/Semantics/Focus/MeaningStructureMapping.lean` (NEW, ~50 lines): the polymorphic `MeaningStructureMapping {U S I} (licensed : U ŌåÆ Prop) (struct : U ŌåÆ S) (interp : U ŌåÆ I) : Prop` predicate plus `refute_by_witness` lemma. The MSMH is a typology-level claim, not a per-language definition; lifting it to the Theory layer makes the Hausa/Hungarian contrast a difference of verdict on a *single shared hypothesis* rather than two parallel-but-unrelated statements. **(2)** `Fragments/Hungarian/Focus.lean` (NEW, ~210 lines): `Position` (preverbal/postverbal), `FocusType` (identificational/information) with `IsExhaustive : FocusType ŌåÆ Prop`, `ConstituentClass` (regular/universal/onlyPhrase/someIndef) with the ┬¦3 `compatibleWith` distributional table, `FocusConfig` structure with `Licensed` predicate combining positionŌĆōtype pairing and class compatibility, smart constructors `mkIdentificational`/`mkInformation` taking compatibility proofs, and three universal theorems: `licensed_position_determines_type`, `onlyPhrase_forces_identificational` (csak X must move to Spec,FP ŌĆö paper ┬¦3 last ┬Č), `someIndef_never_licensed` (valami/valaki barred from both ŌĆö paper eq. 17e). **(3)** `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/Kiss1998.lean` (NEW, ~190 lines): six paper cells (`preverbal_identificational`/eq. 5a, `postverbal_information`/5b, `preverbal_hat`/8a, `postverbal_hat`/8b, `starred_universal_identificational`/17b, `universal_information`/19b); `HungarianMSMH` instantiates the lifted predicate with `Licensed`/`position`/`focusType`; `hungarian_satisfies_MSMH` proves Hungarian validates the hypothesis (the same theorem statement that fails for Hausa); `preverbal_iff_exhaustive` composes positionŌĆōtype with typeŌĆōexhaustivity to derive the semantic payoff; four distributional-restriction cell-level theorems. **(4)** `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/HartmannZimmermann2007.lean` refactored: dropped local `MeaningStructureMappingGeneric`/`MeaningStructureMapping`, replaced with `HausaMSMH` instantiating the lifted predicate (filter = `IsHausaLicensed`, struct = `cfg.strategy`, interp = `pragType`); `hausa_falsifies_MSMH` now uses `MSMH.refute_by_witness`; `hausa_falsifies_MSMH_inSitu` adds explicit `IsHausaLicensed` witness conjuncts to match the lifted predicate's signature. **(5)** `kiss-1998` bib entry added with verified Language 74(2):245-273 metadata and JSTOR stable URL (DOI omitted per CLAUDE.md hallucination-prevention discipline ŌĆö the paper PDF has no DOI on the front matter). All four files (theory, fragment, study, refactored Hausa) build clean. The deeper integration is **typological symmetry by construction**: same predicate, opposite verdicts; future languages plug in by instantiating the `MSMH.MeaningStructureMapping` type at their own utterance/struct/interp choices.

## [0.229.988] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Zheng2025 ┬¦5: integrated felicity bridge between ┬¦2 (Kernel-bias) and ┬¦4 (Issue-singleton).** Closes the interconnection-density gap surfaced by 0.229.986's alias cleanup: nandao had two unrelated felicity stories living in the same study file ŌĆö `nandaoFelicitous (k : Kernel) (u : Background) (Žå : World ŌåÆ Prop)` (the discourse-context bias check, ┬¦2) and `IsSingleton (declarative p)` (the Issue-level singleton presupposition on the sister, ┬¦4) ŌĆö with no theorem connecting them. New ┬¦5 introduces `nandaoFullFelicity (Q : Core.Issue World) (k : Kernel) (u : Background) (p : Set World) [DecidablePred p] : Prop := alt Q = {p} Ōł¦ nandaoFelicitous k u p` as the conjunction: the sister `Q` denotes a one-cell issue with witness `p` (Layer 1), AND the Kernel-bias check holds for `p` (Layer 2). The witness is supplied externally so decidability is concrete. **Bridges**: `nandaoFullFelicity_isSingleton` and `nandaoFullFelicity_kernel` project to the two component felicities (┬¦4 and ┬¦2 respectively); `nandao_polar_no_witness` proves the universal Layer-1 obstruction (a non-trivial two-cell `polar pŌéĆ` admits no integrated-felicity witness for any `(k, u)`, because the ┬¦4 type-level barrier blocks the `alt Q = {p}` requirement); `nandaoFullFelicity_declarative_iff` proves that on the canonical felicitous case `declarative p`, integrated felicity collapses to the ┬¦2 Kernel-bias check on `p` (Layer 1 holds trivially via `alt_declarative`). The bridge makes the layered architecture of nandao felicity explicit: presupposition at the semantics-syntax interface, bias at the discourse-context interface, with the witness serving as the unique connecting referent. Verified via scratch (`scratch/test_section5.lean` with stub Kernel/Background/nandaoFelicitous matching the post-migration `World ŌåÆ Prop` signatures); the file itself currently fails to build because of an unrelated in-progress `Bool ŌåÆ Prop` migration in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean` from a parallel session ŌĆö once that lands, both ┬¦2 (which also has a stale `World ŌåÆ Bool` signature in `kernel_requires_evidence`) and ┬¦5 will typecheck against the migrated Kernel.

### Changed
- **Stage I of Numerals API cleanup: lift `typeLower` to `Core/Scales/Scale.lean` as a scale-general operation.** Stage H left `typeLower` (existential lowering of an exact-style degree property: `Ōłā d' Ōēź d, exact d' w`) inside `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean` even though the operation has no dependence on `Nat` or numerals ŌĆö it lives over any reflexive linear order. Per CLAUDE.md "import don't restipulate", any operation polymorphic over `Core.Scale` primitives belongs in `Core`. **(1)** New ┬¦6c "Existential Lowering (Type-Shifting)" added to `Core/Scales/Scale.lean` (within the `[LinearOrder ╬▒]` variable scope) containing: `def typeLower {W} (exact : ╬▒ ŌåÆ W ŌåÆ Prop) (d : ╬▒) (w : W) : Prop := Ōłā d', d' Ōēź d Ōł¦ exact d' w`; the collapse theorem `typeLower_eqDeg_iff : typeLower (eqDeg ╬╝) d w Ōåö atLeastDeg ╬╝ d w` (proved by `╬╝ w` witness in one direction, equation rewriting in the other); the generic `Decidable (typeLower (eqDeg ╬╝) d w)` instance via `decidable_of_iff` against `atLeastDeg`; `atLeastDeg.decidable` (lifted from being implicit in numeral context); and the universal-closure failure lemma `distinct_no_universal_witness {╬▒} (kŌéü kŌéé : ╬▒) (hne : kŌéü ŌēĀ kŌéé) : ┬¼ Ōłā x, ŌłĆ k, k = kŌéü Ōł© k = kŌéé ŌåÆ x = k` (no `LinearOrder` needed ŌĆö pure equality reasoning). **(2)** `Numerals/Basic.lean` ┬¦6 reduced from 14 lines to a thin re-export: `theorem typeLower_bareMeaning_iff (m n : Nat) : Core.Scale.typeLower bareMeaning m n Ōåö atLeastMeaning m n := Core.Scale.typeLower_eqDeg_iff id m n`. The `bareMeaning ŌēĪ Core.Scale.eqDeg id` and `atLeastMeaning ŌēĪ Core.Scale.atLeastDeg id` `abbrev` definitions make the instantiation definitional. Local `def typeLower`, `theorem typeLower_uniqueness`, the explicit Numeral-specific Decidable instance, and `theorem universal_closure_fails` all deleted. **(3)** Deleted ┬¦7 duplicates `typeLower_iff_atLeast` and `exh_typeLower_roundtrip` ŌĆö both were literal restatements of `typeLower_bareMeaning_iff` masquerading as separate theorems for the duality table. The ┬¦7 docstring narrative now points at `exhNumeral_iff_bare` and `typeLower_bareMeaning_iff` directly as the two halves of the duality, no synonym layer. **(4)** `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/ScontrasPearl2021.lean` `typeshift_resolves_tension` re-pointed: `Semantics.Numerals.typeLower` ŌåÆ `Core.Scale.typeLower` (one call site). **Net** the type-shifting operation is now usable on any scale (gradable adjectives, time intervals, mass nouns, modal degrees), not just numerals; `Core.Scale` gains a foundational existential-lowering primitive that `Numerals/Basic.lean` now derives by passing `id`. All touched files (Core.Scale, Numerals.Basic, ScontrasPearl2021, Spector2013, KaoEtAl2014/Hyperbole) build clean.

## [0.229.986] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mathlib-PR cleanup of the kya:/nandao singleton-presupposition stack: collapse the alias triple into one foundational predicate + one subtype.** The 0.229.982 build had three layered aliases ŌĆö `IsSingleton` (Core), `singletonPresup`/`singletonInterp` (Theories felicity wrapper), `kyaPresup`/`kyaInterp` + `nandaoSingletonPresup`/`nandaoSingletonInterp` (per-particle) ŌĆö plus `highlightedPolar p := declarative p` as a fourth alias. Subjected to mathlib-PR-review discipline: any layer that's `def f := g` (zero added structure) gets dropped; partial functions become subtypes; the polar-failure proof's inline `alt`-membership computation gets extracted into a reusable lemma. **(1)** `Core/Issue/Hamblin.lean`: added `alt_polar_of_nontrivial : alt (polar p) = {p, pßČ£}` (when `p ŌēĀ Ōłģ, univ`), the maximality characterization that drives the polar Q vs. highlighted Q contrast. Replaces the ~30-line inline `alt`-membership computation in `not_isSingleton_polar_of_nontrivial`. **(2)** `Core/Issue/Singleton.lean`: simplified `not_isSingleton_polar_of_nontrivial` from 30 lines of inline `alt` proofs to 8 lines invoking `alt_polar_of_nontrivial`; added `SingletonIssue W := {Q : Issue W // IsSingleton Q}` subtype with `issue`/`witness` extraction and `ofDeclarative` constructor ŌĆö the mathlib-style replacement for the `Option`-valued partial-function `singletonInterp`. **(3)** Deleted `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Highlighted/Basic.lean` entirely: `highlightedPolar p := declarative p` was a pure alias with no semantic content. The "highlighted polar" terminology of @cite{roelofsen-farkas-2015}/@cite{bolinger-1978} now lives as a comment in `Singleton.lean`'s docstring; consumers use `declarative p` directly. **(4)** Deleted `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Presupposition/Singleton.lean` entirely: `singletonPresup Q := IsSingleton Q` (alias) and `singletonInterp Q : Option (Issue W)` (Option-valued partial fn) were a redundant middle layer. The Option pattern is replaced by the `SingletonIssue` subtype. **(5)** `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/BhattDayal2020.lean` ┬¦4 rewritten: dropped `kyaPresup`/`kyaInterp` aliases; theorems now state directly in terms of `IsSingleton (declarative p)` and `SingletonIssue.ofDeclarative` (`kya_felicitous_declarative`, `kya_interp_declarative`, `kya_infelicitous_two_cell_polar`, `kya_polarQuestion_signals_singletonPresup`). **(6)** `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean` ┬¦4 rewritten: dropped `nandaoSingletonPresup`/`nandaoSingletonInterp` aliases; the cross-particle agreement theorem `nandao_kya_share_felicity` now states the proof terms are *definitionally equal* (both reduce to `isSingleton_declarative`) rather than wrapping an `Iff.rfl` over alias-unfolding. **(7)** `Linglib.lean` drops the two deleted module imports. **Net** four files instead of five, three layers of aliasing collapsed to one (the foundational `IsSingleton` predicate) plus one subtype, and the convergence noted in @cite{bhatt-dayal-2020} fn. 11 holds true by construction ŌĆö both particles' felicity theorems reduce to the same `isSingleton_declarative`. All touched files build clean (Hamblin, Singleton, BhattDayal2020 verified directly; Zheng2025 ┬¦4 verified via scratch ŌĆö the file currently fails to build because of an unrelated in-progress BoolŌåÆProp migration in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean` from a parallel session).

## [0.229.985] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Condoravdi 2002 adverb-compatibility section refactored to derive from AT primitives** (`Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Condoravdi2002.lean`). The previous section encoded the conclusions as definitions (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern): `ReferenceDirection.reaches : ReferenceDirection ŌåÆ TemporalRegion ŌåÆ Bool` was a 3├Ś3 lookup table, `compatible` was a Bool composition over it, and the eight (in)compat theorems were `by decide` over the table ŌĆö so the "theorems" only unpacked what was stipulated. Replaced with a structural derivation: (1) **Three inductives collapse to one structure**: `TemporalRegion`/`FrameAdverb`/`ReferenceDirection` deleted; `FrameAdverb (Time : Type*)` carries `name : String` + `period : Time ŌåÆ Set Time` per @cite{condoravdi-2002} [28]'s intersective-predicate-modifier schema (`YESTERDAY ŌēĪ ╬╗P╬╗w╬╗t[AT(t Ōł® yesterday, w, P)]`). Concrete adverbs `yesterday`/`now_`/`tomorrow` define `period now := {t | t < now}` / `{now}` / `{now < t}`. (2) **`projectedRegion : Orientation ŌåÆ Time ŌåÆ Set Time`** reads off the AT relation directly: future maps to `{t | now Ōēż t}` (from `atEventForward`/`atStateForward`'s `now Ōēż e.start`/`now Ōēż e.finish`); past maps to `{t | t < now}` (from `perfC`'s `t' < now`). (3) **`compatible : FrameAdverb Time ŌåÆ Orientation ŌåÆ Time ŌåÆ Prop`** is now non-empty intersection of adverb period with projected region ŌĆö BoolŌåÆProp migration per project-wide policy. (4) **All 8 theorems reproved structurally**: `tomorrow_present_compat [NoMaxOrder Time]` via `exists_gt`+`le_of_lt`; `yesterday_present_incompat` via `lt_of_lt_of_le`+`lt_irrefl`; `now_present_compat` via `le_refl`; `yesterday_past_compat [NoMinOrder Time]` via `exists_lt`; `tomorrow_past_incompat` via `lt_trans`+`lt_irrefl`. The eventive-vs-stative distinction at "now" is dropped from the formal predictions (AT permits `now = e.start` for `atEventForward`); the docstring of `now_present_compat` flags Condoravdi's "??He may get sick now" marking as a pragmatic event-duration fact, not a temporal-logic incompatibility ŌĆö surfacing the previously-hidden mismatch between the lookup table and the AT semantics. Hacquard2006 (only consumer, uses `Perspective`) builds clean.

## [0.229.984] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage H of Numerals API cleanup: mathlib-style PR review fixes to `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean`.** (1) **Unified `NumeralExpr`/`NumeralAlternative`**: deleted the duplicate `NumeralAlternative` inductive (same five constructors as `NumeralExpr`) and its `meaning`/Decidable instance; `lowerAlternatives`/`upperAlternatives` now return `List NumeralExpr`. Two consumer docstrings updated (no code consumers). (2) **Split off `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Degree.lean`**: the `HasDegree`-typed predicates (`numeralExact`) and `CardinalityDegree : HasDegree Nat ŌäÜ` instance moved to a new sibling file; deleted `numeralAtLeast`/`numeralApprox`/`numeralWithPrecision` (zero consumers). `KaoEtAl2014.lean` (Hyperbole) re-pointed to the new file. `Linglib.lean` updated. (3) **Deleted ┬¦7/┬¦8 spot-check theorems**: `lowerBound_from_exact_typeshift`, `exh_lowerBound_eq_exact`, `typeShift_exact_eq_lowerBound`, `exh_typeShift_roundtrip`, `typeShift_subsumes_exh` were all `decide`-closed over `standardWorlds ├Ś [.one, .two, .three]` ŌĆö replaced by general theorems `typeLower_bareMeaning_iff` (the collapse) and `exhNumeral_iff_bare` (proved via `omega`). `typeShift_subsumes_exh`'s reflexive padding conjuncts dropped. (4) **`exhNumeral` lost its unused `_maxN` parameter**: signature is now `exhNumeral (m n : Nat)`. Spector2013.lean's eight call sites updated (`exhNumeral 3 N n` ŌåÆ `exhNumeral N n`). (5) **`typeLower` made unbounded**: now `Ōłā k, k Ōēź m Ōł¦ exact k n` (no `maxN` / `List.range` bound); Decidable instance provided for the `bareMeaning` case via `decidable_of_iff` against `atLeastMeaning`. ScontrasPearl2021.lean's `typeLower bareMeaning 4 2 2` ŌåÆ `typeLower bareMeaning 2 2`. (6) **`universal_closure_fails` generalized** from the m=2/maxN=3 spot check to: `ŌłĆ kŌéü kŌéé, kŌéü ŌēĀ kŌéé ŌåÆ ┬¼Ōłā n, ŌłĆ k, k = kŌéü Ōł© k = kŌéé ŌåÆ n = k`. (7) **Class A/B proofs rewritten in direct term mode**: `classA_gt_excludes_bare := Nat.lt_irrefl m`, `classB_ge_includes_bare := Nat.le_refl m`, `classB_entailed_by_bare m n h := h Ō¢Ė Nat.le_refl m`, `classA_not_entailed_by_bare m := Ō¤©rfl, Nat.lt_irrefl mŌ¤®` ŌĆö replaced 5-lemma `simp only` chains. (8) **Renamed `numerals_not_horn_scalar` ŌåÆ `bare_not_horn_scale`** for parse clarity (only docstring consumers updated). (9) **Consolidated `open` blocks**: GQT bridges (┬¦8) wrapped in `section GQTBridge / open Classical Core.IntensionalLogic Semantics.Quantification` instead of three repeated `open ... in`. (10) **Deleted ┬¦10 (single `Iff.rfl` `moreThan_iff_gt`), ┬¦11 (12 verification examples), ┬¦6 (`standardNumWords`/`standardWorlds` ŌĆö used only by deleted spot-checks).** (11) **Header citations expanded**: added `@cite{chierchia-fox-spector-2012}`, `@cite{partee-1987}`, `@cite{fox-2007}`. Section list now 8 sections (was 14). All touched files (Numerals.Basic, Numerals.Degree, Spector2013, ScontrasPearl2021, KaoEtAl2014, FoxHackl2006, Alternatives.Lexical) build clean.

## [0.229.983] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Focus/Studies/HartmannZimmermann2007.lean` mathlib-PR-review pass: deepen the load-bearing claims, remove the window dressing.** Re-audited the file from a mathlib-maintainer perspective and either deepened or deleted every flagged item. **Deepenings** (1) `selkirk_BFR_fails_for_hausa` was a one-line `Ōłā u, u.IsHausaLicensed Ōł¦ u.cfg.strategy = .inSitu` that held by destructuring the registry and proved nothing about any actual universalist claim. Replaced with a structural triad: `FocusUtterance.HasMorphosyntacticReflex u := u.cfg.strategy = .exSitu Ōł© u.cfg.hasStab = true` (the property the BFR demands), `UniversalBFR : Prop := ŌłĆ u, u.IsHausaLicensed ŌåÆ u.HasMorphosyntacticReflex` (the universalist claim, restricted to morphosyntactic reflexes ŌĆö already strong enough to fall on Hausa), and `hausa_falsifies_UniversalBFR : ┬¼ UniversalBFR` proved structurally via `inSitu_newInfo` (eq. 23, *Naa tahoo d├Āg├Ā B├Ł┼Önin Kw├Ānni* ŌĆö in-situ, no stabilizer). The refutation now engages an *actual* universal-quantifier statement, not an existence proof in disguise. **Removals** (2) `multi_focus_attested` was a vacuous `And.intro` on two registry lookups ŌĆö it didn't model two-focus *clauses*, just claimed two utterances are independently licensed; deleted. (3) `exSituMatrix`, `inSituMatrix`, `subjectFocusMatrix` were defined and never consumed; deleted (the flat `hzMatrix` is the only registry the universal theorems iterate). (4) `PragType.exhaustive` and `kiss_1998_falsified_for_hausa` removed: the Kiss-1998 refutation as written was tautological (`PragType.newInfo ŌēĀ .exhaustive` holds by `decide` regardless of constructor semantics). The ┬¦3.2.5 docstring header now flags exhaustivity as out-of-scope pending an alternatives-semantics exhaustivity operator that can carry actual semantic content. **Renamings** (5) `FocusUtt` ŌåÆ `FocusUtterance` (mathlib doesn't truncate). (6) `FocusUtt.HausaLicensed` ŌåÆ `FocusUtterance.IsHausaLicensed`; downstream theorems renamed in lockstep (`all_hzMatrix_HausaLicensed` ŌåÆ `all_hzMatrix_IsHausaLicensed`, `starred_inSitu_subject_not_HausaLicensed`, `licensed_exSitu_subject_HausaLicensed`, `inSitu_subject_subjunctive_HausaLicensed`). (7) `MSMH` / `MSMHGeneric` ŌåÆ `MeaningStructureMapping` / `MeaningStructureMappingGeneric` (acronym in identifier flagged); `hausa_falsifies_MSMH` ŌåÆ `hausa_falsifies_MeaningStructureMapping`. (8) Cell helpers `es`/`is_` ŌåÆ `mkExSituUtt`/`mkInSituUtt`; cell aliases `es_*`/`is_*` ŌåÆ `exSitu_*`/`inSitu_*` (eight cells ├Ś two name-form changes). **Polish** (9) Smart constructor `mkExSituUtt` no longer carries a tactic-default proof argument (`:= by intro _; rfl`) ŌĆö explicit `(fun _ => rfl)` at every call site; mathlib avoids tactic defaults in core constructor APIs. (10) Header docstring stripped of unsupported claims: dropped the false promise to bridge `Semantics.FocusInterpretation.qaCongruent` for ┬¦4.3 (the integration was never delivered) and the prose claim that `Focused` should derive from a Fragment-side syntactic-position predicate (placeholder TODO that was distracting from the actual content). The `Focused` enum stays as a paper-specific tag for the ┬¦2.2.2 generalization; future syntactic-position derivation can be added when there's a concrete consumer demanding it. **Net** the file shrinks ~25 lines, gains one genuinely deeper theorem (`hausa_falsifies_UniversalBFR`), loses three vacuous theorems and three unused registries, and reads as Lean rather than as project shorthand. `lake build Linglib.Phenomena.Focus.Studies.HartmannZimmermann2007` clean (615 jobs).

## [0.229.982] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Singleton-alternative presupposition foundation for kya: + nandao (mathlib-style layered design).** Three new files plus particle bindings in two existing study files factor the @cite{bhatt-dayal-2020} eq. 23 analysis of Hindi-Urdu *kya:* (and the parallel Mandarin *nandao* analysis cited in fn. 11) into a foundational predicate, a felicity wrapper, and per-particle aliases ŌĆö enforcing the CLAUDE.md "derive don't stipulate" discipline. (1) `Core/Issue/Singleton.lean`: foundational predicate `IsSingleton P := Ōłā p, alt P = {p}` over `Core.Issue W`, plus characterization lemmas `isSingleton_iff_subsingleton_and_nonempty`, `IsSingleton.of_isDeclarative`, `not_isSingleton_of_two_alternatives`, the canonical-success case `isSingleton_declarative`, the `ŌŖż`/`ŌŖź` simp lemmas, and the structural failure theorem `not_isSingleton_polar_of_nontrivial` proving the standard two-cell @cite{ciardelli-groenendijk-roelofsen-2018} polar `polar p` (= `declarative p ŌŖö declarative pßČ£`) is **not** singleton when `p ŌēĀ Ōłģ, univ`. The latter captures algebraically why kya: cannot license a Hamblin polar ŌĆö it requires the one-cell highlighted denotation. (2) `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Highlighted/Basic.lean`: the @cite{roelofsen-farkas-2015}/@cite{bolinger-1978} highlighted polar Q, defined as `highlightedPolar p := declarative p`. The dedicated alias signals consumer intent (singleton-presupposition reasoning, not informativity); `highlightedPolar p` is singleton by construction (`isSingleton_highlightedPolar` inherits from `isSingleton_declarative`); the bridge `polar_ne_highlightedPolar_of_nontrivial` makes the two-cell vs one-cell distinction crisp. `info_highlightedPolar : (highlightedPolar p).info = p` contrasts with `info_polar p = univ` ŌĆö highlighted polar is informative about `p`, two-cell polar is not. (3) `Theories/Semantics/Questions/Presupposition/Singleton.lean`: particle-agnostic felicity wrapper. `singletonPresup Q := IsSingleton Q` is the predicate; `singletonInterp Q : Option (Core.Issue W)` is the partial-function interpretation pattern (mathlib's standard predicate + Op + bridge correspondence) returning `some Q` on felicitous inputs, `none` otherwise ŌĆö faithful to eq. 23's "defined only when..., returns Q unchanged" semantics. Bridge `singletonInterp_isSome_iff` and the canonical-input theorems `singletonPresup_highlightedPolar`/`not_singletonPresup_polar_of_nontrivial`/`singletonPresup_declarative`. (4) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/BhattDayal2020.lean` ┬¦4: `kyaPresup` and `kyaInterp` are particle-level aliases over the algebraic core, plus four empirical-prediction theorems (`kya_felicitous_highlightedPolar`, `kya_interp_highlightedPolar`, `kya_infelicitous_two_cell_polar`, `kya_polarQuestion_signals_singletonPresup`) and a fragment-bridge that connects kya's `polarQuestion = true` field to the singleton-presupposition analysis. (5) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean` ┬¦4: `nandaoSingletonPresup` and `nandaoSingletonInterp` parallel aliases, the cross-particle agreement theorems `nandao_kya_share_singleton_presup` and `nandao_kya_share_interp` proving the @cite{bhatt-dayal-2020} fn. 11 cross-linguistic generalization holds **definitionally** (both unfold to the same `singletonPresup`) ŌĆö true by construction rather than via bridge theorem, the desired CLAUDE.md "interconnection density" pattern. `Linglib.lean` adds the three new module imports. The mathlib-discipline win: every empirical claim about kya:/nandao felicity factors through the same algebraic predicate over `Issue`, so future particles encoding the same presupposition (e.g. additional Q-particles in other languages) can plug into the same foundation by aliasing.

## [0.229.981] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage G of Numerals API cleanup: ground the 5 meaning functions in `Core.Scale.{...}Deg id`.** Per the CLAUDE.md "import don't restipulate" principle, the five named numeral meanings in `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean` ┬¦2 are now `abbrev`s over the polymorphic degree-property primitives in `Core/Scales/Scale.lean`: `bareMeaning := Core.Scale.eqDeg id`, `moreThanMeaning := Core.Scale.moreThanDeg id`, `fewerThanMeaning := Core.Scale.lessThanDeg id`, `atLeastMeaning := Core.Scale.atLeastDeg id`, `atMostMeaning := Core.Scale.atMostDeg id`. Connection to the maximal-informativity scale infrastructure now holds by construction, not by bridge theorem ŌĆö ┬¦12 (Core.Scale Bridge Theorems) deleted entirely (was 4 trivial `Iff.rfl`s). `Decidable` instances re-derived via `inferInstanceAs (Decidable (n Ōēź m))` (etc.) since `abbrev` over a `def` doesn't auto-synthesize. Class A/B theorems (┬¦5) and GQT bridges (┬¦9, `gqt_atLeast_agrees`/`gqt_atMost_agrees`/`gqt_exactly_agrees`) had their `simp only` lists extended with `Core.Scale.{...}Deg, id_eq` to unfold the abbrev's right-hand side. Section list renumbered in the docstring (12 ŌåÆ 14 sections, no Core.Scale Bridge section). `FoxHackl2006.lean` updated: deleted ┬¦1 (`atLeast_3_is_atLeastDeg`/`moreThan_3_is_moreThanDeg`) ŌĆö vacuous after grounding; renumbered remaining sections; reworded module docstring to acknowledge that the bridge holds by construction. `Numerals.Basic` and `FoxHackl2006` build clean.

## [0.229.979] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Focus/Studies/HartmannZimmermann2007.lean` audit + mathlib-style refactor + 6 new theorems; `Fragments/Hausa/TAM.lean` PAC inventory widening.** Re-verified the H&Z 2007 paper end-to-end and corrected several memory-driven inaccuracies and structural concerns. (1) **PAC widening (`TAM.lean`)**: added five paper-grounded PACs to enable per-example PAC accuracy in the H&Z matrix ŌĆö `cmp_3sf_R` "ta" (eq. 24), `cont_3sf_R` "tak├©e" (eq. 22), `cmp_1sg_G` "naa" (eq. 23), `cont_1sg_R` "nak├©e" (eq. 29), `fut_1sg` "z├ón" (eqs. 25, 30). `representativePACs` registry expanded from 5 to 10 entries; `all_representative_PACs_wellFormed` proof updated to 10 `rfl`s. The Hausa "4sg" impersonal *├Āa/ak├©e* (eqs. 26-27) deferred ŌĆö `Core.Person.Category` does not yet expose an impersonal/4sg cell. (2) **PAC remap in H&Z matrix**: every `es_*`/`is_*` cell now uses the empirically correct PAC instead of the previous `cmp_3sm_R`/`cmp_3sm_G` placeholders ŌĆö `es_newInfo` ŌåÆ `cont_3sf_R`, `es_corrective` ŌåÆ `cmp_3sf_R`, `es_selective` ŌåÆ `cont_1sg_R`, `is_newInfo` ŌåÆ `cmp_1sg_G`, `is_corrective`/`is_selective` ŌåÆ `fut_1sg`. The two impersonal-subject cells (`es_contrastive`/`is_contrastive`) keep `cmp_3sm_R`/`cmp_3sm_G` with explicit docstring caveats noting the approximation. (3) **`HausaLicensed` correctness fix**: the previous unconditional subject-focus rule contradicted paper p. 4 ("subject foci are syntactically and morphologically unmarked in the future, habitual and subjunctive aspects"). New version conditions on `u.cfg.pac.tam.HasRelativeForm`: `u.cfg.Licensed Ōł¦ ((u.focused = .subject Ōł¦ u.cfg.pac.tam.HasRelativeForm) ŌåÆ u.cfg.strategy = .exSitu)`. New theorem `inSitu_subject_subjunctive_HausaLicensed` witnesses the qualification (3sg.M subjunctive subject in-situ is licensed because subjunctive lacks a relative form). (4) **`starred_inSitu_subject` PAC fix**: paper ┬¦2.2.2 example *Daud├Āa ya-n├Āa kir├Ā-nt├Ā* uses *yan─ü* (continuous), not the previous incorrect *y─ü* (completive). Repointed to `cont_3sm_G`. (5) **MSMH polymorphism**: new `MSMHGeneric {╬▒} (interp : FocusUtt ŌåÆ ╬▒) : Prop` polymorphic over the interpretation function; the original `MSMH` is now defined as `MSMHGeneric FocusUtt.pragType`. Lets future interpretation functions (exhaustivity status, discourse-relation tag) reuse the same refutation skeleton. (6) **`PragType.exhaustive` + Kiss 1998 refutation**: added the fifth pragmatic case from ┬¦3.2.5 (where H&Z argue exhaustive is a *pragmatic* ŌĆö not syntactic ŌĆö use of focus, against @cite{kiss-1998}'s identification of ex-situ with structural exhaustivity). New `kiss_1998_falsified_for_hausa : Ōłā u, u.cfg.strategy = .exSitu Ōł¦ u.pragType ŌēĀ .exhaustive` witnesses the refutation via `es_newInfo`. (7) **Selkirk BFR refutation (┬¦6.2)**: `selkirk_BFR_fails_for_hausa : Ōłā u, u.HausaLicensed Ōł¦ u.cfg.strategy = .inSitu` ŌĆö registers the categorical claim that an in-situ Hausa-licensed utterance with no morphosyntactic reflex of focus suffices to refute the universalist Basic Focus Rule. The ┬¦5 prosodic-pilot quantitative tendencies stay in docstring prose per CLAUDE.md (not Lean theorems). (8) **Multi-focus co-occurrence (┬¦2.3)**: new `multi_focus_attested` proves any two H&Z matrix cells are jointly licensed (the ┬¦2.3 closure observation). (9) **Matrix splitting**: `hzMatrix` decomposed into `exSituMatrix` / `inSituMatrix` / `subjectFocusMatrix` for finer-grained registries. (10) **Vacuous theorem deleted**: `stabilizer_tone_independent_of_allomorph` was a one-line `rfl` because `Stabilizer.toneAfter` ignores its first argument by definition; kept the load-bearing `polar_tone_from_polarOf` cross-fragment bridge. (11) **Smart constructors `es`/`is_` generalised**: now take an explicit `PAC` argument (and gender/number for ex-situ) rather than hard-coding `cmp_3sm_R`/`cmp_3sm_G`, threading the relative-form proof obligation through `mkExSitu`. (12) **Subject-focus theorem updated**: `subject_focus_only_exSitu` now takes an `hRel : u.cfg.pac.tam.HasRelativeForm` premise, matching the new conditional licensing rule. The previous CHANGELOG entry [0.229.976] mentioned the example *M├║usaa nßĖĢe ya n├®em├Āa* ŌĆö that was hallucinated; the actual paper ┬¦2.2.2 minimal pair is *Daud├Āa (nee) ya-k├©e kir├Ā-nt├Ā* / *\*Daud├Āa ya-n├Āa kir├Ā-nt├Ā* and the file uses the correct examples. Builds clean for both touched files.

## [0.229.978] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`BhattDayal2020.lean` rewritten as fragment-driven typology bridge + Japanese fragment cleanup + new `Fragments/English/QuestionParticles.lean`.** Completing the QP fragment cleanup pattern (theory-neutral lexical primitives in `Fragments/`; per-paper layer assignments in `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/`): (1) `Fragments/Japanese/Particles.lean` strips its local `Layer` inductive (cp/perspP/sap) and the `layer : Layer` field from `ParticleEntry` ŌĆö matching the same anti-pattern just removed from the 10 other QP fragments. The five Japanese particles (*ka*, *no*, *koto*, *kke*, *daroo*) now carry only distributional fields (inMatrix/inSubordinated/inQuasiSub). Verified `Fragments/Japanese/OutlookMarkers` (Part 2 of the same file) and `Phenomena/Modality/OutlookMarkers.lean` only consume the OutlookMarkers namespace, not the stripped `Layer`. (2) New `Fragments/English/QuestionParticles.lean` houses the *quick/quickly* MQP-like adverb entry (matrix-only urgency adverb), giving English a proper QP fragment file alongside Mandarin/German/Swedish/Slavic. (3) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/BhattDayal2020.lean` rewritten: imports `Fragments/HindiUrdu/Particles`, `Fragments/Japanese/Particles`, `Fragments/English/QuestionParticles`; defines per-particle layer overlays `hindi_urdu_kya_layer`/`japanese_ka_layer`/`japanese_kke_layer`/`english_quick_layer` (each takes the fragment entry as `_` arg, signaling theoretical overlay rather than computed property); the four `QParticleDatum` rows derive `form`/`inSubordinated`/`inQuasiSub` from fragment fields rather than re-stipulating literals. The three typology theorems (`cp_particles_in_subordination`, `perspP_particles_not_in_subordination`, `sap_particles_not_in_quasi_sub`) re-prove cleanly against the fragment-driven structure. (4) `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Dayal2025.lean::particle_layer_predicts_embedding` simplified to delegate to the three BhattDayal2020 theorems via `refine Ō¤©?_, ?_, ?_Ō¤®` ŌĆö no longer redundantly enumerates the four particles. (5) Stale `Phenomena.Questions.Studies.QPLayerTypology` references in 7 Slavic QP fragment docstrings (Russian, Bulgarian, Ukrainian, Polish, Slovenian, Serbian, Macedonian) repointed to `Phenomena.Questions.Studies.Simik2024` (the actual home of Slavic layer assignments after the prior session's typology aggregator deletion). (6) `Linglib.lean` adds `import Linglib.Fragments.English.QuestionParticles`. Touched files build clean.

## [0.229.977] - 2026-04-18

### Removed
- **Stage E of Numerals API cleanup: strip `NumeralTheory` + `LowerBound`/`Exact` instances + derived/comparison properties from `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean`.** Per the mathlib-discipline question that motivated the whole cleanup ("is `NumeralTheory` good or are we overengineering?"), the answer was: `(Nat ŌåÆ Nat ŌåÆ Prop) ├Ś Decidable-witness` bundled as a structure is overengineered when both implementations (`LowerBound.meaning = atLeastMeaning Ōłś toNat`, `Exact.meaning = bareMeaning Ōłś toNat`) are pointwise reducible to the underlying meaning functions. Sole external consumer of `LowerBound`/`Exact`/`NumeralTheory.*` was Basic.lean's own ┬¦8/┬¦9 derived-property and comparison-theorem tables (`compatibleWorlds`, `compatibleCount`, `hasAmbiguity`, `isMonotonic`, `isStrongerThan`, `theoriesAgreeAt`, `theoriesAgreeOn`, `theoriesEquivalent`, `divergingWorlds`, `hasMoreAmbiguity` + ~25 `decide`-proof lemmas wrapping them). The ┬¦10 grounding theorems (`bare_eq_exact_*`, `atLeast_eq_lowerBound_*`) collapsed to `rfl` precisely *because* the instances were thin reducibility wrappers ŌĆö they were trivially-true bridges between two names for the same function, the inverse anti-pattern of the "encode conclusions as definitions" warning in CLAUDE.md. Kept: the five core Prop meaning functions (`bareMeaning`, `moreThanMeaning`, `fewerThanMeaning`, `atLeastMeaning`, `atMostMeaning`), `BareNumeral`, `NumeralExpr.meaning` polymorphic over the bare-numeral semantics, alternative sets, Class A/B theorems, type-shifting (Kennedy ┬¦3.1), EXH-typeshift duality (Spector ┬¦6.2), GQT bridges (`gqt_atLeast_agrees`/`gqt_atMost_agrees`/`gqt_exactly_agrees`), Core.Scale degree bridges (┬¦12), HasDegree-based numeral expression semantics, precision-mode numerals. `lowerBound_from_exact_typeshift` now reads `atLeastMeaning w.toNat n Ōåö typeLower bareMeaning 3 w.toNat n` directly (no `LowerBound.meaning` indirection). Renumbered remaining sections sequentially (1-15) and updated the docstring section list. Updated `Theories/Semantics/Alternatives/Lexical.lean` line 220 docstring (replaced stale `NumeralTheory.isStrongerThan` reference with `numerals_not_horn_scalar`/`atLeast_strictly_weaker_than_bare`). Affected files (`Numerals.Basic`, `Alternatives.Lexical`, `Spector2013`, `Kennedy2015`, `BylininaNouwen2020`) build clean; pre-existing failures in `Modality.Studies.Rubinstein2014`/`Ferreira2023` are concurrent-session work, untouched here.

## [0.229.976] - 2026-04-18

### Removed
- **Stage D of Numerals API cleanup: dissolve `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/NegationScope.lean` + `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/ScalarImplicatures/Operations.lean`.** Both were paper-replication files mashed into `Theories/` in violation of the CLAUDE.md TheoriesŌåöPhenomena split. `NegationScope.lean` (275 lines) replicated @cite{horn-1972}'s internal/external negation scope asymmetry as a `NegationScope` enum + `negatedMeaning T w scope k` parameterized over `NumeralTheory` + 8 `decide` lemmas; sole external consumer was `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/ScontrasPearl2021.lean`, which used exactly two of the lemmas (`exact_collapses_negation_scope`/`lowerBound_preserves_negation_scope`) ŌĆö both are now inlined as direct `bareMeaning`/`atLeastMeaning` calls in `ScontrasPearl2021.lean` with no scope enum at all (the asymmetry between exact and lower-bound semantics is now visible in two adjacent `decide` proofs rather than mediated by a `NegationScope` parameter). `Operations.lean` (325 lines) replicated Horn's `assert/contradict/suspend/cancel` implicature operations as an `ImplicatureOperation` enum + `operationFelicitous`/`hasImplicatureTarget` predicates ŌĆö zero external consumers, deleted outright. Both can be revived in proper @cite{horn-1972}-grounded study files (e.g. `Phenomena/Polarity/Studies/Horn1972.lean`, `Phenomena/ScalarImplicatures/Studies/Horn1972.lean`) when those papers receive full formalizations. Removed corresponding imports from `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.229.974] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
