- **Hausa fragments audit cleanup: verb-example dehallucination + cross-fragment polarity bridge.** (1) `Fragments/Hausa/VerbGrades.lean`: dropped `@cite{parsons-1960}` from the header (no bib entry; replaced with prose attribution to Newman's codification), and replaced three hallucinated/wrong verb examples against @cite{newman-2000} ch. 74 ŌĆö `aikee` (wrong tone/vowel for gr5) ŌåÆ `sayar` 'sell' (genuine gr5 efferential of `saya` 'buy', minimal pair); `zoo` 'come' (irregular v*, not gr6) ŌåÆ `koomoo` 'return here' (genuine gr6 ventive); `biyu` (numeral, not a verb at all) ŌåÆ `taaru` 'meet' (genuine gr7 sustentative). The ┬¦7 example block updated to consume the new names (`taaru.voiceType`, `sayar.tones`). (2) `Fragments/Hausa/Focus.lean`: cite key `green-jaggar-2003` ŌåÆ `jaggar-green-2003` (correct author order); section number `┬¦66.2` (FUNCTION) ŌåÆ `┬¦66.1` (FORM) for the stabilizer agreement claim. (3) New ┬¦8 cross-fragment polarity bridge: `Stabilizer.toneAfter (host : ToneFeature) := polarOf host` and `stabilizer_tone_is_polar : s.toneAfter host = polarOf host := rfl` ground the description of *n─ō/c─ō* as polar-toned (@cite{newman-2000} ┬¦66.1) in the same `Tone.polarOf` operator that handles the genitive linker *-n* ŌĆö making the two cases instances of one autosegmental operation rather than parallel idiosyncrasies, with `stabilizer_toneAfter_involutive` derived as a one-line corollary of `Tone.polarOf_involutive_on_HL`. Builds clean for all 5 Hausa fragment files (VerbGrades, Tone, TAM, Focus, Gender). Three corresponding `references.bib` entries (`jaggar-2001`, `hartmann-zimmermann-2007`, `jaggar-green-2003`) are appended in the working tree but deferred to a separate commit due to concurrent bib edits.

## [0.229.973] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Fragments/English/Will.lean`: morphology-only Fragment for English future modals + C&S binding theorems.** New file `Linglib/Fragments/English/Will.lean` declares `FutureModalEntry` (form + `pastTense` flag + gloss, no semantic clause) and the two English entries `will` (present-tense) and `would` (past-tense). Per the typology-only Fragment discipline established by `Fragments/Dutch/Modals.lean` and `Fragments/English/Conditionals.lean`, the Fragment commits only to surface morphology ŌĆö no theory of meaning. The mathlib analogue: `ŌäØ` doesn't pre-commit to one structure (separate `OrderedField`/`MetricSpace`/`TopologicalSpace` instances); a Fragment entry shouldn't pre-commit to one semantic clause when multiple competing analyses (selectional, Kratzerian universal, Klecha modal-cum-tense, Copley future operator) coexist in the literature. The C&S binding lives in `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/CarianiSantorio2018.lean` as `cariniSantorioSemantics : FutureModalEntry ŌåÆ SelectionFunction W ŌåÆ (W ŌåÆ Prop) ŌåÆ Set W ŌåÆ W ŌåÆ Prop` (case-split on `pastTense`: `wouldSem` for past, `willSem` for present), with per-entry verification theorems `cs_will_eq_willSem` and `cs_would_eq_wouldSem` ŌĆö flipping the `pastTense` flag on either Fragment entry breaks exactly one binding, making the morphologyŌåösemantics correspondence visible at the type level. `cs_will_would_share_clause` recasts the ┬¦5.3.2 morphology-as-parameter-shift identity at the Fragment-binding layer (the two clauses are pointwise equal via `wouldSem_eq_willSem`). Future work: a second analysis (Klecha/Kratzerian/Copley) supplies its own binding against the same `Fragments.English.Will.{will, would}` entries, demonstrating the agnostic-Fragment design's payoff. Builds clean for both touched files.

## [0.229.972] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **C&S 2018 Tier 3: Truth3Ōåöwould-conditional bridge, modal subordination (┬¦5.3.1), cross-citations.** (1) `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Counterfactual.lean` gains `selectional_eq_wouldConditional_singleton_universe` ŌĆö the composition of `stalnaker_eq_selectional_singleton` (Truth3ŌåöBool stalnakerCounterfactual under singleton-closest) with `stalnakerCounterfactual_eq_wouldConditional_universe` (BoolŌåöProp would-conditional under universe parameter). Under the same singleton-closest hypothesis that resolves the Truth3 gap, the supervaluation-valued `selectionalCounterfactual sim A B w = .true` iff the Prop-valued `wouldConditional s ... Set.univ w` holds. The two layers ŌĆö Truth3 supervaluation over `Finset` and Prop selection-function over `Set` ŌĆö collapse to the same content. (2) `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/SelectionalRestrictor.lean` gains modal subordination from @cite{cariani-santorio-2018} ┬¦5.3.1: `subordinatedWillDiscourse s A B C f w := willConditional s A B f w Ōł¦ willSem s C (f Ōł® ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢) w` formalises "If A, will B. Will C." where the second *will* inherits the if-clause's restricted parameter `f Ōł® ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢` rather than starting fresh from `f`. `subordinatedWillDiscourse_eq_conj` proves `Iff.rfl` that the discourse picks the *same* world for both prejacents, reducing to `B w' Ōł¦ C w'` at `w' = s.sel w (f Ōł® ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢)`. The selectional analysis predicts modal subordination for free from selection-function single-valuedness ŌĆö the discourse analogue of `compositional_CEM` and `narrow_negation_swap`. `subordinated_eq_unrestricted_iff` shows the subordinated reading differs from a fresh *will* at `f` precisely when restriction shifts the selected world. (3) Cross-citation pointers added: `Phenomena/Conditionals/Studies/Iatridou2000.lean` gains a "See also" pointer to C&S 2018 (Iatridou's past-morphology generalization dovetails with C&S ┬¦5.3.2's identification of *would* with morphological past *will*); `Phenomena/Conditionals/Studies/Stalnaker1975.lean` gains a "See also" pointer to C&S 2018 (C&S extends the Stalnaker selection-function mechanism from conditionals to bare *will*; the *would*-conditional/Stalnaker-counterfactual identification reuses Stalnaker 1975's machinery under universe parameter). Builds clean for all 4 touched files (Counterfactual, SelectionalRestrictor, Iatridou2000, Stalnaker1975).

## [0.229.971] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **SetoidŌĆōIssue bridge: `fromSetoid_le_iff` + Set-flavored partial answerhood / width.** Foundational pieces for unifying the legacy `Discourse.Issue` (Bool/List Hamblin alternatives) under `Core.Issue` (Set-based inquisitive lattice). `Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean` gains `fromSetoid_le_iff : fromSetoid rŌéü Ōēż fromSetoid rŌéé Ōåö rŌéü Ōēż rŌéé` ŌĆö the `Setoid ŌåÆ Issue` embedding is a **lattice embedding** in mathlib's order on `Setoid` (where `rŌéü Ōēż rŌéé` iff `rŌéü` is finer). The forward direction uses the doubleton `{x, y}` as a probe for `rŌéü`-equivalence; the backward direction is monotonicity (extracted as `fromSetoid_mono`). This is the clean Set-based replacement for the Bool/List bridge `refinesOn_iff_questionEntails_of_partition` formerly housed in `Core/Partition.lean` ŌĆö now a one-liner over `Setoid`/`Core.Issue` with no `worlds : List W` quantifier and no `isPartition` precondition. `Core/Issue/Answerhood.lean` gains `isPartialAnswer P Žā := Ōłā p Ōłł alt P, Žā ŌŖå p Ōł© Žā ŌŖå pßČ£` (@cite{roberts-2012} Def. 3a ŌĆö the both-polarity version that catches negative partial answerhood) and `isMoveRelevant R P subquestions` (@cite{roberts-2012} Def. 15 / @cite{ippolito-kiss-williams-2025} assumption iii: a move is relevant if some alternative is a partial answer to the QUD or to a subquestion). New `Core/Issue/Granularity.lean` defines the Deo-Thomas width relation `widerThan P Q` (@cite{deo-thomas-2025} (32)) over `Core.Issue`'s native maximal-alternatives selector `alt`: same informational content + no `Q`-alt properly contains a `P`-alt + some `P`-alt is properly contained in some `Q`-alt. With these foundations in place, the `Discourse.Issue` consumer migrations (DeoThomas2025, Umbach2004, KOS/Rules, Theories/.../Inquisitive bridge) can proceed against the Set-based API. Builds clean for all three touched files.

### Changed
- **C&S 2018: faithful 3-cap Sports Fan + H├Ījek-triviality counterexample + selectional/universal would divergence + ┬¦8.1 transparency strengthening.** The Sports Fan model in `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/CarianiSantorio2018.lean` is rewritten from `{w0=Warriors-win, w1=Warriors-lose, w2=cancelled}` (uniform 1/2 on `{w0,w1}`) ŌĆö which was a memory-driven invention ŌĆö to the paper's actual @cite{cariani-santorio-2018} ┬¦2.3 + ┬¦3 figure 2 model: three cap-choice worlds `{cw=Warriors-cap, cg=Giants-cap, cn=no-cap}` with uniform credence 1/3. The 3-cap structure is what blocks the @cite{hajek-1989} triviality argument (paper ┬¦8.2 footnote 32): no proposition lands on probability 1/2 unconditionally ŌĆö `no_unconditional_one_half : ŌłĆ B : W ŌåÆ Bool, cynthiaPMF.probOf B ŌēĀ 1/2` is proven by exhaustive enumeration over 8 Bool predicates (probabilities Ōłł {0, 1/3, 2/3, 1}). The cancelled-world cooked-up `cancelled_would_not_win` is replaced by paper eq. (30): `cap_warriors_credence_one_half` proves `╬╝(ŌĆ¢cap Ōł® Warriors-capŌĆ¢) / ╬╝(ŌĆ¢capŌĆ¢) = (1/3) / (2/3) = 1/2`. New `cynthiaSel_coherent : cynthiaSel.isCoherent` exhibits the ┬¦5.1 well-ordering rationality constraint by enumeration over 81 quadruples. New helper proposition `wearsCap` and Bool indicator `wearsCapB` for the cap-conditional. `Selectional.lean` ┬¦3 (Content Transparency) gains set-level conjunction/disjunction transparencies ŌĆö `will_and_inter_modalParam_eq` (`ŌĆ¢will (AŌł¦B)ŌĆ¢_f = (ŌĆ¢will AŌĆ¢ Ōł® ŌĆ¢will BŌĆ¢)_f`) and `will_or_union_modalParam_eq` (`ŌĆ¢will (AŌł©B)ŌĆ¢_f = (ŌĆ¢will AŌĆ¢ Ōł¬ ŌĆ¢will BŌĆ¢)_f`) ŌĆö strengthening the existing pointwise `will_and_eq_will_and_will_on_modalParam`. ┬¦7 (Cognitive role) gains compositional lifts `cognitive_role_and`, `cognitive_role_or`, `cognitive_role_not` showing the credence identity passes through Ōł¦/Ōł©/┬¼ ŌĆö direct corollaries of `cognitive_role` applied to the pointwise compound. ┬¦9 (new) introduces multi-premise validity: `Valid2Arg`/`Valid1Arg` for arguments `HŌéü, ŌĆ”, HŌéÖ ŌŖ© C`, with `valid2Arg_implies_valid1Arg` bridge and `valid2_will_modus_ponens` as a worked example. `Valid1` marked `@[reducible]` so it unfolds at use sites without manual `unfold`. `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Counterfactual.lean` gains `divergeSel` (a Stalnakerian selection function on `Fin 3` preferring `1`) and `stalnaker_lewis_would_diverge` ŌĆö a concrete witness where `stalnakerCounterfactual divergeSel A B w = true` but `universalCounterfactual sim A B w = false` for `A = {1, 2}`, `B = {1}`, with the everyone-equally-close similarity ordering. The same structural source ŌĆö single-valuedness of selection vs. universal quantification over a non-trivial closest set ŌĆö drives the *will* / `universalWill` split in `Selectional.lean`, making the ┬¦5.3.2 paper motivation (counterfactuals split selectional from universal) visible as a derived fact. Builds clean for all 3 touched files (Selectional, Counterfactual, CarianiSantorio2018).

## [0.229.969] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Migrate Numerals embedding/operations APIs from `Bool` to `Prop` (mathlib discipline).** `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Embedding.lean` rewrites every meaning constructor to return `Prop` with a bundled `Decidable` instance: `negatedMeaning`/`possibilityMeaning`/`necessityMeaning`/`exactlyMeaning`/`conditionalMeaning`/`exh`/`exhUnderPossibility`/`exhOverPossibility`/`almostMeaning`/`onlyMeaning`/`exhUnderNominalQ`/`exhOverNominalQ` and helper `isProximal`. The `match w.succ with | some w' => ... | none => True` pattern is closed via `unfold f; generalize w.succ = succ; cases succ <;> infer_instance` (the standard `cases` on `w.succ` doesn't reduce the match in the goal). `isConvex` retains its `f : Nat ŌåÆ Prop` parameter with a `[DecidablePred f]` requirement so `decide`-checked theorems still close. `EmbeddingPrediction` keeps Bool tabular fields (preserves `BEq`/`Repr` deriving) but every entry in `embeddingDivergences` wraps Prop-returning meaning calls with `decide`. ~30 theorems converted from `= true`/`= false`/`!=` to bare/`┬¼`/`Ōåö` form. `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/NegationScope.lean` and `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/ScalarImplicatures/Operations.lean` migrate their local `negatedMeaning`/`operationFelicitous`/`hasImplicatureTarget` to Prop with matching Decidable instances; all `native_decide` invocations downgrade to `decide`. Downstream studies updated: `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/{FoxHackl2006, Spector2013, Kennedy2015, BylininaNouwen2020}.lean`, `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/ScontrasPearl2021.lean` (4 grounding theorems chain via `Ōåö` instead of `Bool.not_eq_true'` rewrites), `Phenomena/Persuasion/Studies/CumminsFranke2021.lean` (3 `moreThan_from_lowerBound_*` theorems use `Ōåö`; proofs lose `decide_eq_true_eq`), `Phenomena/ScalarImplicatures/Studies/GoodmanStuhlmuller2013.lean`. The Spector ┬¦3 EXH bridge bridges Prop `exhNumeral`/`exh` to Bool `exhB` via `ŌłĆ n Ōłł exhDomain, exhNumeral 3 k n Ōåö exhB exhDomain (lbAlts k) (lbMeaning k) n = true` (replacing `.all (... == ...)`); `lbAlts`/`lbMeaning` wrap `atLeastMeaning` with `decide` to produce `Nat ŌåÆ Bool` for `exhB` consumption. Builds clean for all 9 touched files (Numerals.Basic, Embedding, NegationScope, Operations, ScontrasPearl2021, CumminsFranke2021, GoodmanStuhlmuller2013, Spector2013, Kennedy2015, BylininaNouwen2020, FoxHackl2006).

## [0.229.968] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **C&S 2018 ┬¦5.3.2: *would* as past-tense morphological derivative of *will*; bridge `stalnakerCounterfactual` Ōåö `willConditional` over the universe.** Exercises the `Core.SelectionFunction` unification from 0.229.966 by formalising @cite{cariani-santorio-2018} ┬¦5.3.2's claim that *would* is the past-tense morphological form of *will*. (1) `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Selectional.lean` ┬¦8: `wouldSem` defined as a definitional alias of `willSem`, with `wouldSem_eq_willSem : wouldSem s A f w = willSem s A f w := rfl` capturing "morphology = parameter shift, not semantic shift"; the entire ┬¦2ŌĆō┬¦7 architecture (Negation Swap, Will Excluded Middle, unembedded collapse, ValidityŌéé) lifts unchanged via `would_negation_swap`, `would_excluded_middle`, `would_unembedded_collapse`, `valid2_would_negation_swap`, `valid2_would_excluded_middle` ŌĆö each a one-line wrapper around the corresponding *will* theorem. (2) `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/SelectionalRestrictor.lean`: `wouldConditional` as a definitional alias of `willConditional` with `wouldConditional_eq_willConditional` rfl bridge, lifting the ┬¦5.3.1 Kratzer-restriction analysis to *would*-conditionals. (3) `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Counterfactual.lean`: bridge theorem `stalnakerCounterfactual_eq_willConditional_universe` proves Stalnaker counterfactuals are exactly will-conditionals whose ambient modal parameter is `Set.univ` ŌĆö the *if*-clause then restricts the universe down to `ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢`, recovering Stalnaker's `s(w, ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢)`. The proof is one `Set.univ_inter` rewrite after unfolding. Restated as `stalnakerCounterfactual_eq_wouldConditional_universe` for the more natural surface gloss (counterfactuals are past-tense). The Counterfactual.lean header gains `import Linglib.Theories.Semantics.Modality.Selectional` + `import Linglib.Theories.Semantics.Conditionals.SelectionalRestrictor`. (4) `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/CarianiSantorio2018.lean` ┬¦5: `cancelled_would_not_win` ("if the game had been cancelled, the Warriors *would* have won" is FALSE) ŌĆö restriction by `{w | w = w2}` sends the modal parameter to `{w2}`; Inclusion picks `w2`; `warriorsWin w2 = False`. `cancelled_would_eq_will` is the matching `rfl` showing the would- and will-conditionals are the same proposition. The four operators (bare *will*, bare *would*, will-conditional, Stalnaker counterfactual) now derive from a single `Core.SelectionFunction` substrate, differing only in modal parameter. Builds clean (1001 jobs).

## [0.229.967] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Drop the `DTS.Issue` wrapper ŌĆö store `topic : W ŌåÆ Bool` directly on `DTSContext`, derive the inquisitive view via `Core.Issue.polar` projection (mathlib `Filter.principal` pattern).** The 1-field `structure Issue (W : Type*) where topic : (W ŌåÆ Bool)` in `Theories/Pragmatics/DecisionTheoretic/Core.lean` was a redundant single-field wrapper around a Bool predicate that contributed no semantic content beyond the field it held ŌĆö its only role was to collide nominally with `Core.Issue` (the inquisitive-content lattice). Following mathlib's `Filter.principal` discipline (no `PrincipalFilter` wrapper type ŌĆö code just writes `Filter.principal s` at consumption sites), `DTSContext` now stores `topic : (W ŌåÆ Bool)` directly, and the polar interrogative view is recovered on demand via `def DTSContext.toCoreIssue (ctx) : Core.Issue W := Core.Issue.polar {w | ctx.topic w = true}`. Two grounding theorems anchor the projection: `toCoreIssue_info` (`info = Set.univ`, the dichotomic question is non-informative) and `toCoreIssue_isInquisitive_iff` (genuine inquisitiveness Ōåö topic is non-trivial), both inheriting from `Core.Issue.{info_polar, isInquisitive_polar_iff}`. Bulk migrated ~50 sites of `ctx.issue.topic` ŌåÆ `ctx.topic` across `Core.lean`, `But.lean`, `Even.lean`, `Also.lean`, `MerinBridge.lean`, `ScalarImplicature.lean`, plus `Phenomena/Focus/Studies/IppolitoKissWilliams2025.lean`. `swapIssue` updates from `{ issue := Ō¤©Decidable.pnot W ctx.issue.topicŌ¤®, prior := ctx.prior }` to `{ topic := Decidable.pnot W ctx.topic, prior := ctx.prior }`. `defaultBut` (the redundant identity wrapping `b` in an `Issue`) is dropped from `But.lean`; `defaultButCtx` constructs `Ō¤©b, priorŌ¤®` directly. `MerinBridge.lean`'s pattern matches drop one layer: `intro Ō¤©Ō¤©topicŌ¤®, priorŌ¤®` becomes `intro Ō¤©topic, priorŌ¤®`. The `IppolitoKissWilliams2025.dtsToInquisitive` helper (already a `Discourse.Issue.polar` projection in spirit) is unaffected by the API change since it operates on a raw `topic : W ŌåÆ Bool` rather than a `DTS.Issue`. The full unification of `Discourse.Issue` (Bool/List Hamblin alternatives) with `Core.Issue` (Set-based inquisitive lattice) remains deferred (12 remaining `Discourse.Issue` consumers requiring Roberts 2012 relevance machinery rewrites over Set), but DTS now consumes only the Set-based `Core.Issue.polar` projection at theory-bridging sites. Builds clean (1002 jobs through `IppolitoKissWilliams2025`).

## [0.229.966] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **C&S 2018 architectural cleanup: unify `SelectionFunction`, fix eq.(22) hallucination, lift EM/Negation Swap to Core, add missing ┬¦7 theorems.** (1) Deleted the duplicate `Semantics.Conditionals.SelectionFunction` (with field `select`) from `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Basic.lean`; that namespace now `open _root_.Core (SelectionFunction selectionPrefers)` and re-exports the canonical `Core.SelectionFunction` (with field `sel`). Migrated all downstream call sites ŌĆö `coherentSelectionToSimilarity`, `selectionConditional`, `pragmaticConstraint`, `selectionConditional_eq_material_within_context` ŌĆö from `s.select`/`s.success` to `s.sel`/`s.inclusion`. `selectionPrefers` and `SelectionFunction.isCoherent` (pure selection-function structure) graduate from `Conditionals/Basic.lean` to `Core/SelectionFunction.lean` since they depend only on the selection-function structure, not on conditional semantics. `Phenomena/Conditionals/Studies/Stalnaker1975.lean`'s `s_subj3` instance updated (`select`ŌåÆ`sel`, `success`ŌåÆ`inclusion`); `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Counterfactual.lean`'s `stalnakerCounterfactual` and `stalnaker_eq_selectional_singleton` drop the awkward `_root_.Core.SelectionFunction` qualifications now that there is a single type. (2) Fixed docstring hallucination in `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/SelectionalRestrictor.lean`: the restrictor rule was cited as "┬¦7 eq. (22)" but eq. 22 in @cite{cariani-santorio-2018} is the informal English gloss; the actual semantic clause is **eq. 20 in ┬¦5.3.1**. Module + `willConditional` docstrings corrected, with a clarifying note distinguishing the ┬¦5.3.1 rule from its ┬¦7 consequences. (3) Added `Core.SelectionFunction.sel_em` and `sel_neg_swap` ŌĆö the structural lemmas from which Will Excluded Middle and Negation Swap derive. `Selectional.will_excluded_middle`/`negation_swap` and `SelectionalRestrictor.compositional_CEM`/`narrow_negation_swap` now derive from these via `s.sel_em`/`s.sel_neg_swap`, making the structural origin (single-valuedness of selection) visible at the call site instead of opaque `Classical.em`. (4) Added missing ┬¦7 theorems: set-level Content Transparency (`will_inter_modalParam_eq` ŌĆö the set-equality `ŌĆ¢will_f AŌĆ¢ Ōł® f = ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢ Ōł® f` underwriting `cognitive_role`, previously only pointwise via `will_eq_A_on_modalParam`); `Valid1` definition + `valid2_implies_valid1` bridge (paper ┬¦6); `postsemantic_will_excluded_middle` (matrix); `wide_negation_swap` + `valid2_wide_negation_swap` and `postsemantic_CEM` (conditional, paper ┬¦7).

## [0.229.965] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Polymorphize `Core.Scale.HasDegree` over its codomain.** The class signature changes from `class HasDegree (E : Type) where degree : E ŌåÆ ŌäÜ` to `class HasDegree (E : Type) (╬▒ : outParam Type) where degree : E ŌåÆ ╬▒`. With `outParam ╬▒`, instance resolution still infers ╬▒ from the entity type, so use sites need no annotation; instance declarations gain the explicit codomain. Heights can now use ŌäØ (physical), durations ŌäĢ (ticks), prices ŌäÜ (USD), cardinalities ŌäÜ-via-cast ŌĆö all under one typeclass instead of forcing every degree to be ŌäÜ. `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean` ┬¦15 collapses: the five `*Meaning_from_degree` bridge theorems (which just unfolded `CardinalityDegree`'s `Nat ŌåÆ ŌäÜ` cast) are dropped ŌĆö equivalent bridges are available directly through `Nat.cast_lt` / `Nat.cast_le` / `Nat.cast_inj`; only the `CardinalityDegree : HasDegree Nat ŌäÜ` instance remains. ┬¦16 (`numeralExact`, `numeralAtLeast`, `numeralApprox`) becomes polymorphic over ╬▒ with appropriate typeclass constraints (`[BEq ╬▒]`, `[LE ╬▒] [DecidableLE ╬▒]`, `[Sub ╬▒] [Add ╬▒]`); the comparison predicates use `decide` explicitly instead of relying on Bool/Prop coercion. ┬¦17 (`MeasurePredicate`/`DegreePhrase`/`measureSentence'`) and ┬¦18 (`numeralWithPrecision`) remain ŌäÜ-typed (the `MeasurePredicate.╬╝ : E ŌåÆ ŌäÜ` field intrinsically commits to ŌäÜ; rework deferred to the ┬¦17 cleanup task) and now require `[HasDegree E ŌäÜ]` explicitly. Two transitive call sites updated: `MeasureFn.toHasDegree` (`Theories/Semantics/Probabilistic/Measurement/Basic.lean`) and `measure_to_degree` (`Theories/Semantics/Comparison/Hierarchy.lean`) annotate their return types as `HasDegree E ŌäÜ`. `Phenomena/Nonliteral/Hyperbole/KaoEtAl2014.lean`'s `instance : HasDegree PriceState ŌäÜ` gains the explicit codomain (the body `degree := PriceState.value` already returns ŌäÜ).

## [0.229.964] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Unify lowercase `Case` constructor abbrevs under the canonical `UD.Case` namespace.** `Core/Case/Basic.lean` previously redeclared the full set of `match_pattern` aliases (`nom`, `acc`, `gen`, ŌĆ”, `abess` ŌĆö 19 entries) in its own `namespace Case` block, exact duplicates of the set already defined in `Core/Lexical/UD.lean`'s `namespace UD.Case`. Two parallel namespaces accreted because `Core.Case := UD.Case` is just an `abbrev` (the namespaces are disjoint from Lean's name-resolution perspective even though the underlying type is one). Deleted the `Core.Case.*` block; dot-notation `(.nom : Core.Case)` continues to resolve through the underlying `UD.Case.nom` aliases (Lean's elaborator unfolds the abbrev for dot-notation lookup). Migrated the 32 existing qualified `Core.Case.X` references across 9 consumer files (`Bubnov2026`, `Dargwa/Case`, `Turkish/Definiteness`, `CoonMateoPedroPreminger2014`, `Woolford1997`, `Aissen2003`, `Alignment/Typology`, `Baker2015`, `Pesetsky2013`) to bare `.X` dot-notation via `perl -pi -e 's/\bCore\.Case\.(...)\b/.\1/g'`. Two internal Core/Case files (`Order.lean`, `Grammaticalization.lean`) needed `(.acc : Case)` / `(.all : Case)` annotations where the bare name lookup inside `namespace Case` would otherwise resolve to the now-deleted block. Also removed the 3 redundant non-`match_pattern` `Case.nom`/`Case.acc`/`Case.gen` abbrevs from `Core/Lexical/Word.lean` (they shadowed UD.lean's `match_pattern` versions with strictly weaker abbrevs). Builds clean (1027 jobs through Pesetsky2013).

## [0.229.963] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Rename `Core/Inquisitive/` ŌåÆ `Core/Issue/` and `InquisitiveContent W` ŌåÆ `Core.Issue W`; split Bool/List InfoState primitives into standalone `Core/InfoState.lean`.** "Issue" is the umbrella name for question-content types (more neutral than "InquisitiveContent"; less collision-prone than "Resolution", which clashes with anaphora/ellipsis/agreement resolution across linglib). The Set-based downward-closed-family inquisitive content type now lives at `Core.Issue` with subfiles `Core/Issue/{Basic,Hamblin,Answerhood}.lean` (`SetLike`, `CompleteDistribLattice`, Heyting derivatives, `polar`/`which`/mention-some/all). The Bool/List finite-alternative `Discourse.Issue` (Roberts-style Hamblin alternative lists used by relevance/Strategy/Partition machinery and by Focus/dialogue studies) remains in `Core/Inquisitive.lean` for now ŌĆö the two representations serve complementary purposes (formal lattice reasoning vs. finite computation over world enumerations). The `InfoState`/`absurdState`/`trivialState`/`InfoState.{inter,union,subset,isEmpty,isNonEmpty}`/`supports`/`propEntails`/`properlyContains` Bool primitives split out into `Core/InfoState.lean` (the dynamic-semantics propositional algebra was always Bool-based and is out of scope for the Set migration); downstream dynamic-semantics consumers pick them up transitively. `Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean` and `Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018.lean` migrated to the renamed `Core.Issue` API. Full migration of the 12 remaining `Discourse.Issue` consumers (Strategy, Partition, QUD/Relevance, 5 Focus studies, KOS/Rules, Particles/Additive, ProbabilisticAnswerhood, Questions/Denotation/Inquisitive) and the unification of `DecisionTheoretic.Issue` and `Dynamic.State.Issue` with `Core.Issue` is deferred ŌĆö it requires reformulating Roberts 2012 relevance machinery (`questionEntails`, `partiallyAnswers`, `widerThan`) over Set and re-proving granularity-width theorems, ~4500 lines of consumer code with intricate proof rewrites.

## [0.229.962] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Excise the `OrderingRel` enum + `maxMeaning` dispatcher in `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean` ŌĆö replace with five named meaning functions (mathlib-discipline cleanup).** The old design encoded conclusions as definitions: a 5-constructor enum `OrderingRel := .eq | .gt | .lt | .ge | .le` paired with a `maxMeaning : OrderingRel ŌåÆ Nat ŌåÆ Nat ŌåÆ Bool` dispatcher just to pick which mathlib relation (`==`, `>`, `<`, `Ōēź`, `Ōēż`) to apply. The dispatcher added an indirection that defeated `simp`/`decide` and required custom unfolders downstream. Replaced with five direct `@[simp] def`s ŌĆö `bareMeaning`, `moreThanMeaning`, `fewerThanMeaning`, `atLeastMeaning`, `atMostMeaning` ŌĆö each a thin Bool wrapper over the corresponding mathlib `Nat` relation. `NumeralExpr` and `NumeralAlternative` go from a single dispatched `modified : OrderingRel ŌåÆ Nat ŌåÆ ...` constructor to four named constructors each (`.atLeast`, `.moreThan`, `.atMost`, `.fewerThan`). `NumeralTheory` swaps `bareRel : OrderingRel` for `bareSemantics : Nat ŌåÆ Nat ŌåÆ Bool` (a function field, since only two distinct values exist ŌĆö `bareMeaning` for Kennedy/Exact, `atLeastMeaning` for Horn/LowerBound ŌĆö passed directly without the enum dispatch); the old derived `derivationalDirection` accessor becomes an explicit field. The five Core.Scale bridge theorems were renamed: `maxMeaning_ge_iff_atLeastDeg ŌåÆ atLeastMeaning_iff_atLeastDeg` (and the parallel four). Eight downstream files updated to use the named meanings: `Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Embedding.lean` (`exactlyMeaning` body + ~25 `native_decide`ŌåÆ`decide` conversions), `Theories/Semantics/Probabilistic/Measurement/Basic.lean` (docstring), `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/ScontrasPearl2021.lean` (4 `_matches_maxMeaning` theorems renamed `_matches_bareMeaning`/`_matches_atLeastMeaning` + 4 `maxMeaning_agrees_gqt_*` bridge theorems renamed accordingly + `typeshift_resolves_tension` + 2 `native_decide`ŌåÆ`decide`), `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/Spector2013.lean` (5 sites: `lbAlts`/`lbMeaning` bodies, `exh_cannot_derive_atMost`, `indirect_si_overgeneration`, `atMost_wrong_for_threshold` (which now closes by `decide` instead of `simp [maxMeaning]`), `gen41c_atMost`, plus all `native_decide`ŌåÆ`decide`), `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/Kennedy2015.lean` (2 grounding theorems renamed `_eq_maxMeaning` ŌåÆ `_eq_namedMeaning`), `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/FoxHackl2006.lean` (2 bridge theorems use the new names directly), `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/BylininaNouwen2020.lean` (docstring), `Phenomena/Persuasion/Studies/CumminsFranke2021.lean` (3 `simp` calls converted to `simp only` lemma sets without `maxMeaning`), and `Core/Scales/Scale.lean` docstring. Also: header docstring + ┬¦7b/┬¦7c docstrings in `Basic.lean` rewritten to reference the named meanings instead of `maxMeaning.X` notation. `Basic.lean` no longer references `OrderingRel`, `maxMeaning`, `modifierClassOf`, or `boundDirectionOf`. The retained `ModifierClass` and `BoundDirection` enums are still used by Fragment lexical entries and don't carry the dispatcher antipattern.

## [0.229.961] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`RespectsCahaContainment` reformulated as a lower-set predicate over the canonical `PartialOrder Case`.** Replaces the rank-arithmetic body (`(hasRank 1 ŌåÆ hasRank 0) Ōł¦ ŌĆ”`) with the standard order-theoretic `ŌłĆ c Ōłł inv, ŌłĆ d, d Ōēż c ŌåÆ d Ōłł inv`, going through the Caha order extracted in `Core/Case/Order.lean` rather than bypassing it via raw `containmentRank` `Option`-arithmetic. Off-hierarchy cases impose no constraint automatically (in the Caha order, off-hierarchy `c` only has `c Ōēż c`, so the lower-set condition is vacuous). All 22 conformer + 2 violator theorems in `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Caha2009.lean` still discharge by `decide` with the new definition. The 4-rank hardcode is gone ŌĆö the definition would lift unchanged if the Caha hierarchy were extended.

## [0.229.960] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **@cite{cariani-santorio-2018} *Will done Better* ŌĆö selectional `will`-conditionals + cognitive-role probability theorem.** New `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/SelectionalRestrictor.lean` (lifts the selectional analysis of `will` to conditionals via Kratzer restriction): `willConditional s A B f w := willSem s B (f Ōł® ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢) w`, `compositional_CEM` (paper ┬¦7) discharged via `Classical.em`, `narrow_negation_swap` as `Iff.rfl`, `valid2_*` lifts, `willConditional_collapse` (Centering on the restricted parameter), `willConditional_redundant` (when `f ŌŖå ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢`, restriction is a no-op). `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Selectional.lean` extended with `Valid2`, `willHistorical` (selectional `will` against the metaphysical base), `universalWill` counterfoil + `universal_negation_swap_fails`, and `cognitive_role : ╬╝.probOf (A Ōłś s.sel ┬Ę f) = ╬╝.probOf A` when `╬╝` is supported on `f` (Centering inside the sum, supported worlds collapse the selection function pointwise). The study file `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/CarianiSantorio2018.lean` now formalizes Predictions 6 & 7 (paper ┬¦8.1): `cynthia_credence_half : cynthiaPMF.probOf (warriorsWinB Ōłś cynthiaSel.sel ┬Ę histAlt) = 1/2` (selectional reading) vs `universal_will_credence_zero : cynthiaPMF.probOf universalWillWarriorsB = 0` (universal-quantifier collapse ŌĆö `histAlt` contains the loss world, so the universal is always false), demonstrating that the selectional analysis matches the empirically attested non-extreme credence while the universal-base reading collapses to 0. `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Counterfactual.lean` gains `stalnakerCounterfactual` (the @cite{stalnaker-1968} single-selection-function variant using `Core.SelectionFunction` ŌĆö the same infrastructure C&S 2018 reuse for *will*) + `stalnaker_eq_selectional_singleton` bridging it to the existing supervaluation analysis when the closest-worlds set is the singleton chosen by the selection function. The two `SelectionFunction` types (the one in `Core` with field `sel` vs the legacy one in `Conditionals/Basic` with field `select`) coexist for now; the Stalnaker variant uses `_root_.Core.SelectionFunction` to disambiguate. Bib entry `cariani-santorio-2018` title-case fixed (`Will Done Better` ŌåÆ `Will done Better` matching the paper). Builds clean for the affected files (`Selectional`, `SelectionalRestrictor`, `Counterfactual`, `CarianiSantorio2018`).

## [0.229.959] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Migrate Case-area Bool predicates to `Prop` + `Decidable` (mathlib idiom).** Five predicates in `Theories/Interfaces/Morphosyntax/CaseContainment.lean` flip from Bool to Prop with capitalized names: `hierarchyAdjacent ŌåÆ HierarchyAdjacent`, `inventoryAdjacent ŌåÆ InventoryAdjacent`, `respectsCahaContainment ŌåÆ RespectsCahaContainment`, `AllomorphyPattern.violatesABA ŌåÆ ViolatesABA`, `AllomorphyPattern.isContiguous ŌåÆ IsContiguous`. Each carries a `Decidable`/`DecidableRel` instance built either inline (`inferInstanceAs (Decidable (_ Ōł© _))`) or via `unfold; infer_instance` for compound bodies. The bridge `case_violatesABA_iff_generic` (formerly `_eq_generic`) now states `p.ViolatesABA Ōåö Morphology.Containment.violatesABA [...] = true`, with the proof composing `violatesABA_four` + `simp only` + `tauto` to discharge Ōł©-associativity residue. All in-file theorems migrated (sections 2, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9), plus 7 external consumers: `Fragments/Telugu/Case.lean` (5 `IsNonnominative` + `nom_vs_oblique_contiguous`), `Fragments/Latin/Case.lean` (3 `HierarchyAdjacent`/`InventoryAdjacent` theorems), `Fragments/Tamil/Case.lean` (`com_inst_adjacent`), `Fragments/Finnish/Case.lean` (`abl_inst_distinct`), `Phenomena/Allomorphy/Studies/Aitha2026.lean` (5 theorems including `central_argument` triple conjunction + `hasACC_eq_isNonnom` bridge using `decide (...)` to land back in `Bool`), `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Caha2009.lean` (22 conformer theorems + 2 negative theorems; the old `cahaSurvey : List (String ├Ś Bool)` table + its 3 coverage theorems were deleted ŌĆö the table wasn't actually enforcing coverage since adding a new fragment didn't break it, and the per-language theorems already give precise blame). Builds clean (2750 Case-related jobs).

## [0.229.958] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Numerals theory: rename `Semantics.lean` ŌåÆ `Basic.lean` and lift the directory out of `Quantification/` (mathlib idiom).** The unified numeral theory module moved from `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Quantification/Numerals/Semantics.lean` to `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Numerals/Basic.lean`, sitting alongside its Theory-siblings (`Gradability/`, `Plurality/`, `Modality/`, `Quantification/`, ŌĆ”) directly under `Theories/Semantics/`. The double nesting `Semantics/Quantification/Numerals/Semantics.lean` was redundant ŌĆö `Basic.lean` is mathlib's standard name for a foundational module file, and the file is no more "quantificational" than its siblings. Namespace renamed `Semantics.Quantification.Numerals` ŌåÆ `Semantics.Numerals`. All 21 importer files updated (Linglib.lean + the Numerals.{Embedding,Polysemy,Precision} sibling files + 6 study files in `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/` + `Phenomena/Quantification/Studies/ScontrasPearl2021` + `Pragmatics/Implicature/{NegationScope,ScalarImplicatures/Operations}` + 4 study files in `Imprecision/Persuasion/Hyperbole` + `Fragments/English/NumeralModifiers` + `blog/lean/KennedyRSA/KennedyExact`). Also: eliminated all ~50 `native_decide` calls in `Basic.lean` in favor of structural `decide` (kernel-checkable), fixed the duplicate ┬¦14 numbering bug (renumbered ┬¦14bŌåÆ┬¦15, duplicate ┬¦14ŌåÆ┬¦16, ┬¦15ŌåÆ┬¦17, ┬¦16ŌåÆ┬¦18 with a refreshed module-docstring section listing), and converted the remaining bare `simp [...]` calls in ┬¦9/┬¦10/┬¦11/┬¦14 to explicit `simp only [...]` lemma sets (`maxMeaning`, `decide_eq_true_eq`, `beq_iff_eq`, `id_eq`, plus the relevant `Quantifier.at_least_n_sem`/`atLeastDeg`/etc. unfolds). Concrete `Nat`-level cases (`classA_classB_diverge_on_bare_world`, `classB_strictly_weaker_than_bare`) closed by `decide`. Builds clean (5169 jobs).

## [0.229.957] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Extract canonical Caha containment order to `Core/Case/Order.lean` (mathlib `Mathlib/Data/<Type>/Order.lean` style).** The `Preorder Case` / `PartialOrder Case` instance and the underlying `containmentRank : Case ŌåÆ Option Nat` lived in `Theories/Interfaces/Morphosyntax/CaseContainment.lean` ŌĆö but a partial-order on `Case` is foundational, theory-neutral substrate, not interface-specific morphosyntax. The new `Core/Case/Order.lean` carries: `containmentRank`, `cahaLT`/`cahaLE` with structural `DecidableRel` instances (split through a clean `match`-based body so `Decidable` reduces without `Eq.rec` blocking), the `LE Case` / `Preorder Case` / `PartialOrder Case` instances, and `IsNonnominative (c : Case) : Prop := Case.acc Ōēż c` (with `Decidable`) ŌĆö replacing the old Bool `isNonnom`. The sanity chain `nom_le_acc < acc_le_gen < gen_le_dat < dat_le_loc` uses direct `Or.inr` term proofs (rank `n < m`); ERG/NOM incomparability theorems (`erg_incomparable_nom`, `nom_incomparable_erg`) destructure the disjunction and discharge each branch via `UD.Case.noConfusion` / `False`-elim. `CaseContainment.lean` is deduplicated: deletes ┬¦1-┬¦3 (containmentRank + containedIn + isNonnom + their per-pair `rfl` theorems) and ┬¦13 (the duplicated PartialOrder), opens `Core.Case (containmentRank)` for the surviving inventory-contiguity predicate, sections renumbered 1ŌĆō10, and now occupies only the morphology-interface niche it was named for (allomorphy patterns, syncretism adjacency, `respectsCahaContainment`). Three external consumers updated: `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Bubnov2026.lean` (`containedIn .nom .acc = true` ŌåÆ `(.nom : Case) Ōēż .acc`), `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean` (`Interfaces.ŌĆ”containmentRank` ŌåÆ `Core.Case.containmentRank`), `Theories/Morphology/Nanosyntax/Core.lean` (`caseToRank` body), `Fragments/Telugu/Case.lean` (`isNonnom .acc = true` ŌåÆ `Core.Case.IsNonnominative .acc`), `Phenomena/Allomorphy/Studies/Aitha2026.lean` (`hasACC = isNonnom c.toCore` ŌåÆ `decide (Core.Case.IsNonnominative c.toCore)`). Builds clean (5169 jobs).

### Changed
- **Split `Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean` (1026-line grab bag) into a mathlib-style `Core/Inquisitive/` directory with one concept per file.** New layout: `Core/Inquisitive/Basic.lean` carries the structural core (`InquisitiveContent W` bundle + `SetLike` + `info`/`alt`/`isInformative`/`isInquisitive`/`isDeclarative` + `declarative` + lattice ops `conj`/`inqDisj`/`top`/`bot`/`sSupContent`/`sInfContent` + `CompleteLattice` and `CompleteDistribLattice` instances + Resolutions Theorem + principal-ideal characterization of declaratives + `HeytingAlgebra` derivatives `compl`/`proj`/`nonInfo` + `not_lem_inquisitive_content`); `Core/Inquisitive/Hamblin.lean` carries the Hamblin constructions (`polar` + `which` + their `info_*` and `isInquisitive_*` theorems); `Core/Inquisitive/Answerhood.lean` carries the answerhood predicates (`isMentionSomeAnswer`, `isMentionAllAnswer`, plus the two `@[simp]` characterization lemmas for `ŌŖź` and `declarative`). The placement under `Core/Inquisitive/` (not `Theories/Semantics/`) matches mathlib's `Filter` precedent: `InquisitiveContent` is general lattice infrastructure usable by any inquisitive-flavored module, not a domain-specific semantic theory. `Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean`'s `fromSetoid` now extends `Core.Inquisitive.InquisitiveContent` (with the POSWQ bridge living in `Core.Mood.POSWQ` via `open Core.Inquisitive (InquisitiveContent)`). The two consumer modules ŌĆö `PartitionAsInquiry` and `Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018` ŌĆö migrated to import the new files and `open Core.Inquisitive InquisitiveContent`. `Linglib.lean` registers the three new modules; the old `Linglib/Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean` is deleted. The speculative `widerThan` / `properlyContains` predicates (no consumer in the current tree ŌĆö DeoThomas2025 still uses the legacy `Discourse.Issue` Bool/List shadow) were dropped from the split and will be re-added when the Issue ŌåÆ InquisitiveContent migration reaches that study.

## [0.229.955] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Dissolve `Phenomena/Numerals/Compare.lean` into per-paper `Studies/` files (pilot for the broader `Compare.lean` dissolution).** The 10 cross-theory bridges in `Compare.lean` are redistributed by *primary contribution*: NSAL-as-RSA-cost + k-ness-grounds-`PrecisionMode` + Lasersohn halo-width + the lexical-bridge tolerance theorem move into a new `Phenomena/Numerals/Studies/Cummins2015.lean` (since @cite{cummins-2015} is the source of the cost/precision/halo unification); the `weightedRoundnessScore` RSA-prior monotonicity chain (`100 > 50 > 7`) is appended to `Studies/WoodinEtAl2024.lean`; the polarity/framing valence theorems for "many/few" are appended to `Studies/ClausWalch2024.lean`; the @cite{bylinina-nouwen-2020} 0-3 RSA bridge becomes its own `Studies/BylininaNouwen2020.lean` (`lbNumeralCfg` + 3 `rsa_predict` strengthening theorems + `lbNuttMeaning_eq_lowerBound` grounding); the @cite{kennedy-2015} alternative-set RSA bridge becomes `Studies/Kennedy2015.lean` (`kennedyLowerCfg`/`kennedyUpperCfg` + 7 `rsa_predict` Class-A/Class-B theorems + grounding); the `enrichmentWidth` definition + monotonicity moves into `Phenomena/Persuasion/Studies/CumminsFranke2021.lean` where it is actually used. The `CardinalityDegree : HasDegree Nat` instance + 5 `maxMeaning_*_from_degree` rewrites graduate to `Theories/Semantics/Quantification/Numerals/Semantics.lean ┬¦14b` as Theory-level infrastructure (the abstraction belongs in Theory, not Phenomena). One bridge (Bridge 6, a redundant unitless `roundnessScore 100 > roundnessScore 50` chain already proved by existing `roundness_*` theorems) is dropped. All proofs are structural ŌĆö `decide` for `Nat`-level reductions (`nsalViolations 100 = 0`, `roundnessScore 100 = 6`, `RoundnessProperties` field equality), `norm_num`/`simp`/`linarith` for `ŌäÜ`-level arithmetic, `Nat.cast_lt`/`Nat.cast_le`/`Nat.cast_inj` for the degree bridges, no `native_decide` introduced. `Phenomena/Numerals/Compare.lean` deleted; `Linglib.lean` updated with 3 new study imports and the deletion. Establishes the dissolution policy for the remaining 11 `Compare.lean` files: each cross-theory bridge migrates to the `Studies/` file of its primary contribution paper, and shared infrastructure (typeclass instances, scale-level definitions) graduates upward to `Theories/` or `Core/` rather than living in a per-phenomenon `Compare.lean`.

## [0.229.954] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core/Case/`: complete the mathlib-style discipline pass ŌĆö `caseInventory` is `Finset Core.Case`, `validInventory` is `Case.IsValidInventory : Finset Case ŌåÆ Prop`, `caseExtension` is the `Case.Extends : Case ŌåÆ Case ŌåÆ Prop` relation with `Relation.TransGen` reachability.** Inventory migration: every `caseInventory` (and aliases ŌĆö `coreInventory`, `inventoryWithLocative`, `agreementCaseInventory`, `fullCaseInventory`, `npInventory`, `pesetskyCore`, ŌĆ”) across ~25 fragment and study files is now a `Finset Core.Case` literal `{.nom, .acc, .gen, ŌĆ”}`, and the contiguity check is `example : Core.Case.IsValidInventory caseInventory := by decide` (or `┬¼ ŌĆ” := by decide` where the inventory deliberately fails strict contiguity, e.g. Latin's 5-case core sans LOC, Finnish without DAT). Subset/membership queries (`pesetskyCore ŌŖå Russian.caseInventory`, `Core.Case.erg Ōłł fullCaseInventory`, `ŌłĆ p Ōłł kaqArgPositions, p.case Ōłł caseInventory`) all use Finset's decidable `Ōłł` / `ŌŖå`. `inventoryAdjacent` (the syncretism-relevance check that no rank falls strictly between two cases) flipped from `List Case` + `inv.any` to `Finset Case` + `decide (ŌłĆ c Ōłł inv, ŌĆ”)`. Anderson's `CaseRelation.canonicalTheta` rewritten from old positional pattern `Ō¤©_, true, trueŌ¤®` to feature-membership (`if .src Ōłł cr then if .loc Ōłł cr then ŌĆ”`), since `CaseRelation = Finset CaseFeature` now (not a positional triple). Two `erg_inst_share_src` / `nom_acc_share_abs` syncretism theorems in `CaseContainment.lean` rewritten from `Option.map CaseRelation.src = some true` (broken when accessors became `Prop`-returning) to `Option.any (.src Ōłł ┬Ę)`. Extension migration: `caseExtension : Case ŌåÆ List Case` (table-as-data) becomes `Case.Extends : Case ŌåÆ Case ŌåÆ Prop` (relation-as-data) with `DecidableRel`, plus a derived `Case.extensionTargets : Case ŌåÆ Finset Case` and `mem_extensionTargets` characterization for direct queries. The bespoke `CaseExtensionReachable2` (two-step) is replaced by `Case.ExtensionReachable := Relation.TransGen Case.Extends` from mathlib ŌĆö multi-step extension is now first-class transitive closure, not a hand-rolled depth-2 predicate. Two existing reachability witnesses (`acc_reachable_from_all` via DAT, `inst_reachable_from_loc` via COM) are rebuilt as `.tail (.single ŌĆ”) ŌĆ”` constructions. Latin/Finnish fragment theorems updated from `Core.caseExtension .abl` membership style to `Core.Case.Extends .abl .inst := by decide`. Removed the conflicting `Preorder.lift Case.hierarchyRank` instance from `Core/Case/Hierarchy.lean` ŌĆö Caha containment (in `CaseContainment.lean`) is the canonical `Preorder Case` per its docstring, and Blake rank stays available as a plain function `Case.hierarchyRank : Case ŌåÆ Fin 7`. All Case-related modules build clean (2787 jobs through downstream consumers).

## [0.229.953] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/Selectional.lean` and `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/CarianiSantorio2018.lean`: formalize @cite{cariani-santorio-2018}'s selectional semantics for *will*.** The new theory module defines `willSem s A f w := A (s.sel w f)` against a `Core.SelectionFunction W` and a modal parameter `f : Set W` (the historical alternatives), and proves the four properties that distinguish the selectional analysis from quantificational alternatives: `negation_swap` (`will ┬¼A Ōåö ┬¼will A`, scopelessness in matrix uses), `will_excluded_middle` (`will A Ōł© will ┬¼A` at every point, the Stalnaker-CEM analogue for futures), `unembedded_collapse` (when `w Ōłł f`, `will A Ōåö A w` ŌĆö Centering-driven, the source of apparent factivity), and `transparency` (`ŌĆ¢will AŌĆ¢ = ŌĆ¢AŌĆ¢` on the selected world, ┬¦8.1). The companion study file replays Cariani & Santorio's Sports Fan scenario in a 3-world model with a totally-defined `selFn` (preferring `w Ōłł A`, else first-available among `w0, w1, w2`); `selFn_inclusion` and `selFn_centering` discharge the Stalnaker axioms via `split_ifs`/`if_pos`. Five paper-derived theorems verify the analysis: `cynthia_will_win` (Cynthia at `w0` asserts *Warriors will win*, true by collapse), `will_em_at_w0`, `swap_at_w0`, `nonmember_no_collapse` (when the modal parameter excludes `w0`, the selection picks `w1` and assertion fails despite `warriorsWin w0 = True` ŌĆö modal subordination), and `member_collapses` (the eq. (17) collapse pattern). Both the underlying `selFn` and `cynthiaSel` are `noncomputable` because `Set` membership requires `Classical` decidability.
- **`Core/SelectionFunction.lean`: extract Stalnaker selection functions as a Core-level primitive.** New `SelectionFunction W` structure with `sel : W ŌåÆ Set W ŌåÆ W`, `inclusion` (`A.Nonempty ŌåÆ sel w A Ōłł A`), and `centering` (`w Ōłł A ŌåÆ sel w A = w`) ŌĆö the @cite{stalnaker-1968} axioms. Two convenience theorems: `sel_singleton` (`sel w {w} = w` via Centering on a `rfl` membership) and `sel_mem` (Inclusion). This is now the shared substrate for both `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Counterfactual.lean`'s selectional counterfactual and `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Selectional.lean`'s *will*-semantics; the two semantic operators were stipulating their own selection-style functions before.
- **`blog/data/references.bib`: add `cariani-santorio-2018`** (Mind 127(505):129-165, DOI 10.1093/mind/fzw004).

### Changed
- **Move `Theories/Semantics/Tense/BranchingTime.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Modality/HistoricalAlternatives.lean`.** The "branching time" infrastructure (world-history relations, `historicalBase`/`actualHistoryBase`/`futureHistoryBase`, `histEquiv` setoid, `metaphysicalBase`, settledness/diversity) is framework-agnostic substrate for any modal theory that quantifies over historical alternatives ŌĆö not specifically a tense-system construct. Repositioning under `Core/Modality/` makes it directly available to selectional semantics for *will* (@cite{cariani-santorio-2018}) without going through a tense module. Namespace renamed `Semantics.Tense.BranchingTime` ŌåÆ `Core.Modality.HistoricalAlternatives`. Updated 9 importers (Mood, IntensionalCDRT/{Situations,ModalDonkeyAnaphora}, Tense/ConditionalShift, Conditionals/{Anderson,Iatridou}, Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Condoravdi2002, Phenomena/TemporalConnectives/Studies/BeaverCondoravdi2003, Phenomena/TenseAspect/Studies/Mendes2025) plus two `sources` lines in `references.bib`. The bridge theorems to `Theories/Semantics/Modality/TemporalConstraint` are preserved at the cost of a CoreŌåÆTheories import edge ŌĆö flagged for a follow-up that promotes `TemporalConstraint`'s time-predicate fragment to Core or splits the bridge into a separate module.

## [0.229.952] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean`: port `Issue` operations from the Bool/List shadow to the Set-valued mathlib-style API.** First migration step toward dissolving `Core/Inquisitive.lean`. Added the load-bearing `mem_iSup_iff` (membership in an indexed `iSup`: `q Ōłł Ō©å i, f i Ōåö q = Ōłģ Ōł© Ōłā i, q Ōłł f i`) by unfolding through `sSup (Set.range f)` and `mem_sSup_props`; the `q = Ōłģ` disjunct is structural since `Ōłģ` lies in every content's `props` via `contains_empty`. Bounded version `mem_biSup_iff` derives by composing the two iSups. **Wh-question content**: `which {E} (D : Set E) (P : E ŌåÆ Set W) : InquisitiveContent W := Ō©å e Ōłł D, declarative (P e)` is the Hamblin construction with one alternative per element of the domain `D`; the membership characterization `mem_which : q Ōłł which D P Ōåö q = Ōłģ Ōł© Ōłā e Ōłł D, q ŌŖå P e` and the informative-content identity `info_which : info (which D P) = Ōŗā e Ōłł D, P e` (@[simp]) match the Bool/List `Discourse.Issue.which` API but operate on arbitrary `Set`-valued predicates without `worlds : List W` or `domain : List E` enumeration parameters. **Answerhood**: `isMentionSomeAnswer` (settles at least one alternative without settling all) and `isMentionAllAnswer` (settles every alternative) replace the `Discourse.Issue.isMentionSomeAnswer`/`isMentionAllAnswer` Bool predicates; the boundary lemmas `isMentionAllAnswer_bot_iff : Ōåö Žā = Ōłģ` (only `Ōłģ` mention-all-answers `ŌŖź` since `alt ŌŖź = {Ōłģ}`) and `isMentionAllAnswer_declarative_iff : Ōåö Žā ŌŖå p` (a mention-all answer to `declarative p` is exactly a substate of `p`) are both `@[simp]`. **Granularity** (@cite{deo-thomas-2025}): `properlyContains` and `widerThan` capture the asymmetric "more granular than" relation between two questions on the same informative content, used in granularity-based question construals. The universe declaration was widened from `universe u` to `universe u v` to admit the second universe parameter `E : Type v` for `which`. Build clean (961 jobs through `Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018`).

## [0.229.951] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean`: complete the Heyting-derivative API and the standard `proj`/`nonInfo` decomposition.** The `CompleteDistribLattice` instance gives `PßČ£` and `Ōć©` for free, but the universal-property proofs are opaque. The single structural identity `compl_eq : PßČ£ = declarative (info P)ßČ£` (proved using `inf_compl_self` to derive any singleton in `P ŌŖō PßČ£` is `Ōłģ`, and `le_himp_iff`/`himp_bot` for the reverse direction) makes pseudo-complementation transparent: it is the principal ideal of the Boolean-complemented informative content. From this single fact we derive `info_compl : info PßČ£ = (info P)ßČ£` (`info` commutes with complement, even though the lattice is only Heyting), `proj_eq_compl_compl : proj P = PßČ£ßČ£` (the standard inquisitive identification of `!P` with the Heyting double-negation), `nonInfo P := P ŌŖö PßČ£` (the non-informative projection `?P`), and the **division law** `proj_inf_nonInfo : proj P ŌŖō nonInfo P = P` (every inquisitive content factors uniquely into its informative and inquisitive components). Also added `isDeclarative_iff_alt_eq_singleton` (the alternative-set characterization of declarativeness, complementing `isDeclarative_iff_eq_declarative_info`), `isDeclarative_iff_not_isInquisitive`, `mem_declarative` (@[simp]), `info_declarative` upgraded to @[simp], `alt_top` and `alt_bot` (@[simp]), and the witness `not_lem_inquisitive_content` showing that LEM `P ŌŖö PßČ£ = ŌŖż` fails ŌĆö the lattice is genuinely Heyting and not Boolean. Build clean (961 jobs through downstream consumers).

### Changed
- **`Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean`: drop dead-code wrapper definitions, fix all citation hygiene, trim imports.** Removed `trueAt`, `not`/`not_eq_compl`, and `imp`/`imp_eq_himp` ŌĆö these were thin renames over `info`-membership / `PßČ£` / `Ōć©` with zero external callers, and the `SetLike` membership API plus the Heyting structure already give the same operations under their canonical names. Dropped the unused `Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Powerset` import. Per CLAUDE.md citation policy, removed all unverifiable equation/figure/page references from docstrings: `theiler-etal-2018 Definition 1`, `Definition 2`, `Figure 2(b)`, `Figure 2(a)`, `footnote 7`, and `puncochar-2019 p. 298` are all replaced with content-descriptive prose. Fixed the stale "DistribLattice separately" comment (we register `CompleteDistribLattice` directly, no separate `DistribLattice` instance). Fixed the docstring comparison `LowerSet (Set W)ßĄÆßĄł` ŌåÆ `LowerSet (Set W)`.

## [0.229.950] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/Constraints/NumericalExpressions.lean`: mathlib-grade overhaul of an early file.** Net -107 lines after relocating bridges to where they are consumed. (1) **Dead surface deleted** ŌĆö the decorative `OTConstraint` struct, the four named instances `INFO`/`GRANULARITY`/`QSIMP`/`NSAL`, the unused `defaultRanking` (note: `harmonicallyBounds` indexed by fixed field order, not by `defaultRanking` ŌĆö confirming the metadata layer was inert), the bespoke `lexLessThan`/`harmonicallyBounds` lex implementation, the never-called `optimalCandidate`, the dangling `NumeralCandidate.actualValue` field, and `zero_bounds_any_violated` (only consumer of `harmonicallyBounds`). None of these had downstream callers (verified by grep). (2) **`ViolationProfile` regrounded as `Core.Constraint.Profile Nat 4`** (= `Fin 4 ŌåÆ Nat`), replacing the bespoke 4-named-field struct. The pattern-matched constructor `violationProfile c admittedCount : Fin 4 ŌåÆ Nat` is more direct than allocating a struct literal, and the whole `Core/Constraint/Pareto.lean` apparatus (Pareto preorder, qualitative coarsening, `coarsen_via_monotone`) applies without an extra projection layer. (3) **Pareto + qualitative `FeaturePreorder` instances added** as `paretoPreorder` (identity feature, pointwise `Ōēż`) and `qualPreorder` (feature = `Core.Constraint.ConstraintSystem.violatedSetOf`, subset on `Finset (Fin 4)`); `paretoPreorder_le_implies_qualPreorder_le` is one line via `coarsen_via_monotone` ŌĆö exact same shape as the worked example in `Core/Constraint/Pareto.lean`, demonstrating the abstraction extends to a second concrete instance. The pre-existing `cumminsOrdering : SatisfactionOrdering` is preserved as the legacy criterion-based view. (4) **`enrichmentWidth` and `nsalAsRSACost` moved to `Phenomena/Numerals/Compare.lean`** (their only consumer) ŌĆö framework-bridging defs (RSA-cost normalization, pragmatic enrichment-width prediction), not OT constraint definitions, and they belong under `Phenomena/` per dependency-discipline. The redundant `Mathlib.Data.Rat.Defs` import drops out of `NumericalExpressions.lean`. (5) **`native_decide` eliminated** from this file ŌĆö the surviving theorems (`nsal_is_complement`, `round_beats_nonround_nsal`, `zero_violations_best`) use structural proofs (`omega`, `cases p <;> rfl`); concrete-data `#guard`s remain as compile-time sanity checks. Both touched files build clean (2708 jobs).

## [0.229.949] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Order/FeaturePreorder.lean`: extract the feature-pullback abstraction subsuming `SatisfactionOrdering` and the Pareto bridge.** Mathlib-style refactor recognizing that the satisfaction-ordering and constraint-system preorder constructions are both instances of one pattern: a `Preorder Carrier` realized as the pullback of a `Preorder Feature` through `feature : Carrier ŌåÆ Feature`. The new structure `FeaturePreorder Carrier Feature` `extends Preorder Carrier`, bundles `feature : Carrier ŌåÆ Feature` plus `decLE` (per-pair decidability, since multiple `FeaturePreorder`s share a carrier and `Preorder` cannot be a typeclass instance), and carries the coherence field `le_iff_feature : ŌłĆ a a', a Ōēż a' Ōåö feature a Ōēż feature a'`. Smart constructor `FeaturePreorder.ofFeature` builds the canonical instance using mathlib's `Preorder.lift` under the hood, with `le_iff_feature := Iff.rfl`. The load-bearing reusable theorem `coarsen_via_monotone` states: given two `FeaturePreorder`s on the same carrier with feature maps `f1 : Carrier ŌåÆ F1` and `f2 : Carrier ŌåÆ F2`, plus a monotone `Žå : F1 ŌåÆ F2` such that `f2 = Žå Ōłś f1`, dominance under `f1` implies dominance under `f2`. This is the schema behind every "qualitative coarsening" bridge in the library ŌĆö pointwise-Pareto-on-violations ŌćÆ subset-of-satisfied, satisfied-set inclusion ŌćÆ ordering-source dominance, etc. Wired into `Linglib.lean` after `Core/Order/Satisfaction`. Build clean (333 jobs).

### Changed
- **`Core/Constraint/Pareto.lean`: rebuild the Pareto bridge as a pair of `FeaturePreorder` instances + a one-line application of `coarsen_via_monotone`.** Demonstrates the new abstraction concretely. `paretoFeaturePreorder` is now `FeaturePreorder Cand (Profile ╬▓ n)` with `feature := s.score` and pointwise `Pi.preorder` on the codomain. `qualitativeFeaturePreorder` is `FeaturePreorder Cand (Finset (Fin n))` with `feature := violatedSet s` (constraint indices where score ŌēĀ 0) and standard subset order. The implication theorem `paretoFeaturePreorder_le_implies_qualitativeFeaturePreorder_le` is reduced from a hand-written proof to `FeaturePreorder.coarsen_via_monotone` applied to `violatedSetOf : Profile ╬▓ n ŌåÆ Finset (Fin n)` (with `violatedSetOf_monotone` proven separately under the zero-as-min hypothesis). Switching from satisfied-set / reverse-subset to violated-set / standard-subset eliminates the `OrderDual` boilerplate. The Pareto bridge file now serves as the worked example showing how to refactor any future "two-views-of-the-same-carrier" preorder bridge through `FeaturePreorder`. Build clean (2660 jobs).

## [0.229.948] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Constraint/Pareto.lean`: bridge between `ConstraintSystem` and `SatisfactionOrdering`.** New file relating the quantitative-scoring + decoder API (`Core/Constraint/`) to the qualitative satisfied-by-criteria preorder API (`Core/Order/Satisfaction.lean`). Two views on a `ConstraintSystem Cand (Profile ╬▓ n)`: (1) `paretoPreorder` ŌĆö pointwise `Preorder Cand` where `c Ōēż c'` iff `ŌłĆ i, score c i Ōēż score c' i` (genuine Pareto dominance on the violation vector under the OT/HG "lower is better" convention); (2) `toSatisfactionOrdering` ŌĆö `SatisfactionOrdering Cand (Fin n)` coarsening that defines `c` "satisfies" constraint `i` iff `score c i = 0`, packaging the system into the shared `best`/`undominated`/`refine` machinery alongside Kratzer modal ordering sources and Phillips-Brown desire. The implication `paretoPreorder_le_implies_toSatisfactionOrdering_le` proves pointwise dominance ŌćÆ qualitative dominance whenever zero is the minimum (e.g. `╬▓ = Nat`, raw violations); the converse fails by design ŌĆö the qualitative coarsening forgets the magnitude trade-off that lex-min and weighted-sum decoders exploit. Module docstring documents the gap with worked OT/HG examples (a quantitative winner can be qualitatively dominated when it loses many violations on a low-weight constraint to gain a single satisfaction on another). The bridge lets future code talk about either the strict "Pareto frontier of violations" view or the looser "satisfied-set inclusion" view of the same system without re-stipulating either. Build clean (2661 jobs).

## [0.229.947] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage 6 InquisitiveContent mathlib polish: `SetLike` + `CompleteDistribLattice` + non-inquisitive projection.** Architectural cleanup pass over `Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean` addressing what a mathlib PR reviewer would flag. (1) `SetLike (InquisitiveContent W) (Set W)` instance auto-derives `Membership`, `CoeSort`, and the `ext` machinery; mirrors mathlib's `Submonoid`/`Subgroup`/`LowerSet` pattern. `mem_props : q Ōłł P.props Ōåö q Ōłł P` is the canonical simp normalization (rewriting projections to coerced membership, following mathlib's `mem_carrier` convention in `SetLike.Basic`). `ext` restated in membership-quantification form (`ŌłĆ q, q Ōłł P Ōåö q Ōłł Q`) and tagged `@[ext]`. (2) `CompleteDistribLattice (InquisitiveContent W)` registered via `CompleteDistribLattice.ofMinimalAxioms` from two pointwise inequalities (`inf_sSup_le_iSup_inf_aux`, `iInf_sup_le_sup_sInf_aux`) ŌĆö replaces the standalone `DistribLattice` instance and gives `Frame`, `Coframe`, `HeytingAlgebra`, `BiheytingAlgebra` plus `inf_sSup_eq`/`sup_sInf_eq`/`inf_iSup_eq`/`sup_iInf_eq` for free. The two inequalities follow from set distributivity and a binary case split on `q = Ōłģ Ōł© Ōłā R Ōłł S`. (3) Finiteness hypothesis on `exists_alt_above`/`info_eq_sUnion_alt`/`eq_iSup_declarative_alt` weakened from `[Finite W]` (typeclass on the world type) to `(hP : P.props.Finite)` (data on the specific content) ŌĆö the latter is the actually-needed condition (the maximality argument is about `P.props`, not `Set W`), and lets the Resolutions Theorem apply on infinite world spaces with finitely many alternatives. (4) `mem_alt_iff_maximal` promoted to `@[simp]` (the canonical normal form, connecting `alt P` to mathlib's order-theoretic `Maximal` API); `mem_alt` retained as the unfolded form but no longer `@[simp]`. (5) `polar_eq_sup` no longer `@[simp]` (kept `polar p` as a meaningful surface primitive); `info_polar` now `@[simp]` directly so polar-question informative content reduces in one step. (6) Non-inquisitive projection added: `proj P := declarative (info P)` (the `!P` operator from inquisitive-semantics literature) plus `info_proj`/`isDeclarative_proj`/`proj_proj`/`proj_eq_self_iff` (idempotency + characterization of declarative-fixed-points). Linguistic-flavored aliases `not P := PßČ£` and `imp P Q := P Ōć© Q` expose the Heyting structure under inquisitive-semantics names. (7) `PartitionAsInquiry.lean` POSWQ bridge: `POSWQ.inquiryContent c := InquisitiveContent.fromSetoid c.inquiry` lifts every existing POSWQ-using study to a consumer of the inquisitive-content API (`info`/`alt`/`isInquisitive`/lattice ops/forcing arguments) without touching the underlying Setoid state. (8) `TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018.lean` mention-some example renamed `msCellulose ŌåÆ msPaperworld` (Cellulose City is the *non*-selling store in the original Janna scenario; Paperworld is the second selling store that creates the overlap at world `1`). Module docstring updated with `SetLike`/`CompleteDistribLattice` story plus the rationale for rejecting `LowerSet (Set W)ßĄÆßĄł` as a base (`LowerSet.ŌŖź = Ōłģ` vs our `{Ōłģ}` ŌĆö the non-emptiness constraint is essential and lost in `LowerSet`). All downstream (`PartitionAsInquiry`, `TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018`) builds clean.

## [0.229.946] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Case` unified with `UD.Case` ŌĆö single canonical case type, two surface namespaces.** Previously `Core.Case` and `UD.Case` were two parallel inductives with overlapping but non-coincident constructor sets (Core had 19; UD had 26). Code that needed to bridge between Universal Dependencies treebank annotations and the linglib Case API had to maintain a translation table. They are now the *same Lean type*: `Core.Case` is `abbrev Core.Case := UD.Case`, with `UD.Case` chosen as canonical because it tracks the UD CoNLL-U `Case=...` feature spec, which is the de facto annotation tagset shared across treebanks. UD.Case is grown from 26 to **28 constructors** (added `Per` perlative and `Abe` abessive ŌĆö both standard UD values absent from the original enum). Both namespaces (`UD.Case` and `Core.Case`) carry parallel `@[match_pattern] abbrev`s for the lowercase short forms (`nom`, `acc`, `erg`, `abs`, `gen`, `dat`, `loc`, `abl`, `all`, `inst`, `com`, `voc`, `part`, `perl`, `ben`, `caus`, `ess`, `transl`, `abess`) so that existing pattern-matching code continues to work unchanged: `cases c with | nom => ... | acc => ...` works whether the type was written as `Core.Case` or `UD.Case`, and `.nom` works in expected-type position for both. The PascalCase UD constructors (`.Nom`, `.Acc`, `.Ade`, `.Ine`, `.Ill`, `.Ela`, `.Sub`, `.Sup`, `.Del`, `.Ter`, `.Tem`, ...) are accessible from both namespaces too. `Core.Case.allCases` enumerates all 28 constructors; `Core.Case.hierarchyRank` is extended to handle the new constructors with documented ranks (locative-cluster cases at rank 1, peripheral cases at rank 0). `containment_transitive` (`Theories/Interfaces/Morphosyntax/CaseContainment.lean`) was reproved via a `containedIn_iff` helper to avoid the 28┬│ = 21,952 case-split that the old `cases a <;> cases b <;> cases c <;> simp_all` proof would have required after the constructor count went up; the new proof reduces transitivity to `Nat.le_trans` on unwrapped ranks via `Option.some.inj`. All 42 downstream files importing `Linglib.Core.Case` (Fragments for Latin/Czech/Russian/Finnish/Hungarian/Hindi/Japanese/Korean/Turkish/Mongolian/Yakut/Telugu/Tamil/Greek/Icelandic/Polish/Slovenian/Serbian/Ukrainian/Dargwa/Georgian/Basque/Swiss German/German/Mayan; Phenomena studies for Pesetsky 2013, Karlsson 2017, Stassen 1985, Shieber 1985, Aitha 2026, Comparison/Alignment typology; Theories for Minimalism Features, Verb AgentivityLattice, Syntax Case Licensing/Dependent) verified clean. `Core/Word.lean`'s `abbrev Case := UD.Case` now refers to the same type rather than maintaining a separate Core.Case shadow. The unification eliminates a long-standing source of duplication and makes the UD annotation pipeline genuinely typed-through rather than translated.

## [0.229.945] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core/Order/Satisfaction.lean`: `SatisfactionOrdering` now `extends Preorder ╬▒` (mathlib idiom).** The structure was previously a plain bundle of `satisfies : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Criterion ŌåÆ Bool` + `criteria : List Criterion` with a `toPreorder : SatisfactionOrdering ŌåÆ Preorder ╬▒` projection. It now `extends Preorder ╬▒` directly and carries a `decLE : ŌłĆ a a', Decidable (a Ōēż a')` field so `decide`-based selectors (`best`, `undominated`) keep working without a typeclass instance commitment on `╬▒` (multiple `SatisfactionOrdering`s can share a carrier ŌĆö Kratzer worlds with different ordering sources, Cummins violation profiles with different constraint sets ŌĆö so `Preorder ╬▒` cannot be a typeclass instance). Smart constructor `SatisfactionOrdering.ofCriteria (satisfies) (criteria)` builds the canonical satisfaction-based preorder where `Ōēż` is subset-inclusion of satisfied criteria; `decLE` is filled in automatically. The induced `Ōēż` is now exposed as `o.le` (mathlib name) with `atLeastAsGood`/`strictlyBetter`/`equivalent` retained as `@[reducible] def` aliases for domain-friendly readability. Theorems `atLeastAsGood_refl`/`_trans`/`equivalent_refl`/`_symm`/`_trans` are now thin wrappers over the underlying `Preorder` methods. Empty-criteria boundary lemmas restated as `ofCriteria_empty_le`/`_equivalent`/`_undominated`/`_best` (the boundary is now a property of the smart-constructor result, not arbitrary `SatisfactionOrdering`s). Two callers migrated: (1) `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/Constraints/NumericalExpressions.lean` ŌĆö `cumminsOrdering` now uses `ofCriteria`; `zero_violations_best` is a 2-line `cases p <;> rfl` proof. (2) `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Desire.lean` ŌĆö `propositionOrdering`/`worldSatisfactionOrdering` use `ofCriteria`; `propositionPreorder`/`worldPreorder` collapse to `(o).toPreorder`; `atLeastAsPreferred`/`worldAtLeastAsGood` flipped from `Bool` to `Prop`; `empty_desires_indifferent` delegates to `ofCriteria_empty_le`; `empty_bouletic_no_strict_preference` restated as `┬¼ strictlyBetter` (Prop) instead of `strictlyBetter = false` (Bool). (3) `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kratzer/Ordering.lean` ŌĆö `worldOrdering` uses `ofCriteria`; bridge `atLeastAsGoodAs_eq_generic` renamed to `atLeastAsGoodAs_iff_generic` and restated as `Bool Ōåö Prop` (the local `atLeastAsGoodAs : ... ŌåÆ Bool` is preserved for downstream callers in `Operators`/`XMarking`/`ProbabilityOrdering`/`Directive`/`Restrictor`/`Temporal`/`BiasedPQ`); `kratzerPreorder` collapses to `(worldOrdering A).toPreorder`; `orderingEquiv_refl`/`_symm`/`_trans` updated to use `equivalent_*` (new names). The Bool API at the Kratzer/Ordering layer was deliberately preserved to keep blast radius small ŌĆö downstream consumers that want the Prop/Preorder view can opt in via `(worldOrdering A).toPreorder`. All immediate downstream files build clean: `Modality.Kratzer.Operators`, `Modality.Kratzer.XMarking`, `Modality.ProbabilityOrdering`, `Modality.Directive`, `Conditionals.Restrictor`, `Modality.Temporal`, `Pragmatics.Implicature.Constraints.NumericalExpressions`, `Modality.Desire`. Pre-existing concurrent-session breakage in `Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean`, `Core/Temporal/Reichenbach.lean`, `Core/Context/Basic.lean` is unrelated and left for those sessions per the no-blanket-revert rule.

## [0.229.944] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 7 of the Tense-API redesign: `TenseSystem` and `AspectSystem` type classes (mathlib pattern).** New file `Core/Temporal/System.lean` adds two type classes that abstract over specific tense/aspect frameworks, in the **mathlib carrier-type style** (`class Module R M`, `class MeasurableSpace ╬▒`) ŌĆö *not* the higher-kinded `(╬▒ : Type ŌåÆ Type)` style which mathlib reserves for `Functor`/`Monad`. **`TenseSystem (╬▒ : Type u) (Time : outParam (Type u)) [LinearOrder Time]`** says "the type `╬▒` is a way of describing tense over a `Time` line"; commits to (i) `toDomain : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Domain Time`, (ii) `anchorLabel : String` (the role label of the TO that tense locates the situation against ŌĆö `"P"` for Reichenbach, `"TOŌéü"` for Declerck), (iii) `situationLabel : String` (the situation TO ŌĆö `"R"` for Reichenbach, `"TO_sit"` for Declerck). `Time` as an `outParam` lets the elaborator infer it from the carrier (`f : ReichenbachFrame Ōäż` resolves Time = Ōäż automatically). Generic predicates `TenseSystem.isPast`/`isPresent`/`isFuture` defined as `Domain.relatedByName` queries against named atom-sets ŌĆö the same predicates work uniformly across systems; what's specific to each system is the choice of role labels. **`AspectSystem`** is the analogous class for the event/reference relation: `eventLabel`/`referenceLabel` plus generic `isPerfective`/`isPerfect`/`isProspective`. **Reichenbach instances** in `Core/Temporal/Reichenbach.lean`: `reichenbachFrame_tenseSystem` (anchor=P, situation=R) and `reichenbachFrame_aspectSystem` (event=E, reference=R), each an `instance` referring to the existing `ReichenbachFrame.toDomain` builder. Six `*_iff_*System` bridge theorems (`isPast_iff_tenseSystem`, `isFuture_iff_tenseSystem`, `isPresent_iff_tenseSystem`, `isPerfect_iff_aspectSystem`, `isProspective_iff_aspectSystem`, `isPerfective_iff_aspectSystem`) prove the concrete predicates and the generic class predicates agree ŌĆö each bridge is a one-line delegation to the corresponding Step 4 `*_iff_relatedByName` theorem. **Declerck instances** in `Theories/Semantics/Tense/TOChain.lean`: `declercianSchema_tenseSystem` (anchor=`"TOŌéü"`, situation=`"TO_sit"`) and `declercianSchema_aspectSystem` (event=`"TO_sit"`, reference=`"TO_sit"`). The aspect instance collapses both labels to `"TO_sit"` exactly because Declerck's universal `TS = TO_sit` principle means E = R always holds ŌĆö so the generic `AspectSystem.isPerfective` is trivially satisfied for every Declercian schema, and `AspectSystem.isPerfect` can never hold; the "perfect" lives in the chain structure (TO_sit before TOŌéé), not in the E/R relation. This typeclass commitment makes Declerck's claim explicit and machine-checkable rather than buried in prose. With Steps 1-7 complete, the Tense-API redesign delivers a layered structure: Allen algebra (Step 1) ŌåÆ predicate projections (Step 2) ŌåÆ `Domain` substrate (Step 3) ŌåÆ `ReichenbachFrame` lifts to Domain (Step 4) ŌåÆ `DeclercianSchema` lifts to Domain + `Zone` enum (Step 5) ŌåÆ `RelationOrigin` tags + PUTI defaults (Step 6) ŌåÆ `TenseSystem`/`AspectSystem` typeclasses unifying the two frameworks (Step 7). Wired into `Linglib.lean` after `RelationOrigin`. Build clean (871 jobs).

## [0.229.943] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 6 of the Tense-API redesign: `RelationOrigin` tag + Declerck's PUTI lemma.** New file `Core/Temporal/RelationOrigin.lean` adds two layered abstractions over Step 1-5's Allen algebra. **`RelationOrigin` enum** (six constructors): tags *where* a temporal relation between two TOs came from ŌĆö `.tense` (encoded by tense morphology), `.aspect` (lexical-aspectual), `.adverbial` (overt temporal modifier), `.puti` (Declerck's pragmatic default), `.context` (prior discourse/world knowledge), `.stipulated` (direct assumption). The tag is **content-free**: an origin doesn't change which Allen atom holds, only why. `RelationOrigin.isDefeasible` cleanly partitions the six origins: `.puti` and `.context` are defeasible (a hearer can override them); `.tense`/`.aspect`/`.adverbial`/`.stipulated` are not. Six `@[simp]` `rfl`-theorems verify the partition. **`OriginTaggedRelation` structure**: bundles an Allen atom-set (`List AllenRelation`) with a `RelationOrigin` ŌĆö "this atom-set holds, and here's why." `Compatible` predicate (atom-sets share at least one atom) enables conflict detection: when an encoded tense relation and a PUTI default disagree on a non-defeasible-only pair, that's a genuine theoretical contradiction; when they agree, the encoded content is consistent with the unmarked default. **`putiDefault : SituationBoundedness ŌåÆ SituationBoundedness ŌåÆ List AllenRelation`** formalizes Declerck (1991) ch. 3 ┬¦1.2's *Principle of Unmarked Temporal Interpretation*: `(bounded, bounded) ŌåÆ beforeSet` (iconic/sequential), `(unbounded, unbounded) ŌåÆ equalSet` (simultaneous), `(bounded, unbounded) ŌåÆ containmentSet` (bounded inside unbounded), `(unbounded, bounded) ŌåÆ reverseContainmentSet`. Each case stated as a separate `@[simp]` theorem (`puti_bounded_bounded`, `puti_unbounded_unbounded`, `puti_bounded_unbounded`, `puti_unbounded_bounded`) so call sites can reduce by `simp`. **`putiTaggedRelation`** packages the default as an `OriginTaggedRelation` with origin `.puti` ŌĆö explicitly defeasible, so consumers see at a glance that the result is an inference rather than an entailment. The **PUTI lemma** (`puti_lemma`) states that `putiTaggedRelation bŌéü bŌéé` has atoms = `putiDefault bŌéü bŌéé` and origin = `.puti`; the lemma's content is the four-case classifier itself, made manipulable as a tagged Allen atom-set rather than embedded in ad-hoc match expressions across files. `puti_default_defeasible` confirms PUTI defaults always carry the defeasible tag. The redesign goal: when `Phenomena/TenseAspect/Data.lean`'s sequential/simultaneous/inclusion theorems (currently 4 separate `theorem`s with `native_decide`/`rfl` proofs) get reformulated through this layer, they become consequences of `puti_lemma` applied to specific boundedness profiles ŌĆö surfacing the common structure that PUTI is the thing producing all four readings, not four independent stipulations. Wired into `Linglib.lean` after `Domain`. Build clean (866 jobs).

## [0.229.942] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 5 of the Tense-API redesign: `DeclercianSchema` re-expressed via `Domain` + `Zone` enum.** Adds two parallel layers to `Theories/Semantics/Tense/TOChain.lean`. **Domain bridge**: `DeclercianSchema.toDomain : DeclercianSchema Time ŌåÆ Core.Time.Domain Time` lifts the chain structure into a generic `Domain` whose central TO is `TŌéĆ` (the temporal zero-point) and whose sub-TOs are `TOŌéü` followed by every chain link as a point interval; two `@[simp]` `rfl`-theorems (`toDomain_central`, `toDomain_subTOs`) plus `toDomain_labels` (= `"TŌéĆ" :: "TOŌéü" :: chain.map TOLink.name`). The chain's `relation` field is **not** consulted by `toDomain` ŌĆö its job is to constrain admissible time assignments at the call site, not to reproduce information already implicit in the linear order on `Time` (which `Interval.allenRel` projects on demand). **Zone enum**: six-constructor inductive (`prePast`/`past`/`postPast`/`prePresent`/`present`/`postPresent`) encoding Declerck's two time-spheres ├Ś three sphere-internal positions (anterior/central/posterior). `DeclercianSchema.zoneOf : DeclercianSchema Time ŌåÆ Zone` classifies a schema based on `(timeSphere, chain length, last chain-link relation)` ŌĆö the relation of `TO_sit` to its outer anchor combined with the sphere of that anchor. Eight `rfl`-theorems verify each named English tense lands in its expected zone: `preterit_zone = .past`, `present_zone = .present`, `presentPerfect_zone = .prePresent`, `future_zone = .postPresent`, `pastPerfect_zone = .prePast`, `conditional_zone = .postPast`, `futurePerfect_zone = .prePresent`, `conditionalPerfect_zone = .prePast`. The capstone theorem `preterit_presentPerfect_differ_zone` proves `(preterit t0 toSit).zoneOf ŌēĀ (presentPerfect t0 toSit).zoneOf` ŌĆö sharpening the existing `preterit_presentPerfect_same_frame` (identical Reichenbach projection) into a non-trivial *distinguishing* claim. This is exactly Declerck's central thesis (ch. 7 ┬¦1, ┬¦3): the perfect/preterit distinction lives in the time-sphere/zone, not in the underlying R/P configuration. The Zone classifier surfaces what `toFrame` flattens. The simple-tense classifier maps `(past, before)` ŌåÆ `past` (preterit's TO_sit anchored to tŌéĆ in the past sector) but the compound-tense classifier maps `(past, before)` ŌåÆ `prePast` (past perfect's TO_sit anterior to a past anchor) ŌĆö the chain length is the discriminator. Step 5 is **additive** (matching Steps 2 and 4): the `DeclercianSchema` structure, the eight named-tense constructors, and the existing `toFrame`/`depth`/sphere theorems are unchanged.
