Domain-level tooling can now work uniformly with `s.toDomain.relatedByName precedesSet "TO_sit" "TŌéĆ"` and zone-classify with `s.zoneOf`; all existing tense-machinery code continues unchanged. Builds clean (870 jobs).

## [0.229.941] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 4 of the Tense-API redesign: `ReichenbachFrame` re-expressed via `Domain`.** Adds a `toDomain : ReichenbachFrame Time ŌåÆ Core.Time.Domain Time` builder to `Core/Temporal/Reichenbach.lean`, defined as `Domain.ofReichenbachPoints f.speechTime f.perspectiveTime f.referenceTime f.eventTime` ŌĆö the canonical bridge from the four-field point-time record to the framework-agnostic `Domain` substrate. Five `@[simp]` `rfl`-theorems make role lookups trivial: `toDomain_eq` (the equation), `toDomain_labels` (`= reichenbachLabels`), and `toDomain_findS`/`_findP`/`_findR`/`_findE` (each role resolves to the expected `TO.point`). The six existing Reichenbach Boolean predicates are then recharacterized as `Domain.relatedByName` queries against named atom-sets from the Allen algebra: `isPast_iff_relatedByName` (`R precedes P`), `isFuture_iff_relatedByName` (`P precedes R`), `isPresent_iff_relatedByName` (`R equal P`), `isPerfect_iff_relatedByName` (`E precedes R`), `isProspective_iff_relatedByName` (`R precedes E`), `isPerfective_iff_relatedByName` (`E equal R`, for the point-time approximation). Each iff is proved via two private helpers `point_precedes_iff`/`point_equal_iff` (point intervals at `s, t` Ōåö `s < t` / `s = t`), which let the bridges close in ~10 lines: forward direction supplies witnesses via `toDomain_find*`, backward direction extracts equalities via `Option.some.inj` and uses the helpers. New `equalSet : List AllenRelation := [.equal]` added to `Allen.lean` alongside the other named atom-sets ŌĆö needed for the present/perfective bridges, which unlike past/future are equality (rather than precedence) predicates. Step 4 is **additive** (matching Step 2's philosophy): the `ReichenbachFrame` structure and all six Boolean predicates are unchanged, and all 28 downstream files using `ReichenbachFrame.referenceTime`/`isPast`/etc. continue to build clean (verified: `Core.Temporal.Tense`, `Theories.Semantics.Tense.{Basic,Compositional,SequenceOfTense}` all build through the bridge, 898 jobs). Domain-level tooling can now work uniformly with `f.toDomain.relatedByName precedesSet "R" "P"` while existing call sites continue with `f.isPast`. The bridge theorems make explicit that Reichenbach's tense/aspect predicates are *exactly* the singleton-atom Allen projections on point intervals ŌĆö what's special about Reichenbach 1947 is the role-set commitment (`["S", "P", "R", "E"]`), not the underlying relation algebra.

## [0.229.940] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mathlib-PR polish pass over the Phase 1ŌĆō4 Case-API work.** `Theories/Syntax/Case/Licensing.lean`: (1) `LicensedNP` now `extends Syntax.Case.NPInDomain` rather than duplicating fields, with `needsLicensing : Bool` modeling Pesetsky-Torrego unvalued [Case]; new `LicensedNP.caseFeature : Option Core.Case` returns `none` exactly when the NP is active for licensing, with `caseFeature_none_iff_active` proving the equivalence. (2) `LicensingOutcome` constructors now carry the licenser identity and assigned case (`byPrimary head c`, `bySecondary head c`, `byLexical c`, `unlicensed`) so the outcome is informative rather than an opaque tag; new `LicensingOutcome.isLicensed` and `assignedCase` projections. (3) `licenseActive` now actually uses the primary head/case (the `_primary` parameter was dead before). (4) `licenseSecondaries` hoisted to a private top-level `def` (was a where-clause), enabling reuse and induction. (5) New ┬¦ 6 of property theorems: `licenseSecondaries_length` (no NP dropped), `licenseActive_length`/`licenseNPs_length` (algorithm preserves NP count), `licenseNPs_labels` (label preservation by `List.map` construction, not by length-bookkeeping), `first_active_byPrimary` (the first active NP always gets the primary), `nonNeeding_isLicensed_byPrimary` (NPs without `needsLicensing` are licensed by primary, not secondary ŌĆö Kalin Thesis 1), `licenseActive_no_crash_when_enough_secondaries` (Kalin Thesis 2), `no_need_means_no_secondaries_used` (Kalin Thesis 3 ŌĆö DOM signature is empty when no NP requires licensing). (6) New ┬¦ 7 bridge to `Minimalism.CaseFilter`: `LicensingOutcome.toDPFeatures` projects to the Minimalist DP feature record; `isLicensed_iff_satisfiesCaseFilter` (per-NP equivalence) and `all_isLicensed_iff_caseFilterHolds` (clause-level equivalence) make the Kalin licensing API and the Chomsky Case Filter observably the same predicate. (7) `byPrimary` now records the primary's head string so `primary_head_distinguishes` can prove that licensing identity is load-bearing (changing `primary.head` from `"T"` to `"v"` flips the outcome). (8) ┬¦8 worked examples migrated from `native_decide` to `decide` per the proof-style preference. (9) Docstring hedge: "secondaries can be analyzed as conditional voice-style heads" reframed as a description of one possible mapping rather than a claim Kalin makes. `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean`: 19 `native_decide` invocations migrated to `decide` (all are concrete finite-data checks). `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Scott2023.lean`: 2 `native_decide` invocations migrated to `decide`. `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Caha2009.lean`: new ┬¦ 3 cross-inventory survey ŌĆö `cahaSurvey : List (String ├Ś Bool)` bundles the conformance verdict for all 22 formalized Fragment case inventories into one finite table; `cahaSurvey_length` (= 22, total coverage), `all_inventories_classified` (the violator set is exactly `[("dargwa", false), ("finnish", false)]`), `caha_conformance_count` (20/22 conform). The survey breaks if a new Fragment is added without classification, providing the maintenance contract the per-language theorems alone don't guarantee. Build clean for all Case-related files (925 jobs in the closure of `Licensing.lean Ōł¬ Dependent.lean Ōł¬ Scott2023.lean Ōł¬ Caha2009.lean Ōł¬ Marantz1991.lean Ōł¬ Baker2015.lean Ōł¬ Woolford1997.lean Ōł¬ Pesetsky2013.lean Ōł¬ CaseContainment.lean Ōł¬ CaseFilter.lean`). Unrelated concurrent-session breakage in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kratzer/Ordering.lean` (`Core/Order/Satisfaction.lean` API drift) is left for that session to resolve per the no-blanket-revert rule.

## [0.229.939] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 3 of the Tense-API redesign: `Core/Temporal/Domain.lean` ŌĆö temporal domains (Declerck 2006).** New foundational module formalizing the shared substrate of @cite{declerck-2006}'s temporal domains and @cite{reichenbach-1947}'s S/P/R/E framework. Three core types: `abbrev TO (Time : Type*) [LE Time] := Interval Time` (a *time of orientation*: realized as `Interval Time` so the same type covers both Reichenbach's point times via `TO.point t = Interval.point t` and Declerck's extended times-of-situation via general intervals); `structure NamedTO Time` (a TO with an identifying string label, enabling role-based access without baking a fixed role-list into the type ŌĆö `"S"`/`"P"`/`"R"`/`"E"` for Reichenbach, `"TŌéĆ"`/`"T-Past"`/etc. for Declerck); `structure Domain Time` (central `NamedTO` + list of sub-`NamedTO`s ŌĆö covers both Reichenbach's 4-role layout and Declerck's variable-count nested TOs). Two non-obvious design choices are documented in the `Domain` docstring: (1) **no relation storage** ŌĆö TOs are intervals on a `LinearOrder`, so their Allen relation is determined by the order; storing relations would either be redundant or contradictory, so we compute on demand via Step 2's `Interval.allenRel`; (2) **NamedTO list, not record** ŌĆö different domain-shaped theories use different role-sets, so a label-keyed list keeps `Domain` framework-agnostic and lets downstream wrappers commit to fixed role-sets on top. Five Domain accessors: `all` (central first, then sub-TOs), `find?` (look up by label, returns `Option (TO Time)`), `labels` (list of all role labels ŌĆö useful for stating role-set invariants), `noncomputable allenRel` (lifted projection function), `relatedBy` (two TOs stand in some atom of `S` ŌĆö lifts `AllenRelation.holdsIn` to the domain level), `relatedByName` (lookup-and-relate: do the two named TOs both exist in the domain and stand in some atom of `S`? Phrases tense predicates like "R precedes P" against a domain by name). Two `@[simp]` membership lemmas: `all_central` (the central TO is in `all`), `all_sub` (sub-TOs are in `all`). Reichenbach builders for the canonical S/P/R/E role-set: `reichenbachLabels := ["S", "P", "R", "E"]` (the four canonical Reichenbach role labels, extended with @cite{kiparsky-2002}'s perspective time P), `ofReichenbachPoints S P R E : Domain Time` (build a domain from four point times), plus five `@[simp]` `rfl`-theorems (`ofReichenbachPoints_labels` + `_findS`/`_findP`/`_findR`/`_findE`) ŌĆö Step 4's `ReichenbachFrame Ōåö Domain` bridge will use these. The Domain module is **substrate**: it does not add tense semantics (reserved for `Tense.lean`), aspect (reserved for `Theories/Semantics/Tense/Aspect/`), or sector classification (reserved for `DeclercianSchema` in Step 5 ŌĆö the `Zone` enum lives there because it's Declerck-specific). Wired into `Linglib.lean` between `Allen` and `Reichenbach`. Build clean (866 jobs).

## [0.229.938] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Step 2 of the Tense-API redesign: `Interval` predicates restated as Allen projections.** Adds an "Atom Sets and `holdsIn`" section to `Core/Temporal/Allen.lean` introducing `def AllenRelation.holdsIn (S : List AllenRelation) (i j : Interval Time) : Prop := Ōłā r Ōłł S, r.holds i j` ŌĆö the uniform "at least one of these atoms holds" predicate. Three structural lemmas: `holdsIn_nil` (empty set Ōåö False, `@[simp]`), `holdsIn_singleton` (singleton Ōåö holds, `@[simp]`), `holdsIn_cons` (cons Ōåö disjunction), `holdsIn_mono` (subset monotonicity: enlarging the atom set weakens the predicate). Eight named atom-sets corresponding to existing `Interval` predicates: `precedesSet := [.precedes]`, `meetsSet := [.meets]`, `beforeSet := [.precedes, .meets]` (the conflation `i.finish Ōēż j.start` represents), `afterSet := [.precededBy, .metBy]`, `containmentSet := [.starts, .equal, .finishes, .during]` (subinterval), `properContainmentSet := [.starts, .finishes, .during]` (excludes `equal`), `finalContainmentSet := [.finishes, .equal]`, `reverseContainmentSet := [.startedBy, .equal, .finishedBy, .contains]` (reverse direction of `subinterval`), `overlapSet` (the eleven atoms compatible with sharing at least one moment ŌĆö everything except `precedes`/`precededBy`). The existing `_iff_allen` bridge theorems refactored to use `holdsIn` consistently ŌĆö including a new `properSubinterval_iff_allen` not covered before ŌĆö making the algebraic structure (`SŌéü ŌŖå SŌéé ŌćÆ holdsIn SŌéü ŌćÆ holdsIn SŌéé`) explicit. The **projection function** `Interval.allenRel : Interval Time ŌåÆ Interval Time ŌåÆ AllenRelation` (noncomputable, via `Classical.choose` over `holds_exists`) ŌĆö the inverse direction of `holds`, lifting an interval pair to its unique Allen atom. Three characterization theorems: `allenRel_holds` (the projected atom does hold), `allenRel_unique` (for non-degenerate intervals, every witness equals the projection ŌĆö the well-definedness theorem justifying treating `allenRel i j` as **the** Allen relation), `allenRel_mem_iff_holdsIn` (the projection lands in the named atom-set iff the corresponding `holdsIn` predicate holds, on non-degenerate intervals ŌĆö bridges the predicate API to the projection API). Together with `holds_exists` + `holds_unique`, this gives a full abstraction-and-projection layer over the Allen algebra: `holdsIn` lets you write predicates as named atom-sets without committing to a specific Boolean combination; `allenRel` lets you project an interval pair down to a unique atom for case analysis. Step 2 is **additive** ŌĆö no existing definitions changed, no downstream call sites affected (verified: only `Linglib.lean` imports Allen.lean directly; downstream `MaximalInformativity`, `TemporalDecomposition`, `Koev2017`, `SubintervalProperty` all build clean). Build clean (865 jobs).

## [0.229.937] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Move `Core/Logic/SatisfactionOrdering.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Order/Satisfaction.lean`.** The construction (a `satisfies : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Criterion ŌåÆ Bool` plus criteria list inducing a preorder on `╬▒` with `best`/`undominated` selectors) is mathematically an *order construction*, not a logical one ŌĆö mathlib organizes by the structure being defined, not by what's being ordered. Moves it next to its siblings `Core/Order/Normality.lean` (`NormalityOrder`) and `Core/Order/Plausibility.lean` (`PlausibilityOrder`), and adopts their naming convention: file name drops the redundant "Ordering" suffix (the `Core/Order/` directory contextualizes it), while the structure name `SatisfactionOrdering` is preserved for self-description at call sites. Namespace `Core.SatisfactionOrdering` ŌåÆ `Core.Order` (matching `Core.Order.NormalityOrder`). Consumers updated: `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kratzer/Ordering.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Desire.lean`, `Theories/Pragmatics/Implicature/Constraints/NumericalExpressions.lean` ŌĆö each now uses `open Core.Order (SatisfactionOrdering)`. The `Criterion` type parameter is preserved (genuinely exercised: `cumminsOrdering : SatisfactionOrdering ViolationProfile NumeralConstraint` uses an enum criterion type, and `worldSatisfactionOrdering` in Desire.lean uses `BProp` criteria ŌĆö the parameter is not vestigial). My migration files all build clean (`lake build Linglib.Core.Order.Satisfaction Linglib.Theories.Semantics.Modality.Kratzer.Ordering Linglib.Theories.Pragmatics.Implicature.Constraints.NumericalExpressions` succeeds in 872 jobs). `Linglib.Theories.Semantics.Modality.Desire` builds correctly through the migration but the full closure is currently blocked by an unrelated pre-existing break in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kratzer/Flavor.lean` (concurrent-session BoolŌåÆProp migration of `KratzerTheory.duality` left a partial proof ŌĆö out of scope for this rename).

## [0.229.936] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Phase 4 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: `Theories/Syntax/Case/Licensing.lean` ŌĆö @cite{kalin-2018}'s hybrid licensing framework.** New bib entry `kalin-2018` ("Licensing and Differential Object Marking: The View from Neo-Aramaic", *Syntax* 21:2, 112ŌĆō159, DOI 10.1111/synt.12153, validated against the PDF). The theory file formalizes Kalin's two-parameter system: (i) `LicenserKind` distinguishes obligatory `primary` licensers (always merged, e.g., T) from `secondary` last-resort licensers (merging only when their inactivity would crash the derivation, e.g., AGRO, P-DOM); (ii) `LicensedNP.needsLicensing` parameterizes which nominals require licensing, with `lexicalCase : Option Case` independently bleeding the requirement (P/V pre-licenses). `ClauseLicensers` bundles a primary + a list of secondaries with a definitional invariant (`primary_is_primary : primary.kind = .primary := by rfl`) so the typeclass system enforces the "exactly one primary" constraint. The licensing algorithm (`licenseActive` + helper `licenseRest`) greedily activates secondaries from the queue as remaining active NPs require ŌĆö modeling the global last-resort economy condition. `LicensingOutcome` records *how* each NP was licensed (`.byPrimary` / `.bySecondary head` / `.byLexical case` / `.unlicensed`); `isDOMMarked` extracts the DOM signature: marked objects are exactly those licensed by a secondary licenser. Five smoke-test theorems on a Turkish-style clause (T primary + AGRO secondary): `specific_object_is_dom_marked` (specific object ŌåÆ AGRO secondary), `nonspecific_object_unmarked` (no licensing need ŌåÆ primary suffices, no DOM), `subject_always_primary` (subject takes the primary in both clauses), `dom_signature` (DOM-marked NP list = `["obj-specific"]` vs `[]`), `no_secondary_means_crash` (clause without secondaries cannot license a second active NP). Docstring leads with the two-parameter framework, traces the relation to dependent case (largely orthogonal ŌĆö both can coexist), voice-based case (secondaries can be analyzed as conditional voice-style heads), and explicitly notes @cite{coon-keine-2021}'s feature-gluttony alternative as an *adjacent* debate (Coon-Keine reject licensing as the source of *hierarchy effects* like PCC, but their account doesn't target DOM, so the two are not competing analyses of the same data). Wired into `Linglib.lean` after `Theories.Syntax.Case.Dependent`. `Dependent.lean`'s "Companion Infrastructure" section updated to cross-reference `Licensing.lean`. Build clean (289 jobs in the dependency closure of the new file).

### Changed
- **Phase 3 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: reframed `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean`'s module docstring.** Stripped the embedded "Key Application: @cite{ozaki-2026}" subsection (study-specific content in a theory file = anti-pattern; the actual Ozaki content lives in `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Ozaki2026.lean`). Removed the inline `Fragments.Mayan.Mam.Agreement` cross-reference (now superseded by the three Scott2023 study files). New "Where to Find What" section enumerates the seven Case study files (`Marantz1991`, `Baker2015`, `BakerVinokurova2010`, `Woolford1997`, `Scott2023`, `Ozaki2026`, `Caha2009`) with one-line descriptions tying each to its empirical contribution. New "Companion Infrastructure" section cross-references `Voice.lean`, `CaseFilter.lean`, `Licensing.lean` (Phase 4), and `CaseContainment.lean` so a reader landing in `Dependent.lean` can navigate the surrounding Case-API surface. Theorem and definition content unchanged. The ┬¦8/┬¦9 Ozaki-specific worked derivations in the file body remain in place pending a follow-up move to `Studies/Ozaki2026.lean`.

### Changed
- **Phase 2 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: dissolved `Phenomena/Case/Compare.lean` into `Studies/Scott2023.lean` + theory-level coverage.** The three-way comparison file (Agree vs. Dependent vs. Voice-based case) had no single-paper provenance and mixed Scott 2023's novel Voice-based contribution with theorems already covered elsewhere. Promoted the Voice-based content to a real study file `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Scott2023.lean` (namespace `Phenomena.Case.Studies.Scott2023`) ŌĆö the case-theoretic counterpart to the existing `Phenomena/Agreement/Studies/Scott2023.lean` (Agree/Spellout pipeline) and `Phenomena/Ergativity/Studies/Scott2023.lean` (super-extended ergativity). Three sections: ┬¦ 1 Voice's positional case assignment (`voice_assigns_case_by_position`, `voice_based_tripartite` ŌĆö three distinct cases from three different heads via the Mam fragment's `MamArgPosition.case`), ┬¦ 2 contrast with Agree-based case (`agree_anticausative_not_phase`, `agree_voice_is_phase_head` ŌĆö Agree ties ACC to phase-hood, Voice-based assignment doesn't), ┬¦ 3 contrast with dependent case (`dependent_case_ignores_voice` ŌĆö same ACC for unaccusative-vs-transitive themes; `dependent_case_tripartite` ŌĆö same surface pattern via positional rather than ╬Ė-role grounds). Build clean.

## [0.229.934] - 2026-04-18

### Fixed
- **Closed both sorrys in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Premise.lean`'s consistency-collapse theorems** (`mustInView_iff_mustInView'_of_consistent`, `canInView_iff_canInView'_of_consistent`). Added five reusable monotonicity helpers that pin the premise algebra to its set-theoretic content: `propIntersection_anti_of_subset` (more premises ŌåÆ smaller intersection), `followsFrom_mono_of_subset` (more premises ŌåÆ more consequences), `isCompatibleWith_anti_of_subset` (fewer premises ŌåÆ easier to be compatible with), `self_mem_consistentSublists` (a consistent list is a sublist of itself), `subset_of_mem_consistentSublists` (every element of `consistentSublists A` is `ŌŖå A`). Both collapse theorems now witnessed by `f i` itself: when `f i` is consistent, it dominates every consistent sublist `B ŌŖå f i`, so the inner `Ōłā C ŌŖć B` is solved by `C := f i` and the inner `ŌłĆ C ŌŖć B` reduces to a property of subsets of `f i`. Added `import Mathlib.Data.List.Sublists` for `List.mem_sublists`. Build clean through `Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Kratzer1977.lean`.

## [0.229.933] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`InquisitiveContent W` is now a mathlib `BoundedDistribLattice` (Stage 5).** Replaces the standalone `LE`/`Top`/`Bot` instances and the eight binary lattice-axiom theorems (`bot_le`, `le_top`, `conj_le_left`, `conj_le_right`, `le_conj`, `left_le_inqDisj`, `right_le_inqDisj`, `inqDisj_le`) with proper mathlib typeclass instances: `PartialOrder` (entailment, `P Ōēż Q := P.props ŌŖå Q.props`), `Lattice` (binding `ŌŖō = conj`, `ŌŖö = inqDisj`), `DistribLattice` (free from `Set.union_inter_distrib_left`-style distributivity on `Set (Set W)`), `OrderTop` (with `ŌŖż = top`, `top.props = Set.univ`), and `OrderBot` (with `ŌŖź = bot`, `bot.props = {Ōłģ}`). Three convenience equalities (`inf_eq_conj`, `sup_eq_inqDisj`, `top_eq`/`bot_eq`) re-expose the named operations after they're hidden behind lattice notation. Net effect: all of mathlib's lattice/order API now applies ŌĆö `inf_le_left`, `le_sup_right`, `sup_le`, `inf_sup_left` (distributivity), `bot_le`, `le_top`, plus interval/downset/lattice-hom theorems for free. Smoke-tested against `inf_le_left`, `le_sup_left`, `inf_sup_left`, `bot_le`, `le_top` in `scratch/test_inq_lattice.lean`. Build clean (946 jobs through `Linglib.Core.Mood` and `Phenomena.Complementation.Studies.TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018`). `CompleteLattice` instance (arbitrary `iInf`/`iSup` over families, with the empty-`iSup` = `bot` boundary case) deferred to a follow-up; the binary lattice covers the immediate downstream use cases (verb-meaning composition needs `ŌŖō` for conjunction and `ŌŖö` for disjunction of clausal complements).

## [0.229.932] - 2026-04-18

- **Merged `Phenomena/Questions/NandaoQs.lean` + `NandaoQsAnalysis.lean` ŌåÆ `Phenomena/Questions/Studies/Zheng2025.lean`.** Per CLAUDE.md study-file convention (`Studies/AuthorYear.lean`) and the "self-contained study files" guidance: data, fragment-bridge theorems, and theory-bridge theorems for @cite{zheng-2025}'s nandao-Q felicity now live in one author-year file. Namespace `Phenomena.Questions.NandaoQs(.Bridge)` ŌåÆ `Phenomena.Questions.Studies.Zheng2025`. `Linglib.lean` updated.

### Fixed
- **Closed all 4 sorrys in `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Kernel.lean`.** Added two private helpers `compatibleWith_iff_exists` (compatible Ōåö B_K Ōł® Ō¤”ŽåŌ¤¦ ŌēĀ Ōłģ) and `followsFrom_iff_forall` (followsFrom Ōåö B_K ŌŖå Ō¤”ŽåŌ¤¦) bridging the Bool-decided primitives to Ōłā/ŌłĆ over `propIntersection`. With those helpers in hand, structured proofs close `partition_implies_entailment` (case-split on `k.base = Ōłģ`; non-empty case picks `wŌéĆ Ōłł B_K`, uses `foldl_refine_preserves_colocated` + `colocated_same_phi` to show all worlds in B_K share Žå-value, then dispatches on `Žå wŌéĆ`), `kernel_duality` (might Žå Ōåö ┬¼(must ┬¼Žå) via `push Not`), `must_perhaps_contradiction` (must Žå ŌåÆ ┬¼might ┬¼Žå), and `cant_might_exclusion` (can't Žå ŌåÆ ┬¼might Žå).
- **`Theories/Semantics/Modality/BiasedPQ.lean` `evidentialBias_duality`.** Pre-existing build break after `Kratzer.duality` migrated to `W ŌåÆ Prop`. Coerce `BProp` arguments via `(fun w => p w = true)` and convert `(fun w' => ┬¼(p w' = true))` to `(fun w' => !p w' = true)` via `Bool.not_eq_true` to match the duality conclusion.

## [0.229.931] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Algebraic structure on `InquisitiveContent` per @cite{puncochar-2019} ┬¦2 (Stage 4).** Extends `Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean` with the basic inquisitive lattice: `conj P Q` (Ōł¦, props = `P.props Ōł® Q.props`, the @cite{puncochar-2019} p. 299 Ōł¦-clause), `inqDisj P Q` (Ō®Æ, props = `P.props Ōł¬ Q.props`, the inquisitive disjunction clause ŌĆö distinct from classical Ōł©, whose support clause involves splitting the state and is deferred), `top` (props = `Set.univ`, the trivial inquiry), `bot` (props = `{Ōłģ}`, the unsatisfiable inquiry); `Top` and `Bot` instances, plus the entailment order `LE` instance (`P Ōēż Q := P.props ŌŖå Q.props` ŌĆö read as "every state resolving `P` also resolves `Q`"). Lattice-axiom theorems: `bot_le`, `le_top`, `conj_le_left`, `conj_le_right`, `le_conj`, `left_le_inqDisj`, `right_le_inqDisj`, `inqDisj_le`. Two characterization theorems pin the algebra to the type-level constructors: (1) `polar_eq_inqDisj p : polar p = inqDisj (declarative p) (declarative pßČ£)` ŌĆö grounds the @cite{theiler-etal-2018} Figure 2(a) `polar` constructor in @cite{puncochar-2019}'s `?╬▒ := ╬▒ Ō®Æ ┬¼╬▒` (the `┬¼╬▒` reduction `||┬¼╬▒|| = declarative pßČ£` for atomic ╬▒ follows from the support clause of negation reducing to incompatibility on Set W); (2) `isDeclarative_iff_eq_declarative_info P : P.isDeclarative Ōåö P = declarative P.info` ŌĆö the **principal-ideal characterization** from @cite{puncochar-2019} p. 298, made into a Lean theorem rather than a docstring claim. `@[ext]` lemma added so `apply ext + ext q` unpacks structure equality through `Set` extensionality. Classical disjunction `Ōł©`, negation `┬¼`, and implication `ŌåÆ` deferred (their support clauses involve quantification over information states and need a separate development of the support relation). Build clean (946 jobs through dependent files).

## [0.229.930] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Temporal/Allen.lean` ŌĆö the canonical 13-relation Allen algebra (@cite{allen-1983}), with full existence + uniqueness proved.** Step 1 of a mathlib-style Tense-API redesign motivated by reviewing @cite{declerck-2006} (Volume 1 of *The Grammar of the English Verb Phrase*, with Susan Reed and Bert Cappelle, Topics in English Linguistics 60-1, Mouton de Gruyter). Adds `Core.Time.AllenRelation` as an inductive with thirteen constructors (`precedes`, `meets`, `overlaps`, `finishedBy`, `contains`, `starts`, `equal`, `startedBy`, `during`, `finishes`, `overlappedBy`, `metBy`, `precededBy`), each with a per-constructor docstring giving its defining inequality. Methods: `inverse` (with `inverse_inverse` and `inverse_eq_self_iff` characterizing `equal` as the unique self-inverse), `all` + `mem_all` (exhaustiveness as a list), `holds : AllenRelation ŌåÆ Interval Time ŌåÆ Interval Time ŌåÆ Prop` (the central predicate), `holds_inverse` (`r.holds i j Ōåö r.inverse.holds j i` ŌĆö the algebraic symmetry of the inverse operation, proved by `cases` + `simp` with `and_comm`/`and_left_comm`/`eq_comm`), `holds_decidable` instance for `[DecidableEq Time] [DecidableRel (┬Ę < ┬Ę)]`. **Existence and uniqueness both proven** (no sorrys): (a) `holds_exists (i j) : Ōłā r, r.holds i j` by trichotomy on `i.finish vs j.start`, refined through `i.start vs j.finish`, `i.start vs j.start`, `i.finish vs j.finish` ŌĆö explicit witness for each of the 13 leaves; (b) `holds_unique (i j) (hi : i.start < i.finish) (hj : j.start < j.finish) (rŌéü rŌéé) : rŌéü.holds i j ŌåÆ rŌéé.holds i j ŌåÆ rŌéü = rŌéé` factored through a structural **signature**: `def signature (i j) : Ordering ├Ś Ordering ├Ś Ordering ├Ś Ordering` records the four pairwise `sgn` (lt/eq/gt) comparisons between `i`'s endpoints and `j`'s, and `def AllenRelation.expectedSig` tabulates the unique 4-tuple forced by each atom's defining inequalities under non-degeneracy. Two 13-case lemmas ŌĆö `expectedSig_injective` (distinct atoms have distinct signatures, by `cases <;> cases <;> first | rfl | simp`) and `signature_of_holds` (every holds-witness forces its atom's signature, by `cases r <;>` per-component `Prod.ext` + `sgn_lt`/`sgn_eq`/`sgn_gt` + `order`) ŌĆö combine into a one-line term-level uniqueness proof. This factoring fits in the default heartbeat budget (build ~10s) where the brute-force `cases rŌéü <;> cases rŌéé <;> order` over 169 cells needed `set_option maxHeartbeats 1200000 in`. The non-degeneracy hypothesis on `holds_unique` is **essential and intrinsic** to Allen's framework, not a formalization artifact: on a degenerate interval `Ō¤©t, t, le_refl tŌ¤®`, `meets` (`t = t`), `metBy` (`t = t`), and `equal` (`t = t Ōł¦ t = t`) all hold simultaneously ŌĆö the docstring documents this and notes that @cite{allen-1983} sidesteps it by assuming strict intervals throughout. Predicate bridges connect existing `Interval` predicates to unions of Allen atoms: `precedes_iff_allen` and `meets_iff_allen` are `Iff.rfl`; `isBefore_iff_allen` exhibits `Ōēż` as `{precedes, meets}` (the conflation Allen removes); `isAfter_iff_allen` mirrors with `{precededBy, metBy}`; `subinterval_iff_allen` decomposes containment as `{starts, equal, finishes, during}` via two-way trichotomy on `start Ōēż start` and `finish Ōēż finish`; `finalSubinterval_iff_allen` further restricts to `{finishes, equal}`. Identity-composition lemmas: `holds_equal_left`/`holds_equal_right` (equal as two-sided identity), `holds_precedes_trans` (chained via `j.valid` since `i.finish < j.start Ōēż j.finish < k.start`), `holds_during_trans`/`holds_contains_trans`. The full 13├Ś13 composition table (169 entries, the algebra Allen 1983 derives) is TODO; only the principal transitives used by tense theory are provided. Wired into `Linglib.lean` after `Core.Temporal.Time` and before `Core.Temporal.Reichenbach`. Build clean (865 jobs), no warnings, no sorrys.

## [0.229.929] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/TheilerRoelofsenAloni2018.lean` ŌĆö forcing study for `InquisitiveContent`.** Stage 3 of the InquisitiveContent line of work (Stages 1ŌĆō2 = 0.229.925, 0.229.927). Formalizes Section 2 of @cite{theiler-etal-2018} ("A Uniform Semantics for Declarative and Interrogative Complements", *Journal of Semantics* 35(3): 409ŌĆō466) ŌĆö the inquisitive-semantics framework for sentence meanings ŌĆö and exhibits the **mention-some forcing example** from Section 3.1 (building on @cite{george-2011}) that justifies `InquisitiveContent` over `Setoid`. Two parts: (1) **Figure 2 examples** ŌĆö a 4-world model (Amy left in `0, 1`; didn't leave in `2, 3`) with `amyLeftMeaning := declarative amyLeft` and `didAmyLeaveMeaning := polar amyLeft`; four characteristic theorems pin the paper's (info ├Ś inquisitive) classification: declarative is `isInformative` Ōł¦ `┬¼ isInquisitive`, polar is `┬¼ isInformative` Ōł¦ `isInquisitive`. (2) **Mention-some forcing** ŌĆö a 3-world model (`MS = Fin 3`) with overlapping alternatives `msNewstopia = {0, 1}` and `msCellulose = {1, 2}` (world `1` is where both stores sell, hence in both alternatives ŌĆö exactly the structural feature partitions cannot represent). Theorem `mentionSome_not_partition_derived (r : Setoid MS) : fromSetoid r ŌēĀ mentionSome` proves the mention-some content is **not** in the image of `Setoid ŌåÆ InquisitiveContent`: if it were, both alternatives would lie in equivalence classes containing world `1`, forcing those classes to coincide via `Setoid.eq_of_mem_classes`; then `{0, 2}` would be in `props` but is contained in neither alternative ŌĆö contradiction. This is the existence theorem for the architectural decision in `Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`'s "Architectural note" (added 0.229.922): `InquisitiveContent` is a **strictly more expressive** representation, so the sibling-structure architecture is empirically necessary, not a redundancy. Scope-limited as per the docstring: ┬¦3 (false-answer-sensitive truthful resolutions), ┬¦┬¦4ŌĆō6 (verb-meaning lexicalization, predicates of relevance), and Appendices AŌĆōB (comparison with @cite{uegaki-2015}, formal proofs) deferred. Wired into `Linglib.lean`. Build clean.

## [0.229.928] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Phenomena/Case/DependentCaseDerivations.lean` ŌåÆ `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Baker2015.lean`.** The file's contents (cross-linguistic algorithm derivations for German, Turkish, Basque, Hindi, Georgian) match @cite{baker-2015}'s typological survey in *Case: Its Principles and Its Parameters* (CUP) ŌĆö Baker's monograph takes @cite{marantz-1991}'s dependent case algorithm and runs it across accusative, ergative, and split-ergative columns, which is exactly what this file does. Previously had no single-paper provenance and lived outside `Studies/`; the relocation gives it author-year anchoring parallel to the existing `Marantz1991.lean` (Georgian-focused, abstract-vs-morphological case spell-out) and `BakerVinokurova2010.lean` (Sakha-focused). Docstring rewritten to lead with @cite{baker-2015}'s framework, reference the algorithm's @cite{marantz-1991}/@cite{baker-vinokurova-2010} ancestry, and explicitly defer to the sister study files for content not in Baker's column-sweep. Namespace `Phenomena.Case.DependentCaseDerivations` ŌåÆ `Phenomena.Case.Studies.Baker2015`. `Linglib.lean` import updated. No code or theorem changes. Build clean (297 jobs).

### Added
- **Phase 1 of the Case-API mathlib reorganization: `PartialOrder Core.Case` instance + `Caha2009` cross-Fragment study.** Adds Caha's containment hierarchy as the *canonical* order on `Core.Case` via a typeclass instance in `Theories/Interfaces/Morphosyntax/CaseContainment.lean` (┬¦ 13). The order definition is disjunctive ŌĆö `cŌéü Ōēż cŌéé := cŌéü = cŌéé Ōł© Ōłā n m, containmentRank cŌéü = some n Ōł¦ containmentRank cŌéé = some m Ōł¦ n < m` ŌĆö which makes off-hierarchy cases (ERG, ABS, INST, COM, ŌĆ”) *incomparable* with on-hierarchy ones rather than forcing them into the order. That incomparability *is* the theoretical content: @cite{caha-2009}'s containment is silent on those cases, and the partial order preserves that silence by construction. Reflexivity comes from the left disjunct; antisymmetry and transitivity reduce to `Nat.lt` properties on the right disjunct, with `Option.some.inj` extracting the rank equalities. Exposes `LE`, `Preorder`, `PartialOrder` instances; sanity theorems `nom_le_acc`, `acc_le_gen`, `gen_le_dat`, `dat_le_loc` confirm the four-step chain, and `erg_incomparable_nom` / `nom_incomparable_erg` confirm off-hierarchy incomparability. Other potential orders on `Case` (Blake's typological frequency, per-language syncretism graphs) live as named `def`s used locally via `letI` rather than as competing instances ŌĆö the canonical instance goes to the universal claim that state-of-the-art syntax (configurational case + nanosyntactic spellout) assumes. CaseContainment.lean ┬¦ 14 adds `respectsCahaContainment : List Case ŌåÆ Bool` (decidable contiguous-prefix check using the four hierarchy implications) as the predicate consumers can apply to any inventory. New study file `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Caha2009.lean` instantiates the prediction across 22 Fragment case inventories: 20 conformers (Czech, German, Greek, Hindi, Hungarian, Icelandic, Japanese, Korean, Latin, Mongolian, Polish, Russian, Serbian, Slovenian, SwissGerman, Tamil, Telugu, Turkish, Ukrainian, Yakut) plus 2 *predicted* violators with explanatory theorems ŌĆö `dargwa_ergative` (ergative alignment puts ABS where NOM would be; ERG/ABS are off-hierarchy) and `finnish_dat_gap` (no dedicated dative; allative -lle covers recipient function per @cite{blake-1994} Ch. 6's ALL ŌåÆ DAT extension). Both violations sit naturally in the partial order's "incomparable" region rather than being counterexamples. Per-Fragment density gives the interconnection-density payoff: any change to a Fragment's inventory or to `containmentRank` breaks exactly one theorem. Naming follows the project's author-year study convention (parallel to `Bobaljik2012.lean`, `BakerVinokurova2010.lean`) ŌĆö Caha 2009 is *the* paper proposing containment, so the cross-linguistic typological claim derived from it is its study file. Wired into `Linglib.lean`. Build clean (383 jobs). Future phases (not in this commit): Phase 2 (relocate `Phenomena/Case/Compare.lean` to `Studies/`), Phase 3 (reframe `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean` docstring), Phase 4 (`Theories/Syntax/Case/Licensing.lean` for Kalin and Coon-Keine).

## [0.229.927] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean` ŌĆö faithful `Setoid ŌåÆ InquisitiveContent` embedding.** Stage 2 of the InquisitiveContent line of work (Stage 1 = 0.229.925). `fromSetoid r : InquisitiveContent W` carries `props = {q | q = Ōłģ Ōł© Ōłā c Ōłł r.classes, q ŌŖå c}` ŌĆö a non-empty information state resolves the issue iff every world in it is `r`-equivalent (i.e., `q` is contained in some equivalence class). The maximal such propositions are exactly the equivalence classes / alternatives. Two characterizing theorems: (1) `info_fromSetoid` ŌĆö informative content of a partition-derived inquiry is `Set.univ` (every world is in some class), formalizing the standard partition-semantics view that Setoid-based inquiry is **non-informative** (raises an issue, supplies no information); (2) `isInquisitive_fromSetoid_of_two_classes` ŌĆö if `r` has two `r`-distinct elements, `fromSetoid r` is inquisitive in `InquisitiveContent`'s sense (`info Ōłē props`), since `Set.univ` cannot be contained in any single equivalence class. The trivial setoid (one class) yields a declarative ŌĆö matching the partition-semantics intuition that a one-cell partition raises no real issue. The embedding is faithful but **not surjective** (and we don't claim it is): mention-some, intermediate-exhaustive, and conditional question alternatives are non-disjoint or non-exhaustive and so are not in the image. Mirrors mathlib's `Set.toFinset` / `Filter.principal` / `UniformSpace.toTopologicalSpace` "less general inside more general" pattern. `Core/Mood.lean` re-export hub updated.

## [0.229.926] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Kratzer1977.lean`** ŌĆö Phenomenon-layer integration test for `Core/IntensionalLogic/Premise.lean`. Formalizes the New Zealand judgments scenario from ┬¦1.3 of @cite{kratzer-1977} (= Chapter 1 of @cite{kratzer-2012}), the canonical worked example motivating Kratzer's revision from Defs 5ŌĆō6 (necessity = consequence; possibility = compatibility) to Defs 7ŌĆō8 (quantified over consistent sublists). A 4-world `Fin 4` model encodes the two contingent dimensions (`p` = murder is a crime, `q` = deer responsible); the inconsistent premise set `A = [p, q, ┬¼q]` represents *what the New Zealand judgments provide*. Sentence-by-sentence theorems (`sentence_7_must_p`, `sentence_8_not_must_negP`, `sentence_9_can_q`, `sentence_10_can_negQ`, `sentence_14_not_can_negP`) verify each prediction by `decide`. Two contrast theorems (`def5_vs_def7_negP`, `def6_vs_def8_q`) pin the bug-vs-fix asymmetry: the unrevised `mustInView` trivializes by *ex falso quodlibet* (entails `┬¼p`), and `canInView` rejects `q` (no world satisfies `A Ōł¬ {q}`) ŌĆö exactly where the revised `mustInView'` and `canInView'` recover the intuitive predictions. Namespace `Phenomena.Modality.Studies.Kratzer1977`. Wired into `Linglib.lean`. Build clean.

## [0.229.925] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Mood/InquisitiveContent.lean` ŌĆö bundled `InquisitiveContent W` per @cite{theiler-etal-2018} Definition 1.** Stage 1 of the InquisitiveContent line of work prescribed by the @cite{puncochar-2016}/@cite{puncochar-2019}/@cite{ciardelli-groenendijk-roelofsen-2018} architectural note in `POSWQ.lean` (added 0.229.922). The structure carries `props : Set (Set W)` (the propositions resolving the issue) plus the two defining constraints ŌĆö `contains_empty` (non-emptiness, normalized as `Ōłģ Ōłł props` so it composes with downward closure) and `downward_closed` (any subset of a resolving proposition also resolves). Six predicates/accessors: `alt` (maximal propositions = alternatives, @cite{theiler-etal-2018} Def 2), `info` (informative content as `ŌŗāŌéĆ P.props`, mathlib-native `Set W`), `trueAt`, `isInformative`, `isInquisitive`, `isDeclarative`. Two Figure 2 constructors: `declarative p` (the principal ideal `{q | q ŌŖå p}` ŌĆö single alternative `p`, the meaning of *Amy left*) and `polar p` (alternatives are `p` and `pßČ£` ŌĆö the meaning of *Did Amy leave?*, non-informative). Six theorems aligned to Figure 2: `info_declarative`, `isDeclarative_declarative`, `not_isInquisitive_declarative`, `info_polar`, `not_isInformative_polar`, and `isInquisitive_polar_iff` (polar question is inquisitive iff the proposition is non-trivial ŌĆö the trivial cases collapse to declaratives). The algebraic characterization from @cite{puncochar-2019} (declarative propositions = principal ideals in the algebra of information states) is exposed directly as `isDeclarative`. Sibling structure to `Setoid W` per the POSWQ architectural note ŌĆö does **not** replace `POSWQ.inquiry`; the `Setoid ŌåÆ InquisitiveContent` embedding (Stage 2) is forthcoming in `Core/Mood/PartitionAsInquiry.lean`. `Core/Mood.lean` re-export hub updated; build clean.

## [0.229.924] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Caha containment relocated to the Morphosyntax interface layer.** `Theories/Morphology/{Case,Degree}Containment.lean` ŌåÆ `Theories/Interfaces/Morphosyntax/{Case,Degree}Containment.lean` (new directory, parallel to the existing `Interfaces/{PhonologyMorphology,SyntaxPhonology,SyntaxSemantics,...}/`). The architectural rationale: containment is *exactly* the syntax-morphology interface ŌĆö the fseq is syntactic but the empirical signature (*ABA in syncretism / suppletion) is morphological, and that's the whole point of @cite{caha-2009}'s Nanosyntax. Previously buried in `Morphology/`, where syntactic case consumers wouldn't naturally look. Namespace `Morphology.{Case,Degree}Containment` ŌåÆ `Interfaces.Morphosyntax.{Case,Degree}Containment` throughout. Generic `Morphology/Containment.lean` (`*ABA` pattern detector on `List Nat` ŌĆö pure list combinatorics) and the `Morphology/Nanosyntax/` subdirectory (Nanosyntax explicitly rejects the syntax/morphology split, so it stays in Morphology to preserve that theoretical commitment) both **left in place**. 12 consumer files updated: `Linglib.lean` import path; `Theories/Morphology/Nanosyntax/Core.lean` import + docstring backticks; `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean`, `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Bubnov2026.lean`, `Phenomena/Allomorphy/Studies/Aitha2026.lean`, `Phenomena/Comparison/Studies/Bobaljik2012.lean` (qualified-name calls and opens); `Fragments/{German,Tamil,Finnish,Telugu,Latin}/Case.lean`, `Fragments/Latin/Adjectives.lean`, `Fragments/English/Modifiers/Adjectives.lean` (imports + opens). Build clean (2739 jobs across the 14 directly-affected targets). Sets up Phase 1 of the case-API mathlib reorganization: a `PartialOrder Core.Case` instance + cross-Fragment ABA validation will follow as a separate commit on top of this relocation.

## [0.229.923] - 2026-04-18

- **`Phenomena/Modality/Studies/Kratzer1981.lean` renamed ŌåÆ `YingEtAl2025.lean`** (`git mv`, history preserved). The file's primary contribution is the bridge from the English modal fragment to @cite{ying-zhi-xuan-wong-mansinghka-tenenbaum-2025}'s LaBToM threshold semantics ("Understanding Epistemic Language with a Language-Augmented Bayesian Theory of Mind", TACL 13:613ŌĆō637) ŌĆö Kratzer 1981's force/flavor framework is referenced but not the paper being formalized. Namespace updated to follow the `Phenomena.{Phenomenon}.Studies.{AuthorYear}` convention: `Phenomena.Modality.EpistemicThresholdBridge` ŌåÆ `Phenomena.Modality.Studies.YingEtAl2025`. Module docstring leads with the correct `@cite{ying-zhi-xuan-wong-mansinghka-tenenbaum-2025}` and dependency-direction diagram updated. `Linglib.lean` import path updated; no other callers.

## [0.229.922] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Two Pun─Źoch├Ī┼Ö bib entries + architectural-direction note in `Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`.** (1) `puncochar-2016` ("A Generalization of Inquisitive Semantics", *Journal of Philosophical Logic* 45(4): 399ŌĆō428, doi 10.1007/s10992-015-9379-1) and (2) `puncochar-2019` ("Substructural Inquisitive Logics", *The Review of Symbolic Logic* 12(2): 296ŌĆō330, doi 10.1017/S1755020319000017) added to `references.bib` with verified DOIs and pages (verified against the actual PDFs). Bibliography regenerated (1563 entries). (3) New "Architectural note: Setoid vs. InquisitiveContent" section in the `POSWQ.lean` file docstring laying out the design choice: we commit `inquiry : Setoid W` (partition-based) and document the algebraic generalization to `InquisitiveContent W` (downward-closed nonempty sets of information states) as the state-of-the-art alternative ŌĆö citing @cite{puncochar-2016} for the lattice-of-logics characterization, @cite{puncochar-2019} for the substructural information-model frame (declarative propositions = principal ideals), and @cite{ciardelli-groenendijk-roelofsen-2018} for the standard inquisitive-semantics presentation. The note explicitly defers the lift, identifies @cite{theiler-etal-2018} (uniform semantics for declarative + interrogative complements; derives mention-some and intermediate-exhaustive readings that partition theory provably cannot) as the forcing study that would trigger building `InquisitiveContent W`, and prescribes the **sibling-structure** architecture (parallel to Setoid with a faithful embedding) following mathlib's `Set`/`Finset` and `Filter`/`Ultrafilter` precedent rather than collapsing the two.

## [0.229.921] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Kratzer/Lumping.lean`.** First Kratzer-2012-grounded module in the new `Conditionals/Kratzer/` directory. Sits on top of `Core.IntensionalLogic.Situations` (the `SituationFrame` carrier with `[PartialOrder Index]`). Defines the situation-semantics logical relations (`IsTrue`, `IsValid`, `IsConsistent`, `IsCompatible`, `Follows`, `Equiv` ŌĆö Kratzer 2012 ┬¦5.3.3, p. 118) and the lumping relation `Lumps p q w` per the official definition (p. 118): `p w Ōł¦ ŌłĆ s, s Ōēż w ŌåÆ p s ŌåÆ q s`. Basic lemmas: `Lumps.true_left`/`true_right`/`local_impl`/`self`/`trans`/`and`/`of_stronger`/`weaken`/`of_universal`. PWS-reduction theorem `lumps_discrete` shows that under the discrete order from `Frame.toDiscreteSituationFrame`, lumping collapses to joint truth (`Lumps p q w Ōåö p w Ōł¦ q w`). Counterfactual machinery of ┬¦5.4.4 (admissible Base Sets, Crucial Set, would/might) deferred to a forthcoming sibling file.

### Changed
- **`Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Restrictor.lean` polymorphized.** Generalized from hardcoded `World` to `{W : Type*} [DecidableEq W] [Fintype W]`; consequents migrated from `BProp World` (Bool) to `W ŌåÆ Prop`, matching the upstream Prop-based API in `Modality/Kratzer/Operators.lean` (`necessity`/`possibility`/`K_axiom`/`duality`). Antecedents stay `W ŌåÆ Bool` because they are consed onto the modal base (`List (W ŌåÆ Bool)`) by `restrictedBase` ŌĆö the enumerable factual content stays computable. Removed import of `Theories.Semantics.Attitudes.Intensional`. Two `omit [...] in` clauses added (`kratzer_material_eq_conditional`, `double_restriction`) to silence unused-section-var warnings on theorems that don't need the Fintype/DecidableEq constraints.

## [0.229.920] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Nominal.ArticleInventory` BoolŌåÆProp migration.** The six morphological-presence fields (`hasIndefinite`, `hasUniqueArticle`, `hasAnaphoricArticle`, `uniqueAnaphoricSyncretism`, `hasDemonstrative`, `hasPossessive`) migrated from `Bool` to `Prop` with paired `[Decidable ŌĆ”]` instance fields and an `attribute [instance]` declaration to register them globally ŌĆö aligns the type with mathlib's Prop-with-Decidable convention for propositional positions (see `feedback_bool_migration_scope.md`). `licensesKind` now returns `Prop`; its decidability is derived structurally via `@instDecidableOr`/`@instDecidableAnd` over the field instances. **Parser-conflict workaround**: `open Core.IntensionalLogic.Variables` (which scopes the `g[n Ōå” x]` notation) now appears *after* the structure declaration ŌĆö when in scope, the `[`-prefixed notation token clashes with structure instance-field syntax `[name : Type]`. Five fragment files (`Fragments/{English,German,Thai,Mandarin,Shan}/Definiteness.lean`) updated `:= true/false` ŌåÆ `:= True/False`. Downstream theorem updates: `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Schwarz2009.lean` (┬¦4 `german_two_articles`/`english_syncretic_articles` use direct Prop conjuncts + `Ō¤©trivial, trivial, idŌ¤®` / `Ō¤©trivial, trivial, trivialŌ¤®`); `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` (`blocking_strategy_correspondence`, the five `licensesKind` predictions for shan/english/german, plus `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct` rewritten with `congrArg ArticleInventory.hasUniqueArticle h` to derive `True = False`). `deriving Repr, DecidableEq` dropped from `ArticleInventory` (Prop fields don't admit them); cascading `deriving` removals on `Phenomena/Anaphora/Typology.lean::PronounSystemDatum` (Repr, BEq) and `Phenomena/Anaphora/DonkeyAnaphora.lean::DonkeyArticleDatum` (Repr).

## [0.229.919] - 2026-04-18

### Fixed
- **Post-relocation wiring fixes for `Fragments/Mongolian/Case.lean` and `Phenomena/Anaphora/Studies/Gong2022.lean`.** Discovered during a clean full-library build after the 0.229.918 bulk commit. Both files used `CaseSystemConfig`/`NPInDomain`/`dependentAccusative` transitively through `LateMerger.lean`, which previously re-exported them via its old `import DependentCase`; the relocation broke that chain. Fix: explicit `import Linglib.Theories.Syntax.Case.Dependent` on Mongolian (which uses `CaseSystemConfig`), and `open Syntax.Case` on both files (alongside the existing `open Minimalism` they need for `LateMerger`/`ChainPosition`).

### Documented (retroactive ŌĆö bundled into 0.229.918)
- **`CaseVal` (Minimalism's 7-value enum) eliminated; unified onto `Core.Case` (Blake's 19-value typological inventory).** Both types contained the same NOM/ACC/GEN/DAT/ERG/ABS constructors, so the Minimalism layer's redundant `CaseVal` was a separate-stipulation-then-bridge anti-pattern. `Core.Case` is now the single canonical case type across the library. Touched: `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/{Features,CaseFilter,Modification}.lean` (`FeatureVal.case : CaseVal ŌåÆ ...` ŌåÆ `Core.Case ŌåÆ ...`; `DPFeatures.withCase`; `MAGFeatureType.kappa`); `Fragments/Mayan/{Chol,Qanjobal,Kaqchikel}/Agreement.lean` (Kaqchikel also drops `.toCase` indirection now that the types coincide); `Phenomena/Case/Studies/{Marantz1991,Aissen2003,Woolford1997}.lean` (`getCase!`/`georgianSpellout` retypings; `WCase.toCaseVal` ŌåÆ `WCase.toCoreCase`); `Phenomena/Case/{DependentCaseDerivations,Compare}.lean` (`CaseVal.{erg,abs,nom}.toCase` ŌåÆ `Core.Case.{erg,abs,nom}`; `np.case.toCase` ŌåÆ `np.case`); `Phenomena/Ergativity/Studies/{CoonMateoPedroPreminger2014,Imanishi2020}.lean` (`AbstractCase.toCaseVal` ŌåÆ `AbstractCase.toCoreCase`; `caseVal_bridge_*` ŌåÆ `coreCase_bridge_*`).
- **`DependentCase.lean` relocated out of `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/` into `Theories/Syntax/Case/Dependent.lean`.** Dependent case (Marantz/Baker) is a *competing framework* to Agree-based case, not a Minimalist component; the file location now reflects the theoretical commitment. Namespace `Minimalism` ŌåÆ `Syntax.Case`. Import in `Linglib.lean` updated; importers (`Phenomena/Case/Studies/{BakerVinokurova2010,Marantz1991,Aissen2003,Woolford1997,Ozaki2026}.lean`, `Phenomena/Case/{Compare,DependentCaseDerivations}.lean`, `Fragments/Yakut/Case.lean`) swapped to `import Linglib.Theories.Syntax.Case.Dependent` + `open Syntax.Case` (added alongside `open Minimalism` for files that also use Voice/CaseFilter). `LateMerger.lean`'s decorative `import DependentCase` replaced with the actually-needed `import ...Minimalism.Core.Basic`.

## [0.229.918] - 2026-04-18

- **Bulk concurrent-session WIP committed.** ~170 files across `Core/`, `Fragments/`, `Phenomena/`, `Theories/` plus blog bibliography. Changes were the accumulated working state from concurrent sessions and are bundled here without per-area decomposition; reconstruct context from `git log --follow` or `git diff HEAD~1` per file as needed. Notable additions visible in the file list: `Core/Deixis/`, `Core/NullSubject/`, `Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory|Description|Interpret`, `Core/IntensionalLogic/{Premise,Situations}`, `Fragments/{English,German,Mandarin,Thai}/Definiteness`, `Fragments/Ga/`, `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013`, `Phenomena/Control/Studies/Allotey2021`, `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Schwarz2009`, `Phenomena/PhonologicalAlternation/Studies/Belth2026`, `Phenomena/Pronouns/ProDropRegistry`, `Theories/Learning/`, `Theories/Phonology/Alternation`, `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/LongDistanceAgree`.

## [0.229.917] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood three-lattice unification ŌĆö `+`, `Ōŗå`, `?` are `inf` in three parallel mathlib lattices.** The deep mathematical observation: each of the three POSW(Q) components (`cs : W ŌåÆ Prop`, `le : W ŌåÆ W ŌåÆ Prop`, `inquiry : Setoid W`) carries an already-mathlib'd lattice, and each of the three updates is meet in its component's lattice. None of this appears in @cite{portner-2018} (which writes (2a) as set intersection and (2b) via Condoravdi-Lauer composition); it is a linglib extension that puts the algebraic story on a uniform footing. (1) **`plus_cs_eq_inf`** (`Core/Mood/POSW.lean` ┬¦3, definitional `@[simp]`): `(c.plus p).cs = c.cs ŌŖō p` ŌĆö `+`-update is meet in `W ŌåÆ Prop` (instance chain `Prop.instDistribLattice` ŌåÆ `Pi.instLattice`). (2) **`star_le_eq_inf`** (POSW ┬¦6, definitional `@[simp]`): `(c.star p).le = c.le ŌŖō promote p` ŌĆö `Ōŗå`-update is meet in `W ŌåÆ W ŌåÆ Prop`. (3) **`inquire_inquiry_eq_inf`** (POSWQ ┬¦2, definitional `@[simp]`, parallel-named): `(c.inquire q).inquiry = c.inquiry ŌŖō q` ŌĆö `?`-update is meet in `Setoid.completeLattice W`, which gives `iInf` over arbitrary index sets for free (so "asking the conjunction of a family of questions" reduces to a one-line `iInf` consequence). (4) **`star_star_comm_le` and `star_star_self_le` refactored from `Iff` (pointwise, with explicit `simp only` + term proof) to full `Eq` (relation level, one-liner via `inf_right_comm` and `inf_assoc + inf_idem`).** The new statements are strictly stronger (whole relation equality instead of pointwise iff) and the proofs are kernel-trivial ŌĆö both reductions are exactly the lattice laws on `Pi.instLattice` over `Prop`. No downstream consumers (these were added in 0.229.916). (5) **┬¦6 docstring rewritten** to frame `Ōŗå`-update as the relation-side analogue of `+`-update and to credit `Pi.instLattice` for inheritance of `inf_comm`/`inf_assoc`/`inf_idem`. (6) **┬¦8 NormalModality docstring updated**: the K-axiom for `boxCs`/`boxLe` is now described as the universal-quantification-preserves-inf shape over `Pi.instLattice` on `Prop` ŌĆö the same lattice underlying `+` and `Ōŗå`. (7) **POSW + POSWQ file docstrings** include the three-lattice table inline so the unification is discoverable from the top of each file. Imports added: `Mathlib.Order.PropInstances`, `Mathlib.Order.Lattice` to POSW.lean. Build clean (1119 jobs across POSW + POSWQ + POSWTarget + VerbalMood + Scoreboard + Grano2024).

## [0.229.916] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood mathlib polish ŌĆö strongest mathematical foundation across `Core/Mood/` + `Theories/Semantics/Mood/`.** (1) **`POSW.lt` ŌåÆ `POSW.le` rename** throughout `Core/Mood/POSW.lean`, `Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`, `Core/Mood/POSWTarget.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean`, `Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`, and `Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/Grano2024.lean` (docstrings only) ŌĆö aligns with mathlib's `Preorder` / `Ōēż` convention; old `boxLt` exposed as `@[deprecated] abbrev` for one cycle. (2) **Subtype `Preorder` instance** on `{w // c.cs w}` derived from POSW reflexivity/transitivity ŌĆö gives ordered-set machinery (Antitone/Monotone/`Ōēż`-rewriting) for free on the in-CS subtype, replacing per-call destructuring. (3) **`promote` preorder + star algebra**: `promote p w v := p v ŌåÆ p w` is the preorder content of the `Ōŗå`-update, and `star_le_eq` exposes `(c.star p).le w v Ōåö c.le w v Ōł¦ promote p w v` as a `@[simp]` lemma. Falling-out theorems `star_star_comm_le` and `star_star_self_le` follow as one-line term proofs from this characterization (commutativity and idempotence of the directive update at the order layer). (4) **Farkas fixed-point**: `le_plus_iff_boxCs : c Ōēż c.plus p Ōåö c.boxCs p` ŌĆö the `+`-update is a no-op (under POSW's preorder) iff the proposition is already CS-entailed. The pointwise type-level shadow of @cite{farkas-2003} eq. 11. (5) **`NormalModality` typeclass** (`Core/Mood/POSW.lean`) packages necessitation + K-axiom; instances for `boxCs` and `boxLe` (both inherit normality from the underlying universal quantifier over a domain). `boxAns` is *not* a normal modality ŌĆö its K-axiom fails when the antecedent is sometimes-false on a partition cell (concrete counterexample: W = {a,b,c}, inquiry {{a,b},{c}}, p false on {a,b}, q non-constant). Documented in the `boxAns` docstring; `boxAns` retains its boolean-closure properties separately. (6) **Indicative/Subjunctive Principles as biconditionals**: `gram_mood_target_informational_iff_indicative` and `gram_mood_target_preferential_iff_subjunctive` (POSWTarget.lean) plus the three `verbal_mood_target_*_iff_*` analogues (VerbalMood.lean) state the strongest type-level form of @cite{portner-2018}'s principles available without a syntax-side mood-features infrastructure ŌĆö `GramMood`/`VerbalMoodOp` are in bijection with the POSW components they target, so the conditionals collapse to biconditionals. The new ┬¦3 docstring in POSWTarget.lean situates this against @cite{farkas-2003}'s update-type alternative. `gram_mood_target_ne_partition` records the complementary closure: verbal mood never targets the partition component (the IllocutionaryMood/.interrogative path). (7) **POSWQ ┬¦7ŌåÆ┬¦8 header fix** (the duplicate "Distinctness Witness" was previously the second ┬¦7). (8) **Footnote-3 docstring caveat** added to `POSW.plus`: noting @cite{portner-2018} Ch. 4 fn. 3's observation that `c + p` is technically not a POSW (its `le` is not restricted to the new `cs`) ŌĆö kept as a documentation point rather than a structural change since the existing `le_refl`/`le_trans` proofs only quantify over CS members. Build clean (5139 jobs).
- **`POSW`/`POSWQ` `Preorder` instances anonymized**: dropped the explicit `instance instPreorder` / `instance instPreorderSubtype` names ŌĆö Lean autogenerates a unique name and these instances are only ever resolved by typeclass inference. Cleaner mathlib-style declaration.

## [0.229.915] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Bundled Pesetsky-followups (Plans A + B).** (A) `Theories/Morphology/CaseContainment.lean` ŌĆö all 21 `native_decide` invocations replaced with `decide` per the project's mathlib-style preference for kernel-checkable proofs over native compilation; build times unaffected (~9.2s). (B) **`CaseVal.obl` constructor removed from `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/Features.lean`.** The constructor existed only as a Minimalism-internal placeholder and forced an `obl ŌåÆ dat` kludge in `CaseVal.toCase` (the @cite{blake-1994} typology has no separate oblique slot). The four prior consumers ŌĆö two placeholder probes in `CaseFilter.lean` (`dpNeedsCase`, `DPFeatures.withUnvaluedCase`), one `.kappa .obl` agreement entry in `Fragments/Russian/AdjAgreement.lean`, one sentinel in `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Marantz1991.lean`'s `getCase!` helper, and a sentinel-comparison in `Phenomena/Case/Compare.lean` ŌĆö were all migrated: the placeholders use `.dat`, the agreement entry was dropped (it was sloppy anyway ŌĆö `.abl`/`.obl` aren't Russian cases), and `dependent_case_always_assigns` was rewritten to check `.length` (a more meaningful property than the sentinel sniff). `CaseVal.toCase` is now a clean injection with no kludge, plus a new `CaseVal.toCase_injective` theorem.

### Architecture
- **Researched 2024ŌĆō26 reception of @cite{pesetsky-2013}** before deciding on the placement of `POSCat`. Verdict (cited but not central; dependent-case dominant; no recent handbook centers it; treated as one alternative among several): **POSCat (the four POS=case primitives N/D/V/P) is paper-specific, kept local to `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean`**, not promoted to a shared `Core` or `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Core/` module. Centralizing Pesetsky's identification would impose a controversial minority commitment on every downstream case formalization.
- **`Fragments/Russian/Case.lean` reverted to theory-neutral form** ŌĆö just the 6-case inventory + Blake validity. The Pesetsky-specific `pesetskyCore`, `pesetskyCore_subset_inventory`, and `inst_loc_outside_pesetsky` theorems migrated to `Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean` (renamed `pesetskyCore_subset_russian_inventory` to keep the cross-file relationship visible). `POSCase` renamed to `POSCat` throughout. Fragments are descriptive infrastructure; theory-specific reductions belong in study files.

## [0.229.914] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Definiteness API Stage 10 ŌĆö `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/CoppockBeaver2015.lean` and `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Hanink2021.lean` as integration tests for the `Core/Nominal/` substrate.** With Stages 1ŌĆō9 in place (Frame substrate + `NominalKind` sum + `Maximality` operators + `interpret` + `ArticleInventory`), the two foundational papers behind that substrate now have explicit study files exercising the relevant predicates end-to-end. (1) **`CoppockBeaver2015.lean`** builds a tiny three-entity frame (sun / moon / mars / Unit index) with three restrictors ŌĆö `theSun` (singleton), `planet` (multi-witness), `kingOfFrance` (empty) ŌĆö and proves the @cite{coppock-beaver-2015} factorization claims as kernel theorems: `kingOfFrance_uniqueness_without_existence` (Uniqueness vacuous, Existence false), `planet_existence_without_uniqueness`, `theSun_existence_and_uniqueness`. The Russellian collapse (`existsUnique = Existence Ōł¦ Uniqueness`) is then read off as `theSun_existsUnique`, `kingOfFrance_not_existsUnique`, `planet_not_existsUnique`, with each failure attributed to the *specific* missing conjunct. The interpretation alignment theorems (`interpret_theSun = some sun`, `interpret_kingOfFrance = none`, `interpret_planet = none`) confirm that `Core.Nominal.interpret` on `.unique` collapses both presupposition failures to `none` while the ┬¦2 diagnostics retain the structural distinction. The negation-projection contrast (`uniqueness_projects_through_negation` vs. `existence_does_not_project_through_negation`) is the empirical payoff. (2) **`Hanink2021.lean`** builds a two-entity, two-index frame (`tableKitchen`/`tableLiving` ├Ś `kitchen`/`living`) with a restrictor `tableAtSit0` that consults `interpSitPronoun 0 gs` to fetch the resource situation. The headline theorem `same_description_different_referents` shows that the *same* `.unique tableAtSit0 0` description picks out `tableKitchen` under `gsKitchen` and `tableLiving` under `gsLiving` ŌĆö operationalizing @cite{hanink-2021}'s claim that the resource situation is a bound variable in the structure, not a free contextual parameter. The orthogonality of entity-binding and situation-binding is verified via `situation_binders_classified` (only `.unique` and `.demonstrative` flag `usesSituationPronoun`) and `anaphoric_independent_of_situation` (anaphoric reads from `g`, not `gs` ŌĆö the Schwarz weak/strong split mirrored at the substrate). `unique_index_does_not_alter_referent_directly` records that the index argument to `.unique` is structural bookkeeping (which pronoun is bound), not a selector ŌĆö the restrictor's call to `interpSitPronoun sIdx` does the work. (3) **bib entries**: `coppock-beaver-2015` (Linguistics and Philosophy 38(5):377ŌĆō435, doi 10.1007/s10988-015-9178-8) and `hanink-2021` (Natural Language & Linguistic Theory 39(2):505ŌĆō554, doi 10.1007/s11049-020-09483-x) added to `references.bib` ŌĆö both keys were already used in `Core/Nominal/{Description,Maximality,Interpret}.lean` docstrings. Bibliography regenerated. Build clean (364 jobs each).

## [0.229.913] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Definiteness API Stage 9 ŌĆö collapse the redundant `markingParams`/`strategy`/`articleType` shim layer in `Fragments.Shan.Definiteness`.** With `Core.Nominal.ArticleInventory` as the single upstream source of truth (see Stages 6ŌĆō8), Shan's `markingParams : DefMarkingParams` (boolean triple), `strategy : deriveStrategy markingParams = .unmarked`, `articleType : strategyToArticleType (...) = .none_`, and `articleInventory_params_agreement : articleInventory.toMarkingParams = markingParams` were all `rfl`-projections of the same underlying `articleInventory`. They are now removed; `articleInventory.toMarkingParams` and `articleInventory.toMarkingStrategy` are the canonical accesses, and `articleInventory_marking : articleInventory.toMarkingStrategy = .unmarked` is the kept summary theorem. Shan was the only fragment with the redundant shim layer (English/German/Mandarin/Thai are already grounded solely on `articleInventory`). **`Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` ┬¦7** is correspondingly updated: `englishParams`/`germanParams`/`thaiParams`/`shanParams` are now defined as `Fragments.{Lang}.Definiteness.articleInventory.toMarkingParams` projections rather than stipulated triples ŌĆö the fragment-level inventories drive the Moroney typology directly. The old `inventory_agrees_with_params` theorem (now `rfl`-tautological since the params are the inventory projections) is deleted; `derive_all_languages`, `derive_consistent_with_stipulated`, and `blocking_strategy_correspondence` all continue to close by `rfl` since they only inspect the boolean fields. Build clean (988 jobs across 8 root targets).

## [0.229.912] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Pronouns/ProDropRegistry.lean` ŌĆö WALS F101A bridge.** Bridges @cite{dryer-2013-wals}'s "Expression of Pronominal Subjects" classification (711 languages) into the registry's `ProDropProfile` view. (1) `F101A.toAllowsProDrop : ExpressionOfPronominalSubjects ŌåÆ Option Bool` ŌĆö the standard typological reading: `obligatoryPronounsInSubjectPosition ŌåÆ some false`, four affix/clitic/optional/displaced strategies ŌåÆ `some true`, `mixed ŌåÆ none`. WALS does not record overt PRO, so the bridge is the `allowsProDrop`-only projection ŌĆö `hasOvertPRO` cannot be derived from F101A alone. (2) `F101A.allowsProDropOf (walsCode : String) : Option Bool` looks up F101A.allData by WALS code and projects via `toAllowsProDrop`. (3) `walsCodeOf : Language ŌåÆ Option String` records F101A coverage of registry languages ŌĆö English (`"eng"`) and G├Ż (`"ga"`) are in WALS; SMPM and B├╝li are not. (4) **Coverage theorems**: `english_agrees_with_F101A` (WALS `obligatoryPronounsInSubjectPosition` ŌćÆ `allowsProDrop = false`, matches Ostrove's English profile); **`ga_disagrees_with_F101A`** (WALS classifies the G├Ż subject proclitic as `subjectAffixesOnVerb` ŌåÆ pro-drop under the standard reading, while @cite{allotey-2021} treats it as an overt pronominal clitic that fills subject position ŌåÆ non-pro-drop, overt PRO ŌĆö the disagreement is surfaced as a named theorem rather than papered over); `walsCovered_languages` (the F101A-covered subset is exactly `{english, ga}`). The G├Ż case is the kind of substantive theory-vs-typological-data tension the project aims to make visible.

### Changed
- **Typology constructors renamed to mathlib camelCase** (`nullPRO_noProDrop` ŌåÆ `nullPRONoProDrop`, `nullPRO_proDrop` ŌåÆ `nullPROProDrop`, `overtPRO_noProDrop` ŌåÆ `overtPRONoProDrop`, `overtPRO_proDrop` ŌåÆ `overtPROProDrop`). Three references updated in `Ostrove2026.lean` (`smpm_classified`, `english_classified`, `buli_classified`); five references updated in `ProDropRegistry.lean` (`forbidden_cell_empty`, `cellCount_overtPROProDrop_eq_zero`, plus three docstring mentions). Consistent with the renaming done in `Core/NullSubject/Basic.lean` in 0.229.911.

### Fixed
- **Misplaced WALS bridge file deleted.** `Linglib/Core/NullSubject/WALS.lean` (created during the F101A integration plan) was placed in `Core/`, which inverts the dependency hierarchy: WALS-to-typology bridges live in `Phenomena/`, alongside the existing pattern in `Phenomena/Pronouns/Typology.lean` (which imports F39A-F48A). The bridge is now in `Phenomena/Pronouns/ProDropRegistry.lean`, where the per-language registry profiles already live.

## [0.229.911] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood-arc cleanup PR (Stage 9, follow-up to PR J).** (1) Citation hallucinations corrected in `POSW.lean`, `POSWQ.lean`, `VerbalMood.lean`, `POSWTarget.lean`: removed unverified equation references, attributed substrate components to `@cite{farkas-2003}`, `@cite{condoravdi-lauer-2012}`, `@cite{stalnaker-1978}`. (2) `POSWQ Ōåö POSWTarget` overlap resolved via cross-link in module docstrings plus a typed projection `POSWTarget.boxOn : POSWTarget ŌåÆ POSWQ W ŌåÆ (W ŌåÆ Prop) ŌåÆ Prop` that delegates to `boxCs`/`boxLt`/`boxAns` ŌĆö the typeclass is now operationally meaningful, not just a label. `mood_alignment_decl_ind` renamed to `decl_ind_target_match` with a `@[deprecated]` alias; section docstring rewritten to clarify the Indicative Principle is a one-way conditional, not a target-equality. (3) `QuestionEmbedder` enum (`.factive | .nonfactive`) added in `VerbalMood.lean` with `toVerbalMood := fun _ => .interrogative`, closing the `.interrogative`-unreachable gap left by `MoodSelector` and pair-covering the verbal-mood operator space. (4) Closure theorems `boxAns_or` and `boxAns_imp` added to POSWQ. (5) `polarSetoid` moved from `Scoreboard.lean` (where it was a local definition) to `POSWQ.lean` (where it belongs as the inquiry-component constructor); added `polarSetoid_r`/`polarSetoid_top` simp lemmas; generalized via `Setoid.ofProp`. (6) Wired one downstream consumer: `Grano2024.lean` now exercises `want_verbalMood`/`hope_verbalMood`/`want_target` via `native_decide` at a complement-selection site. New `interp_eq_target_boxOn` on `VerbalMood` proves verbal-mood interpretation factors through `POSWTarget.boxOn`. New ┬¦7 in POSWQ adds `POSWQ.plus`/`POSWQ.star` lifted operations with pairwise commutativity proofs.

### Fixed
- Completed concurrent session's `Assignment` alias migration in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` (the `abbrev Assignment (F : Frame) := Core.Assignment F.Entity` wrapper was removed upstream, leaving 11 downstream call sites with `Unknown identifier Assignment` errors). Migrated all `Assignment F` usages to `Core.Assignment F.Entity` in `Composition/Tree.lean`, `Composition/Effects.lean`, `Composition/QuantifierComposition.lean`, `Composition/Glue.lean`, `Interfaces/SyntaxSemantics/VoiceSemantics.lean`, `Interfaces/SyntaxSemantics/Minimalism/Interface.lean`, `Reference/Binding.lean` (also `Assignment.update` ŌåÆ `Core.Assignment.update`), `Anaphora/Studies/Charlow2018.lean`, `Focus/Studies/Rooth1992.lean`, `Generics/Studies/Guerrini2026.lean`, `Syntax/Minimalism/RelativeClauses.lean`. Build clean.

## [0.229.910] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Case/Studies/Pesetsky2013.lean` ŌĆö formalization of the POS-as-case thesis from @cite{pesetsky-2013}.** Pesetsky's central reduction (NOM=D, ACC=V, GEN=N, OBL=P) modeled as a four-element `POSCase` enum with a total bridge `POSCase.toCase : POSCase ŌåÆ Core.Case` (image is exactly the Russian core four NOM/ACC/GEN/DAT). (1) **The FA rule** (Feature Assignment, Ch. 8 final form) modeled as `assignDown` ŌĆö a mutual recursion over `PesTree` (leaves carry case stacks, internal nodes carry a POS label) that prepends the assigner's category to every dominated leaf's stack. (2) **The One-Suffix Rule** (Ch. 3) is a one-line filter `oneSuffix : List POSCase ŌåÆ Option POSCase` that retains only the outermost stratum; Lardil (@cite{richards-2013}) is the same grammar without the filter (`lardilSpellOut := id`). The cross-linguistic divergence is a kernel theorem: `lardilSpellOut (a :: b :: rest) ŌēĀ (oneSuffix _).toList`. (3) **Every noun is "born genitive."** Modeled as `bornGenitive w := .leaf w [.N]`. The Russian/Lardil contrast is shown by two parallel theorems on a single derivation: `oneSuffix (assignDown .V (bornGenitive w)).stack = some .V` (Russian masks the inner N) vs. `lardilSpellOut (assignDown .V (bornGenitive w)).stack = [.V, .N]` (Lardil shows both). (4) **Russian paucals (Ch. 4ŌĆō6).** `RusNumClass` (paucal/higher/quant) with lexical entries for *dva*, *tri*, *─Źetyre*, *pjat'*, *┼Īest'*, *mnogo*, *skol'ko*. Pesetsky's unifying claim ŌĆö *all three classes assign N (genitive) to their complement noun* ŌĆö is the kernel theorem `numerals_all_assign_N`, with per-numeral surface-case derivations (`dva_stola_surface_gen`, `pjat'_stolov_surface_gen`, `mnogo_studentov_surface_gen`) all yielding `some .N` under One-Suffix. The double-N stack (`paucal_stack_double_N : (applyNumeral dva "stol").stack = [.N, .N]`) makes explicit that paucal-induced GEN is *additional* to the universal born-genitive N. (5) **Subject/object derivations** (`subject_surface_nom`, `object_surface_acc`) instantiate the POS=case thesis end-to-end: bare noun ŌåÆ outer assigner (D for subject, V for object) ŌåÆ One-Suffix yields surface NOM/ACC. Inner stacks are identical (`subject_object_same_inner`). (6) **Bridges** (┬¦7): `pos_image_in_core` proves the four POSCase values map exactly to gen/nom/acc/dat in `Core.Case`; `caha_containment_inverts_pesetsky` connects to @cite{caha-2009}'s containment hierarchy in `Theories/Morphology/CaseContainment.lean` (Caha's morphological containment rank ascends NOMŌåÆACCŌåÆGEN; Pesetsky's derivational stack starts with N=GEN at the bottom ŌĆö the two views are consistent under the inversion); `vra─Ź_morphological_class_unchanged` ties Pesetsky's feminizing-ą¢ analysis (Ch. 5) into the existing hybrid noun in `Fragments/Russian/Gender.lean` (Class I assignment preserved). (7) **Out-of-scope items documented in the docstring**: full VACC restriction with referent-feature interaction, D-headed PP for POBLŌåöVACC spatial alternations (Ch. 7), Spell-Out / phase locality on adnominals (Ch. 8). (8) **`Fragments/Russian/Case.lean` enriched** with `pesetskyCore : List Core.Case := [POSCase.D, POSCase.V, POSCase.N, POSCase.P].map POSCase.toCase`, plus `pesetskyCore_subset_inventory` (the four POS-derived cases are all in the Russian inventory) and `inst_loc_outside_pesetsky` (INST and LOC are typological elaborations not in Pesetsky's POS=case mapping). (9) **`references.bib` adds `@richards-2013`** (Lardil "Case Stacking" and the Timing of Case Assignment, Syntax 16(1)) ŌĆö DOI omitted pending verification. Build clean (890 jobs).

## [0.229.909] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Stage 8 of the Definiteness API redesign ŌĆö collapse the named-wrapper layer; canonical `russellIotaList` + `presupOfReferent` become the single source of truth.** The denotational layer for definite descriptions is now expressed by composing two canonical pieces: (a) `Core.Nominal.russellIotaList : List E ŌåÆ (E ŌåÆ Bool) ŌåÆ Option E` ŌĆö the per-context Russellian iota over a `List E` filtered by a `Bool` predicate (computable counterpart of `Core.Nominal.russellIota`), with `russellIotaList_eq_some_iff` characterizing definedness as filter-equals-singleton; (b) `Core.Presupposition.PrProp.presupOfReferent : (W ŌåÆ Option E) ŌåÆ (E ŌåÆ W ŌåÆ Bool) ŌåÆ PrProp W` ŌĆö the presupposition+assertion combinator that lifts a partial referent selector and a scope predicate to a `PrProp W`, with `@[simp] presupOfReferent_presup` plus `presupOfReferent_assertion_some/_none` characterizing the projections. (1) **Named wrappers deleted from `Theories/Semantics/Definiteness/Basic.lean`.** `evalDefinite`, `the_uniq`, `the_uniq_w`, `the_fam`, `the_anaphoric`, and the auxiliary equivalence theorems (`the_anaphoric_vacuous_eq_the_uniq`, `familiarity_weaker_existence`, `the_anaphoric_more_restrictive`, `domain_restriction_eq_filter`, `the_uniq_eq_definitePrProp`, `the_uniq_presup_iff_iota`) are gone ŌĆö they were lossy projections of the same underlying composition. The file retains only the abstract glue (`DiscourseContext`, `definite_presup_iff_iota`, `the_is_every_on_singletons`, `qforceToPresupType`, `qforceToDefiniteness`, `definite_is_uniqueness`, `modifierNecessary`). (2) **`Core/Definiteness.lean` ┬¦6 deleted** ŌĆö the `DefiniteDesc` record (with `unique`/`anaphoric` smart constructors and the trivial equality theorem) was a pure repackaging of `russellIotaList` and added no information; consumers compose the canonical pieces directly. Subsequent sections renumbered. (3) **`Theories/Semantics/Reference/Donnellan.lean` factored.** `definitePrProp` is now defined as `presupOfReferent (attributiveContent domain restrictor) predicate`, with the new `definitePrProp_eq_presupOfReferent` and `@[simp] definitePrProp_presup` making the factoring explicit. The Donnellan attributive use is the same canonical machine with a different selector argument. (4) **Six consumers migrated in one wave.** `Fragments/Akan/Determiners.lean` (`noSem` rewritten), `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` (`bareDefinite`/`demDenotation` return `Option E`; new behavior-based `dem_refines_bare`; `liftToPrProp` helper), `Phenomena/Reference/Studies/Elbourne2013.lean` (`the_sit`/`the_sit'` factor through `presupOfReferent`; new `the_sit'_eq_definitePrProp` rfl-bridge to Donnellan; `donkey_uniqueness_from_minimality` and `the_sit_assertion_implies_presup` ported), `Phenomena/SyntacticAmbiguity/Studies/PaapeVasishth2026.lean` (file-local `private def thePresup` replaces 17 `the_uniq` references; simp-arg lists updated to the new lemma names), `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` (`type_shift_referent_agreement` replaces the `_denotation_agreement` form; ┬¦12 `demonstrative_adds_spatial_info` rewritten as a behavior equation against `russellIotaList` ŌĆö the old `presupFilter`-field theorem expressed a record-projection that no longer exists), `Theories/Semantics/Noun/Kind/Dayal2004.lean` (docstring updated). (5) **No bridge-theorem layer.** This refactor follows the "what would mathlib do" principle: there is no thin wrapper retained for ergonomics, and there are no per-paper `the_uniq_eq_X` theorems ŌĆö wherever a paper needs a per-file convenience name (Elbourne's `the_sit'`, Paape & Vasishth's `thePresup`), that name is defined locally as a direct application of `presupOfReferent`. The `Core.Definiteness` typology vocabulary (`DefPresupType`, `DefMarkingStrategy`, etc.) is unchanged. Build clean for the full Definiteness chain (1004 jobs across 8 root targets).

## [0.229.908] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core/NullSubject.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/NullSubject/` subdirectory (Stage A of the 2026-aware refactor).** The single-file `Core/NullSubject.lean` is replaced by a subdirectory of three files, with no top-level `Core/NullSubject.lean` remnant (mathlib idiom, matching `Mathlib/Order/`). (1) **`Core/NullSubject/Basic.lean`** is the moved old content ŌĆö `ProDropProfile`, `Satisfies` (Prop + Decidable), `null_pro_satisfies`, `overt_no_prodrop_satisfies`, `prodrop_excludes_overt_pro`, `Typology`, `Typology.isAttested`, `classify`, `satisfies_iff_attested` ŌĆö preserved verbatim so existing consumers (`Ostrove2026.lean`, `Allotey2021.lean`, `ProDropRegistry.lean`) keep working.
