(2) **`Core/NullSubject/Defs.lean` (new) ŌĆö subject-context vocabulary.** Four orthogonal axes that suffice for the empirical cases 2026-era pro-drop literature engages with: `Person` (aliased to `UD.Person` for cross-linguistic compatibility ŌĆö partial pro-drop in Hebrew/BP/Finnish requires per-person predicates), `Finiteness` (finite/nonfinite ŌĆö distinguishes pro-drop loci from PRO loci), `ClauseRole` (matrix/embeddedFinite/controlSubject ŌĆö distinguishes matrix pro-drop from embedded-pro-drop asymmetries from PRO position), `ABarStatus` (insitu/aBarExtracted ŌĆö anti-agreement effect in Tarifit/Fiorentino/Wolof, @cite{baier-2018}). `SubjectContext` is the structure of the four-way cross-classification; `Exponent` is `null | overt`. Convenience constructors `SubjectContext.matrixFinite`/`embeddedFinite`/`controlled`/`matrixExtracted` cover the canonical 3rd-person in-situ loci that study files reach for. (3) **`Core/NullSubject/Universals.lean` (new) ŌĆö abstract universal interface.** `SubjectAssignment := SubjectContext ŌåÆ Exponent` is the abstract interface a syntactic theory must provide to be evaluated against the universals. `SubjectAssignment.hasOvertPRO`/`allowsProDrop` are 3rd-person-canonical Boolean projections; `SubjectAssignment.Satisfies` (Prop + Decidable) is @cite{ostrove-2026}'s implicational universal stated abstractly. `toProDropProfile : SubjectAssignment ŌåÆ ProDropProfile` bridges to `Basic.lean`'s legacy two-Boolean view; `toProDropProfile_satisfies_iff` proves the bridge is information-preserving for the universal (`Iff.rfl`). (4) **`Linglib.lean`** adds the two new imports next to the existing (auto-resolved) `Linglib.Core.NullSubject.Basic` line. Consumer imports in `Ostrove2026.lean` / `Allotey2021.lean` resolve to `.Basic` (auto-resolved during the move; no semantic change). Build clean (1020 jobs). The Minimalism-side bridge (`MinPronInventory.subjectAssignment` projection + `subjectAssignment_overtPRO_iff` theorem) and per-language assignments (Italian, BP, Finnish, Hebrew, Tarifit) remain for follow-up stages ŌĆö Stage A is the framework-side scaffolding.

## [0.229.907] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Phenomena/Pronouns/ProDropRegistry.lean` ŌĆö unified endpoint for the pro-drop / overt-PRO universal.** Coverage registry for every language formalized against @cite{ostrove-2026}'s implicational universal. (1) **`Language` enum** ŌĆö inductive type `english | smpm | buli | ga` (with `DecidableEq`, `Repr`, `Fintype` derived) replacing the brittle "list of (String ├Ś Profile) pairs" pattern: the index type *is* the registry. Adding a constructor here is the only registration step. (2) **`profile : Language ŌåÆ ProDropProfile`** ŌĆö the unified endpoint, sourcing each language's profile from its study file (`Ostrove2026.englishProfile`, `Ostrove2026.smpmProfile`, `Ostrove2026.buliProfile`, `Allotey2021.gaProfile`). (3) **Two universal theorems closed by `cases L <;> decide`.** `all_satisfy : ŌłĆ L, (profile L).Satisfies` is the universal asserted concretely over the cross-linguistic database, kernel-checked by case enumeration. `forbidden_cell_empty : ŌłĆ L, (profile L).classify ŌēĀ Typology.overtPRO_proDrop` is the falsifiable empty-cell claim ŌĆö the explicit form of "the typology has a forbidden cell." Adding a future language whose profile lands in `overtPRO_proDrop` breaks both proofs at typecheck. (4) **`cellCount`** via `Finset.univ.filter` enables histogram queries over the registry; `cellCount_overtPRO_proDrop_eq_zero` is the cardinality form of `forbidden_cell_empty`. (5) **Genre placement.** Lives at `Phenomena/Pronouns/` (subject pronoun realization is the relevant typological domain) as a sibling to `Pronouns/Typology.lean` (which stays a pure WALS-data importer per the established `Typology.lean` genre). The registry imports Phenomena/Studies and is therefore not a "data" file in the CLAUDE.md sense ŌĆö it is a survey-level synthesis. **Mathlib idioms employed (per design discussion):** indexable type instead of `String` keys; `Fintype` + `Finset.univ` instead of manually-maintained list; universal as `ŌłĆ L : Language, ...` theorem decidable by case enumeration. Imports added to `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.229.906] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Language inventories moved out of Core into language Fragments (architectural fix).** Stage 6 (0.229.901) and Stage 7 (0.229.905) had stipulated `ArticleInventory.{english,german,mandarin,thai,shan}` and their per-language `_marking` consistency theorems inside `Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean`, breaking the TheoryŌåÆFragment dependency hierarchy: language data lives in `Fragments/`, not in `Core/`. (1) **Four new fragment files** mirror the existing `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` skeleton ŌĆö `Fragments/English/Definiteness.lean`, `Fragments/German/Definiteness.lean`, `Fragments/Mandarin/Definiteness.lean`, `Fragments/Thai/Definiteness.lean` ŌĆö each defines `articleInventory : Core.Nominal.ArticleInventory` and `articleInventory_marking : articleInventory.toMarkingStrategy = ...` proving the language's Moroney cell by `rfl`. (2) **`Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` extended** with `articleInventory`, `articleInventory_marking`, and `articleInventory_params_agreement` (proves `articleInventory.toMarkingParams = markingParams` by `rfl`, witnessing that the inventory is the upstream object from which the existing `markingParams` is the boolean projection ŌĆö both representations live side-by-side in the Shan fragment for now). (3) **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` stripped** to just the `ArticleInventory` structure, `licensesKind`, `toMarkingParams`, `toMarkingStrategy`, `toArticleType`, the universal `licensesKind_bare` simp lemma, and the universal `toArticleType_eq_strategyToArticleType`. The five language defs and five `_marking` theorems are gone. (4) **Cross-language collapse theorems migrated to `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` ┬¦14** (`english_mandarin_articleType_collapse`, `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct`) ŌĆö these are observations *about* a typological pattern, not facts about Core, so they belong at the Phenomena layer. (5) **`Moroney2021.lean` ┬¦14 rewritten** to import the four new fragments (plus the existing Shan one), introduce `englishInv`/`germanInv`/`mandarinInv`/`thaiInv`/`shanInv` abbrevs anchored to `Fragments.X.Definiteness.articleInventory`, and use those in all the existing inventory-bridge theorems (`inventory_agrees_with_params`, `inventory_derives_all_languages`, `shan_anaphoric_not_licensed_via_article`, etc.). New `mandarin_in_markedAnaphoric` makes the @cite{jenks-2018} backdrop visible. (6) **`Linglib.lean`** adds the four new fragment imports near each language's existing block. No semantic change; pure file relocation that brings the redesigned API into compliance with the strict `Theories ŌåÆ Fragments ŌåÆ Phenomena` dependency hierarchy. Build clean (988 jobs).

## [0.229.905] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Stage 7 of the Definiteness API redesign ŌĆö Moroney2021 wired to `Core.Nominal`.** Verifies that the new `ArticleInventory` / `NominalKind` / `interpret` machinery reproduces and refines Moroney's typological analysis end-to-end, treating the existing study file as an integration test for the redesigned API. (1) **Thai inventory added.** `ArticleInventory.thai` (no indefinite, no unique, anaphoric-via-demonstrative, demonstrative paradigm, possessive paradigm) joins the language inventory; `thai_marking : ArticleInventory.thai.toMarkingStrategy = .markedAnaphoric` matches the ┬¦7 derivation. Same `markedAnaphoric` cell as Mandarin under Moroney's typology. (2) **`Moroney2021.lean` ┬¦14 added ŌĆö "Integration with the Core.Nominal API".** Imports `Core.Nominal.{ArticleInventory,Description,Interpret}` and re-derives the four-language Table 4.4 typology from the inventory layer rather than the params layer: `inventory_agrees_with_params` proves `ArticleInventory.X.toMarkingParams = XParams` for English/German/Thai/Shan by `rfl`, witnessing that the inventory is the upstream object from which ┬¦7's `englishParams`/`germanParams`/`thaiParams`/`shanParams` fall out (no information loss for the params projection ŌĆö the inventory carries strictly more data). `inventory_derives_all_languages` mirrors ┬¦7's `derive_all_languages` at the inventory level. (3) **Licensing predictions sharpen Moroney's central finding.** `shan_anaphoric_not_licensed_via_article` ŌĆö Shan's `ArticleInventory.licensesKind` returns `false` for `.anaphoric` `NominalKind`s (no anaphoric article, no syncretic unique article either) ŌĆö yet `shan_bare_licensed` and `shan_demonstrative_licensed` both return `true`, exposing the morphological substrate for Moroney's analysis: Shan expresses anaphoric definiteness through bare nouns + optional demonstratives, never through a dedicated article. The contrast with English (`english_anaphoric_licensed_via_syncretism` ŌĆö *the* covers anaphoric via syncretism) and German (`german_anaphoric_licensed_via_strong_article` ŌĆö independent strong article) makes the three modes of anaphoric licensing explicit at the inventory level. (4) **Interpretation agreement theorems for Shan.** `shan_bare_unique_agreement` is the Shan-named consequence of the universal `Core.Nominal.interpret_bare_eq_unique` ŌĆö bare and uniqueness definites over the same restrictor select the same referent, the type-theoretic correlate of Moroney's claim that Shan bare nouns express weak/uniqueness definiteness via unblocked ╬╣. `shan_demonstrative_anaphoric_agreement` is the Shan-named consequence of `interpret_demonstrative_eq_anaphoric` ŌĆö *n├ój*/*n├ón* and the bare anaphoric reading pick the same entity at a shared discourse index, formalizing Moroney's ┬¦2.1.3 finding that the demonstrative's deictic feature is a presupposition filter, not a referent selector. (5) **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` opens fixed.** `open Core.IntensionalLogic` and `open Core.IntensionalLogic.Variables` added at namespace top so the `Frame` and `DenotGS` references in `licensesKind` and `licensesKind_bare` resolve under `autoImplicit := false`. No new sorrys; no consumer-side changes outside the study file.

## [0.229.904] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Assignment` moved back to `Core` namespace (file-location revert of 0.229.899; wrapper-deletion preserved).** Stage 11 had pushed the polymorphic `Assignment ╬▒ := ŌäĢ ŌåÆ ╬▒` abbrev into `Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` to break a name clash with the now-deleted `Variables.Assignment F` wrapper. The clash is gone, and the IL-namespaced location was conceptually wrong: `Assignment` is **pre-intensional substrate** ŌĆö pure Tarski variable mapping, used by extensional frameworks (plain DPL, classical model theory) just as much as by intensional ones ŌĆö and didn't belong inside the intensional namespace. The intensional substrate (`Frame`, `SitAssignment F := Assignment F.Index`, `DenotGS`) builds on top of `Core.Assignment`, not the other way around. (1) **File:** `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Assignment.lean` deleted; the abbrev + `update` + `update_at` / `update_ne` / `update_overwrite` / `update_comm` / `update_self` lemmas are now at the top of `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` under `namespace Core` (alongside the `PartialAssign`, `PluralAssign`, `VarAssignment` abbrevs already there). The merged file is one logical module ŌĆö variable assignments, total and partial. (2) **Consumers updated:** `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` (import `Linglib.Core.Assignment`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra.lean` (import + `open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/VarAssignment.lean` (drops the redundant IL import; `open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/DynamicSemantics.lean` (`open Core (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/Core/CCP.lean` (import + `open _root_.Core (Assignment)` + re-export switched back: `export _root_.Core (Assignment)` and `export _root_.Core.Assignment (update update_at update_ne update_overwrite update_comm)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/DynamicGQ/UpdateTheoretic.lean` (`Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` ŌåÆ `Core.Assignment`, 9 occurrences). (3) **`Linglib.lean`:** drops the `import Linglib.Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` line. No semantic change; pure namespace move that aligns the file location with the conceptual layer (extensional substrate at top of `Core`, intensional substrate under `Core.IntensionalLogic`).

## [0.229.903] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core/NullSubject.lean` mathlib-style hardening.** Five-part follow-up to 0.229.902. (1) **Prop + Decidable.** `satisfiesImplicational : ProDropProfile ŌåÆ Bool` is now `ProDropProfile.Satisfies : ProDropProfile ŌåÆ Prop` (`p.hasOvertPRO = true ŌåÆ p.allowsProDrop = false`) with a derived `Decidable` instance, so downstream theorems are closed by `decide` rather than `rfl` over the Boolean encoding. The Prop form composes with other logical predicates and matches the mathlib convention for universals. (2) **Contrapositive.** New `prodrop_excludes_overt_pro : p.Satisfies ŌåÆ p.allowsProDrop = true ŌåÆ p.hasOvertPRO = false` ŌĆö the practically useful direction of the universal (given a *pro*-drop language, predict the absence of overt PRO). `Allotey2021.lean` exhibits the contrapositive concretely as `ga_prodrop_would_exclude_overt_pro`. (3) **Typology enum.** New `Core.NullSubject.Typology` enumerates the four cells (`nullPRO_noProDrop`, `nullPRO_proDrop`, `overtPRO_noProDrop`, `overtPRO_proDrop`); `Typology.isAttested` marks the fourth as forbidden; `ProDropProfile.classify` projects a profile to a cell; `satisfies_iff_attested` proves `p.Satisfies Ōåö p.classify.isAttested = true`. The cells are now first-class objects rather than implicit Boolean tuples ŌĆö makes "the forbidden cell" a named referent. `Ostrove2026.lean` adds `smpm_classified`, `english_classified`, `buli_classified` cell-by-cell theorems. (4) **`hasOvertPRO` widened to non-nullness.** `MinPronInventory.hasOvertPRO` now tests `controlForm != .null` rather than `== .pronoun`. The typological criterion @cite{ostrove-2026} states is overt-vs-null, not pronoun-vs-everything-else: an inventory whose controlled-subject form was `.reflexive` would equally count as overt PRO. All current inventories yield either `.null` or `.pronoun` so verdicts are unchanged; the widened definition is faithful to the stated criterion. (5) **Old API removed.** `satisfiesImplicational : Bool`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies` are gone ŌĆö replaced by `Satisfies` + `null_pro_satisfies` + `overt_no_prodrop_satisfies` (Prop versions). The two consumer sites (`Allotey2021.lean`, `Ostrove2026.lean`) are updated to `by decide` proofs and the new lemma names; no other call sites existed.

## [0.229.902] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`ProDropProfile` lifted from `Phenomena/Control/` to `Core/NullSubject.lean`.** Pro-drop / overt-PRO is a typological parameter, not a control phenomenon: any syntactic framework must engage with it, and it is consumed by control studies but not specific to them. New `Core/NullSubject.lean` holds the framework-agnostic parameter (`ProDropProfile`) and @cite{ostrove-2026}'s implicational universal (`satisfiesImplicational`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies`) ŌĆö zero theory dependencies, modeled on the existing `Core/SubjectProperties.lean`. The Minimalism-side bridge function (was `Phenomena.Control.hasOvertPRO : MinPronInventory PronForm ŌåÆ Bool`) is now `MinPronInventory.hasOvertPRO` in `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/MinimalPronoun.lean` ┬¦ 3 (where `MinPronInventory` and `PronForm` already live), so the layer that depends on Minimalism stays inside Minimalism. `language : String` field dropped from `ProDropProfile` ŌĆö Core types should not carry per-language metadata; the calling site (`gaProfile`, `smpmProfile`, etc.) is the natural anchor for the language label. `Studies/Allotey2021.lean` and `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` updated to `open Core.NullSubject` and use `inv.hasOvertPRO` dot-notation; `buliProfile` extracted as a top-level def in `Ostrove2026.lean` (was an inline structure literal). Old `Phenomena/Control/ProDropProfile.lean` deleted; `Linglib.lean` import line moved from the Phenomena block to the Core block.

## [0.229.901] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/ArticleInventory.lean` ŌĆö Stage 6 of the Definiteness API redesign.** Records a language's surface inventory of nominal forms as the upstream object from which the existing typology classifications fall out. (1) **`ArticleInventory` structure** with `hasIndefinite`, `hasUniqueArticle`, `hasAnaphoricArticle`, `uniqueAnaphoricSyncretism` (English *the* covers both vs German distinct weak/strong), `hasDemonstrative`, `hasPossessive`. Bare nominals are not recorded ŌĆö every language allows them at some level; the question is what readings they admit, which is a matter of the type-shift hierarchy, not the article inventory. (2) **`licensesKind {F : Frame} (inv) : NominalKind F ŌåÆ Bool`** ŌĆö pattern-match on the constructor: `.bare Ōå” true` (universal); `.indefinite Ōå” hasIndefinite`; `.unique Ōå” hasUniqueArticle`; `.anaphoric Ōå” hasAnaphoricArticle Ōł© (hasUniqueArticle Ōł¦ syncretism)` (English *the* counts via syncretism); `.demonstrative Ōå” hasDemonstrative`; `.possessive Ōå” hasPossessive`. The morphological-availability claim, not a grammaticality claim about every NP. `licensesKind_bare` is the universal bare-licensing simp lemma. (3) **Derived classifications.** `toMarkingParams : ArticleInventory ŌåÆ Core.Definiteness.DefMarkingParams` projects to the Moroney 3-boolean shape (the inventory carries strictly more information ŌĆö demonstrative and possessive paradigms are discarded). `toMarkingStrategy := deriveStrategy Ōłś toMarkingParams` and `toArticleType := strategyToArticleType Ōłś toMarkingStrategy` re-use the existing derivation chain in `Core/Definiteness.lean` rather than restipulating it. (4) **Language instances** for English (syncretic *the*, full paradigm), German (distinct weak/strong, full paradigm), Mandarin (anaphoric-only, demonstrative + possessive), Shan (no articles, demonstrative + possessive). (5) **Consistency theorems.** `english_marking`, `german_marking`, `mandarin_marking`, `shan_marking` derive the four Moroney cells (`.generallyMarked`, `.bipartite`, `.markedAnaphoric`, `.unmarked`) from the four inventories by `rfl`. `english_mandarin_articleType_collapse` witnesses the lossiness of `ArticleType` relative to `DefMarkingStrategy`: distinct inventories with distinct strategies (`.generallyMarked` vs `.markedAnaphoric`) both collapse to `.weakOnly`; `english_mandarin_inventory_distinct` shows the inventories themselves are not collapsed, so the structure has finer resolution than either downstream typology. `Linglib.lean` import added. No existing call sites touched.

## [0.229.900] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Allotey 2021 cleanup pass.** Six-part rework of the 0.229.896 initial drop after a re-read of @cite{allotey-2021} surfaced inaccuracies and a build failure. (1) **ASCII identifier policy.** Lean 4 rejects IPA `╔ø` (U+025B) and `┼ŗ` (U+014B) as identifier characters; the original `Fragments/Ga/Basic.lean` and `Predicates.lean` did not compile. All constructor and definition names are now ASCII (`ake`, `keji`, `nye`, `kpleno`, `kpang`, `kee`, `finiteAke`, `finiteKeji`, `hiekpano`); IPA orthography lives in the `.form` strings only. Module docstring records the policy. (2) **Verb-list correction.** Replaced `kai` 'remember' (not in the paper) with `hiekpano` 'forget' (Allotey ex 37ŌĆō38, the actual attested negative-implicative subject-control verb); dropped `susu` 'think' (not attested as Allotey CTP); kept `kee` 'say' as the standard `ak╔ø`-clause exemplar with a docstring noting it is the only entry not in Allotey's CTP list (kept so the OC-vs-non-OC contrast can be exhibited end-to-end). (3) **`ProDropProfile` factored out.** Moved `ProDropProfile`, `satisfiesImplicational`, `hasOvertPRO`, `overt_pro_non_prodrop_satisfies`, `null_pro_satisfies` from `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` (where they were stranded) into a new top-level data file `Phenomena/Control/ProDropProfile.lean`. Both `Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` and `Studies/Allotey2021.lean` import from there; the local re-statement in `Allotey2021.lean` is gone. (4) **Derive-don't-stipulate for OC.** `gaOCSignature` is now `if (clauseProperties c).noncoreferentialSubject then ocNone else ocFull` ŌĆö derived from the noncoreferential-subject flag rather than stipulated per clause. `gaAgr` is now `(clauseProperties c).finiteComplementizer`, with bridge theorem `gaAgr_eq_complementizer_isFinite` connecting the Agr flag to the complementizer's finiteness via `complementizer_isFinite_eq_finiteFlag` (also new in `Basic.lean`). New universal `oc_iff_no_noncoreferential` makes the structural derivation visible. The previous `irrealisNi_is_OC : ... := rfl` is no longer a tautology over a stipulated mapping but follows from the derived definition. (5) **Irrealis-vs-subjunctive diagnostic.** New section adds `isSubjunctiveLike (c : EmbeddedClauseType) : Bool := (clauseProperties c).noncoreferentialSubject` (a clause type passes the subjunctive test iff it allows a noncoreferential embedded subject ŌĆö the Romance obviation diagnostic). Three theorems: `irrealisNi_not_subjunctive`, `finiteAke_subjunctive_test_passes`, `finiteKeji_subjunctive_test_passes`. Formalizes Allotey's argument against the @cite{dakubu-2004} / @cite{campbell-2017} subjunctive analysis. The `gaToNoonan` mapping still sends `irrealisNi ŌåÆ .subjunctive` (Noonan's typological label, separate from the generative-grammar subjunctive); docstring records the distinction. (6) **Long Distance Agree analysis.** New `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/LongDistanceAgree.lean` formalizes @cite{szabolcsi-2009}'s three-condition LDA configuration (probe has valued Žå, goal has unvalued Žå, intervening C is non-defective) with `LDAConfig.licenses` and three structural impossibility theorems (`blocking_c_defeats_LDA`, `valued_goal_has_no_LDA_target`, `unvalued_probe_cannot_LDA`). `Allotey2021.lean` now wires `gaLDAConfig`, `ga_satisfies_LDA`, and `ga_LDA_implies_OC` to make Allotey's actual analytical contribution (LDA-driven cross-clausal valuation of the embedded minimal pronoun) visible ŌĆö previously the formalization plugged Allotey only into the @cite{ostrove-2026} minimal-pronoun typology. **Bib:** added stubs for `szabolcsi-2009`, `satik-2019`, `dakubu-2004`, `campbell-2017` (all with `note` fields flagging fields that were not verifiable from training data). `Linglib.lean` adds `Phenomena.Control.ProDropProfile` and `Theories.Syntax.Minimalism.LongDistanceAgree` imports.

## [0.229.899] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`Core.Assignment` consolidated into `Core.IntensionalLogic`.** The polymorphic `Assignment E := ŌäĢ ŌåÆ E` abbrev (and its `update` / `update_at` / `update_ne` / `update_overwrite` / `update_comm` / `update_self` lemmas) move from `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` to a new `Linglib/Core/IntensionalLogic/Assignment.lean` and live under the `Core.IntensionalLogic` namespace. The redundant `abbrev Assignment (F : Frame) := Core.Assignment F.Entity` wrapper in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean` is dropped; entity-pronoun signatures now read `Assignment F.Entity` directly, situation-pronoun signatures keep `SitAssignment F`. Motivation: the wrapper had the same name as the polymorphic abbrev but a different arity (`Frame` vs `Type*`), which silently lost name resolution against `open Core.IntensionalLogic` and forced fully-qualified `Variables.Assignment F` everywhere in `Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean` (Stage 5 of the Definiteness redesign). With both names unified to `Assignment` over `Type*`, `Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean` drops the qualifier. Updated callers: `Linglib/Core/Assignment.lean` (now hosts only `PartialAssign` / `PluralAssign` / `VarAssignment`, imports the new IL/Assignment), `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra.lean` and `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/VarAssignment.lean` and `Linglib/Core/CylindricAlgebra/DynamicSemantics.lean` (`open Core (Assignment)` ŌåÆ `open Core.IntensionalLogic (Assignment)`), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/Core/CCP.lean` (re-export switched to the IL namespace), `Linglib/Theories/Semantics/Dynamic/DynamicGQ/UpdateTheoretic.lean` (`Core.Assignment` ŌåÆ `Core.IntensionalLogic.Assignment` qualifier, 5 occurrences), `Linglib.lean` import added. No semantic change; pure namespace move. Stops the clash that bit Stage 5 and unblocks Stage 6.

## [0.229.898] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR J (interrogative completes the 3├Ś3 Portner table).** Three coordinated additions wire interrogative force into the Mood substrate so that the @cite{portner-2018} unification table gains its third column. (1) **`Core/Mood/POSWQ.lean`** ŌĆö `structure POSWQ extends POSW` adds an `inquiry : Setoid W` (the QUD-induced answer partition; `ŌŖż` = no question). The third sentence-mood update `POSWQ.inquire c q := { c with inquiry := c.inquiry ŌŖō q }` is the partition-side analogue of `POSW.plus` (refining `cs`) and `POSW.star` (refining `lt`); `inquire_targets_disjoint_components` proves the partition operation leaves both `cs` and `lt` untouched (the third leg of @cite{portner-2018}'s thesis that mood updates target disjoint substrate components). The third necessity modal `POSWQ.boxAns c p := ŌłĆ w v, c.cs w ŌåÆ c.cs v ŌåÆ c.inquiry.r w v ŌåÆ (p w Ōåö p v)` says `p` has constant truth value within each inquiry cell ŌĆö informational answerhood, the modal counterpart of `POSW.boxCs`/`boxLt`. The refinement preorder lifts componentwise (`Preorder (POSWQ W)`); `inquire_le_self`, `inquire_mono`, and `boxAns_anti` mirror the POSW preorder layer. `boxAns` is *not* upward-monotone in `p` (a strengthening can break per-cell constancy), but it *is* closed under negation (`boxAns_not`) and conjunction (`boxAns_and`) ŌĆö answers compose like ordinary propositions. Concrete two-cell separation witness `sepPOSWQ` proves `boxAns_not_reducible_to_boxCs`: there exists a POSWQ where `boxAns p` holds but `boxCs p` fails, so the inquiry component is doing genuine work. (2) **`Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean` POSWQ bridge.** `polarSetoid q : Setoid W` (two worlds equivalent iff they agree on `q`) is the smallest piece of partition structure a single yes/no question contributes; `qudInquiry K : Setoid W` folds the QUD stack as iterated meet of polar Setoids (with `ŌŖż` as identity); `toPOSWQ K : POSWQ W := { K.toPOSW with inquiry := K.qudInquiry }` projects the scoreboard into the POSWQ substrate. Bridge theorem `toPOSWQ_interrogation_eq_inquire` proves `interrogationUpdate` is `POSWQ.inquire (polarSetoid q)` on the inquiry side ŌĆö definitional, by the foldr convention chosen so the cons reduces to right-meet. Corollary `boxAns_polar_after_interrogation`: after posing `q`, the polar partition of `q` itself is settled by the resulting POSWQ. The three discourse-update bridges now form a complete table: `assertionUpdate` Ōåö `plus` Ōåö `boxCs_after_assertion`; `directionUpdate` Ōåö `star` Ōåö `direction_demotes_violators`; `interrogationUpdate` Ōåö `inquire` Ōåö `boxAns_polar_after_interrogation`. (3) **`Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean` extended.** `VerbalMoodOp` gains a third constructor `.interrogative`; `interp` lifts to `POSWQ W ŌåÆ (W ŌåÆ Prop) ŌåÆ Prop` with cases `.indicative Ōå” c.toPOSW.boxCs`, `.subjunctive Ōå” c.toPOSW.boxLt`, `.interrogative Ōå” c.boxAns`. The previous uniform `interp_mono` is replaced by `interp_indicative_mono` and `interp_subjunctive_mono` (each delegating to the corresponding `POSW.box*_mono`); `boxAns` has no upward monotonicity in `p`, and the asymmetry is itself part of the unification's content (different operators, different ordering behaviors, different lattice substrates). Separation theorems: `indicative_ne_subjunctive` (lifted from PR I via `POSWQ.ofPOSW sepPOSW`); `interrogative_ne_indicative` (delegates to `POSWQ.boxAns_not_reducible_to_boxCs`). `MoodSelector.toVerbalMood` is left unchanged (declarative-complement classification doesn't include question-embedders); `toVerbalMood_ne_interrogative` makes the closure explicit ŌĆö the `.interrogative` case is reachable by direct construction, not by `MoodSelector` projection. The completed Portner unification:

| layer            | declarative                  | imperative                    | interrogative              |
|------------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------|----------------------------|
| sentence mood    | assertion (`+`-update on cs) | direction (`Ōŗå`-update on lt)  | interrogation (`?` on inq) |
| modal necessity  | `boxCs` (informational)      | `boxLt` (preferential)        | `boxAns` (answerhood)      |
| verbal mood      | `interp .indicative`         | `interp .subjunctive`         | `interp .interrogative`    |

Three components (`cs`, `lt`, `inquiry`), three update operations, three necessity modals, three verbal-mood operators ŌĆö all type-driven on a single substrate. Closes the 7-PR Mood API unification arc. **Refactor:** the standalone `Core/Mood/Refinement.lean` from the prep PR is folded into `Core/Mood/POSW.lean` ┬¦4 (refinement preorder is intrinsic to POSW; mathlib pattern: small natural orders live with the structure). `Mathlib.Order.Basic` is now imported by `POSW.lean`. `Core/Mood.lean` re-exports `POSWQ`. Build: 0 new sorrys; existing pre-PR sorry warnings unaffected.

## [0.229.897] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Interpret.lean`.** The interpretation function `interpret : NominalKind F ŌåÆ Variables.Assignment F ŌåÆ Variables.SitAssignment F ŌåÆ Option F.Entity`, mapping the `NominalKind` sum to a referent (or `none` for presupposition failure / inapplicability) by composing the maximality/uniqueness operators in `Core/Nominal/Maximality.lean`. Per-constructor reductions: `bare R` and `unique R _` Ōå” `russellIota (R g gs ┬Ę)` (default Core reading is weak/uniqueness; mereologically-aware fragments can opt into `sharvyMax` separately); `indefinite _` Ōå” `none` (existential closure isn't an entity at this layer); `anaphoric R d` Ōå” `some (g d)` if `R g gs (g d)` else `none`; `demonstrative R _ _ d` Ōå” same as anaphoric (deictic feature is a presupposition filter, not a selector); `possessive R poss rel` Ōå” `russellIota` over the restrictor conjoined with the possession relation. Six per-constructor `@[simp]` reductions plus three cross-constructor agreement theorems: `interpret_bare_eq_unique` (Core defaults bare to weak/uniqueness), `interpret_demonstrative_eq_anaphoric` (deictic doesn't change referent), `interpret_unique_index_irrelevant` (the situation-pronoun index records *which* pronoun is bound, not what the operator computes ŌĆö the actual situation comes via the `SitAssignment`). Three witness theorems: `interpret_unique_witness_satisfies` (whatever the operator returns satisfies the restrictor), `interpret_anaphoric_witness_is_indexed` (anaphoric never picks anything other than `g d`), `interpret_anaphoric_isSome_iff` (anaphoric succeeds iff the restrictor accepts the indexed entity). All proofs use `Classical.dec` locally via `letI`. `Linglib.lean` import added.

## [0.229.896] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Allotey 2021 (G├Ż obligatory control) formalized.** Three new files: (1) `Linglib/Fragments/Ga/Basic.lean` ŌĆö G├Ż (Kwa, Niger-Congo) fragment: pronoun proclitic paradigm (mi/o/e/w╔ö/ny╔ø/am╔ø ├Ś sg/pl), TAM (`baa-` future, `mii-` progressive, `├®-` perfect, `├Ī` irrealis tone), three complementizers (`ak╔ø`, `k╔øji`, `ni`) with `Complementizer.isFinite` (`ni` = irrealis), three-way `EmbeddedClauseType` (`finiteAk╔ø | finiteK╔øji | irrealisNi`) with `ClauseProperties` (unrestrictedTAM, noncoreferentialSubject, finiteComplementizer). (2) `Linglib/Fragments/Ga/Predicates.lean` ŌĆö G├Ż CTPs with Landau predicate class: subject control `tao` 'want', `kpl╔øn╔ö` 'agree', `kpa┼ŗ` 'plan' (desiderative), `kai` 'remember', `ny╔ø` 'manage' (implicative); object control `kenya` 'urge', `laka` 'persuade' (desiderative), `dai` 'force' (implicative), `wa` 'help' (unclassified); finite complement `k╔ø╔ø` 'say', `susu` 'think' (propositional). `CTP.controlTier` derives Landau tier per verb. (3) `Linglib/Phenomena/Control/Studies/Allotey2021.lean` ŌĆö `gaOCSignature : EmbeddedClauseType ŌåÆ OCSignature` (irrealisNi ŌåÆ ocFull, finite* ŌåÆ ocNone) with per-clause-type `isOC` theorems by `rfl`; `landau_predicts_control` (Landau cSubjunctive/finite + Agr ŌåÆ G├Ż OC pattern) by `cases <;> rfl`; per-verb OC theorems for all CTPs in the inventory; `oc_determined_by_clause_type` universal (any CTP whose complement is irrealis shows OC) by `cases <;> rfl`; `gaInventory : MinPronInventory PronForm` with `ga_overt_pro` and `ga_has_reflexive`; pro-drop / overt-PRO universal application (`gaProfile`, `ga_satisfies_universal`); Noonan complement-type bridge `gaToNoonan`. The Pollock 1989 V-movement parallelism in Allotey is deliberately not formalized ŌĆö Pollock's diagnostic requires a free Neg head (Spec-NegP) and G├Ż `-ee/-ko` is suffixal, so the inference doesn't license without independent morphological argumentation outside the paper. Removed stranded `gaInventory`/`ga_overt_pro`/`ga_satisfies_universal` from `Ostrove2026.lean` (relocated to their proper home in the G├Ż-focused study). Bib entry `allotey-2021` upgraded role: cited ŌåÆ formalized.

## [0.229.895] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Maximality.lean` ŌĆö Sharvy/Kri┼Š + CoppockŌĆōBeaver operators** ŌĆö Stage 4 of the Definiteness API redesign. Predicate operators over `F.Denot .et` for Stage 5 to compose. (1) **CoppockŌĆōBeaver factorization** (@cite{coppock-beaver-2015}): `Existence P := Ōłāx, P x` and `Uniqueness P := ŌłĆx y, P x ŌåÆ P y ŌåÆ x = y` as independent predicates; `existsUnique := Existence Ōł¦ Uniqueness` is the classical Russellian condition; `existsUnique_iff_existence_and_uniqueness` is `Iff.rfl` (by construction); `uniqueness_does_not_imply_existence` proves the empty predicate satisfies vacuous Uniqueness without Existence ŌĆö the data point that motivates @cite{coppock-beaver-2015}'s split. (2) **Russellian iota** (order-free): `russellIota P : Option F.Entity` returns the unique witness when `existsUnique P`; three theorems wire it together (`russellIota_isSome_iff_existsUnique`, `russellIota_witness_satisfies`, `russellIota_witness_unique`). (3) **Sharvy maximality** (@cite{sharvy-1980}): `IsMaximal P e := P e Ōł¦ ŌłĆ x, P x ŌåÆ x Ōēż e` and the selector `sharvyMax P : Option F.Entity` over `[PartialOrder F.Entity]` ŌĆö collapses to Russellian iota on flat orders, returns the @cite{link-1983} sum on join semilattices for plurals; `isMaximal_unique` shows antisymmetry collapses two maxima into one. (4) **Kri┼Š homogeneity** (@cite{kriz-2015}): `Homogeneous P S := (ŌłĆ x, P x ŌåÆ S x) Ōł© (ŌłĆ x, P x ŌåÆ ┬¼ S x)` ŌĆö the predicate that licenses uniform-truth-or-uniform-falsehood for plural definites; `uniqueness_implies_homogeneous_classical` proves Russellian uniqueness vacuously implies homogeneity (the singular-definite limiting case). All proofs use `Classical.dec` locally via `letI` ŌĆö no `native_decide` and no `noncomputable` leakage outside the maximality module. The interpretation function in Stage 5 will compose these operators with the `NominalKind` sum from Stage 3 and the `DenotGS` substrate from Stage 1. No existing call sites touched.

## [0.229.894] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Faust 2026: universal Faust+Kramer integration theorem.** New `intrusion_wellformed_iff_no_intruder_or_n_with_gen` in ┬¦12 of `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean`: for any `RootTemplateMatch m` and any `CatHead ch`, `m.intrusionLicensed ch.licensesIntrusion = true Ōåö m.hasIntruder = false Ōł© (ch.cat = .n Ōł¦ ch.phi.gender.isSome = true)`. Universally-quantified statement of @cite{faust-2026} (11), derived by composing `intrusionLicensed_iff` (Templates.lean) with `licensesIntrusion_iff_n_and_gen` (Categorizer.lean) ŌĆö no new computational content; the previously per-instance integration of @cite{faust-2026}'s licensing predicate with @cite{kramer-2020}'s `CatHead` taxonomy is now structural. Two corollaries make the verbal/nominal split explicit: `v_plain_licenses_iff_no_intruder` (under v_plain, licensing reduces to intruder-freeness) and `n_uFem_licenses_universally` (under n_uFem, every match licensed regardless of intruder status). Subsumes the 12 per-derivation `decide` theorems (kept as a per-datum verification roll-call) and the 6-conjunct `verbal_nominal_asymmetry_from_kramer` (kept as the empirical-claim digest); the new theorem is the single structural fact behind both.

## [0.229.893] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Core/Nominal/Description.lean` ŌĆö `NominalKind F` sum type** ŌĆö Stage 3 of the Definiteness API redesign. A single sum type, parameterized by a `Core.IntensionalLogic.Frame`, covers the principal flavors of nominal description that the syntaxŌĆōsemantics interface needs to distinguish: `bare` (number-neutral; covert type-shifts decide the reading), `indefinite` (Ōłā; introduces a new discourse referent), `unique` (CoppockŌĆōBeaver weak/uniqueness; Sharvy/Kri┼Š maximal ŌĆö carries a `situationIdx` for the Hanink-bound resource situation), `anaphoric` (Schwarz strong-article / Hanink-indexed familiarity ŌĆö carries a `discourseIdx`), `demonstrative` (Patel-GroszŌĆōGrosz/Davis: a separate object, not "strong the" ŌĆö carries a `Core.Deixis.Feature` deictic, a situation index, and a discourse index), and `possessive` (definite via a possession relation; carries restrictor + possessor + relation). Every restrictor has type `DenotGS F .et`, the bi-assignment-relative type added in Stage 1 ŌĆö both entity assignments and situation assignments are first-class arguments, the @cite{hanink-2021}/@cite{bondarenko-2023} position. Four classification predicates supplied (`isDefinite`, `isAnaphoric`, `usesSituationPronoun`, `expectedPresupType`); two structural theorems wire them together: `isDefinite_iff_expectedPresup_some` and `isAnaphoric_implies_familiarity`. No semantics yet ŌĆö Stage 4 supplies maximality + CoppockŌĆōBeaver uniqueness operators, Stage 5 supplies the interpretation `NominalKind F ŌåÆ DenotGS F .e`. No existing call sites touched. Reuses Stage 2's `Core.Deixis.Feature` for demonstratives.

## [0.229.892] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR I (verbal mood as POSW component selection).** New `Theories/Semantics/Mood/VerbalMood.lean` formalizes @cite{portner-2018} (Ch. 2)'s reduction of the indicative/subjunctive contrast to *which POSW component the embedded clause is evaluated against*. `VerbalMoodOp := indicative | subjunctive`, with `interp : VerbalMoodOp ŌåÆ POSW W ŌåÆ (W ŌåÆ Prop) ŌåÆ Prop` defined as `.indicative Ōå” POSW.boxCs` and `.subjunctive Ōå” POSW.boxLt` ŌĆö type-driven, no auxiliary state. The Truth intuition (Farkas 1985, Portner 1997 tradition) gets `boxCs` (universal quantification over the modal base / informational component); the Comparison intuition (Giorgi & Pianesi 1997 tradition) gets `boxLt` (quantification over the best-ranked subset / preferential component). Theorems: `interp_indicative` / `interp_subjunctive` (definitional simp lemmas), `interp_mono` (uniform upward monotonicity across the Truth/Comparison split), `indicative_ne_subjunctive` (concrete two-world `Bool` POSW where `boxLt sepProp` holds but `boxCs sepProp` fails ŌĆö the type-level reflex of Portner's observation that comparative-meaning predicates entail truth only at the best-ranked subset, not the entire modal base). The `MoodSelector` enum (Mood/Basic.lean) projects to `VerbalMoodOp` via `MoodSelector.toVerbalMood`: `.indicativeSelecting Ōå” some .indicative`, `.subjunctiveSelecting Ōå” some .subjunctive`, variable/neutral Ōå” `none` (their selection pattern is not committed to a single POSW component by lexical class alone). `prefersSubjunctive_eq_subjunctive` proves the existing `prefersSubjunctive` boolean and `toVerbalMood` agree by construction ŌĆö two surface representations of the same lexical-class commitment.

The full Portner unification is now visible at the type level across the three mood layers:

| layer            | indicative-flavored          | subjunctive-flavored          |
|------------------|------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| sentence mood    | assertion (`+`-update on cs) | direction (`Ōŗå`-update on lt)  |
| modal necessity  | `boxCs` (informational)      | `boxLt` (preferential)        |
| verbal mood      | `interp .indicative`         | `interp .subjunctive`         |

The shared substrate is `POSW`. The previous PRs (E.5ŌĆōH) wired the bottom two rows; PR I closes the top of the table by reading the modal row as a complementizer-domain selector triggered by the matrix predicate's lexical class.

## [0.229.891] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Promote `SpatialRelation` to `Core/Deixis/Feature.lean`** ŌĆö Stage 2 of the Definiteness API redesign. The two-constructor `SpatialRelation` enum (`proximal | distal`) that previously lived locally inside `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` is now a framework-agnostic `Core.Deixis.Feature` enum with four constructors (`proximal | medial | distal | unspecified`), covering the WALS Ch. 41 distance-system typology: two-way systems (~54% of attested languages: English, Mandarin, French, Shan, Magahi, most IE languages); three-way distance-oriented systems (Latin, Spanish, Hunzib) via `medial`; distance-neutral demonstratives (Modern German *dieser*, Hawaiian no-contrast forms) via `unspecified`. The file documents that adding finer granularity (person-orientation for Japanese ko/so/a, Korean i/ku/ce; visibility for Quechua, Kwakwaka'wakw, ASL; elevation for Dyirbal, Nepali) should follow the *concrete-then-abstract* discipline ŌĆö add a constructor when a fragment actually needs it, not in anticipation. Auxiliary `Feature.encodesDistance : Feature ŌåÆ Bool` (returns `true` iff non-`unspecified`) with a one-line iff characterization. `Fragments/Shan/Definiteness.lean` migrated: `ShanDemonstrative.spatial : Core.Deixis.Feature` (was `SpatialRelation`); `naj`/`nan` constructors unchanged (constructor names `.proximal`/`.distal` preserved); `demDenotation`, `dem_is_anaphoric`, `dem_refines_bare` signatures updated to take `Core.Deixis.Feature ŌåÆ E ŌåÆ Bool` spatial predicates. `Phenomena/Definiteness/Studies/Moroney2021.lean` follows: `open Core.Deixis (Feature)`; one theorem signature retypes to `Feature ŌåÆ E ŌåÆ Bool`. `Linglib.lean` adds `import Linglib.Core.Deixis.Feature`. The unrelated `SpatialRelation` enum inside `Phenomena/Evidentiality/Studies/Koev2017.lean` (`samePlace | differentPlace`, an event-locality predicate for evidentials, not a deictic feature) is a namespacing-distinct local type and is unaffected.

## [0.229.890] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Faust 2026 mathlib-PR-review pass.** Six fixes to `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean` and its bridges. (1) Fixed docstring hallucination on `hebrew_kala_unfilled` ŌĆö replaced reference to nonexistent `hebrew_kala_unfilled_but_misalign_safe` with the actual joint statement `qataT_qata_three_way_contrast`. (2/3) Replaced 5 bare-`simp` invocations (lines 711, 753, 763, 788, 1070) with `decide` or `simp only [...]` per CLAUDE.md preference. (4) Promoted `Root.satisfiesOCP` from study-local def to `Core/Morphology/ConsonantalRoot.lean` (alongside a new auxiliary `Root.adjDupCount` ŌĆö segment-level OCP infrastructure that other templatic-morphology studies will want; theory-neutral and not coupled to the tier-projection-level `Phonology.Constraints.adjacentIdentical`). (5) Moved `CatHead.licensesIntrusion` from `_root_`-qualified study-local def to its natural home in `Theories/Morphology/DM/Categorizer.lean` (alongside `satisfiesLicense` and the Kramer taxonomy it ranges over), with the 6 per-canonical-head verification theorems (`n_uFem_licenses_intrusion`, `v_plain_blocks_intrusion`, etc.) and the iff characterization `licensesIntrusion_iff_n_and_gen` migrating with it ŌĆö these test the predicate's behavior against canonical `CatHead`s, not Faust-specific data. (6) Collapsed ┬¦10 stub (a content-free pointer to ┬¦13's analysis) into the ┬¦10 (formerly ┬¦11) lead-in; renumbered subsequent sections (┬¦11ŌåÆ┬¦10, ┬¦12ŌåÆ┬¦11, ┬¦13ŌåÆ┬¦12). The Faust2026 file is now ~50 lines shorter and the only Faust-specific content in ┬¦12 is the per-template `CatHead` tagging (`hebrewTaQTiL_locus := CatHead.n_uFem` etc.) and per-derivation licensing verdicts.

## [0.229.889] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Situation-pronoun substrate in `Core/IntensionalLogic/Variables.lean`** ŌĆö Stage 1 of the Definiteness API redesign. Adds `SitAssignment F := Core.Assignment F.Index` (the type-level dual of the entity `Assignment F := Core.Assignment F.Entity`), `interpSitPronoun n gs := gs n` (parallel to `interpPronoun`), `DenotGS F ty := Assignment F ŌåÆ SitAssignment F ŌåÆ F.Denot ty` (bi-assignment-relative denotations), and lift constructors `DenotGS.ofDenotG` / `DenotGS.const`. Reuses `Core.Assignment` at type `F.Index` so all update lemmas (`update_at`, `update_ne`, `update_overwrite`, `update_comm`, `update_self`) come for free at the new instantiation. The trivial `DenotGS.ofDenotG_const` agreement theorem documents that the two lift paths from a frame-level constant coincide. No existing call sites touched; no theorems weakened. Sets up Hanink (2018, 2021) / Bondarenko (2022, 2023) situation-pronoun binding to be used by the upcoming `NominalKind` sum (Stage 3) and `interpret` function (Stage 5) of the Definiteness redesign.

## [0.229.888] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR H (`directionUpdate Ōåö POSW.star`).** Completes the 7-PR arc by wiring the directive side of the Portner 2018 unification thesis. Three additions to `Linglib/Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`: (1) lifted the inline `let rec go` inside `directionUpdate` to a top-level `addGoalAt : List (GoalSet W) ŌåÆ Nat ŌåÆ Nat ŌåÆ Goal W ŌåÆ List (GoalSet W)` helper (out-of-range targets silently leave the list unchanged ŌĆö that's a stipulated property of the discourse update, not of POSW). (2) `Scoreboard.goalContents := K.goals.flatMap GoalSet.toPropertyList` flattens per-agent goal sets into a single property list ŌĆö the substrate of the goal-induced preference ordering. (3) `Scoreboard.toPOSW` no longer carries a trivial `lt`; it now derives it from `goalContents`: `lt w v := ŌłĆ p Ōłł K.goalContents, p v ŌåÆ p w` (`w` is at least as good as `v` iff `w` satisfies every goal `v` satisfies). Reflexivity and transitivity follow trivially from chaining implications. The bridge theorem `toPOSW_direction_eq_star` proves `(K.directionUpdate p s t pr).toPOSW.lt w v Ōåö (K.toPOSW.star p).lt w v` under the precondition `t < K.goals.length`, mirroring the assertion-side bridge from PR G ŌĆö the proof goes through `mem_addGoalAt_flatMap` (the new directive's content is simply added to the goal-content list, with everything else preserved) and `mem_directionUpdate_goalContents`. Corollary `direction_demotes_violators` proves that after directing `p`, no `p`-violator can dominate a `p`-satisfier in the goal-induced ordering ŌĆö the `lt`-side analogue of `boxCs_after_assertion`. The `lt` refactor required updating the simp lemma `toPOSW_lt` (the trivial `Ōåö True` is replaced by the goal-quantifier expansion); `toPOSW_cs` and `toPOSW_assertion_eq_plus` are unchanged because the assertion update doesn't touch `K.goals` (`assertion_preserves_goalContents := rfl`).

The two scoreboard updates that target the POSW substrate now correspond, by construction, to the two POSW updates of @cite{portner-2018}:

| scoreboard      | POSW    | informational consequence                  |
|-----------------|---------|--------------------------------------------|
| assertionUpdate | `plus`  | `boxCs_plus_self` (Stalnakerian principle) |
| directionUpdate | `star`  | `lt`-refinement: p-violators demoted       |

Out of scope for this PR (genuinely beyond Portner's two-update substrate): an unconditional `boxLt p` analogue for direction (Portner's `Ōŗå`-update doesn't make `p` preferentially necessary without an "anchor" assumption ŌĆö the well-known asymmetry between `+` and `Ōŗå`); interrogation update wiring (a third POSW update mode beyond `+`/`Ōŗå` would be a new arc, not a completion of this one).

## [0.229.887] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **`HarmonySystem` now structurally contains its `TierRule`** ŌĆö the bridge architecture from 0.229.884 (parallel `triggerValue` + `toTierRule` + `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` proving them equivalent under blocker-free, transparent-trigger conditions) is replaced by structural containment. The new `Theories/Phonology/Harmony/Defs.lean` defines `HarmonySystem` as a record carrying a `rule : Phonology.Alternation.TierRule Segment` (the formal core ŌĆö @cite{belth-2026}'s tier-based AGREE rule, the central object in modern computational/learnability phonology) plus `feature : Feature`, `isTarget : Segment ŌåÆ Bool`, and `isBlocker : Segment ŌåÆ Bool` (the @cite{rose-walker-2011} typological "spreading discipline" layered on top). `triggerValue` is now defined as `sys.rule.apply` dispatched on `harmonyDomain sys stem` ŌĆö *true by construction*, no bridge theorem, no disjointness side-conditions, no possibility of the typological wrapper and the formal rule silently disagreeing.
- **`HarmonySystem.mk'` smart constructor** exposes the @cite{rose-walker-2011} six-way decomposition (feature / isTrigger / isTarget / isTransparent / direction / isBlocker) as named arguments; the constructor compiles these into the underlying `TierRule` (tier projects out transparent segments; side derives from direction; context class is the trigger predicate; relation is `.agree`; featureValue extracts `s.spec feature`). This is the public construction API ŌĆö direct positional record syntax (`{ feature := ..., isTrigger := ..., ... }`) no longer works, since `isTrigger`/`isTransparent`/`direction` are no longer top-level fields. Migration: Turkish (`palatalHarmony`, `labialHarmony`), Finnish (`finnishHarmony`), Hungarian (`hungarianPalatalHarmony`, `hungarianLabialHarmony`, `palatalHarmony_eBlocks`) all switched to `HarmonySystem.mk'`.
- **`HarmonyDir.toSide` bridge** (`HarmonyDir ŌåÆ Phonology.Alternation.Side`): rightward / bidirectional Ōå” `.left` (context precedes the suffix slot); leftward Ōå” `.right`. Bidirectional collapses to rightward operationally (the same `harmonyDomain`/`triggerValue` computation handles both); the typological distinction lives only at the `mk'` argument site.
- **Convenience accessor** `Phonology.Harmony.isTrigger sys s := sys.rule.targetIsContext s` recovers the Rose-Walker trigger field from the underlying rule.
- **Deleted**: `HarmonySystem.toTierRule` (now redundant ŌĆö `sys.rule` IS the TierRule), `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` bridge theorem (true-by-construction now), `filter_through_byClass` private lemma (was only there to support the bridge), and the `direction : HarmonyDir` field on `HarmonySystem` (encoded in `rule.side`; preserved as a smart-constructor argument only). One Hungarian fragment theorem (`hungarian_finnish_same_direction`) restated in terms of `rule.side = .left` rather than the deleted `direction` field.
- **`Theories/Phonology/Alternation.lean` docstring** updated: the wrapping relationship is now described as "Rose-Walker harmony systems structurally contain a `TierRule` as their value-prediction core" (replacing "wrapped via `HarmonySystem.toTierRule`"). The architectural commitment is now visible in the type, not deferred to a bridge theorem.

### Why
The bridge-theorem architecture from 0.229.884 left the relationship between Rose-Walker harmony and Belth-style tier rules implicit: two parallel implementations, glued by an equivalence proof that only held under restrictive hypotheses (no blockers, triggers non-transparent, rightward direction). That dodged exactly the cases where the typology actually does work ŌĆö blockers are the whole point of opaque vs. transparent. The structural containment matches the field's center of gravity: in the modern computational/learnability framing (Heinz/Jardine/Chandlee TSL, @cite{belth-2026}), tier projection plus a local rule on it is the formal primitive; the @cite{rose-walker-2011} trigger/target/transparent/blocker/direction decomposition is the typological lens for cataloguing what languages do, not a separate formal object. So `HarmonySystem` *contains* a `TierRule` (descriptive wrapper around formal core) rather than *bridging to* one (peer relationship via equivalence).

## [0.229.886] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR G (POSW substrate bridge for `Scoreboard`).** `Linglib/Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean` gains a `toPOSW : Scoreboard W ŌåÆ Core.Mood.POSW W` projection: `cs := K.contextSet`, `lt := fun _ _ => True` (trivial preference ordering ŌĆö directive update via `POSW.star` deferred). Two simp lemmas (`toPOSW_cs`, `toPOSW_lt`) characterize the projection. The Stalnakerian assertion principle ("asserting `p` makes `p` informationally necessary") is derived from `Core.Mood.POSW.boxCs_plus_self` via the bridge: `toPOSW_assertion_eq_plus` proves `(K.assertionUpdate p a).toPOSW.cs = (K.toPOSW.plus p).cs` (one-line `simp` + `And.comm` ŌĆö the assertion update *is* POSW's `plus` after projecting); `boxCs_after_assertion` then chains them to derive `(K.assertionUpdate p a).toPOSW.boxCs p`. The CG storage stays as `List (W ŌåÆ Prop)` (move-tracking history is preserved); only the projected POSW substrate is exposed for properties about the context set itself. This completes the 7-PR mood-API unification arc started in PR A; the remaining gap is wiring directive updates through `POSW.star`, which would require deriving a preference ordering from goals (deferred ŌĆö a substantive Cohen-style modeling decision rather than mechanical bridging).

## [0.229.885] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR F (rename local `ClauseType` enums to disambiguate from `Core.Grammar.ClauseForm`).** Six files defined a local `inductive ClauseType` whose name collided with `Core.Grammar.ClauseForm` (the form-level matrix-vs-embedded distinction renamed in PR D) and its `Core.Mood.ClauseType` cousin (the force ├Ś verbal-mood pairing). Audit revealed that all six are *orthogonal* to clause form ŌĆö they classify embedded-complement subtype, voice construction, argument structure, or fine-grained question subtypes that `ClauseForm` does not make distinctions among. The right fix is naming, not refit: each enum gets a more specific name reflecting what it actually classifies. Six renames: `Fragments/Mixtec/SMPM/Basic.lean` `ClauseType` ŌåÆ `EmbeddedClauseType` (finite vs subjunctive embedded complements; consumer `Phenomena/Control/Studies/Ostrove2026.lean` updated); `Fragments/Malayic/VoiceSystem.lean` `ClauseType` ŌåÆ `VoiceConstruction` (active/diPassive/barePassive/objectExtraction; consumer `Phenomena/FillerGap/Studies/ErlewineSommerlot2025.lean` updated); `Phenomena/Possession/Studies/AissenPolian2025.lean` `ClauseType` ŌåÆ `ArgumentStructureClass` (unaccusative/transitive/unergative ŌĆö vP-layer presence); `Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/Westergaard2009.lean` `ClauseType` ŌåÆ `V2ClauseType` (closest to `ClauseForm` shape but adds exclamative + embedded-decl that `ClauseForm` doesn't model); `Phenomena/Focus/DiscourseOnly.lean` `ClauseType` ŌåÆ `DiscourseOnlyClauseType` (canonical/biased/rhetorical question subtypes); `Theories/Semantics/Attitudes/Preferential.lean` `ClauseType` ŌåÆ `HighlightingClauseType` (declarative/polar-interrog/constituent-interrog for highlighting analysis). No `Core.Grammar.ClauseForm` indexed-inductive refits attempted ŌĆö `ClauseForm` lacks `exclamative` and several finer-grained question subtypes, so a partial refit would produce more friction than the rename. Naming-only change; no semantic content moved.

## [0.229.884] - 2026-04-18

### Added
- **`Theories/Phonology/Alternation.lean`** ŌĆö canonical SPE-style tier-driven alternation schema. `TierRule ╬▒` bundles a `Core.Tier ╬▒ ╬▒` projection, a `Side` (left/right context), a triggering natural-class predicate `targetIsContext : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Bool`, an Agree/Disagree `Relation`, a partial feature extractor `featureValue : ╬▒ ŌåÆ Option Bool` (honestly partial ŌĆö no junk-default sentinels), and an Elsewhere `default : Option Bool`. `apply` consumes both pre- and post-context lists; `applyAt` is the left-only convenience used by Belth's Latin / Turkish-VH / Finnish-VH rules. `Relation.flip_flip` and `flipRelation_flipRelation` are the involutivity simp lemmas. `id_tier_left_is_strict_local` proves the strict-locality special case (when the tier is the identity, the rule reduces to scanning the raw input ŌĆö Belth's eq. 49b voicing-assimilation pattern). This is the single canonical home for tier-driven alternation rules; downstream theories (harmony, Latin liquid dissimilation, Turkish/Finnish VH) compile to or wrap it.
- **`Core.Tier.lastWith` / `Core.Tier.firstWith`** (`Core/StringHom.lean`) ŌĆö derived helpers for "the last/first tier-projected segment matching predicate `q`". Used by `TierRule.apply` to locate the triggering context. Pure conveniences over `(apply T xs).filter q |>.getLast?` / `.head?`; no new abstraction.
- **`HarmonySystem.toTierRule` bridge** (`Theories/Phonology/Harmony/Defs.lean`) ŌĆö Rose-Walker harmony systems compile to `TierRule` via the unified schema: tier projects out transparent segments (`Tier.byClass (┬¼ isTransparent)`), side mirrors `direction` (rightward/bidirectional Ōå” left context, leftward Ōå” right), `targetIsContext := isTrigger`, `relation := .agree`, `featureValue := s.spec sys.feature`. The downstream OT machinery (triggerValue/spreadSuffix/HarmonyConfig) is preserved unchanged ŌĆö the bridge documents the relationship between Rose-Walker and Belth-style schemas without replacing either. Bridge theorem `toTierRule_agrees_with_triggerValue` proves the two compute the same value when (a) direction is rightward, (b) no blockers in the stem, (c) triggers are not transparent. Private lemma `filter_through_byClass` extracts the tier-projection consistency step.
- **`Phenomena/PhonologicalAlternation/Studies/Belth2026.lean` lun-aris empirical-limit demonstration.** Two competing rules: `latinDissimCoarse` (Belth's [+cons] tier ŌĆö the schema in eq. 6) and `latinDissimFine` (Cser-style: [+cons] minus nasals). For `lunaris`, the coarse rule predicts `l` (the last [+cons] segment is /n/, [ŌłÆlat], Disagree ŌåÆ [+lat]) but the surface form is `r`; the fine rule excludes /n/ from the tier, so the last tier segment is /l/ (+lat), Disagree ŌåÆ [ŌłÆlat] = `r`. Theorems `lunaris_coarse_predicts_l_INCORRECT` and `lunaris_fine_predicts_r` exhibit the contrast; `coarse_fine_disagree_on_lunaris` joins them. Per-datum corpus (5 forms ├Ś 2 rules = 10 stimulus-contrast theorems via `decide`) plus `latinDissimFine_covers_all` (the fine tier is empirically adequate over the corpus) and `latinDissimCoarse_misses_lunaris` (the coarse tier is not). Also: `LatSeg.isLat : LatSeg ŌåÆ Option Bool` (L ŌåÆ none ŌĆö the underspecified target), replacing the prior junk-default `LatSeg.isLat L = false` sentinel; `LatSeg.fineTier` is the Cser-style projection. `latinFineOCP : Core.OT.NamedConstraint (List LatSeg)` instantiates `mkOCPOnTier` on the fine tier as the OT-side bridge to existing markedness machinery (plus `latinFineOCP_is_markedness`).

### Changed
- **`Theories/Learning/TolerancePrinciple.lean`** ŌĆö extracted `threshold_nonneg : ŌłĆ n, 0 Ōēż threshold n` as a public lemma (was inlined in `tolerates_zero`); `tolerates_zero` now reduces to `simpa [tolerates] using threshold_nonneg n`. Module docstring trimmed: removed the aspirational paragraph claiming the threshold function `╬Ė(n) = n / log n` is derived "from information-theoretic considerations" (it's a heuristic from @cite{yang-2016}; the file proves only the elementary monotonicity / nonnegativity properties of `tolerates`).
- **`Phenomena/PhonologicalAlternation/Studies/Belth2026.lean`** ŌĆö module docstring no longer cites equation/section numbers from memory (per CLAUDE.md "Do not cite equation, table, or section numbers from memory" rule). Earlier misattribution of the floralis/legalis [+cons] coarse-tier rule to Cser corrected to Belth (Cser's contribution is the [-nasal] refinement). Per-datum verification theorems use `decide` and structural reduction (no `native_decide`).

## [0.229.883] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR E.5 (German fragment extraction + theory file rename).** `Theories/Semantics/Mood/SentenceMoodUCI.lean` mixed two concerns: language-agnostic UC-style sentence-mood operators (DEONT, EPIS, the E modifier, HKNOW) and a German-specific clause-type taxonomy with mood compositions. Per the dependency-discipline rule (Theories ŌŖā Fragments ŌŖā Phenomena), per-language clause-type inventories belong in `Fragments/<Language>/`. New `Linglib/Fragments/German/ClauseTypes.lean` collects the German bits: `GermanClauseType : ClauseForm ŌåÆ Type` (the indexed inductive from PR E), `dassVLMood`/`v2DeclMood`/`v2InterrogMood`/`vlInterrogMood`, `GermanClauseType.moodStructure`, and the German-specific theorems (`every_clause_has_deont`, `imperative_lacks_epis`, `dassVL_matches_imperative`, `v2_vl_differ_only_in_hknow`, `no_german_echo`, `matrix_question_is_v2_interrog`, `embedded_question_is_vl_interrog`, `hknow_iff_matrix_question`, `matrix_question_has_hknow`, `declarative_fiber_underdetermines_mood`, `dassVL_is_pure_deontic`, `v2_vs_vl_interrog`, `imperative_equals_dassVL`). Theory file slimmed to `MoodContext`/`deont`/`epis`/`episModifier`/`hknow`/`MoodStructure`/`epis_preserves_truth` only. The theory file is also renamed: `Theories/Semantics/Mood/SentenceMoodUCI.lean` ŌåÆ `Theories/Semantics/Mood/Gutzmann.lean`, parallel to `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Iatridou.lean` (author-keyed naming for one-theorist frameworks). Namespace `Semantics.Mood.SentenceMoodUCI` ŌåÆ `Semantics.Mood.Gutzmann`. Consumers (`Fragments/German/ModalParticles.lean`, `Phenomena/SentenceMood/Studies/Gutzmann2015.lean`) gain a `Linglib.Fragments.German.ClauseTypes` import and an `open Fragments.German.ClauseTypes` line for `GermanClauseType`.

## [0.229.882] - 2026-04-18

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR E (`GermanClauseType` as `ClauseForm`-indexed inductive).** Rather than defining `GermanClauseType` as a flat enum with a separate forgetful function `toClauseForm : GermanClauseType ŌåÆ ClauseForm` and bridge theorems, `Theories/Semantics/Mood/SentenceMoodUCI.lean` now defines it as an *indexed inductive* `GermanClauseType : ClauseForm ŌåÆ Type`, with each constructor specifying the `ClauseForm` it refines (dassVL / v2Declarative / imperative Ōå” `.declarative`; v2Interrogative Ōå” `.matrixQuestion`; vlInterrogative Ōå” `.embeddedQuestion`). The "projection to `ClauseForm`" is now the type-level index ŌĆö no separate function, no bridge theorem, no `cases ct <;> rfl` proof obligation. Three structural consequences fall out of the indexing rather than requiring side conditions: (a) `GermanClauseType .echo` is uninhabited by typing (`no_german_echo : IsEmpty (GermanClauseType .echo)`) ŌĆö the German fragment's silence on echo questions is type-enforced; (b) `(ct : GermanClauseType .matrixQuestion)` is *exactly* `v2Interrogative` ŌĆö `cases` produces a single branch (`matrix_question_is_v2_interrog`); (c) `hknow_iff_matrix_question` is restated as a fact about the index (`hasHearerKnowledge Ōåö f = .matrixQuestion`). The `.declarative` fiber stays many-to-one ŌĆö `declarative_fiber_underdetermines_mood` shows that `(dassVL : GermanClauseType .declarative).moodStructure ŌēĀ (v2Declarative : GermanClauseType .declarative).moodStructure` (both sides have the *same* type by the indexing, sharpening the contrast). Consumers in `Fragments/German/ModalParticles.lean` and `Phenomena/SentenceMood/Studies/Gutzmann2015.lean` migrated: `licensedInClause`, `wohl_iff_epis`, `ja_denn_complementary`, `root_rule`, `wohl_derived_from_epis`, `ja_denn_partition` all gain an implicit `{f : ClauseForm}` parameter (the proof terms are unchanged ŌĆö `cases ct` still does the right thing). This is the mathlib-style way to encode "type X refines type Y": parameterize the refined type by the parent rather than bolting on a forgetful function afterward.

## [0.229.881] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR C+D (TTR `IllocForce` collapse + `ClauseType` ŌåÆ `ClauseForm` rename).** Two cleanups completing the form/force separation begun in PRs AŌĆōB. (PR C) `Theories/Semantics/TypeTheoretic/Discourse.lean`'s parallel four-way `IllocForce` enum (assertion/query/command/acknowledgement) eliminated in favor of `Core.Mood.IllocutionaryMood` ŌĆö the surface distinction is the declarative/interrogative/imperative slice plus a backchannel constructor; backchannels live in `Theories/Pragmatics/Dialogue/KOS/`, not in TTR. `ForcedSign.illoc` now has type `IllocutionaryMood`. (PR D) `Core/Grammar.lean`'s `ClauseType` (the *form*-level distinction: declarative / matrixQuestion / embeddedQuestion / echo) renamed to `ClauseForm` to disambiguate from `Core.Mood.ClauseType` (the *force* ├Ś verbal-mood pairing). The `clauseType` field on `MinimalPair`/`SentencePair` is preserved as-is ŌĆö only the inductive type renamed. Bridge functions in `Discourse.lean` renamed: `IllocutionaryMood.toClauseType?` ŌåÆ `toClauseForm?`, `IllocutionaryMood.fromClauseType` ŌåÆ `fromClauseForm`. Consumers updated: `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/{Inversion, Formal/Workspace}.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/HPSG/{Inversion, Core/Basic}.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/DependencyGrammar/Core/NetworkIntegration.lean`, plus docstring references in `Phenomena/WordOrder/Studies/Hudson1984.lean` and `Phenomena/FillerGap/Studies/Sag2010.lean`. Files with local `inductive ClauseType` enums (Mixtec/SMPM, Malayic/VoiceSystem, Westergaard 2009, Aissen-Polian 2025, Focus/DiscourseOnly, Attitudes/Preferential) intentionally untouched ŌĆö those are the targets of PR F.

## [0.229.880] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **Faust 2026 ┬¦11: Strict-CV hollow-root NCC asymmetry (eq. 13aŌĆōc).** New `Theories/Phonology/Templates.lean` infrastructure: `RootTemplateMatch.unfilledCSlots` (lists the C-slot indices left unfilled by all associations) and `RootTemplateMatch.violatesNCC` (the No-Crossing Constraint of @cite{goldsmith-1976}: an intruder at slot `i` violates NCC if any root association sits at slot `j > i`). Constraint definitions: `noCross : NamedConstraint` (binary markedness, plus `noCross_is_markedness` proof) and `fill : NamedConstraint` (FILL of @cite{prince-smolensky-1993}, binary markedness, plus `fill_is_markedness`). New `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean` ┬¦11 derives the asymmetry: ŌłÜsma INF [m├żsmat] (final-empty: [t]-intrusion at right edge satisfies template without crossing); ŌłÜsam INF [m├żsam] and ŌłÜhid INF [m├żhid] (medial-empty: hypothetical [t]-intrusion at medial slot would have to cross the final root association). Theorems: `amharic_sma_inf_satisfies`, `amharic_sma_inf_no_ncc`, `amharic_sam_inf_medial_unfilled`, `amharic_hid_inf_medial_unfilled`, `amharic_sam_inf_intrusion_violates_ncc`, `amharic_hid_inf_intrusion_violates_ncc`, plus `hollow_root_intrusion_asymmetry` (joint statement of the (13a) vs (13b/c) contrast).
- **Faust 2026 ┬¦12: Explicit OT tableau, *Misalign >> FILL.** Two `mkTableau` demonstrations on inductive candidate enums (matching the @cite{mccarthy-prince-1995} pattern in `Phenomena/Reduplication/`). (a) `KljCand` (spread/empty) for ŌłÜklj + Hebrew PST.3MSG: `kala_wins_under_misalign_over_fill` proves [kala] is selected under the empirical ranking; `kalal_predicted_under_reversed_ranking` proves the reversed ranking would (incorrectly) select \*[kalal], showing that *Misalignment dominance is doing the empirical work. (b) `TaQTiLCand` (spread/empty/intrude) for ŌłÜdmj + Hebrew taQTiL[+c]: both `tadmit_wins_under_misalign_over_fill` and `tadmit_wins_under_fill_over_misalign` show that the intrusion candidate is *strictly better* than both alternatives (0 violations on both constraints), so it wins under any ranking ŌĆö the formal statement of @cite{faust-2026}'s observation that intrusion lets the grammar "have it both ways".

## [0.229.879] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **Mood API unification, PR B (POSW substrate)** ŌĆö `Core/Mood/POSW.lean` introduces `POSW W`, the Portner 2018 substrate that unifies verbal mood, sentence mood, and modal flavor: a context set `cs : W ŌåÆ Prop` paired with a reflexive transitive ordering `lt`. Two updates: `plus` (`+`-update intersects `cs`, leaves `lt` alone ŌĆö assertion ├Ā la @cite{stalnaker-1978}) and `star` (`Ōŗå`-update refines `lt` by promoting `p`-worlds, leaves `cs` alone ŌĆö directive ├Ā la @cite{portner-2004}). Two necessity modals: `boxCs` (informational ŌĆö `believe`, declarative entailment) and `boxLt` (preferential ŌĆö `want`, deontic/bouletic). Algebraic facts: `plus_cs`/`plus_lt`/`star_cs`/`star_lt` simp lemmas; `updates_target_disjoint_components` is the mathematical content of @cite{portner-2018}'s unification thesis (`+` and `Ōŗå` touch *disjoint* POSW components); `boxCs_plus_self` records the Stalnakerian assertion principle (after `+`-updating with `p`, `p` is informationally necessary); `boxCs_mono`/`boxLt_mono`/`plus_cs_mono` capture standard upward monotonicity.
- **`Core/Mood/POSWTarget.lean`** ŌĆö `POSWTarget` enum (`informational | preferential | partition`) plus `HasPOSWTarget` typeclass with instances for `GramMood` (indicative Ōå” informational, subjunctive Ōå” preferential ŌĆö verbal mood; @cite{portner-2018} eq. 5) and `IllocutionaryMood` (declarative/exclamative Ōå” informational, imperative/promissive Ōå” preferential, interrogative Ōå” partition ŌĆö sentence mood; @cite{portner-2018} eq. 8). `mood_alignment_decl_ind` proves Portner's indicative principle (declarative force and indicative mood target the same POSW component on a canonical `ClauseType.declInd`); `mood_misalignment_polar_question` records the *intentional* misalignment of polar questions (interrogative force partitions `cs` while indicative mood is informational ŌĆö the interrogative reshapes the substrate rather than aligning with it).
- **bib entry** ŌĆö `portner-2018` (Mood, Oxford University Press, Oxford Surveys in Semantics and Pragmatics) added to `blog/data/references.bib`.

## [0.229.878] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
- **Stale `subjectTheta`/`objectTheta` documentation cleanup.** The `subjectTheta`/`objectTheta` label fields and `derivedSubjectTheta`/`derivedObjectTheta` accessors had already been removed from `VerbCore` (verbs now expose `subjectEntailments`/`objectEntailments : Option EntailmentProfile` as the authoritative proto-role representation), but two study-file docstrings still referred to the legacy fields by name. `Phenomena/PsychVerbs/Studies/Kim2024_UPH.lean` rewritten to describe the consistency check in terms of `causalSource` + `opaqueContext` + entailment profiles (the four-field architecture is now three: causal source, opacity, and the proto-role profiles). `Phenomena/ArgumentStructure/Studies/Kratzer1996.lean` module docstring rewritten to clarify that the Severing-vs-Lexicalist comparison machinery now lives in `VoiceTheta.lean` and operates on entailment profiles; the orphaned "removed Parts IŌĆōII" inline note retired (the deletion is recorded in this changelog and in MEMORY.md).

## [0.229.877] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
- **Mood API unification, PR A (`Core/Mood/` consolidation)** ŌĆö gathered the previously scattered mood-category files into a single mathlib-style namespace. Files moved: `Core/GrammaticalMood.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Mood/Basic.lean` (now `namespace Core.Mood`); `Core/ClauseType.lean` ŌåÆ `Core/Mood/ClauseType.lean` (now `namespace Core.Mood`). Files added: `Core/Mood/IllocutionaryMood.lean` (extracted from `Core/Discourse/IllocutionaryForce.lean`; carries `IllocutionaryMood` + `moodAuthority`); `Core/Discourse/Roles.lean` (extracted `DiscourseRole` + `resolveRole` to break the otherwise-circular Mood Ōåö Discourse dependency); `Core/Mood.lean` (re-export hub: `import Linglib.Core.Mood` pulls in all three subfiles). After the move, namespace mirrors directory: `Core.Mood.GramMood`, `Core.Mood.IllocutionaryMood`, `Core.Mood.ClauseType`, `Core.Mood.moodAuthority` all live where their file paths suggest.
- **`Core/Discourse/IllocutionaryForce.lean`** is now a strictly pragmatic-act file: `DirectionOfFit`, `SearleClass`, `PreparatoryCondition`, and the dot-method extensions `IllocutionaryMood.searleClass` / `IllocutionaryMood.directionOfFit` (the latter pair re-namespaced to `Core.Mood.IllocutionaryMood` so dot notation works on the relocated enum). The mood category itself, the discourse roles, and `moodAuthority` no longer live here.
- **`Core/Mood/ClauseType.lean`** gains a `UD.Mood ŌåÆ Core.Mood.ClauseType` bridge (`UD.Mood.toClauseType`) plus the projection theorems `Imp_is_imperative` and `Sub_is_subjunctive`, replacing the previous absence of any explicit UDŌåöClauseType connection.
- **`SAPMood.toIllocutionaryMood` (with the documented `subjunctive Ōå” promissive` hallucination) replaced by `SAPMood.toClauseType : SAPMood ŌåÆ ClauseType`** in `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/SpeechActs.lean`. The new bridge sends S&T's `.subjunctive` (configurational [-finite, hearer does NOT c-command content]) to `Ō¤©declarative, subjunctiveŌ¤®` rather than to Searle's commissive class ŌĆö the correct framework-agnostic projection. Convenience projection `SAPMood.toForce := toClauseType.force` preserved for backward-compat call sites. `seat_of_knowledge_agrees_with_mood_authority` now goes through `m.toClauseType.authority` instead of `moodAuthority m.toIllocutionaryMood`.
- All importers updated: `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Basic.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Conditionals/Iatridou.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Mood/Basic.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/Questions.lean`, `Theories/Syntax/Minimalism/SpeechActs.lean`, `Theories/Pragmatics/Assertion/Krifka.lean`, `Theories/Semantics/Modality/Assert.lean`, `Phenomena/Directives/Studies/RuytenbeekEtAl2017.lean`, `Phenomena/Conditionals/Studies/Stalnaker1975.lean`, `Phenomena/Complementation/Studies/Grano2024.lean`, `Fragments/French/Modals.lean`, `Fragments/German/ModalParticles.lean`, `Core/Discourse/Intentionality.lean`, `Core/Discourse/Scoreboard.lean`, plus `Linglib.lean`.

## [0.229.876] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **Faust 2026 "Intrusion as Template Satisfaction and the QaTaTŌĆōQaTa Problem in Semitic"** @cite{faust-2026} formalized as `Phenomena/TemplaticMorphology/Studies/Faust2026.lean`. Single alignment principle \*Misalignment ("a nonfinal root segment must not occupy the template-final slot") jointly derives (a) the Hebrew QaTaTŌĆōQaTa gap (\*[kalal] from ŌłÜklj blocked, [kala] surfaces, @cite{faust-2026} (4)), (b) Hebrew taQTiL templatic intrusion ([tadmit] from ŌłÜdmj via feminine-gender /t/, @cite{faust-2026} (10)), and (c) Amharic gerund [t]-intrusion ([f├żd╩żto] from ŌłÜfdj, @cite{faust-2026} (8)) ŌĆö with the verbal/nominal asymmetry attributed structurally to the n[+gen] vs Agr[+gen] split (cross-domain hookup to `Phenomena/Gender/Studies/Kramer2020.lean`). Eight per-derivation `decide` theorems plus three cross-derivation theorems (`hebrew_klj_spreading_blocked_by_misalignment`, `hebrew_intrusion_satisfies_template_without_misalignment`, `misalignment_predicts_all_four_cases`).
- **`Core/Morphology/ConsonantalRoot.lean`** ŌĆö single source of truth for consonantal roots as `Root ╬▒ := { segments : List ╬▒ }`, parametric over the segment type. Methods: `arity`, `isFinal`, `isNonfinal`, `biradical`/`triradical`/`quadriradical`, `finalSegment`, `segmentAt`. Mathlib-style: one carrier shared between phonology, morphology, and fragment lexica; no per-study re-stipulation. Distinguished from `Semantics.Verb.Roots.Root` (LexEntailment bundle) and `Core.Lexical.RootFeatures` (semantic quality dims).
- **`Theories/Phonology/Templates.lean`** ŌĆö McCarthy 1981 / Strict-CV (@cite{lowenstamm-1996}) templatic-morphology infrastructure: `CVSlot` (`.C | .V | .Cspec` for [+consonantal]-specified slots), `Template` (sequence of slots with `length`/`cCount`/`isFinalSlot`/`slotAt`), `AssocSource` (`.root | .intruder` ŌĆö the load-bearing distinction that makes \*Misalignment exempt sister-exponent intruders ├Ā la @cite{lowenstamm-2014}), `Association` (root-to-slot link, defaulting to `.root`), and `RootTemplateMatch ╬▒` carrier with derived predicates `isRootFinal`, `isMisaligned`, `allCSlotsFilled`, `satisfies`, `inBounds`. Defines the \*Misalignment constraint of @cite{faust-2026} (2) as `starMisalign := mkAlign "*Misalign" RootTemplateMatch.isMisaligned`. Generic theorems: `isMisaligned_empty`, `isMisaligned_intruder_only`, `starMisalign_is_markedness`.
- **`Fragments/Hebrew/ConsonantalRoots.lean`** ŌĆö Modern Hebrew root inventory typed by `Root String`: [j]-final triradicals (ŌłÜklj, ŌłÜdmj, ŌłÜbnj ŌĆö the QaTaTŌĆōQaTa trigger class) and non-glide-final controls (ŌłÜktv, ŌłÜsbr). Per-root `triradical = true` decide-theorems plus `klj_final_is_j`.
- **`Fragments/Amharic/ConsonantalRoots.lean`** ŌĆö Amharic root inventory typed by `Root String`: [j]-final triradicals (ŌłÜfdj, ŌłÜhid, ŌłÜsma, ŌłÜsam ŌĆö the [t]-intrusion class) plus ŌłÜsbr (control) and ŌłÜwdd (Faust's reanalysis of @cite{broselow-1984}'s "biradical" ŌłÜwd as triradical with identical final radicals). Includes `wdd_is_not_biradical` proving Faust's central reanalysis claim against the OCP-violating Broselow representation.
- **bib entries** ŌĆö `faust-2026`, `mccarthy-1981`, `broselow-1984`, `lowenstamm-1996`, `lowenstamm-2014` added to `blog/data/references.bib`.

## [0.229.875] - 2026-04-17

### Added
- **`Core/StringHom.lean`** ŌĆö unified erasing string-homomorphism abstraction. `StringHom ╬▒ ╬▓ := ╬▒ ŌåÆ List ╬▓` (general letterwise hom, lifted via `List.flatMap`); `Tier ╬▒ ╬▓ := ╬▒ ŌåÆ Option ╬▓` (the erasing case ŌĆö morphism in the Kleisli category of `Option`, lifted via `List.filterMap`). Constructors: `Tier.id'`, `Tier.empty`, `Tier.byClass`, `Tier.total`, plus Kleisli composition `(ŌłśŌé£)`. Monoid-hom laws (`apply_append`, `apply_nil`) hold definitionally; `apply_comp`, `id_comp`, `comp_id` round out the category structure. `applyI` carries source indices to match @cite{belth-2026}'s `proj(x, T)`.
- **`Theories/Phonology/Tier.lean`** ŌĆö phonology-flavored re-export of `Core.Tier` with `byFeature`, `byFeaturePred`, and `tonal` constructors. `Phonology.Tier ╬▒ ╬▓` is *definitionally* `Core.Tier ╬▒ ╬▓` so phonological constraint constructors (`mkOCP`) accept `Tier.apply T` directly with no adapter glue.
- **`mkOCPOnTier`** (`Phonology/Constraints.lean`) ŌĆö tier-driven OCP adapter: `mkOCPOnTier name T extract = mkOCP name (Tier.apply T Ōłś extract)`. Grounding theorem `mkOCPOnTier_eq_mkOCP : rfl`. Markedness-classification theorem `mkOCPOnTier_is_markedness`.

### Changed
- **`BasemapCorrespondence.tonalTier`** now grounds in `Phonology.Tier.apply Phonology.Tier.tonal` (the unified tier interface) instead of inlining `List.map TBU.tone`. New `tonalTier_eq_map` simp lemma re-exposes the historical map form for downstream proofs in `BasemapCorrespondence.lean` and `DominantCophAgreement.lean`.

## [0.229.874] - 2026-04-17

### Changed
